title: Xiao and Xiang Rivers
description: A detail from a long handscroll associated with the Southern Tang master, depicting the misted reaches of the Xiao and Xiang rivers in Hunan. Dong Yuan's soft hemp-fibre brushstrokes and graded ink washes established the canonical southern landscape mode followed for centuries.

title: Travelers Among Mountains and Streams
dateCreated: between circa 1000 and circa 1020 Song dynasty (960–1279)
description: A monumental hanging scroll in which towering peaks rise abruptly above a thread of waterfall, while travelers and pack mules trace a path across the valley floor. A landmark of Northern Song monumental landscape, the work uses dense raindrop texture strokes and a vertiginous vertical scale to express the Confucian ideal of nature dwarfing humanity.

title: Finches and bamboo
description: A handscroll of finches perched on slender bamboo, attributed to the Northern Song emperor and devoted painter Huizong. The work exemplifies the courtly bird-and-flower idiom: minute feather observation paired with the calligraphic restraint of monochrome bamboo.
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Imaginary tour through Xiao-xiang
description: A Southern Song handscroll, nearly four metres long, leading the viewer through the misted river country at the confluence of the Xiao and Xiang. Painted in monochrome ink on paper, it belongs to the contemplative literati tradition and is held by the Tokyo National Museum.

title: Dancing and Singing
description: A handscroll in ink and light colour showing villagers carousing along a path beside gnarled trees, with a vast misted distance left blank to the right. Ma Yuan, a leading Southern Song court painter, was known for his off-centre 'one-corner' compositions, anchoring figures to one edge while suggesting boundless space.

title: Dancing and Singing
description: A close detail of the figural group in Ma Yuan's handscroll, showing peasants linked in song with bent knees and raised arms rendered in supple, calligraphic line. The brushwork demonstrates the lean economy and 'axe-cut' angularity associated with Southern Song court painting.

title: Hungry Ghosts Scroll
description: A late Heian-period handscroll depicting gaki, the emaciated hungry ghosts of Buddhist cosmology, condemned by past greed to wander among the living unseen. Ink and colour on paper carry the doctrine in plain pictorial narrative, the suffering specters set against scenes of human everyday life in twelfth-century Japan.

title: Nine Dragons 九龍圖 九龙图
description: Nine dragons surge through clouds, mist, and crashing water across a long handscroll of ink and wash on paper. Chen Rong, a Southern Song scholar-official, painted the work in 1244; it is celebrated for its splashed-ink dragons and is held by the Museum of Fine Arts, Boston.

title: Early Autumn
description: A handscroll in ink and color on paper showing dragonflies, frogs, and aquatic plants beside a pond. Qian Xuan was a scholar-painter of the late Song and early Yuan, and the work pairs close natural observation with the lyric reserve of literati painting.

title: Eight Flowers
description: A Yuan-dynasty handscroll in ink and light colour on paper, parading eight flowering specimens across a blank silk-pale ground. Qian Xuan, a Song loyalist who refused to serve the Mongol court, cultivated a deliberately archaic boneless manner, letting outline and tinted wash carry the botanical likeness. Held in the Palace Museum, Beijing.

title: Eight Views of Xiao-Xiang
description: A section of the celebrated handscroll cycle Eight Views of the Xiao and Xiang Rivers, in which Muqi distils river, mist and distant peak into broad ink washes. The Chan-Buddhist economy of means deeply influenced later Japanese ink painting.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Traveling on the River in Snow
dateCreated: latter half of the 10th century AD
description: Cargo ships with stern-mounted rudders glide through a snow-stilled river, accompanied by a smaller craft. Painted in fine ink on silk, the work exemplifies early Song dynasty jiehua, the precise ruled-line technique used to render boats and architecture with engineering accuracy.

title: Dwelling in the Qingbian Mountains
description: A towering hanging scroll in ink on paper, its densely textured peaks built from layered hemp-fibre strokes and moss dots. Wang Meng's restless, near-trembling line gives the Qingbian range a vibrating, almost airless density emblematic of late-Yuan literati landscape.

title: Myriad Miles of the Yangtze River
description: An eleven-metre handscroll on silk that travels the Yangtze from its mountain source to the sea, opening section by section in the hand of the viewer. Long attributed to the Southern Song master Xia Gui, it strings together mist, gorges, fishing villages and city walls in characteristic axe-cut brushwork.

title: Durán Madonna
description: The Virgin sits enthroned beneath a Gothic canopy, holding the Christ Child who turns the page of a small book. Painted in oil on panel, the work shows van der Weyden's command of finely modelled drapery and saturated red, set against a flat gold-tooled ground in the manner of Netherlandish devotional images.

title: Saint Christopher
description: Saint Christopher wades through dark water bearing the Christ Child, his giant frame sharply lit against a wooded shore. Witz, working in Basel, was an early Northern realist whose granular textures and naturalistic landscape settings broke with International Gothic abstraction.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: The Battle of San Romano
description: From the three-panel cycle painted for the Bartolini Salimbeni and later Lorenzo de' Medici, this London panel shows Niccolo da Tolentino leading the Florentines against Siena. Uccello's foreshortened lances, fallen soldier and patterned ground are a famous early experiment in linear perspective.

title: Nuremberg chronicles - Dance of Death
description: A celebrated woodcut from Hartmann Schedel's Nuremberg Chronicle of 1493, showing capering skeletons in the late-medieval danse macabre tradition. Wolgemut's workshop produced one of the most ambitious printed-image programs of the incunabula period.

title: Portrait of a Young woman with a Winged Bonnet
description: A Netherlandish bust portrait of a young woman whose elaborate winged linen headdress frames a composed, downcast face. Rogier's precise contours, translucent skin and patient observation of folded cloth set the model for fifteenth-century Burgundian portraiture.

provenance.location: Paintings in the Gemäldegalerie, Berlin - Room IV

title: Deposition
description: The body of Christ is lowered from the cross within a shallow gilded niche, surrounded by ten mourners arranged in a tight, frieze-like rhythm. Van der Weyden's sharp drapery folds and tear-streaked faces compress the Passion into a sculpted relief of grief, a touchstone of Early Netherlandish painting.
credit: Originally from en.wikipedia ; description page is/was here . Source: http://www.kfki.hu/~arthp/html/w/weyden/rogier/01deposi/index.html

title: Seven Sacraments Altarpiece
description: A triptych setting the seven sacraments inside a vast Gothic church, with the Crucifixion at the center of the nave and the rites unfolding in the aisles. Van der Weyden orchestrates devotional precision, contemporary Netherlandish architecture, and an extraordinary system of inscribed banderoles.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"w/weyden/rogier/05sevens/0sevens"

title: A Foolish Virgin in Half-Figure
description: Print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Death of the Virgin
description: Print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Griffin
description: Print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Joseon-Kang Huian-Gosagwansudo
description: A Joseon scholar leans on a rocky outcrop, contemplating the flow of water below in the Daoist trope of the recluse-sage. Kang Hui-an, a fifteenth-century court official and painter, helped establish the Korean literati landscape idiom. The brushwork shows clear debts to early Ming academy masters in the Zhe school manner.

title: Madonna and Child with an Angel
description: An early devotional panel from Botticelli's apprenticeship in the orbit of Filippo Lippi, the Madonna and Child are accompanied by an attendant angel offering fruit. The soft modelling and delicate linearity announce the Florentine grace of his mature style.

title: Ornament with Owl Mocked by Day Birds
description: Collection Ornament & Architecture; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Peasant Family Going to Market
description: Print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Saint John on Patmos
description: Print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: St. Martin
description: Print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: St. Michael
description: Print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: The Madonna and Child in the Courtyard
description: Print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: The Third Wise Virgin
description: Print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Wild Woman Holding a Shield with a Lion's Head
description: Print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: The Tree Man
description: A drawing of the hollow tree-bodied figure that recurs in the right wing of the Garden of Earthly Delights, his cracked eggshell torso supported on legs that end in tiny boats. Long attributed to Bosch's own hand, the sheet is among the few surviving drawings linked to him.

title: Christus on the Cross with Mary and St John
description: A T-shaped cross holding the crucified Christ rises before a red cloth of honour and a stone wall, flanked by the white-robed Virgin and Saint John. The reduced palette and stark spatial setting strip away narrative detail, concentrating on grief and bodily presence in a manner unusual for mid-fifteenth-century devotional painting.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"w/weyden/rogier/14pieta/5crucifi"

provenance.collection: Royal Monastery of San Lorenzo de El Escorial
provenance.location: Royal Monastery of San Lorenzo de El Escorial

title: Crucifixion
description: The central panel of Mantegna's San Zeno altarpiece predella, showing Christ crucified between the thieves on a rocky knoll. Mantegna deploys archaeological architectural detail and sharp foreshortening, treating the figures with the chiselled severity of Roman relief sculpture.

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: Christ on the cross with Mary and Saint John the Evangelist
description: A stark devotional Crucifixion in which the swooning Virgin and grieving Saint John flank the cross against a flat red cloth and grey wall. Rogier strips the scene of landscape and narrative incident, concentrating the drama into sculptural silhouettes and restrained, almost monochrome colour.

title: Lofty Mount Lu 廬山高
dateCreated: Ming dynasty, 1467 明 成化丁亥
description: Towering peaks of Mount Lu rise in vertically stacked planes of ink, a hanging scroll painted by Shen Zhou as a birthday tribute to his teacher Chen Kuan. A founder of the Wu School of Ming literati painting, Shen here pays homage to the Yuan master Wang Meng's dense, crinkled brushwork.
credit: National Library of China. 如何欣赏中国山水画 . ( direct link )

title: Christ Bearing His Cross
description: An engraving of the procession to Calvary thronged with mounted soldiers, jeering onlookers and dogs winding across a vast horizon. Schongauer, the leading German printmaker before Dürer, brought a refined, silvery line and unprecedented compositional ambition to copperplate engraving.
credit: HQGtPypGGry5Rw at Google Cultural Institute , zoom level maximum

title: Hl. Georg im Kampf mit dem Drachen
description: Uccello's late panel of the chivalric saint lancing the dragon, with a tethered princess and a stylised storm gathering overhead. The artist's lifelong fascination with linear perspective is visible in the geometric grid of the cave and patterned ground.

title: Madonna and Child with the Young St. John the Baptist
description: Botticelli treats the popular Florentine subject of the infant Christ greeted by the young John the Baptist, a tondo-like intimacy enclosed within a vertical panel. The crisp contour drawing and Mary's downcast features are hallmarks of his Quattrocento workshop.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"b/botticel/1early/15virgin"

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: Monforte Altarpiece
description: An unfinished panel by the Ghent master, depicting the three kings paying homage to the Christ Child amid a richly textured architectural ruin. Van der Goes builds the scene with sober psychological gravity and minute Netherlandish detail in the brocades, gold vessels, and northern landscape glimpsed beyond.
credit: Flickr Raffaele pagani Taken on 22 August 2019

title: Print
description: An engraving showing the Virgin and Child on the donkey under a date palm, with angels shaking down its fruit while Joseph gathers the dates. Schongauer's fine, controlled burin work and tender narrative detail set the model for later northern printmaking, including the young Dürer.

title: The Return of Judith to Bethulia
description: The Hebrew heroine strides home with her maidservant after slaying Holofernes, the general's severed head balanced in a basket on the older woman's head. The small Uffizi panel pairs Old Testament tyrannicide with Botticelli's lyrical, wind-swept drapery.
credit: Botticelli : de Laurent le Magnifique à Savonarole : catalogue de l'exposition à Paris, Musée du Luxembourg, du 1er octobre 2003 au 22 février 2004 et à Florence, Palazzo Strozzi, du 10 mars au 11 juillet 2004. Milan : Skira editore, Paris : Musée du Luxembourg, 2003. ISBN 9788884915641

title: Vienna Diptych
description: A small devotional diptych pairing the Fall of Man with a Lamentation, in which Van der Goes channels acute emotional weight through pale, exhausted flesh and the slumped diagonal of Christ's body. The meticulous Eyckian observation is carried by a temperament closer to mournful introspection than ceremonial display.

title: Winter Landscape
description: A pair of seasonal landscapes in ink on paper by the Muromachi-period Zen monk-painter, with sheer rock faces, dwellings, and a solitary traveler picking his way through the cold. Sesshū's broken, axe-cut brushwork adapts Chinese Song models into a distinctly Japanese austerity.

title: Creation
description: When closed, the triptych shows the world on the third day of Creation in grisaille: a translucent globe enclosing a dark, watery earth, with God depicted small in the upper left. The muted tonality contrasts sharply with the saturated colour of the interior panels, framing the work as an unfolding cosmological drama.
credit: Originally uploaded to the English Wikipedia by w:User:Blankfaze .

title: Hl.Augustinus
description: Saint Augustine sits in his study, deep in thought before book, inkwell and astronomical instruments. Botticelli's fresco for the Florentine church of Ognissanti casts the Church Father as a Renaissance scholar, painted as a pendant to Ghirlandaio's Saint Jerome.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Idealised Portrait of a Lady
description: A profile bust of a young woman with elaborately plaited hair, long identified, however speculatively, with the Florentine beauty Simonetta Vespucci. Botticelli converts a Quattrocento portrait formula into a half-mythological nymph, the linear refinement balanced by jewels and serpentine ornament.

title: Nativity
description: An Adoration of the Shepherds set in a ruined Romanesque shed, the rough stonework and spiky straw rendered in the crisp, hatched line for which Schongauer was renowned. His Late Gothic prints circulated widely and were copied closely by the young Albrecht Dürer.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"s/schongau/nativit"

title: Schongauer Anthony
description: An engraving in which the hermit saint is hoisted into the air and tormented by a swarm of bristling, scaled, and beaked demons. Schongauer's burin work, with its dense parallel hatching and inventive monstrous anatomy, exerted a famous influence on the young Dürer.

title: St. Sebastian
description: Saint Sebastian stands bound to a fragmented classical column, his body bristling with arrows against a ruined ancient cityscape. Mantegna fuses humanist antiquarianism with Christian martyrdom, a recurring preoccupation in his Mantuan years.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Temptations of Christ
description: A fresco from the south wall of the Sistine Chapel staging all three temptations of Christ around a centrally placed temple based on Rome's Hospital of Santo Spirito. Botticelli folds the gospel narrative into a contemporary Roman setting alive with allegorical figures and Medici allusions.

title: The Garden of earthly delights
description: A panoramic triptych running from Eden through a teeming earthly paradise to a nightmarish hell, populated by hybrid creatures, oversized fruits and fantastical machinery. Bosch's moral allegory is rendered in jewel-bright oils on oak.

title: The Holy Family
description: The Virgin steadies the Christ Child as Joseph reaches up to gather pears from a slender tree, a quiet domestic conceit set in a walled garden. Schongauer's burin builds form through controlled parallel hatching, a technique that became foundational for Northern Renaissance engraving.
credit: JgEImTKh_ZlKTA at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Pallas and the Centaur
description: Botticelli pairs the chaste, armoured Pallas with a subdued centaur in a tempera allegory of reason mastering brute instinct. The work was made for the Medici and is rich with humanist symbolism, including the entwined diamond rings of Lorenzo's emblem.

title: Venus and Mars
description: Mars and Venus

title: Madonna Bardi
description: The enthroned Virgin nurses the Child between Saint John the Baptist and Saint John the Evangelist, set within a vaulted niche dense with lilies, palms and myrtle. Painted for the Bardi chapel in Santo Spirito, Florence, the panel pairs Botticelli's linear elegance with an unusually botanical, symbol-laden setting.

title: The Birth of Venus
description: The newborn goddess arrives at Cyprus on a scallop shell, blown ashore by Zephyr while a Hora prepares to robe her. Painted on canvas for a Medici villa and indebted to the antique Venus Pudica, the image fuses Neoplatonic philosophy with Botticelli's hallmark linear grace.
credit: Adjusted levels from File:Sandro Botticelli - La nascita di Venere - Google Art Project.jpg , originally from Google Art Project. Compression Photoshop level 9.

provenance.location: Uffizi Gallery - Room 11-12, Botticelli's, Florence, Italy

title: Annunciation
description: Painted for the Cestello convent in Florence, the panel shows Gabriel kneeling before a swaying Virgin whose body bends in a lyrical S-curve. The cool palette and tiled floor demonstrate Botticelli's late, more austere mode under the influence of Savonarola's preaching.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"b/botticel/22/81cestel"

title: Coronation of the Virgin
description: A predella scene from the San Marco altarpiece, showing John the Evangelist receiving his vision on the rocky island of Patmos. The miniature format allowed Botticelli's workshop to compress dramatic landscape and visionary light into a narrow horizontal panel.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"b/botticel/8smarco/21predel"

title: Lamentation over the Dead Christ with Saints
description: The dead Christ is supported across the Virgin's lap as Mary Magdalene clasps his feet and saints crowd the dark mouth of the tomb. Botticelli's late Lamentations, painted in the wake of Savonarola's preaching, abandon courtly sweetness for compressed, anguished figures.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"b/botticel/91late/01lament"

title: The Deposition
artist: Follower of Rogier van der Weyden
description: A close adaptation of Rogier van der Weyden's celebrated Descent from the Cross, with the limp body of Christ lowered amid a tightly grouped frieze of mourners. The sharp Netherlandish drawing, jewel-toned drapery, and shallow gilded space follow the prototype's compressed, sculptural staging.
credit: VAH5mmnlns3rfA at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The lamentation over the dead Christ
description: Christ's body is laid on a marble slab and seen in radical foreshortening from the soles of his pierced feet. Mantegna's hard, sculptural line and reduced palette turn the Lamentation into an exercise in perspectival shock.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"m/mantegna/2/dead_chr"

title: The Virgin and Child
description: A late workshop Madonna in the Florentine devotional tradition, the Virgin tenderly cradling the Christ Child against a quiet ground. The flowing contours and elongated hands are characteristic of Botticelli's late manner, when his style turned more austere under Savonarolan influence.

title: Portrait of a Man
description: A bust-length study of a weathered older man, painted with the linear precision and severe modelling for which the Cortona master was celebrated. Signorelli's interest in anatomical truth, sharpened by his fresco cycles at Orvieto, surfaces in every furrow of the sitter's face.

title: Squirrel
description: A watercolour and gouache study of two red squirrels rendered with near-zoological precision, each tuft of fur picked out in fine brush. The sheet belongs to Dürer's lifelong investigation of nature, alongside his hare and great piece of turf.

title: Prodigal Son amid the swine
description: Dürer's celebrated early engraving sets the parable in a meticulously observed German farmyard, the kneeling youth surrounded by snouting pigs and timber outbuildings. The print, made soon after his first Italian journey, fuses Northern realism with new Renaissance ideas of psychological inwardness.

title: The Ship of Fools
description: A surviving fragment of a dismembered triptych depicting passengers crammed into a small boat, drinking, singing, and grasping at food while their craft drifts aimlessly. Bosch borrows the late-medieval Narrenschiff allegory to lampoon clerical vice, populating the scene with a lute-strumming nun and a tonsured friar.

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: Academic Study of a Standing Woman
description: A pen study of a standing female nude seen from behind, holding a staff from which a veil falls. Made during Dürer's first Italian journey, the sheet shows him grappling with classical proportion and the contrapposto idealised by his Venetian contemporaries.

title: Beweinung Christi
description: A late Lamentation showing the dead Christ across the Virgin's knees, ringed by mourners in a steeply compressed group. Botticelli's tightly knotted figures and stark grief reflect the religious severity that gripped Florence in the Savonarola years.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: View of the Arco Valley
description: A topographical watercolour of the Arco valley in the southern Tyrol, made during Dürer's return journey from his first Italian trip. The fortified hilltop town, terraced vineyards, and outcrops of rock are observed with surveyor's precision, an early instance of independent landscape study in Northern art.

provenance.collection: Department of Prints and Drawings of the Louvre

title: Bath House
description: An early woodcut of six men relaxing around a Nuremberg bathhouse pump, several modelled on Dürer's friends and possibly himself. The crowded composition lets him show male anatomy from multiple angles in a vernacular setting.

title: Calumny of Apelles
description: Botticelli reconstructs a lost painting by the Greek master Apelles as described by Lucian: King Midas with ass's ears is approached by Slander, dragged forward by a sinister train of Envy, Deceit and Conspiracy, while naked Truth points to the heavens. The panel is a humanist defense of innocence against false accusation.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: SesshuToyo
description: An autumn landscape in the haboku (splashed-ink) idiom that Sesshū absorbed during his 1467 study trip to Ming China. Sparse, axe-cut brushwork conjures rocky cliffs and a lone traveller, leaving wide expanses of paper to read as mist — a hallmark of Muromachi suiboku ink painting.
credit: Emuseum Original uploader was ToraryuKawabata at en.wikipedia

title: The Flagellation
description: Flagellation of Christ
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the National Gallery of Art . Please see the Gallery's Open Access Policy .

title: Self-Portrait
description: Painted shortly after his first Italian journey, the twenty-six-year-old Dürer presents himself as a gentleman scholar in tasselled cap and silk-trimmed jacket rather than a craftsman. The Alpine landscape glimpsed through the window signals his new cosmopolitan ambitions for the German artist.
credit: Museo Nacional del Prado, Galería online

title: Self-Portrait
description: Dürer presents himself in courtly Italianate dress with striped cap, gloves, and a window onto Alpine peaks at his shoulder. The painting is an early proclamation of the Northern artist as gentleman-humanist, painted shortly after his first Italian journey.
credit: The Prado in Google Earth: Home - 7 th level of zoom, JPEG compression quality: 9.

title: The Four Horsemen
description: First Horseman of the Apocalypse Second Horseman of the Apocalypse Third Horseman of the Apocalypse Fourth Horseman of the Apocalypse
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the National Gallery of Art . Please see the Gallery's Open Access Policy .

title: Death and the Miser
description: The right wing of the dismantled Wayfarer triptych, now in the National Gallery of Art, Washington. A dying man hesitates between an angel at his pillow and a demon offering a sack of gold, while Death enters at the door — a memento mori rendered with the artist's cool, lapidary palette.

title: The Garden of Earthly Delights
description: Bosch's triptych unfolds from the Garden of Eden through a teeming central panel of nude pleasure-seekers to a hellscape of musical instruments turned instruments of torture. Painted for a Habsburg patron, its inventory of fantastical hybrids has resisted definitive iconographic decoding for five centuries.

title: The Trees have Ears and the Field has Eyes
description: A pen drawing illustrating the Flemish proverb that walls and woods betray secrets, with eyes scattered through the meadow and ears sprouting on tree trunks. The sheet, in the Berlin Kupferstichkabinett, is among the few autograph drawings linking Bosch's moralising imagination to his painted demonology.

title: Wing of a European Roller
description: A watercolor and gouache study of a severed roller's wing, its ultramarine, turquoise, and rust feathers laid out with near-scientific precision. The sheet exemplifies Dürer's habit of treating natural specimens as objects of sustained, meditative observation.

title: Nemesis
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the National Gallery of Art . Please see the Gallery's Open Access Policy .

title: Young Hare
description: A young hare crouches alert against a blank ground, its fur built up in fine watercolour and bodycolour strokes that distinguish guard hairs from softer underfur. Among the most exacting nature studies of the Northern Renaissance, the sheet even records the cross-barred reflection of a studio window in the animal's eye.

title: Christ on the cross with Mary and John
description: The Virgin and Saint John mourn beneath the crucified Christ in a windswept landscape that owes much to the young Cranach's time in Vienna. The agitated drapery and emotional pitch place the panel at the heart of the early Danube School.

provenance.location: Alte Pinakothek, upper floor, gallery II

title: Madonna and Child between St. Francis and St. Nicasius
description: A sacra conversazione altarpiece commissioned for the chapel of Tuzio Costanzo in Castelfranco Veneto, with the enthroned Virgin lifted high above SS. Francis and Nicasius. Giorgione's softly fused light and twilight landscape behind the parapet anticipate the lyrical mood of Venetian painting to come.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: The Large Piece of Turf
description: A watercolour and gouache study of a clump of meadow plants, dandelions and grasses, viewed at ground level with botanical exactitude. The work is a touchstone of Renaissance nature study and prefigures modern ecological imagery.

title: Adam and Eve
description: Dürer's celebrated copperplate engraving applies the canon of human proportion he had absorbed from Jacopo de' Barbari in Nuremberg. Adam and Eve flank the Tree of Knowledge amid a forest stocked with elk, ox, rabbit, and cat — emblems of the four humours soon to be unbalanced by the Fall.

title: Triptych of Temptation of St Anthony
description: The desert hermit Anthony is besieged by a phantasmagoria of hybrid demons, burning villages and blasphemous rites across three panels now in Lisbon. Bosch's most influential triptych, it codified the visual vocabulary of grotesque temptation that fed Bruegel and the later Surrealists alike.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"b/bosch/90anthon/1triptyc"

title: Adam and Eve
description: Two life-size oil panels showing the first humans isolated against black grounds, Dürer's first attempt to model nudes in the Italian manner after his second Venice trip. Each figure is treated as a study in idealized anatomy, paired across the diptych held by the Prado.
credit: Galería online del Museo del Prado de Madrid: Adán y Eva

title: Praying Hands
description: A brush study in white and grey on blue prepared paper, drawn as a detail for an apostle in the Heller Altarpiece. The folded hands have since detached from their context to become one of the most reproduced devotional images in Western art.

title: Sleeping Venus
description: A nude Venus reclines along a hillside, eyes closed, her body echoing the gentle curves of the landscape. Begun by Giorgione and finished after his death by Titian, who is thought to have added the drapery and setting, the picture launched the long line of reclining Venuses culminating in the Urbino Venus.
credit: Google Art Project: Home – pic Maximum resolution.

title: Adam and Eve in Paradise
description: An early treatment of the Fall by the Wittenberg court painter, set within a paradise teeming with stags, lions, and small game. Cranach's slender, mannerist nudes and crisp linear drawing reflect his proximity to Luther's circle, where the subject carried fresh theological weight.
credit: Fine Arts Museums of San Francisco

title: Escola de Atenas - Vaticano 2
description: Painted for Pope Julius II's private library in the Stanza della Segnatura, this fresco assembles the philosophers of antiquity beneath a vast coffered vault, with Plato and Aristotle striding through the central arch. Raphael's lucid one-point perspective and idealised figures became a touchstone of High Renaissance harmony.

title: Pastoral Concert
description: A Venetian poesia of two clothed gentlemen and two nude female figures, attended by music, in a pastoral landscape. Begun by Giorgione and likely completed by the young Titian, the picture established the lyrical pastoral genre that would echo through Manet's Déjeuner sur l'herbe.
credit: This file was derived from: Le Concert champêtre, by Titian, from C2RMF retouched.jpg :

title: Leda and the Swan
description: Leda and the Swan
credit: Own work Alvesgaspar Taken on 10 September 2015

title: Madonna under the fir tree
description: The Virgin cradles the Christ Child against a dark fir backdrop, a German alpine motif that replaces Italian landscape conventions with Saxon woodland. Cranach's panel, painted for Breslau Cathedral, balances tender intimacy against the spiky verticals of conifer needles, a hallmark of his court style at Wittenberg.

title: The Expulsion from Paradise
description: A small woodcut from the Small Passion series, showing an angel driving Adam and Eve from Eden through a narrow gate. Dürer compresses the drama into densely cut parallel hatching that gives the bodies sculptural weight.

title: The Martyrdom of Saint Barbara
description: Saint Barbara kneels as her own father raises the sword, the executioner's act that, according to legend, struck him down with lightning. The early panel shows Cranach still close to the dramatic, expressive line of the Danube School.
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Portrait of Pope Julius II
description: The aged della Rovere pope is seated in three-quarter view, beard untrimmed in mourning for the loss of Bologna, hands resting on the arms of his chair. Raphael's psychologically piercing portrait introduced a new informal type that would dominate papal imagery for over a century.

title: Studies for the Libyan Sibyl
description: A red-chalk preparatory study for the Libyan Sibyl on the Sistine Chapel ceiling, drawn from a male studio model whose torsion and shoulder muscles Michelangelo refined toward the finished figure. Detail studies of the toes and a turning head fill the sheet, now held by the Metropolitan Museum of Art.

title: The Creation of Adam
description: A fresco at the centre of the Sistine Chapel ceiling, in which God reaches across the void to a languid Adam. Michelangelo compresses the moment of ensoulment into the famously unmet gap between the two extended fingers.

title: Concert of Angels
description: A photographic record of the Concert of Angels panel from Matthias Grünewald's Isenheim Altarpiece (c. 1512–16), housed at the Musée Unterlinden in Colmar. The original shows angel musicians serenading the Virgin in jewel-coloured robes, part of the great folding altarpiece made for the monastery hospital of St Anthony.

title: The Massacre of the Innocents
description: Print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Knight
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the National Gallery of Art . Please see the Gallery's Open Access Policy .

title: Barbara Dürer
description: A charcoal portrait of the artist's mother drawn weeks before her death, capturing her gaunt cheekbones and sunken eye in unsparing detail. Dürer's inscription records her age and condition, turning the sheet into a private memorial as much as a likeness.

title: Fire in the Borgo
description: A fresco in the Vatican's Stanza dell'Incendio del Borgo, depicting the miraculous extinguishing of a fire in Rome's Borgo district by Pope Leo IV. Heroic nudes and a fleeing Aeneas-like figure carrying his father give the scene a deliberately classical cast.

title: Melencolia I
description: An engraving from Dürer's so-called Master Prints in which a winged figure broods amid scattered tools of geometry, a polyhedron, and a magic square. The image has been read as an emblem of artistic genius stalled by melancholic temperament.

title: Portrait of Baldassare Castiglione
description: Raphael portrays his friend, the courtier and author of The Book of the Courtier, in a restrained palette of grey, black, and pearly white. The composed three-quarter pose and steady gaze became a touchstone for European court portraiture, admired and copied by Rembrandt and Rubens.
credit: Photographed by Elsa Lambert of C2RMF on 2010-06-23, retouched from File:Balthazar Castiglione, by Raffaello Sanzio, from C2RMF.jpg , originally C2RMF: Galerie de tableaux en très haute définition : image page

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: Young Man Carrying an Old Man on His Back
description: A red-chalk drawing of Aeneas carrying Anchises from burning Troy, made as a study for the figure group in the Fire in the Borgo fresco. The athletic torsion and confident hatching show Raphael's response to antique sculpture and to Michelangelo.

title: Bindo Altoviti
description: A portrait of the young Florentine banker Bindo Altoviti, who turns over his shoulder to meet the viewer's gaze. The cool palette, soft modeling, and elegant turn of the head exemplify Raphael's late Roman portrait style.
credit: National Gallery of Art, Washington, D. C., online collection

title: Christ on the Mount of Olives
description: The only surviving copper printing plate from Dürer's hand, engraved with Christ praying in Gethsemane while the apostles sleep. Held by the Bamberg State Library, the worn matrix attests to the dense burin-work behind his late religious prints.

title: Landscape with Saint Jerome
description: Patinir places the penitent Saint Jerome amid a sweeping panoramic landscape of crags, rivers, and distant towns. The Flemish painter is credited with elevating landscape to a primary subject in Western art, the figure dwarfed by the world he renounces.

title: Raimondi Lucretia's suicide
description: An engraving after a design by Raphael showing the Roman matron Lucretia about to plunge a dagger into her breast. Raimondi's burin work helped establish reproductive printmaking as a vehicle for disseminating Italian Renaissance invention across Europe.

title: St Jerome in the Desert
description: The hermit saint kneels before his crucifix in a fissured wilderness, the lion at his side and a distant village dissolving into atmospheric blue. Patinir uses the eremitic theme to display his trademark world-landscape, with stratified geology dwarfing the figure of penitence.
credit: 1. Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"p/patenier/s_jerome" 2. GalleriX

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: The Hay Wagon
dateCreated: circa 1515 Prado from 1510 until 1516 Jheronimus Bosch - Visions of genius
description: The central panel of a triptych in the Prado, drawn from the Flemish proverb that the world is a haystack from which each takes what he can. Crowds claw at a giant cart of hay drawn by demons toward hell, Bosch's signature grotesques staging human greed with miniaturist precision.

title: The Judgment of Paris
description: The shepherd Paris hands the golden apple to Venus while river gods recline at the foreground edge. Known chiefly through Marcantonio Raimondi's engraving after Raphael's lost design, the composition shaped European depictions of the nude for centuries.

title: The Miraculous Draft of Fishes
description: A cartoon for the Sistine Chapel tapestry series commissioned by Pope Leo X, showing Christ blessing Peter and Andrew as their nets break under the weight of fish. The kneeling apostle and the cranes on the shore are among the most studied figures in Renaissance art.
credit: ywH0tF2CcmYUxA at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: A Prince of Saxony
description: A young Wettin prince stares out in a feathered cap and gold-chained jerkin, the kind of dynastic likeness Cranach produced in pairs for the Saxon nursery. The flat black ground throws the boy's pale features and finely drawn costume into relief.
credit: National Gallery of Art, Washington, D. C., online collection

title: Correggio
description: A standing woman in dark mourning dress holds a bowl in a wooded landscape, her gaze direct and reserved. Signed with the Latinised "Anton. Laet.", the portrait is sometimes identified as Veronica Gambara and marks Correggio's transition into a more refined, psychologically attentive manner.

title: Rest during the Flight to Egypt
description: Mary nurses the infant Christ in a meadow as Joseph rests nearby, the Holy Family settled into a sweeping panorama threaded with rivers and jagged blue rock formations. Patinir treats the devotional subject as a pretext for landscape, layering brown foreground, green middle ground, and azure distance.

title: La Fornarina
description: Long identified as the baker's daughter Margherita Luti, Raphael's lover, the sitter wears an oriental turban and a slim arm-band inscribed with the painter's name. The picture, completed near the end of Raphael's life, has the tactile sfumato modelling and direct erotic charge of his late Roman period.

title: Self-portrait with a friend
description: Raphael places himself behind a foregrounded companion who turns to point outward, the two men linked by a cascade of hands and gazes. The double portrait is among the late, most psychologically intricate works produced in his Roman studio.
credit: Retouched from File:Portrait de l'artiste avec un ami, by Raffaello Sanzio, from C2RMF retouched.jpg , originally C2RMF: Galerie de tableaux en très haute définition : image page

title: Crossing the River Styx
description: Charon ferries a soul across the underworld river, with paradise and a fiery hell flanking the panel in classic Netherlandish duality. Patinir, credited as the first dedicated landscape painter, expands the mythological subject into a panoramic geography of cliffs, water and atmospheric distance.

title: Landscape with the Destruction of Sodom and Gomorrah
description: Lot and his daughters flee toward foreground rocks while behind them the cities of the plain erupt in apocalyptic fire under a livid sky. Patinir treats the Old Testament cataclysm as a stage for tectonic landscape painting, his cool blue distances offset by the lurid orange of brimstone.

title: Samson battling with the lion
description: The Hebrew judge Samson straddles the lion and pries its jaws apart in the act recounted in Judges 14. Cranach treats the subject with the heraldic clarity and brilliant local colour favoured by his patron, the Elector of Saxony.
credit: 1. Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"s-e.html?/html/c/cranach/lucas_e/06/3samson" 2. nevsepic.com.ua

title: Shōshō Hakkei - Enji Banshō
description: Ink-painting from the Zen monk-painter's interpretation of the Eight Views of Xiao and Xiang, the canonical Chinese landscape suite of distant temples, mist and migrating geese. Shōkei trained in Kamakura under Geiami and brought a flecked, slightly nervous brush to the Muromachi suiboku tradition.

title: Head of an Old Man
description: A late brush drawing of a bearded ninety-three-year-old man Dürer met in Antwerp, prepared as a study for a never-executed Saint Jerome. The economical washes catch sagging skin and patriarchal beard with the artist's signature analytic eye.

title: Saint Christopher Facing Left
description: print medium QS:P186,Q11060274
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: The Body of the Dead Christ in the Tomb
description: Holbein — Dead Christ

title: Landscape with St. Christopher
description: Saint Christopher wades across a wide river bearing the Christ Child on his shoulder, dwarfed by a panoramic landscape of jagged rocks, distant towns and luminous water. The sweeping high viewpoint is characteristic of Patinir, often credited as the first specialist in Western landscape painting.
credit: 1. Escorial, Real Monasterio de San Lorenzo 2. pinturaycomunicacion.blogspot.de

provenance.collection: Royal Monastery of San Lorenzo de El Escorial
provenance.location: Royal Monastery of San Lorenzo de El Escorial

title: Coat of Arms of Albrecht Dürer
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Portrait of Federico II Gonzaga
description: A court portrait of the Marquis (later Duke) of Mantua, shown in slashed silk and resting one hand on a small white dog as an emblem of fidelity. Titian sets the half-length figure against a neutral ground, balancing courtly polish with naturalistic detail.

title: Adam and Eve
description: The first parents stand on either side of the apple tree, slim and pale against a forest dense with deer, lions and stags. Cranach made the Fall a workshop staple, prized by Lutheran patrons as a meditation on sin made flesh.

title: Cupid Complaining to Venus
description: A naked Venus wears only a wide-brimmed hat and gold chain while a small Cupid, stung by bees, complains of the honey he has stolen. Cranach often paired the subject with a Latin epigram warning that pleasure is mixed with pain.

title: Danse Macabre. I. The Creation
description: The opening plate of Holbein's Dance of Death, designed in Basel in the 1520s, shows the Creation that frames the cycle's moral argument. Cut by Hans Lützelburger from Holbein's miniature designs, the series compresses scriptural and social satire into woodblocks scarcely larger than a thumbprint.
credit: Taken from Die imposante Galerie , originally uploaded by Soilwork

title: Portrait of Hieronymus Holzschuher
description: A late portrait of the Nuremberg patrician and councilman, rendered with almost forensic attention to white curling hair and weathered skin. Painted the year before Dürer's Four Apostles, it distils a lifetime of observing individual character.
credit: Online catalogue of Staatliche Museen zu Berlin

title: Tuft of Cowslips
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the National Gallery of Art . Please see the Gallery's Open Access Policy .

title: Lovers
description: Identificatie Titel(s): Liefdespaar Objecttype: prent Objectnummer: RP-P-OB-12. 233 Catalogusreferentie: The Illustrated Bartsch 14. Opmerking: tweede staat van drie Opschriften / Merken: verzamelaarsmerk, verso linksonder, gestempeld: Lugt 240 Omschrijving: Een liefdespaar zittend in een bos.

title: Portrait of the painters' wife and their two oldest children
dateCreated: between 1528 and 1529 / between circa 1528 and circa 1529
description: A family portrait, traditionally identified as Holbein's wife Elsbeth with their two eldest children, painted soon after his return to Basel from England. The careworn faces are recorded with Northern Renaissance candour; the panel was later cut down and mounted, accounting for its arched silhouette.

title: Allegory of Law and Grace
description: A programmatic Lutheran image divided down the middle: on the left, Death and the Devil drive a sinner toward Hell beneath the Tablets of the Law; on the right, John the Baptist directs the same figure to the crucified Christ. Cranach worked closely with Luther in Wittenberg, and this composition became a template for Reformation visual theology.

title: The Battle of Alexander at Issus
description: Commissioned by Duke William IV of Bavaria, Altdorfer compresses Alexander's 333 BC victory over Darius III into thousands of tiny figures swarming under a cosmic sky. The Danube School master treats landscape and weather as protagonists, the curving horizon and apocalyptic sun lifting the battle into world-historical drama.
credit: 1. Bridgeman Art Library : Object 879692 2. Google Arts & Culture

title: Hercules Relieving Atlas of the Globe
description: Hercules takes the celestial globe onto his own shoulders so that Atlas may fetch the apples of the Hesperides, an Antique fable updated in slim, elongated nudes. Cranach's late style favours linear elegance and a smooth, almost enamel-like finish over Italianate volume.
credit: National Gallery of Art, Washington, D. C., online collection

title: Judith with the head of Holofernes
description: The Hebrew widow displays the severed head of the Assyrian general she has just decapitated to save her city, set against Cranach's signature inky black ground. Pearled jewels and a slashed velvet bodice betray the panel's origin as a courtly Saxon trophy rather than a devotional image.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"c/cranach/lucas_e/06/2judith2"

title: Moses and the Pillar of Cloud
artist: Workshop of Lucas Cranach the Elder
description: Moses leads the Israelites through the wilderness beneath the divine pillar of cloud described in Exodus 13. A workshop production from the busy Cranach studio, the panel keeps the master's compact figure types and bright, decorative landscape.
credit: 1. M.S. Rau, New Orleans 2. Stockholms Auktionsverk, Stockholm, 7 December 2021, Lot 345 via auctionet

title: The Golden Age
description: Naked figures dance in a ring beneath fruit trees in the mythical age of innocence described by Hesiod and Ovid. Cranach turns the classical theme into a playful, decorative idyll suited to the humanist tastes of the Saxon court.
credit: 1. Unknown source Unknown source 2. Nasjonalgalleriet, Presse, aktuelle Utstillinger i Oslo

title: Portrait of the Elector John Frederic the Magnanimous of Saxony
description: Johann Friedrich, the Lutheran champion who would lose his electorate after the battle of Mühlberg, shown here in jewelled chain and flat black cap. Cranach was court painter at Wittenberg for half a century and produced numerous variants of this loyal Reformation portrait.
credit: This file was derived from: Lucas Cranach d. Ä. 044.jpg :

title: Leda and the Swan
description: An erotic mythology painted for Federico II Gonzaga of Mantua as part of a series of Loves of Jupiter intended as a gift to Charles V. Correggio orchestrates Leda's encounter in soft chiaroscuro and a continuous lateral rhythm of figures, the smoky modelling that would inspire Parmigianino and the Carracci.

title: Adam and Eve in paradise
description: Adam reaches for the apple Eve has plucked while the serpent watches from the branches above, the trees of paradise teeming with creatures. The panel is among Cranach's most fully developed treatments of the subject, painted for the Lutheran market that prized its theological clarity.

provenance.location: German painting from the 13th to 16th centuries, room III

title: Madonna and Child with Angels
description: Known as the Madonna of the Long Neck, this unfinished altarpiece is a touchstone of Italian Mannerism. Parmigianino elongates the Virgin's anatomy and tilts the spatial logic of the column and miniature prophet for an unsettling, refined elegance.

title: Madonna mit dem langen Hals
description: Parmigianino's celebrated late altarpiece, in which the serpentine Madonna cradles an unusually elongated Christ Child. The exaggerated proportions and ambiguous architecture exemplify the Mannerist taste for grace pushed beyond classical norm.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Caritas
description: The personification of Christian charity stands nude, suckling one infant while two others cluster at her feet, the traditional emblem of the virtue. Cranach reduced the iconography to slender contours and unbroken flesh, producing the composition in many small-scale variants.

provenance.collection: Royal Museum of Fine Arts Antwerp
provenance.location: Royal Museum of Fine Arts Antwerp

title: Justice
description: Justice personified as a nude woman holding sword and scales, a humanist subject Cranach's Wittenberg workshop produced in many versions for Saxon patrons. The sinuous body type and dark ground are signatures of his late mythological and allegorical nudes.

title: Reclining Venus
description: An exact-size replica of Titian's Venus of Urbino that Ingres made during his Florentine years, copying directly from the original at the Pitti Palace. The exercise was both a personal homage to the High Renaissance and a way of carrying the Venetian model home to France.
credit: Walters Art Museum : Home page Info about artwork

title: Pine Trees / right hand screen
description: The right wing of the celebrated Pine Trees screens, designated a National Treasure of Japan. Tōhaku, founder of the Hasegawa school, paints the pines with rapid sumi ink in pale wash and dry brush, leaving vast areas of unpainted paper to suggest swirling mist — a high point of Momoyama monochrome ink.

title: Hunting near Hartenfels Castle
description: The Saxon court drives stags into the Elbe before the towers of Schloss Hartenfels, with the Elector and his guests stationed at crossbows along the bank. Cranach made several versions of this hunt, half topographical record and half dynastic propaganda.
credit: 1./2. The AMICA Library 3. Unknown source Unknown source 4. The Cleveland Museum of Art

title: Anatomy
description: A skeletal study from Vesalius' Fabrica, the bones articulated in a contemplative pose against an architectural ruin. The volume's woodcuts carried Renaissance memento-mori conventions into the empirical project of anatomy.

title: On the Fabric of the Human Body
description: A muscleman écorché stands in a Paduan landscape, layers of musculature peeled back to reveal underlying anatomy. The plate, cut for Vesalius' 1543 treatise, was carved in the Titian workshop circle and revolutionised medical illustration by binding observation to a humanist visual language.

title: On the Fabric of the Human Body
description: Another of the Fabrica's flayed figures, posed against a continuous Veneto skyline that links the seven plates into a single panorama when arranged in sequence. Vesalius insisted on direct dissection, and the woodcut renders the layered musculature with unprecedented anatomical accuracy.

title: On the Fabric of the Human Body
description: A deeper écorché stage from the Fabrica, with skin and superficial muscle stripped away to expose the trunk and limbs. Printed in Basel by Oporinus, the plates set a new standard for the integration of artistic skill and dissection-based medical knowledge.

title: The venous and arterial system of the human body. Engraving
description: The venous and arterial system of the human body. Engraving by J. Wandelaar, 1726, after a woodcut, 1543.

title: The fountain of youth
description: Aged women are carried into a stone basin on the left and emerge as nubile young brides on the right, an allegory of rejuvenation popular at the Saxon court. Cranach unfolds the fantasy across a panoramic landscape with the crisp outlines and porcelain flesh tones of his Wittenberg workshop.

provenance.location: German painting from the 13th to 16th centuries, room III

title: Equestrian Portrait of Charles V
description: The Habsburg emperor rides out armoured in pike and lance to commemorate his 1547 victory over the Schmalkaldic League at Mühlberg. Painted in Augsburg and now in the Prado, Titian's portrait fixed the equestrian image of sovereign authority that Velázquez and Van Dyck would inherit.
credit: The Prado in Google Earth: Home - 7 th level of zoom, JPEG compression quality: Photoshop 10.

title: The Conversion of Mary Magdalene
description: An early devotional canvas in which Christ raises the kneeling Magdalene from her former life. Veronese's Venetian instinct for sumptuous fabric, architectural staging, and silvery light is already evident in this youthful composition.

title: Google Art Project
description: Courtiers picnic beneath flame-coloured maples on the slopes of Mount Takao, eating, dancing and flirting in vignettes spread across the gold-ground screen. One of the earliest surviving Japanese genre paintings, it shifts the Kano workshop's brush from Chinese-derived ink subjects toward the everyday life of contemporary Kyoto.
credit: oQF0nQM_PVBZeQ at Google Cultural Institute zoom level Scaled down from second-highest

title: St Christopher Bearing the Christ Child
description: The giant ferryman wades a river bearing the Christ Child on his shoulder, the saint's labor dwarfed by Patinir's signature world-landscape stretching to a hazy blue horizon. The Antwerp master pioneered this compositional formula in which figures recede before vast geological panoramas.

title: Susanna and the Elders
description: The chaste Susanna recoils as two elders intrude on her bath, the biblical pretext for a study of startled flesh against shaded foliage. Carracci stages the assault with the firm contour and idealised anatomy he was simultaneously developing for the Farnese ceiling.

title: The Creation of the Animals
description: A photographic record of Tintoretto's Creation of the Animals (c. 1551–52) for the Scuola Grande della Trinità, now in the Gallerie dell'Accademia, Venice. God the Father sweeps across the sky propelling birds, fish, and beasts into existence in a single rushing diagonal.

title: The Fall of Man
description: Adam and Eve beneath the Tree of Knowledge, the serpent rendered as a child clutching the fatal fruit. Titian gives the Genesis subject a sculptural, classicizing weight, drawing on Northern prototypes filtered through Venetian colorism.

title: Venus and organist and little dog
description: Venus reclines nude on a draped bed, her small dog beside her, while an organist turns from his keyboard to gaze at her body. Part of a series Titian produced for European courts, the composition stages a debate between sight and sound as competing routes to the experience of beauty.

title: Juno Showering Gifts on Venetia
description: A ceiling allegory painted for the Doge's Palace, in which Juno rains crowns, jewels, and ducal regalia onto a personified Venice. Veronese exploits the di sotto in sù viewpoint to send the goddess plunging through illusory clouds.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"/v/veronese/08/dieci/3dieci"

title: Venus and Adonis
description: Venus, seen from behind, twists to embrace the youthful hunter Adonis as he pulls away with his hounds towards the hunt that will kill him. One of the mythological poesie made for Philip II of Spain, the canvas exemplifies Titian's late, freely brushed handling of flesh and landscape.

title: Diana and Callisto
description: One of Titian's mythological poesie made for Philip II of Spain, depicting Diana's discovery of the pregnant nymph Callisto among her bathing companions. Diagonal drapery, a marbled fountain and richly varied flesh tones articulate the moment of accusation.

title: The Fight Between Carnival and Lent
description: A teeming village square stages an allegorical joust between a barrel-riding Carnival, armed with a spit of meat, and a gaunt Lent on a church cart. Bruegel packs the panel with proverbs, Shrovetide revels, and Lenten almsgiving in a panoramic moral inventory of Netherlandish life.
credit: 2. Kunsthistorisches Museum Wien, Bilddatenbank . 1. The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Danaë
description: Danaë reclines nude on her bed as Jupiter descends as a shower of gold, while an old nursemaid stretches up an apron to catch the falling coins. Among Titian's poesie for the Spanish court, the scene contrasts youthful flesh and wizened greed in loose, atmospheric paint.
credit: Official gallery link Enlace a la galería oficial

title: The Rape of Europa
description: A late poesia for Philip II showing the Phoenician princess carried across the sea on the back of Jupiter disguised as a bull. Loose, atmospheric handling and a glowing pink-gold sky transform the abduction into a turbulent seascape.

title: Marriage at Cana
description: Christ's first miracle, the turning of water into wine, is staged at the far end of a steeply receding banquet table in the refectory of Santa Maria della Salute, Venice. Tintoretto exploits the long perspective to merge the painted feast with the friars' real dining hall.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"t/tintoret/3_1560s/2cana"

title: Self-Portrait
description: A late self-portrait of the Venetian master, shown in three-quarter profile with brush in hand and the gold chain of imperial favor across his black robe. The looser handling and reserved gaze typify Titian's introspective final decade.

title: The Triumph of Death
description: An army of skeletons sweeps across a scorched landscape, herding the living towards a vast coffin while gallows, wheels and burning ships fill the horizon. The panel translates the late-medieval danse macabre into a panoramic moral allegory, indebted to Bosch but rendered with Bruegel's denser observation of human types.

title: Allegory of Winter
description: From Arcimboldo's Four Seasons cycle painted for the Habsburg court, Winter is a gnarled tree-stump head with bark for skin, ivy for hair and a lemon and orange hanging from broken branches. The conceit flattered the emperor as the season that endures and rules over all others.
credit: Unknown source Unknown source , scanned by uploader

title: Landscape with the flight into Egypt
description: The Holy Family threads a precipitous mountain path while a vast world landscape opens beneath them, with rivers, cliffs, and a shadowed pagan idol toppling on a column. Bruegel's panoramic Weltlandschaft tradition dwarfs the sacred travelers within Alpine and Flemish topographies.

title: The Summer
description: Summer rendered as a profile head built from ripe produce — peach cheeks, a cucumber nose, garlic teeth, with a straw collar inscribed with the artist's signature. Part of the Four Seasons series for Maximilian II, it pairs Mannerist invention with sharp still-life observation.
credit: EAHRfTFyVNYeEA at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Tower of Babel
description: King Nimrod inspects stonemasons in the foreground as the colossal tower rises above a Flemish port, its arcades modeled on the Colosseum. The painting marries Genesis with a sober warning about overreaching ambition, rendered with engineering exactitude.
credit: Levels adjusted from File:Pieter_Bruegel_the_Elder_-_The_Tower_of_Babel_(Vienna)_-_Google_Art_Project.jpg , originally from Google Art Project.

title: Winter Landscape with a Bird Trap
description: Villagers skate and slide on a frozen river beside snow-covered houses, while at right a propped board baited with grain forms a trap for unwary birds. One of around a hundred surviving versions after Pieter Bruegel the Elder's composition, it pairs winter genre with a quiet emblem of human precariousness.

title: Allegory of Wisdom and Strength
description: Divine Wisdom, crowned and turned upward, stands beside a brawny Hercules whose attention is fixed on the worldly riches at his feet. The pairing, painted for the Habsburg court, sets celestial contemplation against earthly power in characteristic Venetian color.
credit: 2. Frick Collection : online database: entry 277 1. Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"v/veronese/10/1allego3"

title: Massacre of the Innocents
description: Soldiers ransack a snowbound Flemish village in a transposition of Herod's biblical slaughter into the contemporary Low Countries under Spanish occupation. In the Royal Collection version many of the slain children were overpainted as bundles, geese, and game by later hands.
credit: qgGZ6pq1mTaabw at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: monthly cycle
description: Returning hunters and their lean dogs trudge across a snowy ridge above frozen ponds where villagers skate, the panel that anchors Bruegel's celebrated cycle of the months commissioned by the Antwerp merchant Niclaes Jonghelinck. Now in the Kunsthistorisches Museum in Vienna, it set the template for European winter landscape painting.
credit: WgFmzFNNN74nUg at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Family of Darius before Alexander
description: The defeated Persian queen and her household kneel before Alexander the Great after Issus, in a vast frieze-like composition acquired by London's National Gallery in 1857. Veronese fills the picture with contemporary Venetian costume, courtly architecture, and a noble gesture of clemency.

title: Fire
description: Part of the Four Elements cycle painted for Maximilian II in Vienna, Fire builds a head from candles, oil lamps, flintlocks and burning kindling. Arcimboldo's witty composite portraits flatter the Habsburgs as masters of the elements while showcasing his Mannerist appetite for paradox.

title: The Air
description: Birds of every order roost on and form a human profile, one of four allegories of the elements painted for the Habsburg court. Arcimboldo's composite portraits flatter the encyclopaedic ambitions of Maximilian II while showing off the painter's natural-history library.
credit: Arcimboldo. 1526-1593. Ausstellungskatalog des Kunsthistorischen Museums Wien 2008, p. 147. ISBN 978-3-85497-118-4 , Scanned by uploader

title: The allegory of Water
description: A profile fashioned from fish, crustaceans, coral and pearls, completing the Vienna set of the four elements. Arcimboldo treats the composite as both witty courtly device and a serious display of zoological knowledge.
credit: Arcimboldo. 1526-1593. Ausstellungskatalog des Kunsthistorischen Museums Wien 2008, p. 1152. ISBN 978-3-85497-118-4

title: The Earth
description: A bust assembled from terrestrial mammals — stag, lion, hare, ox — interlocking to form a crowned Habsburg profile. The conceit, painted at the Prague court, casts imperial dominion as a literal incorporation of the natural world.
credit: Arcimboldo. 1526-1593. Ausstellungskatalog des Kunsthistorischen Museums Wien 2008, p. 1152. ISBN 978-3-85497-118-4 , Scanned by uploader

title: The Ill-matched Pair
description: A satirical Antwerp moral subject in which a young woman fingers the purse of an enamoured old man. Matsys updates a theme inherited from his father Quinten with sharper caricature and a cool grey-green palette.

title: The adoration of the Magi in the snow
description: Bruegel sets the Adoration of the Magi inside a snowbound Flemish village, the Holy Family barely visible amid woodcutters, skaters, and falling flakes. Painted in tempera on panel, it is one of the earliest snow scenes in Western painting.

title: The building of the tower of Babel
description: The smaller of Bruegel's two surviving Babel panels shows the spiraling brick ziggurat looming over a coastal plain, swallowed by storm clouds at its summit. Painstaking architectural detail draws on Roman ruins Bruegel had studied in Italy.
credit: Levels adjusted from File:Pieter_Bruegel_the_Elder_-_The_Tower_of_Babel_(Vienna)_-_Google_Art_Project.jpg , originally from Google Art Project.

title: The Parable of the Blind Leading the Blind
description: A line of six blind men stumbles diagonally across the panel toward a ditch, each face individually studied for the disfigurements of disease. Bruegel illustrates Christ's parable from Matthew 15 with clinical precision and an unforgiving rhythm of falling bodies.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"b/bruegel/pieter_e/11/01parabl"

title: Christ at the Sea of Galilee
description: A Venetian rendering of the Gospel episode in which Christ appears to the disciples on a storm-tossed sea. The dramatic torsion of the figures, raking light and turbulent green-grey waves are characteristic of the Tintoretto workshop, though the attribution has been debated in favour of Lambert Sustris.
credit: 1. The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 . 2. National Gallery of Art

title: The Flaying of Marsyas
description: Apollo punishes the satyr Marsyas for his musical hubris, suspending him upside down to be skinned alive. Painted in the artist's final years, the canvas is built from open, almost dissolving brushwork that anticipates later European painting.
credit: 1./2. Scanned from the book The Complete World of Greek Mythology , ISBN 0500251215 (orginal uploaded by en:User:BorgQueen as en:Image:The Flaying of Marsyas.jpg ) 3. Bridgeman Art Library : Object 388734

title: Feast in the House of Levi
description: A vast Venetian banquet staged in a Palladian loggia of three Serlian arches, populated by guests, servants, dwarves, dogs and a parrot. Originally painted as a Last Supper, the work was retitled after the Inquisition objected to its profane figures.
credit: This image has been extracted from another file

title: Primavera
description: Spring personified as a profile bust assembled entirely from blossoms, leaves and budding stems. Painted for the Habsburg court, it belongs to Arcimboldo's celebrated Four Seasons cycle, where Mannerist wit and botanical precision combine to flatter the emperor as ruler over a harmonious natural order.
credit: iwHATomxJenU4w at Google Cultural Institute , zoom level maximum

title: Perseus Freeing Andromeda
artist: Paolo Veronese / Formerly attributed to Francesco Montemezzano / Formerly attributed to Carlo Caliari
description: Andromeda, chained to the rock, twists toward the descending hero as Perseus drives his lance into the sea monster below. The picture's silvery palette and elongated figures, long debated among Veronese and his workshop circle, exemplify late-sixteenth-century Venetian narrative painting.

title: Ecce homo
description: A bound and crowned Christ is presented to the crowd, his torso lit against a brooding ground in the manner Rubens used to dramatize Passion scenes. The taut musculature and fall of light over the shoulders intensify the Counter-Reformation appeal to compassion.

title: Hercules and the Nemean Lion
description: Hercules grapples the invulnerable Nemean lion in a knot of straining limbs, the hero's first labor reduced to its essential close-quarters violence. The Apsley House version follows a Rubens prototype that translates antique sculptural sources, especially the Belvedere Torso, into Baroque flesh and sinew.

title: Madonna and Child
description: A workshop-tradition Madonna and Child in which the Virgin cradles a robust infant Christ against a dark ground. The composition follows one of Rubens's many devotional formulas, refined in his Antwerp shop for export across Catholic Europe.

title: Man in a Ruff
description: A bust-length portrait of a bearded gentleman in the stiff white ruff fashionable in early seventeenth-century Antwerp. The Colchester picture is an after-Rubens version, its frontal pose and economical handling typical of workshop replicas produced for civic and merchant patrons.

title: The Brazen Serpent
description: After the Old Testament episode in Numbers, Moses raises a bronze serpent on a pole to heal the Israelites bitten by venomous snakes, a type of the Crucifixion. The Shipley version follows a Rubens design of swirling, fallen and pleading bodies that emphasizes Counter-Reformation themes of intercession.

title: The Lamentation
description: A school-of-Rubens Lamentation in which the dead Christ is supported by Mary, John and the holy women in a tightly knit pyramid of grief. The piled drapery and waxen flesh follow patterns Rubens established in altarpieces for Antwerp's churches after his return from Italy.

title: The Laughing Philosopher
description: A workshop image of Democritus, the Greek thinker known in antiquity as the laughing philosopher, mocking the follies of humankind. His broad smile and weathered features draw on the Northern tradition of philosopher portraits revived by Rubens and his circle.

title: Tarquin and Lucretia
description: A late Mannerist treatment of the Roman legend in which Tarquin's assault on Lucretia precipitates the founding of the Republic. Tintoretto stages the violence with twisting bodies, broken pearls and torn drapery, the diagonal composition lit by sharp Venetian chiaroscuro.

title: The Baptism of Christ
description: A Mannerist treatment of the Baptism in which crowds press toward the Jordan beneath a luminous descent of the Holy Spirit. Tintoretto orchestrates space through deep diagonals and silvery, restless light, characteristic of his late Venetian altarpieces.

title: The Prayer in the Garden
description: A nocturnal Agony in the Garden, with Christ kneeling in radiant spotlight while the disciples slumber and torch-bearing soldiers approach through the olive grove. The picture exemplifies Tintoretto's preference for elongated figures, plunging diagonals and theatrical chiaroscuro.

title: Allegorische voorstelling van het werk en de vlijt
description: Print depicting an allegorical representation of work and diligence. Created by Hendrick Goltzius.

title: Saint Mary Magdalen
description: A solitary image of the penitent Magdalen withdrawn in a rocky wilderness with her book and ointment jar. Tintoretto isolates the saint against a brooding twilight landscape, animating the meditation with flickering, sketch-like brushwork.

title: Allegory of sight
description: Sight, the most celebrated of the Five Senses series produced jointly with Jan Brueghel the Elder, sets Venus and Cupid in a kunstkammer crowded with paintings, scientific instruments and rarities. The picture doubles as an inventory of the Archdukes' collection in Brussels.

title: Christ wearing the Crown of Thorns
description: An early devotional work from the Bolognese reformer of late-Mannerist painting, the composition tightens onto the suffering Christ as winged attendants steady his body. Carracci leans on the warm chiaroscuro and sculptural drapery he absorbed from Correggio and Venetian sources.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"c/carracci/annibale/1/thorns"

title: Lamentation over the Dead Christ
description: A nocturnal Lamentation in which the slumped body of Christ is supported by mourning figures emerging from deep shadow. Tintoretto compresses the scene around a single shaft of light, using elongated forms and rapid brushwork characteristic of his late Venetian manner.

title: Leda and the Swan
description: A late Venetian rendering of Jupiter's seduction of Leda, painted in Veronese's distinctive cool palette of silver-greys, dusty pinks and shot silks. The picture shows the master's assured handling of the reclining female nude within an intimate, theatrically lit interior.

title: Horatius Cocles
description: Identificatie Titel(s): Horatius Cocles. De Romeinse helden (serietitel) Objecttype: prent Serienummer: 2/8 Objectnummer: RP-P-OB-10. 336 Catalogusreferentie: The Illustrated Bartsch 97-1(2).

title: Goltzius's right Hand
description: A virtuoso pen-and-ink study of the artist's own drawing hand, deformed since childhood by a burn. Goltzius turns the disability into a manifesto of skill, building the form from his signature swelling-and-tapering parallel hatchings copied across Europe.

title: The Fall of Ixion
description: Ixion plunges into Tartarus among a tangle of rebellious giants in this Haarlem Mannerist tour de force of foreshortened nudes. Cornelis exaggerates musculature and contorted pose under crisp lighting, the kind of athletic, learned composition prized by Karel van Mander's circle.

title: The Fall of the Titans
description: The defeated Titans tumble headlong from heaven, their nude bodies twisting around each other in a virtuoso lattice of foreshortening. Cornelis treats the mythological subject as an excuse for anatomical display, central to the late-sixteenth-century Haarlem Mannerism he developed with Goltzius and Van Mander.
credit: 2AEcYz8BknPYRg at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Great Hercules
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the National Gallery of Art . Please see the Gallery's Open Access Policy .

title: Four Seasons in One Head
description: A late composite portrait fusing all four seasons into a single weathered face, with autumnal fruit, withered branches and bare wood standing in for flesh and hair. Arcimboldo, court painter to the Habsburgs in Prague and Vienna, treats the head as both Mannerist puzzle and emblem of time's passage.
credit: WwHACWGI-pgLUQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: River Landscape
description: An ideal river view in which figures crossing a wooden bridge are dwarfed by the broad waterway and distant hills. Carracci helped invent the classical landscape tradition later codified by Poussin and Claude, balancing observed Italian topography with a calm, ordered geometry.
credit: WAHqTJM81QDcpw at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: A monk and a nun [ 3 ]
description: A monk and a beguine paired in earthy proximity, a stock anticlerical jab popular in late-sixteenth-century Dutch genre. Cornelis softens his Mannerist drawing for the satirical mode, fixing the figures in close focus against a plain dark ground.

title: Vertumnus
description: The Roman god of seasons assembled entirely from fruit, vegetables, grains and flowers, each species chosen to flatter Emperor Rudolf II as the bringer of perpetual abundance. The composite portrait is the most refined example of the painter's signature conceit, made for the Prague court.
