title: A Foxglove in Bloom
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the National Gallery of Art . Please see the Gallery's Open Access Policy .

title: Farnese Hercules
description: The colossal Farnese Hercules, leaning on his club with the apples of the Hesperides behind his back, observed from below by two awed connoisseurs. Goltzius' celebrated engraving exaggerates the antique's swelling musculature with his trademark swelling-and-tapering burin lines.

title: The Apostle Peter
description: Saint Peter holds the keys of the Kingdom, his furrowed brow and grizzled beard rendered with apostolic gravitas. An early work in Rubens's Apostolado tradition, the figure typifies the half-length devotional saint favored by Counter-Reformation patrons.

title: The Emperor Commodus as Hercules
description: An engraving from Goltzius's Roman Heroes series, depicting the megalomaniac emperor in the lion-skin and club of Hercules. The muscular low-angle figure and virtuoso swelling burin lines exemplify the technical bravura of Northern Mannerist printmaking.

title: The Last Supper
description: Painted for the chancel of San Giorgio Maggiore in the last year of Tintoretto's life, the scene tilts the table on a sharp diagonal and dissolves the upper room in smoky angelic apparitions. It marks the radical conclusion of his Mannerist experiments with light and off-axis composition.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"t/tintoret/5_1580s/3lastsup"

title: Boy with a Basket of Fruit
description: An early Roman work made soon after Caravaggio entered the household of Cardinal del Monte, the youth offers a basket spilling with grapes, peaches, and figs. The naturalistic still life, with its bruised leaves and worm-eaten apples, helped found a new Italian tradition of painting fruit from life.

title: Francis of Assisi in Ecstasy
description: The saint swoons in the arms of an angel after receiving the stigmata, his companions sleeping in the moonlit landscape behind. One of Caravaggio's earliest religious nocturnes, the picture introduces the raking light and tender naturalism that would shape his mature style.

title: The Lute Player
description: A youthful musician tunes a lute at a stone table strewn with flowers, sheet music and ripe fruit, painted for Cardinal Francesco Maria del Monte. Caravaggio's tenebrist contrast and forensically observed still life details — dewdrops, a cracked pear — anchor the figure in a charged, palpable present.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: The Musicians
description: Four young musicians, one tuning a lute, prepare to perform in a tightly packed half-length composition. Commissioned by Cardinal Francesco Maria del Monte for his Roman household, the picture launched Caravaggio's career and introduced his trademark modelling of flesh against deep shadow.

title: The Lute Player
description: An early half-length picture from Caravaggio's time in the household of Cardinal del Monte, showing an androgynous youth absorbed in playing a lute. The still life of fruit, flowers and a violin is rendered with the same forensic light that became the artist's signature, the figure pulled forward against a dark wall. The New York version is in the Metropolitan Museum.
credit: Wildenstein Collection de:Image:1596 Caravaggio, The Lute Player Wildenstein Collection.jpg

title: View of Toledo
description: A spectral storm rolls over the Spanish hill city, with the Alcázar and cathedral picked out against acid-green hills and ink-black clouds. One of the earliest pure landscapes in Western art, El Greco rearranged Toledo's topography for symbolic, almost visionary effect.

title: Adam and Eve
description: An early treatment of the Fall of Man, with Eve offering the apple beneath the Tree of Knowledge as the serpent coils above. Painted in Antwerp before Rubens's Italian sojourn, the panel still shows the linear, Northern manner he would soon temper with Venetian color.

title: Basket of Fruit
description: A wicker basket of grapes, figs, peaches and a withered apple sits at eye level on a bare ledge, projecting slightly into the viewer's space. Often cited as the first autonomous still life in Italian painting, the work is unsparing about wormholes and decay; its plain ground is a later overpaint.

title: Jupiter and Juno
description: Jupiter and Juno (1602), Palazzo Farnese

title: Medusa
description: The Gorgon's severed head shrieks the instant of decapitation, her snakes still writhing. Painted on a convex tournament shield as a gift from Cardinal del Monte to the Grand Duke of Tuscany, the work conflates mirror, weapon, and self-portrait.

title: Nicolas Rubens
description: A red chalk drawing of the painter's young son, his round face and tousled hair caught with the directness of family observation. Such intimate studies of his children are among the most personal works in Rubens's draughtsmanship.

title: The Conspiracy of the Batavians under Claudius Civilis
description: The surviving central fragment of Rembrandt's largest history painting, commissioned for Amsterdam's new Town Hall. Civilis raises a sword in a one-eyed oath-taking with his Batavian rebels — a coarsely lit, almost barbaric image that the city soon rejected.

title: Judith Beheading Holofernes
description: The Hebrew widow grips the Assyrian general's hair and saws through his neck while her aged maid waits with a sack. Caravaggio renders the ugly mechanics of beheading with forensic clarity, the spurting blood lit as starkly as the white sheets.

title: Bust of Pseudo-Seneca
description: Drawing; Drawings
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: English: The Feast of Herod
description: Salome presents the head of John the Baptist on a salver as Herod recoils at the banquet table. Rubens stages the gruesome moment with theatrical lighting and a dense crush of guests, balancing horror against the gleam of silver and silk.

title: Head of Medusa
description: The severed head of Medusa lies on a rocky outcrop, her hair a writhing nest of vipers, scorpions and salamanders. Rubens collaborated with the still-life specialist Frans Snyders on the reptilian foreground, and the panel was traditionally kept curtained in the Habsburg collections to spare visitors a sudden shock.
credit: 1. Moravská Galerie v Brně 2. schwerin-news.de 3. Web umenia, on-line katalóg

title: Naked old man
description: An academic figure study of an aged male model, drawn from life with the meaty corporeality typical of the Antwerp Baroque. Jordaens repeatedly used such studies as a stockpile for the satyrs, river gods, and apostles populating his crowded canvases.

provenance.collection: Department of Prints and Drawings of the Louvre

title: Narcissuss
description: The youth Narcissus leans over a dark pool, his lit shoulder mirrored by his reflected face. Caravaggio reduces the Ovidian myth to a single closed circle of body and image, with the harsh tenebrist contrast that defined early Baroque painting in Rome.

title: Sine Cerere et Libero friget Venus
description: The Terentian motto — without Ceres and Bacchus, Venus grows cold — is set as a chiaroscuro nocturne, the goddess illuminated by a torch held by a satyr. Goltzius, the virtuoso Haarlem engraver, here works with brush on prepared ground in his late painterly mode.
credit: kgEEzyit7xGqTg at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Dream of Saint Joseph
description: An angel appears to the sleeping Joseph in a candlelit interior, the standard Lorraine nocturne formula put to a religious end. The shielded flame, smooth modelling and quiet psychology are characteristic of La Tour's mature devotional pictures.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Amor Vincet Omnia
description: A naturalistic Cupid in the Berlin Gemäldegalerie, who clambers grinning over scattered emblems of music, war, science, and rule. Caravaggio's tenebrist lighting and the boy's frank nudity made the picture notorious among Roman collectors.

title: Leda and the Swan
description: An early treatment of the myth from Rubens's Italian years, when his study of Michelangelo and Venetian colour was reshaping his approach to the sensual nude. The composition pairs the swan's white plumage against warm flesh tones in the kind of erotically charged mythology Rubens would refine for decades.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"es-e.html?/html/r/rubens/index"

title: The Incredulity of Saint Thomas
description: Christ guides the apostle's finger into the wound in his side as two other disciples crane forward to watch. Caravaggio collapses the moment into a tight, half-length composition lit from the upper left, the brows knit in shared concentration.

title: Prometheus bound
description: Prometheus writhes in chains as Zeus's eagle tears at his liver, his body cascading head-down across the canvas. Rubens collaborated with the animal specialist Frans Snyders on the predator, fusing classical myth with anatomical violence in a thoroughly Baroque key.

title: Sacred and Profane Love
description: Baglione's allegory pits divine love against earthly desire, a youthful angel descending upon a slumbering Cupid. The picture famously rivalled Caravaggio's treatment of the same subject and helped fuel the bitter feud between the two Roman painters.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"b/baglione/sacred2"

title: The Deposition
description: The lifeless Christ is lowered from the cross into the arms of the mourning Virgin and disciples, his pale flesh contrasted with the deep crimson of Joseph of Arimathea's robe. An early Roman-period work, it absorbs lessons from Caravaggio and the Carracci.

title: The Entombment of Christ
description: Christ's lifeless body is lowered onto the anointing stone, his arm and shroud dropping toward the viewer. Painted for the Chiesa Nuova in Rome, the altarpiece compresses the mourners into a powerful diagonal lit by Caravaggio's signature raking shaft.

title: The Taking of Christ
description: Judas presses forward to deliver his kiss as an armoured soldier seizes Christ's neck and a fleeing apostle howls behind. A self-portrait of the painter raising a lantern at the right edge introduces the artist into the violent nocturne.

title: Pietà with two angels
description: The dead Christ slumps across the Virgin's lap as two grieving angels hold up his arm and a crown of thorns. Carracci's restrained classicism, Bolognese in its rigour, became a key model for seventeenth-century devotional painting.

title: Portrait of Jan Govertsz. van der Aer
description: The Haarlem brewer and shell connoisseur is shown surrounded by exotic conches and a nautilus, emblems of the Wunderkammer culture flourishing in the early Dutch Republic. Goltzius records both the sitter's penetrating gaze and the iridescence of his collection with equal precision.

title: The Fall of Phaeton
dateCreated: between circa 1604 and circa 1605 , probably reworked between circa 1606 and circa 1608
description: Phaeton plummets from the chariot of the Sun as Jupiter's thunderbolt scatters the bolting horses and the Hours recoil through the zodiac. An early mythological tour de force, the picture shows Rubens already pushing Mannerist motion toward Baroque theater.
credit: nga.gov National Gallery of Art, Washington D.C.

title: Vincenzo II. Gonzaga
description: A formal portrait of the young Mantuan duke Vincenzo II Gonzaga, painted during Rubens's years at the Gonzaga court. Lace, armour, and the sitter's pale gaze are rendered with the courtly polish the artist absorbed from Italian models.

title: David with the Head of Goliath
description: The young David holds out the severed head of Goliath, in which Caravaggio is widely thought to have painted his own features. Made in his fugitive late period and probably sent to Cardinal Scipione Borghese as a plea for pardon, the canvas distils tenebrist drama into a single beam of light.

title: Saint Jerome
description: The aged Church Father bends over an open volume, his bald skull echoing the memento mori at his elbow. Painted in Rome for Cardinal Scipione Borghese, Caravaggio strips the scholar's cell to a few essentials so that flesh, parchment and shadow can do all the speaking.

title: Susanna and the Elders
description: A photograph of Rubens's Susanna and the Elders in the Galleria Borghese, Rome, recording the early Italian-period canvas. The biblical heroine twists away from the lecherous elders, her pose drawn from antique sculpture Rubens studied at first hand.

title: The Transfiguration of Christ
description: Christ rises in radiant white between Moses and Elijah while the apostles below shield their eyes, a subject drawn from Matthew 17. Rubens organizes the composition around a vertical axis of light, marrying Italian disegno with Flemish painterly warmth.

provenance.collection: Museum of Fine Arts of Nancy
provenance.location: Museum of Fine Arts of Nancy

title: Portrait of Kaspar Scioppius
description: The German Catholic polemicist Kaspar Schoppe sits at his writing table, pen in hand, returning the viewer's gaze with scholarly directness. Painted during Rubens's Italian years, the portrait fuses Venetian color with the sober psychological scrutiny of Northern humanist likenesses.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"r/rubens/41portra/05sciopp"

title: The Judgment of Paris
description: The Trojan prince Paris weighs the golden apple between Juno, Minerva and Venus while Mercury looks on. An early version of a subject Rubens revisited often, the composition arranges three nude goddesses to display Italianate disegno absorbed during his stay in Rome.

title: The Seven Works of Mercy
description: All seven corporal acts of mercy, from burying the dead to Roman Charity, unfold in a single torch-lit Neapolitan alley. Painted for the Pio Monte della Misericordia, the altarpiece set the template for Neapolitan tenebrism that Ribera and the Caravaggisti would extend.

title: Anatomical study
description: An ecorche drawing exposing the muscles of the male torso and limbs, made for the artist's own study of human structure. Such sheets fed the powerfully built figures of his history paintings and were later engraved as a teaching aid.

title: Cain slaying his brother Abel
description: The first murder rendered as violent close-quarters struggle, Cain's club raised above his fallen brother. Rubens compresses the action into a tight pyramidal group, exploiting contorted limbs and chiaroscuro for moral force.
credit: The Courtauld Institute of Art, London

title: Saint Jerome in his study
description: A monumental Saint Jerome at his desk, lion at his feet, translating the Vulgate by lamplight. Rubens emphasizes the hermit-scholar's powerful aged anatomy and the deep red drapery of the cardinal, a saint frequently invoked in Counter-Reformation imagery as a model of biblical orthodoxy.

title: The Beheading of Saint John the Baptist
description: Painted for the Oratory of San Giovanni in Valletta, where it remains the only canvas Caravaggio signed — in the spilled blood of the saint. The huge, half-lit composition stages the executioner mid-action with Caravaggio's signature tenebrist sobriety.

title: Study of Two Heads
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Susana y los viejos
description: Susanna twists away from two leering elders who press in from the shadows of her garden bath, her startled body and rumpled drapery filling the canvas. Painted shortly after Rubens's return from Italy, the picture fuses Venetian colour with the muscular Roman figures he had studied there.
credit: PAFsIkDw-5vK7Q at Google Cultural Institute , zoom level maximum

title: The Adoration of the Shepherds
description: Shepherds press in around the manger, faces lit from below by the Christ child as Mary draws back the cloth. The diagonal massing of figures and theatrical lighting reflect Rubens's recent absorption of Caravaggio during his Italian years.

title: The Holy Family with St. Elizabeth
description: An intimate devotional panel showing the Madonna and Child with St Elizabeth, the infant Baptist and a hovering dove of the Holy Spirit. The warm flesh tones and tender exchange of glances reflect Rubens's recent return from Italy.
credit: Image: http://collections.lacma.org/sites/default/files/remote_images/piction/ma-31767707-O3.jpg Gallery: http://collections.lacma.org/node/230317 archive copy at the Wayback Machine

provenance.collection: Los Angeles County Museum of Art
provenance.location: Los Angeles County Museum of Art

title: The Recognition of Philopoemen
description: The Achaean general Philopoemen, mistaken for a servant, is recognized by his hosts while chopping wood, an exemplum of stoic humility from Plutarch. The lavish kitchen still life of game and produce is by Frans Snyders, a frequent collaborator, while Rubens supplied the figures.
credit: Philopoemen Recognized . Museo del Prado. Retrieved on 23 October 2013 .

title: Virgin with the Child
description: A young Virgin offers her breast to the sleeping Christ Child in a quiet domestic register. Painted near the start of Artemisia's Roman career, it shows her absorbing Caravaggio's bare interiors and her father's silver-cool flesh tones.

title: Anyone who is bitten by a snake is cured by looking at the brazen serpent raised by Moses
description: Moses raises the bronze serpent on a pole as stricken Israelites writhe in the foreground, their wounds healed at a glance. The crowded, twisting figures provide a typological prefiguration of the Crucifixion familiar in Counter-Reformation imagery.

title: Christ as Savior
description: Christ raises his hand in benediction above a crystalline globe, his pale features elongated against a stormy sky. The panel belongs to El Greco's Apostolado series in Toledo, where the saviour heads a cycle of the twelve apostles in the artist's signature steel-grey palette.
credit: JwG7tm_-gzOwJQ at Google Cultural Institute , zoom level maximum

title: Christ between the two malefactors
description: An oil sketch of the Crucifixion, with Christ flanked by the two thieves on Calvary. Rubens uses dynamic diagonals and contorted, muscular bodies to charge the scene with Counter-Reformation pathos, the loose brushwork typical of his preparatory modelli.

title: Christ on the Cross
description: Christ on the Cross - Peter Paul Rubens

title: Juno and Argus
description: Juno gathers the hundred eyes of the slain giant Argus from his severed head and sets them into the tail of her peacock. The Ovidian subject is staged as a brilliantly coloured open-air tableau of nymphs, birds and rich textiles.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"r/rubens/21mythol/08mythol"

title: Lot and his daughters in a rock grotto
description: Inside a shadowed rocky grotto, Lot's two daughters ply their elderly father with wine, the burning city of Sodom glowing in the distance. The compact, candle-lit composition is typical of Rubens's early Antwerp years, when Caravaggesque tenebrism still inflected his treatment of biblical narrative.
credit: 1. akbrzeski.de 2. Staatliches Museum Schwerin 3. Google Art Project works from the Staatliches Museum Schwerin

title: Saint Jerome
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the National Gallery of Art . Please see the Gallery's Open Access Policy .

title: Sotatsu Dragons and Clouds
description: Painter: Tawaraya Sotatsu, Subject: Dragons and Clouds, Object Type: Folding Screen, Date: early 1600s, Current Location: National Museum of Asian Art, Washington D. C.

title: Susanna and the Elders
description: Painted when the artist was seventeen, the canvas turns the usual erotic pretext on its head: Susanna twists from the leering elders in unmistakable distress. The closed marble parapet and tightly wound figures give the scene a claustrophobia rare in the subject's long history.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"g/gentiles/artemisi/susanna"

title: The Apostle Saint Peter
description: Saint Peter is shown in papal vestments holding the keys of heaven, his weathered features turned upward in devotion. Painted soon after Rubens's return from Italy, the half-length follows a Caravaggesque tradition of monumental, close-cropped apostle portraits.
credit: originally uploaded to en.wikipedia by Wlkernan ( talk · contribs ) on 14 May 2005 under the file name Pope-peter pprubens.jpg . ( +/− )

title: The Return from War: Mars Disarmed by Venus
description: Venus and her attendants strip the war-god of his armor while putti tug at his weapons, an allegory of love's pacifying power over violence. Painted in collaboration with Jan Brueghel the Elder, who supplied the rich still life of arms, garlands and shimmering accoutrements.
credit: JwGEkfavTuicrA at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Bacchic scene with the dream of Silenus
description: Silenus sprawls in heavy slumber while satyrs, nymphs and a leopard wreath him with grapes in a leafy clearing. Painted soon after Rubens's return from Italy, the canvas distills Bacchic mythology into a pulsing arrangement of intertwined nudes and animal life.

title: Prometheus Bound
dateCreated: commencée entre 1611 et 1612 et achevée en 1618
description: A celebrated collaboration in which Rubens painted the writhing Titan and Frans Snyders supplied the savage eagle tearing at his liver. Painted in Antwerp around 1611-12, the canvas is a touchstone of Flemish Baroque cooperation and now anchors the Philadelphia Museum of Art's European collection.
credit: User:Jean-Pol GRANDMONT (2013) This illustration was made by Jean-Pol GRANDMONT Please credit this : Jean-Pol GRANDMONT An email to Jean-Pol GRANDMONT would be appreciated.

title: Prometheus Bound
description: The Titan, chained to a Caucasian crag, writhes as the eagle of Zeus tears at his liver in a horizontal composition Rubens developed with Frans Snyders, who painted the bird. The torsion of the body shows Rubens's debt to Michelangelo and the Laocoon, fused with North-Italian colorism.

title: St. Andrew
description: From the Twelve Apostles series painted for the Duke of Lerma, Andrew is identified by the X-shaped saltire cross of his martyrdom. Rubens characterizes the fisherman-evangelist as a weather-beaten elder, modelled with the bold chiaroscuro he absorbed in Italy.

title: St. Paul
description: Paul appears as a forceful, balding philosopher gripping the sword of his beheading, in keeping with the Pauline iconography of the Counter-Reformation. The half-length panel belongs to the Apostolado painted in Spain for the Duke of Lerma's collection.

title: St. Simon
description: Simon the Zealot stands holding the long saw of his legendary martyrdom, set against a plain neutral ground that throws the figure into relief. The panel belongs to Rubens's early Twelve Apostles cycle made for the Duke of Lerma during his time in Spain.

title: The Massacre of the Innocents
description: A violent biblical tableau of soldiers wrenching infants from their mothers in a tangle of straining muscle and contorted limbs. Rubens compresses the figures into a writhing pyramid, drawing on his Italian study of Michelangelo, Caravaggio and antique sculpture.

title: The Massacre of the Innocents
description: The Massacre of the Innocents - Peter Paul Rubens

title: Danae
description: The Argive princess reclines as Jupiter's golden shower falls into her lap, her maid scrambling to gather the coins. The picture, long shared between Artemisia and her father Orazio, channels Caravaggesque tenebrism into a sensuous, half-length nude.

title: Descent from the Cross
description: Painted on the outer wing of the Antwerp Cathedral triptych, this panel forms part of one of the central Counter-Reformation altarpieces of the Low Countries. The weight of Christ's body, lowered along a white shroud, anchors a tightly knit group of mourners.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"r/rubens/11religi/07desce5"

title: Jupiter and Antiope
description: The Haarlem master, better known as a virtuoso engraver, treats the mythological seduction with a sculptural nude and the warm flesh tones he absorbed from his late Italian journey. Jupiter approaches in satyr disguise, the composition borrowed from Northern Mannerist prints Goltzius himself had popularised across Europe.
credit: Sotheby's New York, 28 January 2010, N08610, lot 167

title: Roman Charity
description: An interpretation of the antique tale of Pero, who secretly nurses her imprisoned father Cimon to keep him alive. Rubens treats the moralising subject with characteristic warmth of flesh, dramatic chiaroscuro and a tender, tightly compressed embrace.
credit: Originally from en.wikipedia ; description page is/was here . 2006-03-28 (original upload date)

title: Study of the head of a young man turned to the left
description: A tronie-style head study of a youth in three-quarter profile, executed from life as workshop reference. The lively flesh tones and sketchy modeling show how Rubens accumulated a vocabulary of expressions and types for use in larger narrative compositions.

title: The Dying Seneca
description: The Stoic philosopher stands in a basin while a physician opens his veins, his upturned head and outstretched arms recalling the antique sculpture Rubens had drawn in Rome. The picture distills the Baroque taste for Stoic exemplars of virtuous death.

provenance.location: Alte Pinakothek, upper floor, gallery VII

title: The Entombment
description: Christ's body is lowered onto the slab of anointing as the Marys and disciples crowd in grief. Rubens tightens Caravaggio's Vatican prototype into an oval of mourning figures, the dead flesh phosphorescent against the surrounding shadow.

title: The lamentation of Christ
description: A nocturnal Lamentation in which torchlight rakes across Christ's body and the bowed faces of his mourners. The compressed grouping and dark surround intensify the emotional pitch in the Italianate Baroque manner.

title: Christ Giving the Keys to St. Peter
description: Christ confers the keys of binding and loosing on Saint Peter, the foundational image of papal primacy revived for Counter-Reformation polemic. Rubens isolates the two figures against an open sky, emphasizing the gesture's solemnity and the apostle's age-roughened hands.

title: Death of Cleopatra
description: The Egyptian queen lies dead across her bed, asp at the breast, in a foreshortened nude that confronts the viewer at close range. The picture sits within the Gentileschi family's repertoire of half-length heroines of antiquity, attributed variously to Artemisia and her father Orazio.

title: Lot and his Daughters
description: The episode from Genesis 19, in which Lot's daughters ply their father with wine to secure descendants after the destruction of Sodom. Rubens treats the subject with characteristically warm flesh tones and muscular, twisting figures, the canvas later extended at top and bottom in the early eighteenth century.

title: Portrait of Ahmed III al-Hafsi Sultan of Tunis Alternative title
description: Rubens reworks an earlier portrait by Jan Cornelisz Vermeyen of the Hafsid sultan, lending the Tunisian ruler the broad shoulders, ruddy flesh and lustrous turban he reserved for noble sitters. Such copies after Vermeyen fed Rubens's lifelong fascination with cosmopolitan dress and the wider Mediterranean world.

title: The Toilet of Venus
description: The goddess sits at her toilette, gazing at her reflection while an attendant arranges her hair and a putto holds the mirror. Rubens reworks the Venetian theme of Titian and Veronese into an opulent meditation on beauty and self-regard, the pearly flesh playing against dark drapery.
credit: Liechtenstein Museum, Wenen, particuliere collectie Vorst van Liechtenstein, Vaduz.

title: Virgin with the Child
description: An intimate half-length devotional image of the Virgin nursing the Christ Child, modelled in the heavy chiaroscuro Gentileschi absorbed from Caravaggio and her father Orazio. The fall of light across Mary's bowed head and the infant's body gives the picture a quiet, sculptural gravity rather than overt pathos.

title: A child with bird
description: A small child, often identified as Rubens's nephew Philip, looks out from a softly lit ground while gently holding a bird in one hand. The intimate scale and warm flesh tones place the panel within the painter's family portraits, a private counterpart to the grand commissions for which he is better known.
credit: Objekt in der Onlinesammlung der Staatlichen Museen zu Berlin: 870518 Fotograf: Christoph Schmidt

title: Daniel in the Lions' Den
description: Daniel kneels at dawn among nine life-sized lions, hands clasped upward as the beasts prowl, yawn and stare. Rubens drew the lions from observation in the menagerie of Archduke Albert and worked them into a Counter-Reformation meditation on faith preserved.
credit: 1. Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"r/rubens/15biblic/07daniel" 2. National Gallery of Art, Washington D.C.

title: Judith Beheading Holofernes
description: The biblical heroine and her maidservant pin the Assyrian general to his bed, blade drawn across his throat as blood spurts onto the linen. Gentileschi heightens the Caravaggesque tenebrism with a tightly compressed composition and the unflinching, muscular cooperation of the two women.

title: Kitchen Still Life
description: A loaded kitchen table heaves with game, fruit and copper vessels in the lavish Antwerp manner that Snyders developed alongside Rubens. Strong diagonals and life-size scale push the still life toward decorative grandeur, intended for the dining halls of patrician collectors.

title: Lamentation of Christ by Mary and John
description: A spare, intimate Lamentation in which the Virgin and Saint John bend over Christ's pale body laid against a darkened ground. Rubens reduces the cast to its essential mourners, concentrating Counter-Reformation devotional intensity in the contrast of cool flesh and ash-gray drapery.

title: Neptune and Amphitrite
description: The sea-god enthrones his Nereid bride amid a tumult of tritons, sea-horses and rolling waves. Rubens treats the marine pageantry as a fleshy Baroque counterpart to ancient sea-thiasos sarcophagi, the muscular Neptune contrasted with Amphitrite's pearly skin.

title: Saint Teresa of Avila's Vision
description: The Carmelite mystic kneels in ecstasy as Christ appears in a burst of golden cloud. Rubens treats the Counter-Reformation visionary subject with operatic light and surging drapery, in keeping with the Jesuit altarpieces of his Antwerp years.

title: Saint Teresa of Avila's vision of the dove
description: The Carmelite reformer kneels in ecstasy as the Holy Spirit descends in the form of a dove, an episode central to her Vida and to Counter-Reformation devotional propaganda. Rubens renders the moment as a swift, fluid oil sketch built around upward-spiralling drapery and light.

title: The Death of Adonis
description: Venus rushes to the dying Adonis as he collapses beside the boar that has gored him, while putti mourn and his hounds bay. The sweeping diagonal of the lovers' bodies and the velvet handling of flesh against thunderous sky exemplify Rubens's mature Ovidian manner.

title: The Toilet of Venus
description: Venus admires herself in a mirror held by a winged Cupid, a maidservant arranging her golden hair. The intimate composition, drawn from Titian's prototypes, balances cool reflective surface against the warm pink expanse of the goddess's back.
credit: 1. The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Venus and Adonis
description: Venus clings to the departing Adonis as he shoulders his spear and his hounds strain toward the fatal hunt. Rubens reworks Titian's Prado composition, heightening the embrace into a Baroque farewell of foreboding and tender resistance.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"r/rubens/21mythol/16mythol" http://hermitagemuseum.org/wps/portal/hermitage/digital-collection/01.+paintings/48132?lng=en

title: Warrior
description: An oil study of an armoured soldier, executed with the loose brushwork and ruddy palette Rubens reserved for working sketches. The figure's head and gleaming breastplate are modelled with rapid, confident strokes, capturing the swagger of a Baroque military type.

title: Yhe holy Family with the parrot
description: Mary, the infant Christ, Joseph and the young Saint John gather in a domestic interior with a parrot perched nearby, a bird traditionally read as a symbol of the Virgin's purity. The warmly lit grouping shows Rubens reworking a Renaissance devotional formula in his own fleshy idiom.

provenance.collection: Royal Museum of Fine Arts Antwerp

title: Descent from the Cross
description: Christ's body is lowered from the cross in a diagonal cascade of pale flesh and white shroud, supported by mourning saints. Rubens returned to this Counter-Reformation subject repeatedly after his Antwerp Cathedral altarpiece, modelling the dead Christ on classical sculpture studied during his years in Rome.
credit: Władysław Kwiatkowski (1929). Obraz Rubensa w Kaliszu . Towarzystwo Przyjaciół Książki

title: Landscape with the Ruins of Mount Palatine in Rome
description: Roman ruins on the Palatine hill rise above figures and herds in a panoramic landscape conceived from sketches made during the artist's years in Italy. Antiquity is set in dialogue with a fertile, lived-in countryside.

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: Saint Francis
description: Saint Francis of Assisi kneels in ecstatic communion, his gaunt features and brown habit picked out against a darkened ground. The economy of means and devotional intensity reflect Counter-Reformation taste for affective images of penitent saints.

title: Study of an Old Woman
description: An oil sketch on oak panel of an elderly woman, made as preparatory study for figures populating Rubens's larger religious and mythological compositions. The loose, economical handling typifies the head-studies the Antwerp workshop produced from life models.

title: The Four Continents
description: An allegory of the four continents personified as river gods reclining beside their female consorts, traditionally identified with the Danube, Nile, Ganges and Rio de la Plata. Tigers, a crocodile and lush vegetation underscore the cosmographic ambition of the Vienna canvas.

title: The Garden of Eden with the Fall of Man
description: A double-handed cabinet picture in which Jan Brueghel the Elder painted the lush menagerie of Eden — leopards, parrots, horses, fish — while Rubens contributed the soft, full-bodied figures of Adam and Eve at the Tree. Now in the Mauritshuis in The Hague.
credit: Geheugen van Nederland : Home : Info : Pic

title: The hunting of the hippopotamus
description: One of four exotic hunts Rubens painted around 1615 for Maximilian I of Bavaria, blending Flemish pictorial drama with antique pathos. Spear-wielding riders, snarling hounds, and writhing reptiles crash together in a swirling diagonal composition that became a touchstone for Baroque action painting.

title: Isabella Brant as Glycera
description: Rubens's first wife is portrayed in the guise of Glycera, the ancient Greek flower seller, garlanded and surrounded by blooms. The conceit fuses tender domestic likeness with classical learning.

title: Judith with the Head of Holofernes
description: Judith holds aloft the severed head of the Assyrian general while her maidservant looks on in the half-dark of the tent. Rubens treats the Old Testament heroine with the heightened drama and tactile flesh of his early Italianate manner.

title: Lot and his Daughters
description: Beneath a tree the daughters press wine on their father while Sodom burns in the distance, a small dog and a still life of bread and cheese anchoring the foreground. Goltzius, the great Haarlem engraver turned painter, sets the nudes in cool, sculptural light typical of his late Mannerist style.

title: Mariae Himmelfahrt
description: The Virgin is borne aloft by a swirl of angels while the apostles cluster around her empty tomb below. The upward spiralling composition is a hallmark of Rubens's altarpieces for the Antwerp churches of the early seventeenth century.

provenance.location: State Gallery in the New Schleißheim Palace

title: Old woman and boy with candles
description: An aged woman shields a candle flame as a boy leans in to light his own taper from hers, the two faces modelled by the small light source. The picture belongs to the Northern tradition of nocturnal genre studies descending from Caravaggio and the Bassano workshop.

title: Portrait of Clara Serena Rubens
description: An intimate likeness of the artist's daughter Clara Serena, who died at twelve, her round cheeks flushed and chestnut hair loosely brushed. The brisk, affectionate handling, more sketch than commission, ranks among the most tender child portraits of the seventeenth century.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"/r/rubens/41portra/10portra"

title: Study of a Man's Head
description: An oil sketch of a bearded male head, turned in three-quarter view and lit from above. Such tronies served as workshop reference for apostles and biblical elders, and show Rubens's quick, fluent brushwork unburdened by narrative finish.

title: The Fall of Man
description: Adam and Eve are shown beneath the Tree of Knowledge with the serpent coiled overhead, their elongated bodies posed in a Mannerist contrapposto. A late work by the Haarlem master, treating the Genesis subject with the smooth idealism of his post-Italian style.

title: The Ferry Boat to Antwerp
description: Despite the catalogue title, the panel records a bustling Flemish ferry crossing to Antwerp, packed with passengers, cargo and livestock. Jordaens animates the scene with the boisterous, low-life realism that anchored his early Antwerp output.

title: Descent from the Cross
description: A workshop variant of Rubens' Antwerp Cathedral altarpiece, with the pale body of Christ lowered diagonally onto a white shroud held by mourners. The composition's coiling diagonals and contrasted flesh tones became a Counter-Reformation template repeated across Catholic Europe.

title: Diana returning from the Hunt
description: The huntress goddess strides forward with her nymphs, bow in hand and hounds at her heels, fresh game slung from her shoulder. The composition celebrates the chase as a pretext for studies of the female nude in motion.

title: George Villiers
description: The royal favorite is shown in armor with a baton of command, the Order of the Garter prominent at his knee. Long thought to be a copy, dendrochronology and technical analysis of the panel preparation now support attribution to Rubens's own studio of the 1620s.

title: Head Study of a Bearded Man
description: An oil head study, painted directly from a Antwerp model, of the kind Rubens kept on file for reuse as apostles, philosophers and patriarchs in larger compositions. The rapid wet-into-wet handling and ruddy flesh tones show his working method at its most direct.

title: Hearing
description: From the Five Senses cycle painted with Jan Brueghel the Elder for the Archdukes Albert and Isabella in Brussels. Brueghel laid in the encyclopaedic gallery interior crammed with instruments, music books and singing birds; Rubens contributed the figure of Venus listening to a concert of Cupids.

title: Lamentation
description: The dead Christ lies on a sheaf of straw, mourned by the Virgin, John and the Marys at the foot of the Cross. The pallid body, sharply foreshortened, recalls Rubens's debt to Italian models of the Pieta.

provenance.collection: Royal Museum of Fine Arts Antwerp
provenance.location: Royal Museum of Fine Arts Antwerp

title: Mars and Rhea Silvia
description: The war god looms over the Vestal Rhea Silvia, the union that will produce Romulus and Remus and so found Rome. Rubens stages the encounter with cascading drapery, fluttering putti and the warm flesh-tones that mark his mythological inventions.

title: Milkmaids with Cattle in a Landscape
description: Milkmaids tend cattle amid baskets of fruit and vegetables in a fertile Brabant landscape, a cornucopia of rural abundance. Rubens fuses pastoral genre and landscape with sweeping diagonal composition and an almost mythological sense of fertility.
credit: 9AFIa58vJMeU9Q at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Pan and Syrinx
description: Rubens stages the Ovidian myth of Pan pursuing the nymph Syrinx, who becomes a stand of reeds at the moment of capture. The figures and lush riverside vegetation were painted in collaboration with Jan Brueghel the Elder.

title: Smell
description: Brueghel and Rubens collaborated on the Five Senses for the regents of the Spanish Netherlands, this panel devoted to scent. A garden chamber overflows with potted carnations, lilies and roses, civets and a perfumer's still, around a Venus modelled by Rubens.

title: The miracles of St. Francis Xavier
description: The Jesuit missionary Francis Xavier raises the dead and heals the afflicted before a toppling pagan idol, the converted crowding toward him in awe. Painted for Antwerp's Jesuit church, the altarpiece celebrates the global reach of Counter-Reformation evangelism.
credit: mAG-VIH-g-_RNA at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Vision of St Hubert
description: A collaborative panel: Rubens painted the kneeling huntsman Saint Hubert encountering the cross-bearing stag, while Jan Brueghel the Elder provided the meticulous forest setting. Such division of labour between figure-painter and landscape specialist was a hallmark of Antwerp's Baroque studio system.
credit: 1. Unknown source Unknown source 2. Museo Nacional del Prado, Galería online

title: Theodosius and Saint Ambrose
description: Bishop Ambrose bars Emperor Theodosius from Milan Cathedral after the massacre at Thessalonica, a Counter-Reformation parable of clerical authority over secular power. Rubens stages the confrontation on the church steps with massed retinues, reworking a composition first treated by his Venetian model Veronese.

title: Christ at Simon the Pharisee
description: Christ reclines at the Pharisee's table while the kneeling Mary Magdalene bathes his feet, scandalising the assembled guests. Rubens orchestrates the Gospel scene as Baroque theatre, with surging diagonals, gleaming silver and vivid emotional reaction.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"r/rubens/12religi/39religi"

title: Drunken Silenus
description: The drunken tutor of Bacchus staggers forward, supported by satyrs and maenads in a frieze of overripe flesh, grapes and laughter. Rubens treats Silenus as a Christianized parable of intemperance, the figures drawn from antique sources but warmed by Venetian color.

title: Head of Medusa
description: The severed head of the Gorgon lies on a stony ledge, eyes still rolling and snakes writhing into a teeming brood of vipers, scorpions and lizards. Rubens collaborated with the animal specialist Frans Snyders on the reptilian still life, fusing Baroque horror with naturalist precision.
credit: 1. Kunsthistorisches Museum Wien, Bilddatenbank . 2. Unknown source Unknown source 3. GalleriX

title: Kitchen Scene with Christ in the House of Martha and Mary
description: A bodegón that folds a domestic kitchen scene around a small framed vignette of Christ admonishing Martha. The young Velázquez sets garlic, eggs and a glistening fish in tight tenebrist focus, signalling the Sevillian taste for moral allegory dressed as still life.
credit: 1./2. picasaweb 3. gallerix.ru 4. National Gallery, London - online collection

title: Peter Finds the Coin in the Mouth of the Fish
description: The apostle Peter, instructed by Christ to pay the Temple tax, retrieves a silver coin from a fish's mouth (Matthew 17:27). Rubens stages the miracle as a robust half-length figure study, the fisherman's tanned forearms and concentrated gaze given Caravaggesque weight.

provenance.collection: Church of Our Lady across the river Dijle
provenance.location: Church of Our Lady across the river Dijle

title: Pythagoras advocating vegetarianism
description: Pythagoras gestures toward a heaped still life of fruit, vegetables, and beans, urging his listeners to abstain from animal flesh. The figures are by Rubens while the lush produce was painted by Frans Snyders, the partnership's customary division of labor.

title: The Death of Decius Mus in Battle
description: From the Decius Mus cycle, a tapestry-cartoon-scale battle scene in which the Roman consul plunges to his ritual death amid rearing horses and clashing standards. The composition is a swirling diagonal of armoured bodies, an early showcase of Rubensian dynamism.

title: The Rape of the Daughters of Leucippus
description: A vehement Baroque mythology in which Castor and Pollux carry off the daughters of Leucippus, the rearing horses and twisting nudes locked into Rubens's signature swirling diagonal. Painted in Antwerp at the height of his powers, the picture exemplifies the warm, pearly flesh and muscular drama that defined his manner.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"r/rubens/22mythol/28mythol" Originally from en.wikipedia ; description page is/was here . 2005-07-01 (original upload date). Original uploader was Cow at en.wikipedia . Originalmente carregado às 22:31hs do dia 01 maio 2007 por DIEGO RICARDO PEREIRA .

title: Two Satyrs
description: Two satyrs, one with a clutch of grapes and a piercing forward stare, the other grinning behind him, appear in close half-length view. The double tronie distills Rubens's vision of bacchic appetite into an arresting pair of bestial yet humanly knowing faces.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"r/rubens/22mythol/32mythol"

title: Union of Earth and Water
description: An allegorical pairing of Cybele and Neptune, personifying earth and water joining hands in a fertile alliance. Painted during Rubens's diplomatic years, the picture has been read as a plea for free access to the sea for his blockaded native Antwerp.
credit: Рубенс, Питер Пауль - Союз Земли и Воды (Антверпен и Шельда) (1618). Часть 14, высокое разрешение • описание картины, скачать репродукцию • Gallerix.ru

title: Winter: The Interior of a Barn
description: Peasants, livestock, and farm tools fill a thatched Flemish barn lit by a cold doorway onto a snowy yard. Rubens's late-career rural scenes treat the working countryside around his estate at Het Steen with the same vigorous handling he gave to mythological subjects.

title: S. Mary Magdalene in Extasy
description: A devotional half-length of the penitent Magdalene in ecstatic grief, hair loose and gaze raised toward heaven. Painted in the years of Rubens's mature Counter-Reformation manner, the canvas exploits warm flesh against shadowy ground in the persuasive emotional register favoured by Catholic patrons.

title: Allegory of Painting
description: A self-portrait in the guise of Pittura, the female personification of Painting, holding brush and palette in mid-stroke. Gentileschi exploited her gender to claim an allegorical role her male peers could not assume, presenting herself as the embodiment of the art itself.

title: Christ between the Two Thieves
description: Rubens stages the Crucifixion with the two thieves flanking Christ, the muscular bodies twisting against a darkened sky in the dynamic Counter-Reformation idiom. The altarpiece, painted for an Antwerp Augustinian context, deploys the diagonal energy and ruddy flesh tones characteristic of his Flemish Baroque workshop.

provenance.location: Église du Musée des Augustins de Toulouse

title: Drunken Silenus supported by Satyrs
description: The wine-soaked Silenus, fleshy and unsteady, is propped up by satyrs and a maenad in a tightly cropped Bacchic ensemble. The composition descends from Rubens's workshop, where the young van Dyck assimilated the older master's ruddy, full-bodied paint handling.

title: Jael and Sisera
description: Jael drives a tent peg through the temple of the sleeping Canaanite general Sisera, a biblical assassination Artemisia returned to repeatedly. Caravaggesque chiaroscuro isolates the act against deep shadow, with the heroine's calm, deliberate grip typical of Gentileschi's strong-willed women.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"m/g/gentiles/artemisi/jaelsise"

title: Landscape with a Gnarled Tree and a Farm
description: Drawing; Drawings
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Landscape with Philemon and Baucis
description: A storm-drenched Ovidian landscape in which Jupiter and Mercury, sheltered by the pious Philemon and Baucis, unleash a deluge upon the surrounding valley. The boiling sky, falling trees and rain-swollen torrent show Rubens absorbing Venetian and Adam Elsheimer landscape models for cosmic moral drama.

title: Penitent Mary Magdalene
description: The penitent Magdalene twists in her chair, gold gown slipping, hand pressed to her breast in conversion. Artemisia gives the Counter-Reformation subject the muscular, psychologically charged female protagonist that is her hallmark.

title: Pieter Bruegel the Younger
description: An ageing Pieter Brueghel the Younger turns toward the viewer in a quiet half-length portrait. The plate belongs to Van Dyck's Iconography, his series of etched and engraved likenesses of the leading artists, scholars and statesmen of his day.

title: Portrait of a woman with braided blond hair
description: A bust-length portrait of a fair-haired sitter, her hair coiled into intricate braids above a richly trimmed bodice. The luminous skin, lively gaze and confident handling are typical of the workshop portraits Rubens produced for Antwerp's patrician class.

title: Portrait of Anne of Austria
description: An early seventeenth-century studio copy of a lost Rubens portrait of Anne of Austria, queen consort of Louis XIII. The Spanish-born queen wears stiff court dress and an elaborate ruff, recorded with the smooth porcelain finish typical of Rubens's diplomatic likenesses of European royalty.

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: The Holy Trinity
dateCreated: 19 th century , copy of a painting of circa 1620
description: A Counter-Reformation altar composition showing the Trinity enthroned amid swirling clouds and angels. Rubens deploys his characteristic ruddy flesh tones, golden draperies and dynamic Baroque diagonals to lift the eye toward the heavenly group.

title: The raising of the cross
description: Muscular soldiers heave the cross upright on a steep diagonal, Christ's luminous body twisting against a stormy sky. Conceived for Antwerp's St. Walburga, the great triptych translates Italian heroic anatomy into a Flemish altarpiece of unprecedented physical force.

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: The Three Crosses . [ 1 ] Alternative title
description: Christ hangs between the two thieves, the crosses set against a darkening sky in a tightly massed composition. Rubens compresses the Calvary scene into a vertical screen of straining bodies, a Counter-Reformation meditation on suffering and redemption.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"r/rubens/12religi/45religi"

title: Young man with a black hat
description: A bust-length portrait of a young man in a black flat cap, copied after a sixteenth-century original by the Antwerp painter Willem Key. The grave, even lighting and restrained palette preserve the sober character of the Northern Renaissance prototype.

title: The Meeting of Marie de' Medici and Henry IV at Lyons
description: Part of Rubens's twenty-four-canvas Marie de' Medici cycle commissioned for the Luxembourg Palace and now in the Louvre. The royal meeting at Lyons is staged as an allegorical pageant, with personifications of the city, putti and a chariot drawn by lions glorifying a politically uneasy marriage.

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: The Triumphant Entry of Constantine into Rome
description: Part of the Constantine cycle designed for tapestries woven for Louis XIII, showing the emperor's victorious procession through Rome after the Battle of the Milvian Bridge. Rubens stages the parade as a ceremonial relief, packed with banners, classical architecture and rearing horses.

title: The Virgin and Child in a Garland of Flowers
description: The Madonna and Child appear within an exuberant wreath of blossoms, painted in collaboration with the flower specialist Jan Brueghel the Elder. The conceit fuses Marian devotion with Antwerp's expertise in still life, the garland a votive offering of created beauty.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"r/rubens/13religi/51religi"

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: Legend of Tomyris
description: Herodotus' Massagetae queen receives the severed head of the Persian king Cyrus, whom she will plunge into a vessel of blood. Rubens stages the Antwerp workshop's signature pile-up of muscular soldiery and silken courtiers, the carmine drapery and pearled flesh tones unmistakably his.

title: Mary Magdalene as the Melancholy
description: The Magdalene rests her cheek on her hand in the conventional pose of melancholia, jar of ointment beside her. Painted during Gentileschi's Roman years, the work fuses Caravaggesque chiaroscuro with the artist's signature interest in solitary, introspective female protagonists.
credit: Museo Soumaya Donation link to the image painting

title: Perseus Releases Andromeda
description: Perseus, fresh from slaying the sea-monster, unbinds the nude Andromeda as putti and Pegasus crowd the rocky shore. The composition pairs heroic male armor with luminous female flesh in a swirling Baroque celebration of rescue and erotic deliverance.

title: Portrait of an Old Man
description: A bust portrait of an aged sitter in dark robes and a white collar, his face recorded with the unsentimental specificity of Rubens's mature portraiture. Studies of this kind doubled as preparatory material for biblical and historical figures requiring weathered authority.

title: Portrait of Susanna Lunden
description: A portrait long mistitled 'The Straw Hat' — the felt hat is in fact black — depicting Susanna Lunden, sister of Rubens's second wife Helena Fourment. The picture is admired for its luminous flesh painting and the deft handling of cool daylight on the sitter's blue dress and tilted hat brim.

title: The Coronation of Marie de' Medici in Saint-Denis
description: Part of the twenty-four-canvas Marie de' Medici cycle commissioned for the Luxembourg Palace, this scene stages the queen's 1610 coronation at Saint-Denis. Rubens orchestrates a vast crowd of dignitaries in a sweeping diagonal, mythologizing the event the day before Henri IV's assassination.

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: The Disembarkation at Marseilles
description: From the Marie de' Medici cycle commissioned for the Luxembourg Palace, the canvas shows the queen's arrival at Marseilles in 1600, greeted by an allegorical France while Tritons and Nereids churn the water below. Rubens fuses Habsburg state portraiture with Baroque mythological invention.

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: Judith and Her Maidservant
description: Judith and her servant pause in Holofernes's tent, alerted by a sound outside; a single candle throws Judith's shielding hand into silhouette and glances off the discarded gauntlet. The Detroit canvas is among Artemisia's most theatrical exercises in Caravaggesque candle-light.

title: Marchesa Elena Grimaldi Cattaneo
description: The Genoese noblewoman descends a terrace beneath a parasol held by an African page, the pairing a familiar device in van Dyck's Italian portraits of patrician women. Crimson drapery, towering verticals and a reserved, columnar pose set the template for European court portraiture.

title: Christ Blessing the Children
description: Christ, seated, gathers small children to him while apostles and mothers crowd around, taking up the gospel injunction "Suffer the little children to come unto me." Gentileschi treats the religious subject with naturalistic Caravaggesque lighting and grounded, individualized figures.

title: Job mocked by his Wife
description: Job sits naked on his dunghill while his wife berates him by the light of a single candle. La Tour treats the Old Testament confrontation as an intimate two-figure nocturne, the flame between them mapping every fold of skin and cloth.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

provenance.collection: Musée départemental d'Art ancien et contemporain
provenance.location: Musée départemental d'Art ancien et contemporain

title: Massacre of the Innocents
dateCreated: Entre 1625-1626 (selon Jacques Thuillier) et 1628-1629 (selon Anthony Blunt)
description: Compressed to its essential cruelty, the painting isolates a single soldier pinning a mother and child as Herod's slaughter unfolds. Poussin condenses the biblical episode into a sculptural triangle of figures, prefiguring later modern responses to depictions of state violence.

title: Penitent Mary Magdalene
description: Another of La Tour's penitent Magdalens, the saint absorbed in candlelit thought before a skull and crucifix. The repeated subject across his oeuvre allowed him to refine the pictorial logic of a single flame describing flesh, fabric and bone.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 . https://www.metmuseum.org/collection/the-collection-online/search/436839

title: Portrait of a Woman
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Portrait of Marchese Ambrogio Spinola
description: The Genoese-born Habsburg general Ambrogio Spinola, victor at Breda, appears in burnished black armor with a crisp lace collar and the red sash of command. Rubens, who knew the marquess personally during the Twelve Years' Truce, distills authority into a steady, weary gaze.
credit: Národní galerie v Praze - online collection

title: The Matchmaker or The Procuress
description: A candle-lit brothel scene in which an old procuress brokers a young woman to a leering client. Honthorst, nicknamed Gherardo delle Notti in Rome, transplanted Caravaggio's tenebrism north; here a hidden flame carves the faces from the dark in glowing reds and ochres.

title: The Musicians' Brawl
description: Itinerant street musicians come to blows over a hurdy-gurdy, one wielding a knife as a companion squeezes a lemon to feign a wound. La Tour stages the brawl in cool daylight, his earlier Caravaggesque manner before he fully turned to candlelit nocturnes.
credit: fgHS5adsdubLnw at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: River landscape with Pan and Syrinx
description: A collaborative mythological landscape in which Rubens painted the figures of Pan pursuing the nymph Syrinx while Jan Brueghel the Younger supplied the dense reed bed and wooded river setting. Such partnerships between Antwerp masters were a hallmark of Flemish Baroque cabinet painting.

title: The Artist in his Studio
description: A small early panel showing a painter, dwarfed by an enormous panel on his easel, contemplating his work from across a bare studio. The image foregrounds thought rather than craft, framing painting as a fundamentally mental activity.

title: The Hermit and the Sleeping Angelica
description: An aged hermit, drawn from Ariosto's Orlando Furioso, gazes lasciviously upon the sleeping Angelica in a moonlit forest. Rubens contrasts ascetic gauntness with luminous female flesh, transforming the chivalric romance into a Baroque meditation on temptation.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"r/rubens/61other/05hermit"

title: Anton Giulio Brignole-Sale on horseback
description: The Genoese patron rides forward beneath a heavy classical arch, a swaggering equestrian formula van Dyck adapted from Rubens and Titian. Now in the Palazzo Rosso, the canvas anchors the painter's six-year Italian sojourn.
credit: kQG1dKzvnwCU7g at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Christ on the Cross
description: A life-size crucified Christ emerges from impenetrable darkness, the body modelled with sculptural tenebrism that fooled at least one early viewer into thinking the work a polychrome carving. Painted for Seville's Dominican monastery of San Pablo, it announced Zurbarán's austere devotional manner.
credit: 1. Art Institute of Chicago 2. Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"z/zurbaran/1/christ_x"

title: Madonna and Child
description: A Nursing Madonna produced through the Antwerp collaboration of Rubens and Jan Brueghel the Younger, with Rubens supplying the warm, fleshy figures and Brueghel the encircling garland of finely observed flowers. Such joint devotional pictures were a speciality of the Antwerp workshops, marrying Catholic Counter-Reformation piety to Flemish virtuoso still life.

title: Adam and Eve
description: Rubens's homage to Titian, copied during his stay in Madrid where he studied the royal collection. The serpent-child reaches down to Eve as Adam grasps her arm; Rubens warms the Venetian palette and softens the contours toward his own fleshier ideal.
credit: 1. The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 . , [1] 2. Museo del Prado, Madrid

title: Esther before Ahasuerus
description: The biblical queen swoons before the Persian king as she pleads for her people, a subject Gentileschi treats with operatic gesture and sumptuous fabrics. Painted during her Neapolitan years, it shows her assimilation of Caravaggesque drama into a more courtly, decorative key.
credit: Metropolitan Museum of Art, online collection (The Met object ID 436453)

title: Pythagoras advocating vegetarianism
description: Rubens depicts the Greek philosopher gesturing toward a heap of fruit and grain as he urges his listeners to abstain from meat. The composition draws on Ovid's Metamorphoses, with abundant still-life passages painted in collaboration with Frans Snyders.

title: Rembrandt van Rijn - Zelfportret
description: An early Leiden self-portrait in which the young Rembrandt's face is half-lost in shadow beneath a tousled crown of hair. He scratches into the wet paint with the butt of the brush to suggest stray locks, an experimental touch typical of these years.

title: Samson and Delilah
description: Delilah leans forward in shock as Philistine soldiers seize the shorn Samson from her lap, the moment of betrayal pitched between sensuality and violence. Van Dyck reworks Rubens's earlier composition with cooler colour and a more refined, elongated drawing.
credit: ggFmBL7gYcnEbg at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Samson Slaying the Lion
description: Samson grapples a roaring lion to the ground and tears its jaws apart, the biblical strongman rendered with anatomical force and twisting torso. Rubens reworked the subject several times, transposing the heroic male nude of antique sculpture into a violent Old Testament exemplum.

title: The Triumph of Bacchus
description: A pale, half-nude Bacchus crowns a kneeling soldier with vine leaves while a circle of weather-beaten Spanish drinkers grins out at the viewer. Painted in Madrid before Velázquez's first trip to Italy, the canvas pulls classical mythology down to tavern level with a sober, almost documentary realism.

title: Concert of Birds
description: An owl serves as conductor while a riot of songbirds reads from a sheet of music, a witty allegorical conceit Snyders treated repeatedly. The tightly observed plumage of finches, parrots and birds of prey reflects his standing as Antwerp's premier animal specialist.

title: Deborah Kip
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the National Gallery of Art . Please see the Gallery's Open Access Policy .

title: Isabella von Bourbon
description: A state portrait of Isabella of Bourbon, queen consort of Spain, in stiff black court dress and pearls. Rubens softens the rigid Habsburg formula with subtle modelling of the face and a discreet warmth in the flesh tones.

title: Minerva protects Pax from Mars
description: Painted in London for Charles I as a diplomatic gift, the allegory shows Minerva pushing back the war-god Mars while Pax nurses Plutus and children gather around the fruits of peace. The picture distilled Rubens's hopes for an Anglo-Spanish accord into a sumptuous tableau of bodies and abundance.

title: Portrait of Maria Louis de Tassis
description: A standing portrait of the Antwerp heiress in a starched lace ruff and black silk gown set with jewels, her hand resting on an ostrich-feather fan. Van Dyck's Genoese-influenced full-length format and silvery handling of fabric established the elegant template he would soon export to the Stuart court.

title: Portrait of Thomas Howard
description: A state portrait of Thomas Howard, 14th Earl of Arundel, the great English collector of antiquities, painted during Rubens's diplomatic stay in London. The sitter is shown armoured and commanding, in the rich blacks and gleaming highlights of mature Flemish Baroque portraiture.

title: Amor Asleep
description: Cupid lies sprawled in slumber, bow and quiver set aside, his pearly flesh modelled in the warm glazes Rubens favoured for putti and infants. The motif of disarmed love invites the viewer to contemplate desire's vulnerability when stilled.

title: Avondlandschap met boerenkar
description: A peasant cart laden with felled timber rumbles down a rutted track as the day's last light catches clouds and treetops. The naturalistic Flemish countryside and warm low sun place this among the personal landscapes Rubens painted for his own pleasure.
credit: cgFcDUneAqR5KQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Bust of an old man with a fur cap
description: An early tronie, or character study, of a bearded old man in a fur cap, painted in Leiden. Rembrandt explores the fall of light across creased skin and the shaggy textures of the cap as exercises in expressive description.

title: Cats Fighting in a Larder
description: A pantry overturned by brawling cats among hanging game, fish and copper vessels, a kitchen-piece genre popularized by Frans Snyders and continued by his brother-in-law de Vos. The hissing animals serve as a moralized emblem of greed and chaos disrupting domestic order.

title: Diana on the stag hunt
description: The huntress goddess in red, drawing back her bow as hounds drive a stag through dense woodland. The picture belongs to Rubens's late series of aristocratic chase subjects, where landscape, animals and figure painting were typically distributed among studio specialists.

title: Martyrdom of Saint Apollonia
artist: After Jacob Jordaens / Marinus Robyn van der Goes
description: Identificatie Titel(s): Martelaarschap van H. Apollonia Objecttype: prent Objectnummer: RP-P-OB-67. 929 Catalogusreferentie: Hollstein Dutch 10-4(4).

title: Martyrdom of Saints John and Paul
description: A Bolognese Baroque altarpiece depicting the martyrdom of the Roman brothers John and Paul, beheaded under Julian the Apostate. Guercino orchestrates the figures along a steep diagonal, his characteristically warm tenebrism modelling flesh against deep shadow with the loose, painterly touch of his mature Roman period.

title: Portrait of a man
description: A bust-length likeness of an unidentified gentleman in dark dress with a starched white collar. The directness of the gaze and the economical handling of the flesh suggest a private commission painted in the artist's mature Antwerp studio.

title: Saint Luke as a painter
description: Saint Luke, traditionally credited with painting the Virgin's likeness, leans on a palette and gazes up at the crucified Christ, conflating the evangelist with the artist's own self-portrait. The sober brown ground and clipped lighting are characteristic of Zurbarán's monastic Sevillian commissions.

title: Self portrait in a cap
description: An early self-portrait etching from Rembrandt's Leiden years, the young artist studies his own face under a soft cap, probing expression and shadow. Such prints functioned as exercises in physiognomy and as advertisements of his graphic virtuosity.

title: The Fortune Teller
description: A naive young man has his palm read by an old gypsy while her accomplices pick his purse and cut his medal chain. La Tour stages the swindle in cool daylight rather than candlelight, exploiting fixed sidelong glances to spell out the deception.

title: The Garden of Love
description: Elegant couples flirt and recline in a balustraded garden while putti scatter roses overhead. Painted soon after Rubens's marriage to Helena Fourment, the scene celebrates conjugal pleasure in his late, pearly Flemish manner and now hangs in the Prado.
credit: Transferred from de.wikipedia to Commons by Outisnn . Original uploader was Nocturne at de.wikipedia . Later version(s) were uploaded by Louis le Grand at de.wikipedia .

title: The Holy Family under the Apple Tree
description: Reverse panel of the Ildefonso Altar painted for Albert and Isabella of the Spanish Netherlands, in which the Holy Family rests in a luminous garden beneath a fruiting apple tree. Rubens links Eve's tree of knowledge to Christ's redemption through the warm Flemish flesh, billowing draperies, and pearly sky that defined his late Baroque manner.

title: The Three Graces
description: The mythological trio stand intertwined in a wooded glade, their pearly flesh set against deep green foliage. Rubens models the women on his second wife Helena Fourment, celebrating an unidealised, sensuous standard of female beauty.

title: Frolicking Birds in Plum and Willow Trees
description: Songbirds dart through the gnarled, almost calligraphic branches of a flowering plum and a weeping willow on a gold ground. Sansetsu, the eccentric heir of the Kyoto Kanō line, stylises nature into rhythmic arabesques, pushing the school's bird-and-flower repertoire toward a mannered late-Momoyama elegance.

title: The institution of the Eucharist during the Last Supper
description: Christ raises the bread at the centre of the long table while the apostles react in agitation; Judas turns toward the viewer with averted eyes. The picture concentrates on the moment of institution rather than the announcement of betrayal, in line with Counter-Reformation Eucharistic devotion.

title: Hercules as Heroic Virtue Overcoming Discord
description: An allegorical Hercules, club raised, tramples a writhing female personification of Discord underfoot. The image was conceived as a ceiling design celebrating princely virtue triumphing over civil strife.

title: Landscape with a Rainbow
description: A panoramic Flemish countryside after a storm, peasants and cattle moving among glistening fields beneath a translucent rainbow. Painted on his country estate at Het Steen, it shows Rubens turning to landscape in his late years with broad atmospheric breadth.

title: The abduction of Europa
description: Europa is borne away across the water on the back of Jupiter disguised as a white bull, while her companions react in alarm on the shore. The dark, wooded riverbank and tiny carriage on the bridge give the Ovidian subject the weight of a Dutch landscape.
credit: JwHVmnI-bQWnRw at Google Cultural Institute zoom level scaled down maximum

title: The Anatomy Lesson of Dr Nicolaes Tulp
description: A group portrait of the Amsterdam Surgeons' Guild gathered around the dissected forearm of an executed criminal. Tulp's demonstration of the flexor tendons and the differentiated reactions of his colleagues turn a guild commission into a study of attention.

title: Christ in the Storm on the Sea of Galilee
description: The disciples struggle with sail and rigging on a heaving boat as Christ is roused to calm the sea. Rembrandt's only known seascape, it was stolen from the Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum in 1990 and remains missing.

title: Portrait of Saskia van Uylenburgh
description: A portrait of the artist's wife shortly after their marriage, dressed in fanciful pastoral costume with a feathered hat. Rembrandt's warm light glances across pearls, embroidery, and her unguarded expression.

title: Venetia
description: Venetia, Lady Digby is shown the morning after her sudden death, propped against a pillow with a single rose shedding its petals. Commissioned by her grieving husband Sir Kenelm, the picture is a rare seventeenth-century memorial portrait painted from the corpse.
credit: oAFUhDfXH7qEdQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Cook at a Kitchen Table with Dead Game
description: A market cook arranges deer, swans and waterfowl across a generous table while a cat and dog skirmish below, staging abundance as theatre. The display piece, scaled for a Flemish patrician dining room, is typical of Snyders's collaboration with Rubens's circle.

title: Hercules Fighting the Hydra of Lerna
description: Hercules grapples with the many-headed Hydra of Lerna, his muscular body twisting against the dark serpentine coils. The canvas belongs to a series of ten Labours of Hercules painted for the Hall of Realms in the Buen Retiro Palace, where Zurbarán's stark tenebrism set the hero against an almost theatrical void.

title: The Abduction of the Sabine Women
description: Roman soldiers seize Sabine women in a violent twist of bodies, framed by classicizing architecture. Poussin choreographs the abduction as a sculptural relief of opposing diagonals, applying his rigorous study of antique gesture to the legendary founding episode of Rome.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: The Battle of Nördlingen
description: A Rubens-school battlepiece commemorating the Cardinal-Infante Ferdinand's decisive victory over the Swedes in the Thirty Years' War. The cavalry mêlée in the foreground gives way to drifting smoke and a panoramic plain, in the manner Rubens established for Habsburg state imagery.

title: A Landscape with Cows and Duck Hunters
description: A pastoral panorama unfolds along a wooded riverbank, with cattle watered in the foreground and duck hunters moving through the reeds beyond. Painted in the artist's final years at his country estate Het Steen, the work belongs to a group of expansive landscapes notable for their loose handling and golden, atmospheric light.
credit: Objekt in der Onlinesammlung der Staatlichen Museen zu Berlin: 865663 Fotograf: Christoph Schmidt

title: Atalanta and Hippomenes
description: Hippomenes sprints past Atalanta as she stoops for the golden apple of Aphrodite, the wager that costs the huntress her freedom. The Prado canvas is a workshop version after Rubens's design, retaining the diagonal sweep of bodies and the fluttering drapery typical of his Ovidian commissions for Philip IV.

title: Bathsheba at Her Toilet
description: Bathsheba sits at her bath while a black page delivers David's letter, the Old Testament narrative reframed as an excuse for sumptuous flesh, drapery, and still life. The painting now hangs in the Gemäldegalerie Alte Meister, Dresden.

title: Belshazzar's Feast
description: The Babylonian king recoils as a glowing Hebrew inscription, MENE MENE TEKEL UPHARSIN, appears on the wall during his blasphemous feast. Rembrandt stages the moment with theatrical lighting, overturned vessels, and a sumptuous gold-embroidered cloak.

title: Charles I
description: The Stuart king is presented in three-quarter length with elegant hauteur, a pose Van Dyck refined repeatedly to fix the image of Caroline kingship. The painter's silvered palette, fluid drapery, and aristocratic poise shaped English portraiture for a century.
credit: BQHR9te2WWtyOA at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Diana and two nymphs surprised by satyrs while bathing
description: Diana and her companions recoil from leering satyrs at a forest pool in a late mythological invention treating chastity disturbed. Rubens entrusted much of the landscape and animal staffage to specialist collaborators, while reserving the warm, dimpled female nudes for himself.

title: Francisco de Zurbarán 006
description: A bound lamb lies on a grey ledge against a dark ground, an Agnus Dei meditation on Christ's sacrifice rendered with extraordinary fleecy detail. The picture, in the Prado, distils Zurbarán's ascetic Sevillian piety into one of the most economical still lifes of the Spanish Baroque.

title: Market Scene on a Quay - Frans Snyders and Workshop
description: A teeming Antwerp quayside laden with game, fish, fruit and live animals, painted on a near three-metre canvas typical of Snyders's grand market series. The Flemish Baroque specialist often collaborated with Rubens on such still-life pageants, and his workshop reproduced the format for aristocratic dining halls.

title: Nativity of St. John the Baptist
description: An interior crowded with attendants tends to the newborn John the Baptist while Elizabeth rests nearby. Gentileschi treats the sacred subject as a domestic event, modelling figures in the warm tenebrist light typical of her Neapolitan period.

title: Rape of Ganymede
description: Rembrandt reimagines the classical myth as a startled, urinating infant snatched skyward by Jupiter's eagle. The unsettling realism breaks sharply with the idealized Italian tradition of the subject.
credit: Cropped from File:Rembrandt - The Abduction of Ganymede - Google Art Project.jpg , originally from Google Art Project.

title: Still Life with Fruit
description: The Antwerp specialist piles a Baroque larder with grapes, peaches, dead hare and game birds, animated by a thieving monkey, a wary cat and a darting squirrel. Snyders, Rubens's regular still-life collaborator, treats abundance as theatre, a moralising display of plenty under threat.
credit: Hohenbuchau Collection, on permanent loan to Liechtenstein, The Princely Collections, Vienna

title: The Birth of Venus
