description: Venus rises from the sea attended by tritons, nereids and putti in a Baroque treatment of the Anadyomene theme. Poussin organizes the marine cortege into a frieze-like rhythm, marrying Ovidian mythology with the cool classical structure he distilled from antique relief sculpture.
credit: kwEtyhSaKD0s7g at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Brazen Serpent
description: Moses raises the bronze serpent on a pole as Israelites writhe with snakebites below, looking up to be healed (Numbers 21). Rubens packs the foreground with anatomical contortions, a Counter-Reformation allegory of salvation through faith in the Crucified.

title: The lamentation over the dead Christ
description: painting ( http://vocab. getty. edu/aat/300177435 ), Anthony van Dyck( https://viaf.
credit: Royal Museum of Fine Arts Antwerp

provenance.collection: Royal Museum of Fine Arts Antwerp
provenance.location: Royal Museum of Fine Arts Antwerp

title: The rape of Europa
description: Quellinus, a close collaborator of Rubens in Antwerp, retells Ovid's myth of Jupiter abducting the Phoenician princess in the guise of a white bull. The composition pairs Flemish Baroque drama with the polished classicism Quellinus absorbed during his Roman training.

title: The Repentant Magdalen
description: The Magdalen sits before a mirror that reflects only a candle and skull, her jewels discarded at her feet. La Tour pares the vanitas iconography to its essentials and lets the single flame model her face and hands with mathematical calm.
credit: 2QGwZrE8Px8k9A at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Surrender of Breda
description: Justin of Nassau hands the keys of the Dutch city to the Genoese general Ambrogio Spinola, who lays a conciliatory hand on the defeated commander's shoulder. The forest of upright Spanish pikes that gives the painting its nickname, Las Lanzas, organises the right half of the composition, painted for the Hall of Realms in the Buen Retiro.

title: A View of Het Steen in the Early Morning
description: An expansive panorama of the artist's country estate near Antwerp, the chateau set among fields, hedgerows and a low morning sun that throws long shadows across the foreground. Painted on multiple oak panels joined together, it stands among the great late landscapes of the Northern Baroque.

title: A View of Het Steen in the Early Morning
description: Rubens's own country estate near Mechelen lies under a low, golden dawn, with a hunter, hay wagon and far-flung fields stretching to the horizon. The expansive panorama, painted for personal pleasure, helped redirect Northern landscape toward atmospheric naturalism.

title: Adoration of the Magi
description: A Neapolitan-period altarpiece in which the Magi crowd around the Virgin and Child by torchlight. Gentileschi pushes the figures close to the picture plane and stages the gift-giving in heavy raking light, drawing on the Caravaggism she had carried south from Rome.

title: Landscape with a Rainbow
description: A broad Flemish countryside opens out under a sky cleared by a passing storm, a rainbow arching over fields, milkmaids and grazing cattle. Often paired with the View of Het Steen, it expresses Rubens's late identification with the land around his country house.
credit: https://wallacelive.wallacecollection.org:443/eMP/eMuseumPlus?service=ExternalInterface&module=collection&objectId=64938&viewType=detailView Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"r/rubens/5landsca/16landsc"

title: Landscape with a Tower
description: A small panel showing a fortified tower set on a wooded rise, approached by a slender bridge under the warm light of a lowering sun. The intimate scale and rapid, fluid brushwork are characteristic of the late landscape sketches Rubens made around his estate at Elewijt.
credit: Objekt in der Onlinesammlung der Staatlichen Museen zu Berlin: 867302 Fotograf: Jörg P. Anders

title: Landscape with Cows
description: Cattle wade through reedy lowland water beneath a wide Flemish sky, the herd dispersed across pasture and ditch. Rubens turned to such polder views in the late 1630s after retiring to his country estate at Het Steen.

title: Mercury and Argus
description: Mercury, sent by Jupiter, lulls the hundred-eyed guardian Argus to sleep with his pipes before drawing his sword. The Ovidian story is staged in a wooded landscape with the white heifer Io tethered to one side.

title: Orpheus and the animals
description: Orpheus charms a menagerie of beasts and birds with his lyre, the Ovidian subject becoming a pretext for Snyders's encyclopaedic display of fur, feather and scale. The composition crowds exotic species into a forested clearing in the Flemish Baroque manner.

title: Saturn
description: The aged god Saturn devours one of his infant sons, biting into the flesh as the child writhes in his grip beneath the cold light of his eponymous planet. Painted for Philip IV's Torre de la Parada, Rubens fuses Ovidian myth with unflinching late-Baroque violence.

title: Silenus
description: The drunken satyr Silenus, tutor of Bacchus, lurches forward supported by his bacchic retinue, his bloated torso pinkly luminous against shadow. Rubens revisits the antique theme with characteristic relish for unidealized flesh and the comic gravity of intoxication.

title: Susanna
description: Rembrandt's small panel catches Susanna at the moment she steps from her robe, glancing back with sudden alarm as the elders emerge from the dark garden. The pearly nude and deep umber surroundings show the painter testing a Lastman-derived biblical mode in the year of Danaë.

title: Susanna and the Elders
description: Painting of Susanna and the Elders by Rubens

title: The Blinding of Samson
description: A violent rendering of Judges 16, with Philistine soldiers pinning Samson while a blade is driven into his eye and Delilah flees with the shorn hair. Rembrandt extracts maximal drama from a sudden shaft of daylight cutting into the cave-like darkness.

title: The creation of the milky way
description: The infant Hercules, set to Juno's breast by Jupiter, draws milk so forcefully that the spilled stream becomes the Milky Way. Painted for Philip IV's Torre de la Parada, the canvas turns an Ovidian etiology into a swirl of cosmic drapery and divine flesh.

title: The Feast of Venus
description: Worshippers garland a statue of Venus while putti, nymphs and satyrs dance in pagan revelry. Inspired by Titian's Worship of Venus and a passage in Philostratus, the late composition assembles Rubens's lifelong vocabulary of mythic celebration.
credit: XgGjucvfTejNUg at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Rape of Europe
description: The Phoenician princess clings to the white bull-Jupiter as he wades into the sea bound for Crete, while companions wave helplessly from shore. Painted as a copy after Titian for Philip IV, the canvas pays homage to Venetian color while asserting Rubens's own touch.

title: The Sacrament of Ordination
description: Christ hands the keys of the Church to a kneeling Peter, with the apostles ranged in a measured Roman setting. From Poussin's second series of the Seven Sacraments, the painting translates the institutional moment into the artist's mature, archaeologically informed classicism.
credit: GAFmbp1CWRCVZg at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The abduction of the Sabines
description: Roman soldiers seize the Sabine women at Romulus's signal in a frieze of taut, sculptural figures arrayed before classical architecture. Poussin choreographs the violence with archaeological detail and disciplined gesture, exemplifying the rational French classicism he forged in Rome.

title: The Consequences of War
description: An allegory painted for the Medici and addressed to the disasters of the Thirty Years' War. Mars, dragged forward by a Fury, tramples the arts and a despairing Europe, while Venus tries vainly to restrain him — Rubens's late, politically engaged history painting at its most charged.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"r/rubens/30allego/06allego"

title: As the Old Sing
description: A multi-generational Flemish family bellows around a table while a child blows a pipe, illustrating the proverb inscribed at the top: as the old sing, so the young pipe. Jordaens returned to the boisterous moralised feast many times, this version inscribed Ut genus est genius.

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: Bull subdued by dogs
description: A bull-baiting scene of the kind de Vos perfected in Rubens's Antwerp circle, with mastiffs lunging at a tethered bull's flanks. The diagonal tangle of muscle, horn and fang demonstrates the Flemish taste for animal combats as showcases of anatomical drama and tonal bravura.

title: Landscape with the Good Samaritan
description: An imaginary landscape staging Christ's parable beneath a thunder-bruised sky. Rembrandt favoured these dramatic 'heroic landscapes' in the late 1630s, dwarfing the small figures of the Samaritan and the wounded traveller within ruined towers, stormlight and tonal contrasts that recall Hercules Seghers.

title: Landscape with the Tower of Castle "Steen
description: A panoramic view of the country estate Rubens purchased in 1635, with its turreted manor set among golden Brabant fields. The artist treats native landscape with vigorous brushwork and a low, glowing horizon, anticipating later Romantic vistas.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Self-Portrait as the Allegory of Painting
description: Gentileschi merges self-portrait and emblem, depicting herself as the personification of Painting from Ripa's Iconologia: brush raised, gold chain bearing a mask, sleeves pushed back over a working arm. The conceit could only be staged by a woman, since the allegory was female.

title: Selfportrait
description: The painter, near sixty, presents himself in broad-brimmed hat, sword, and gloves — courtier and diplomat as much as artist. The dignified late self-portrait, withholding palette and brushes, asserts his ennoblement and standing among Europe's rulers.

title: The Crucifixion of Saint Peter
description: The apostle is shown crucified upside down at his own request, his contorted, foreshortened body filling a tall altarpiece format. Commissioned for St Peter's Church in Cologne, the work was among the artist's last major altarpieces, completed shortly before his death and dispatched to the parish where his father had once lived.

title: The Judgment of Paris
description: Paris awards the golden apple to Venus while Juno and Minerva recoil in defeat, their nudes arranged in a graceful triad against an Arcadian landscape. Rubens revisited this mythological subject several times; the late Prado version is among his most assured.

title: The Little Fur
description: An intimate, near-life-size portrait of Rubens's second wife Helena Fourment, draped only in a fur cloak. The picture's frank sensuality and tender naturalism mark it as a private image, kept in the artist's own household.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: The Raising of the Cross
description: Straining executioners heave the cross bearing Christ skyward in a violent diagonal of muscular labor. A late reprise of his Antwerp altarpiece, this version concentrates the drama into a tighter, more urgent gathering of bodies and tackle.

title: Virgin with Child and saints
description: Painted as the altarpiece for Rubens's own funerary chapel in St Jacob's Church, Antwerp, the panel surrounds the Virgin and Child with saints in whose features the artist included self-portrait and family likenesses. It is among his last works, executed in the warm, fluid manner of his final Antwerp years.

title: Diana and nymphs attacked by satyrs
description: Diana and her companions, returning from the hunt with hounds and game, are accosted by lecherous satyrs at the edge of a wood. The collision of nude bodies, fur and dead quarry typifies Rubens's late mythological mode.
credit: 1. The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 . 2. Museo del Prado, Galería online

title: Hercules Strangling the Nemean Lion
description: Hercules wraps the invulnerable lion in a strangling embrace, the hero's bunched muscles answered by the beast's twisting flank. The motif derives from antique sarcophagi Rubens had studied in Italy.

title: A Female Saint and Martyr
description: A half-length saint, traditionally identified as Catherine of Alexandria by the spiked wheel of her martyrdom. Gentileschi favoured strong-willed female protagonists set against deep shadow, building flesh in luminous Caravaggesque chiaroscuro across a career that spanned Rome, Florence, and Naples.

title: Four Studies of a Head of a Moor
description: Four oil sketches of the same Black sitter, observed from different angles and in varied light, made as working material for finished compositions including the Adoration of the Magi. The studies are notable for their direct, dignified attention to an individual model.
credit: LgE6l73zlShsKQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

provenance.collection: Royal Museums of Fine Arts of Belgium

title: Portrait of Mary Ruthven
description: A tender likeness of the painter's Scottish wife, posed with a quiet half-smile against a neutral ground. Van Dyck reserves his usual aristocratic finery for a domestic intimacy, the silvery silks softened by his late, more thinly brushed manner.

title: Prometheus Bound
description: Chained to the Caucasus, Prometheus writhes as the eagle of Zeus rends his liver — a punishment renewed each day. Jordaens treats the Titan with brawny, ruddy realism, working in the Rubensian Antwerp idiom of muscular flesh and dramatic chiaroscuro.

title: Rachel de Ruvigny
description: Rachel de Ruvigny, Countess of Southampton, is shown as Fortune perched on a globe, a moralised allegory of worldly mutability much favoured in Caroline court portraiture. Cool blue satins and a still, ovular face are vintage van Dyck.

title: The Feast of the Bean King
description: A boisterous Twelfth Night feast in which whoever finds the bean in the cake reigns as king while companions roar 'The king drinks!' Jordaens returned to the proverbial subject several times, packing the table with three generations and the wet-eyed comedy of overindulgence.
credit: MwEBgGg74T1f2g at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Woman with a Mirror
description: A half-length nude turns toward a mirror held by a maid, her pearly flesh rendered in the warm, fluid manner of Rubens's late period. The composition belongs to a Venetian-derived tradition of the toilette of Venus, transformed here by Flemish opulence.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"r/rubens/30allego/08allego"

title: The Girl in a Picture Frame
description: A young woman leans through a painted frame as if stepping out of the picture into the viewer's room, an illusionistic conceit Rembrandt favoured in the 1640s. Warm Dutch chiaroscuro and a few impasto highlights on the hands and lace collar carry the trompe-l'œil.

title: The Girl in a Picture Frame
description: A young woman leans out of a painted frame, her hands resting on the illusory ledge in a sly play on the picture's own boundary. The trompe l'oeil device reflects Rembrandt's interest in animating the threshold between image and viewer.

title: The Windmill
description: An etching of a stoutly built post mill rising above the bastion of an old town wall. Rembrandt's careful drypoint touches give the brickwork, thatch, and surrounding figures a grainy luminosity prized by collectors of his prints.

title: Peasant Family in an Interior
artist: Attributed to Louis Le Nain / Antoine Le Nain
description: A peasant family pauses around a sparse table in a dim cottage interior, each face turned outwards with quiet directness. The Le Nain brothers' rare empathetic treatment of rural poverty, painted in a sober earthen palette, anticipates the realism of Courbet two centuries later.

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: The Three Trees
description: Three trees crown a low rise above a flat Dutch polder, with a thunderstorm rolling in from the left and tiny figures fishing and sketching at the margins. Rembrandt's largest landscape etching combines drypoint and engraving to model an unusually dramatic sky.

title: Judith and her maid Abra with the Head of Holofernes
description: In the aftermath of the killing, Judith pauses with sword raised while Abra wraps the severed head in a sack. A late Naples-period work, it returns to Gentileschi's signature subject in a quieter, more nocturnal register, lit by a single candle that gilds the drapery.

title: The Mill
description: A windmill stands silhouetted against a turbulent, luminous sky on a high earthen rampart above a quiet river. Long read as a brooding emblem of Dutch identity, the painting fuses topographical record with charged atmosphere.
credit: agGDXrJtBtu64Q at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Rape of Lucretia
description: The Roman matron Lucretia turns the dagger on herself rather than live with dishonour, a subject Gentileschi returned to repeatedly. The compressed half-length composition and raking light typify her late Naples manner, where female protagonists confront violence with stoic resolve.

title: King Candaules of Lydia Showing his Wife to Gyges
The Smoker with Pipe
description: A pipe-smoker leans into his glowing taper, the small flame illuminating cheek, hand and clay bowl against deep shadow. La Tour reduces the genre subject to a meditative still life in light, the kind of intimate nocturne that defines his late Lunéville workshop.

title: Winter Landscape
description: A rare winter landscape in oil, with skaters, a horse-drawn sledge, and low farm buildings beneath a wide pale sky. The thinly brushed surface and silvery palette stand apart from the artist's more familiar dark interiors.

title: Susanna and the Elders
description: The chaste Susanna shrinks as the two elders intrude upon her bath, drawn from the Book of Daniel. Rembrandt focuses on her vulnerability and the elders' menace, modeling flesh and drapery with a soft, glowing chiaroscuro.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"r/rembran/painting/biblic1/suzanna"

title: Portrait of Innocent X
description: A famously unflinching state portrait of the pope, whose shrewd, suspicious gaze emerges from a cascade of crimson and white. Velázquez orchestrates a near-monochrome of vermilions and rose tones, using fluid brushwork to render lace, silk and satin with startling immediacy.

title: Susanna and the Elders
description: A late treatment of the Book of Daniel episode, with the bathing Susanna recoiling from the two judges who threaten to denounce her. Gentileschi returned to the subject across her career, here in a more restrained, tenebrist key than her early Pommersfelden version.
credit: 1. Moravian Gallery in Brno 2./3. Google Arts & Culture — rQH0ja0DwrR2tQ

title: Greyhound Catching a Young Wild Boar
description: A lean greyhound seizes a piglet boar in mid-flight across a wooded clearing, captured with the anatomical precision Snyders brought to Rubens's hunt commissions. Slashing brushwork in the foliage and a smoky sky heighten the violent Baroque drama.

title: Juan de Pareja
description: A portrait of Velázquez's enslaved assistant, an artist of Moorish descent whom the painter would soon free. Painted in Rome as a demonstration piece, it stunned contemporaries with its dignified presence, controlled silvery palette, and the assured economy of its brushwork.

title: Landscape with a Calm
description: A serene Italianate landscape with shepherds, livestock and a distant fortified town beneath still water and clear sky. Poussin's late landscapes treat nature as a measured, almost stoic order, with each motif placed for compositional balance rather than picturesque incident.
credit: xgGrcOwaU1-r9Q at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Lying Lioness
description: A lioness rests in profile against a darkened landscape, her tawny coat brushed with the loose, confident strokes Snyders reserved for living animals. The study likely served as a model for the great Antwerp hunt pieces produced with Rubens's workshop.

title: Narcissus
description: The Antwerp Baroque painter, a pupil of Cornelis de Vos and a follower of Rubens, takes up the Ovidian theme of the youth fixed by his own reflection. The half-length composition turns on a strong oblique light that picks out flesh and stream against deep shadow.
credit: Details of painting at the Prado Museum site Narciso at the Prado

title: Saint Jerome Reading
description: The Church Father is depicted half-length in a rocky grotto, bare-chested and absorbed in his book, a crucifix steadying his left hand. Murillo treats the penitent scholar with restrained naturalism, the warm Sevillian light modelling weathered skin and beard against a deep brown ground.

title: Self portrait
description: A late self-portrait of the Antwerp master, looking directly out at the viewer with the broad, fleshy modelling he applied to all his sitters. The panel is in the Bavarian State Painting Collections in Munich.

title: The Adoration of the Shepherds
description: Shepherds press forward to venerate the naked Christ Child held by the Virgin, a lamb bound at the foreground as a foreshadowing of sacrifice. The earthy palette and tender, everyday types reflect Murillo's early debt to Ribera and the Spanish naturalist tradition before his manner softened in later years.

title: The boar hunt
description: Hounds tear at a cornered boar in a churning Baroque mêlée, the kind of large hunting piece Snyders supplied with workshop assistance for aristocratic galleries. Sweeping diagonals, splayed limbs and a bruised sky echo the dynamic compositions he had honed beside Rubens.
credit: jwHaDccc1u4Icg at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

provenance.collection: Art Gallery of New South Wales
provenance.location: Art Gallery of New South Wales

title: The Holy Family with a Bird
description: Joseph stoops to play with the infant Jesus and a small bird while Mary pauses at her spinning, the goldfinch a traditional emblem of the Passion to come. The domestic warmth and soft chiaroscuro are typical of Murillo's Sevillian devotional pictures, which translated sacred subjects into the rhythms of family life.

title: The Shell
description: A rare still life from Rembrandt's etched oeuvre, depicting a single cone shell (Conus marmoreus) rendered with delicate cross-hatching and drypoint burr. The print attests to the Dutch fascination with exotic naturalia gathered through global trade.

title: The Triumph of Bacchus
description: A Flemish baroque procession of Bacchus and Ariadne, with putti, satyrs, and tipsy revellers wreathed in vine. De Vos, best known for his crisp portraits of Antwerp burghers, here works closer to Rubens's mythological idiom, ordering the throng with broad, ruddy modelling.

title: Triumphant Cupid among Emplems of Art and War
artist: Thomas Willeboirts Bosschaert / Paul de Vos
description: Cupid sits in triumph atop instruments of art, science and war, a Baroque allegory of love's universal sway. Willeboirts Bosschaert painted the figures and de Vos the still-life trophies, reflecting the seventeenth-century Antwerp practice of collaborative specialization.

title: Two young seals on the shore
description: Two young harbor seals lie on a stretch of Flemish shoreline, painted with the close observation of fur, hide, and salt-damp sand for which the de Vos workshop was prized. The Antwerp painter built his career on Baroque animal subjects, often supplying livestock and game for collaborators including Rubens.

provenance.collection: Musée des Beaux-Arts et d'Archéologie de Besançon
provenance.location: Musée des Beaux-Arts et d'Archéologie de Besançon

title: Wind God and Thunder God Screens by Tawaraya Sotatsu hi-res
description: Sotatsu's pair of two-fold screens places Fujin clutching his wind sack and Raijin riding a ring of drums against expanses of unworked gold leaf. The bold negative space and tarashikomi ink pooling became a touchstone for the Rinpa school and a National Treasure of Japan.

title: Stormy Landscape with Pyramus and Thisbe
description: Storm clouds boil over a vast Italianate landscape as the Ovidian lovers meet their tragedy beside a reflective lake. Poussin uses meteorological drama to magnify human catastrophe, with sheet lightning, fleeing horses and bowed trees orchestrated into a single classical machine.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"p/poussin/4/38landsc"

title: Mercury and Argus
artist: After Jacob Jordaens / Attributed to Remoldus Eynhoudt
description: An engraving after Jordaens showing Mercury raising his sword above the slumbering hundred-eyed giant Argus, with Io transformed into a cow at the left. The print belongs to the wide diffusion of Jordaens' mythologies through reproductive engraving, here cataloged in Hollstein as Dutch 7.

title: Psyche receives the cup of immortality on the Olympus
description: On Olympus, Psyche is offered the cup of immortality that will allow her marriage to Cupid, attended by an assembly of gods. Jordaens stages the Apuleian fable with his characteristic warm palette and tightly clustered, bench-front composition.

title: Triumph of Frederick Henry
description: An allegorical celebration of the Dutch stadtholder Frederick Henry, painted for the Oranjezaal at Huis ten Bosch. Jordaens orchestrates a swirling Baroque cavalcade of personifications, captives, and trophies in the Rubensian Antwerp manner, glorifying the prince's military and political triumphs.

title: A View of Delft after the Explosion of 1654
description: A topographical record of the gunpowder explosion that levelled a quarter of Delft on 12 October 1654, killing van der Poel's own daughter. The artist made the catastrophe his recurring subject, painting smouldering rubble, broken roofs and bewildered survivors with documentary specificity.
credit: 1. Transfered from nl.wikipedia 2. - 5. National Gallery, London - online catalogue .

title: Christ in the House of Martha and Mary
description: Vermeer's largest known painting and a rare history piece, made early in his career before he turned to domestic interiors. Christ gestures toward the seated, attentive Mary while Martha presents a basket of bread; the broad handling and warm reds anticipate the artist's mature poise.

title: Christ presented to the people: the oblong plate
description: An etching, in its later state, of Pilate presenting the bound Christ to a crowd that demands his crucifixion. Rembrandt scraped away the foreground onlookers and replaced them with shadowed arches, intensifying the confrontation between judge and viewer.

title: Pallas Athena
description: A figure in gleaming helmet and gorget, traditionally identified as the goddess of wisdom and war. Rembrandt models the armour with thick, gilded impasto against deep shadow, a hallmark of his late chiaroscuro.

title: The Maids of Honour
description: The Spanish court group portrait centred on the Infanta Margarita, her maids and dwarves, with Velázquez himself at the easel and the king and queen reflected in a distant mirror. The picture is a sustained meditation on vision, painted for Philip IV and now the cornerstone of the Prado.
credit: The Prado in Google Earth: Home - 7 th level of zoom, JPEG compression quality: Photoshop 8.

title: Girl Reading a Letter at an Open Window
description: An early interior in which a young woman reads at a leaded casement, her face echoed in the panes. Recent restoration at the Gemäldegalerie in Dresden uncovered a painted Cupid on the back wall that had been overpainted after Vermeer's death, sharpening the picture's amorous subtext.

title: Self-portrait
description: A self-portrait from the artist's later years, in which he sits frontally with a mahlstick and palette implied by his pose. Broad, loaded brushwork records a lined face that meets the viewer with sober self-knowledge.

title: The Wine Glass
description: A genteel courtship scene in which a man waits attentively while a young woman drains a roemer of wine. The stained-glass roundel at the upper window depicts Temperance with bridle and square, an emblem ironically warning against the very excess being staged below.

title: View of Houses in Delft
description: A modest stretch of Delft brickwork, with figures absorbed in domestic chores in a doorway and an alley. One of only two surviving cityscapes by Vermeer, the picture distills the painter's quiet attention to weathered surface, mortar, and clouded northern light.

title: Figure Studies
description: Drawing; Drawings
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Self-portrait
description: A late self-portrait in which the painter sits with hands folded, dressed in a heavy gold-trimmed robe and dark cap. The frank, unflattering record of his own ageing has come to define how subsequent centuries imagine the artist.
credit: -gHQe8vbiHn2xw at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Milkmaid
description: A sturdy kitchen maid pours a thin stream of milk into an earthenware bowl, the room hushed by daylight from a leaded window. Vermeer's pointillé highlights flicker on bread crusts and copper, and the lapis-derived blue of her apron exemplifies his costly pigment habits.
credit: Google Arts & Culture — AHrw. Z3. Av6. Zhjg 9. AHrw. Z3. Av6. Zhjg

title: View of Delft
dateCreated: between circa 1660 and circa 1661 Baroque (late 16 th century –1750s )
description: A panorama of the artist's home city seen across the Schie, rendered with the peculiar optical fidelity for which he is celebrated. Pinpoint dabs of impasto on the boats and roofs, possibly studied via a camera obscura, mimic the glint of refracted morning light.

title: St. Bartholomew
description: The apostle holds the knife of his martyrdom and turns aside in pensive reflection. Painted late in Rembrandt's career, the figure is built from rough, palpable strokes that give the saint the weathered weight of a real man.
credit: EQETBP79d47TVQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The evangelist Matthew and the angel
description: The evangelist pauses over his manuscript as an angel leans in to whisper at his ear. Rembrandt models both faces with a quiet warmth, treating divine inspiration as an intimate exchange.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: The Lute Player
description: A young man tunes his lute in a quiet Rotterdam interior, a music sheet open before him. Sorgh, a pupil of Teniers and Buytewech, specialised in such genteel domestic genre scenes, building tone with a warm brown ground beneath crisp local colour.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"s/sorgh/luteplay"

title: Girl with a Pearl Earring
description: A tronie rather than a portrait: an anonymous young woman turns over her shoulder, lips parted, against a deep glazed-black ground. Vermeer reserves his most expensive pigments, ultramarine and lead-tin yellow, for the turban, and renders the famous teardrop earring with two soft dabs of lead white. In the Mauritshuis, The Hague.

title: Portrait of a couple as figures from the Old Testament
description: Long known as The Jewish Bride, the painting shows a couple in tender embrace, often identified with Old Testament pairs such as Isaac and Rebecca. Built up with thickly trowelled pigment, the gold sleeve and ruby skirt are among Rembrandt's most celebrated passages of paint.

title: Rest on the Flight into Egypt
description: The Holy Family pauses on its journey into Egypt, Mary cradling the sleeping Christ Child while Joseph watches over them and the donkey grazes nearby. Now in the Hermitage, the picture exemplifies Murillo's mature estilo vaporoso, in which figures emerge from a hazy, golden atmosphere with notably softened contours.

title: The Art of Painting" Alternative title
dateCreated: circa from 1666 until 1668 Baroque (late 16 th century –1750s )
description: An allegory of painting in which an artist seen from behind sets brush to a portrait of Clio, the muse of history, identified by her trumpet and laurel. Vermeer kept the canvas in his Delft studio until his death, and the heavy map of the Seventeen Provinces above the model carries political weight.

title: The Astronomer
description: A scholar in a long robe leans over a celestial globe, his hand brushing its surface as if to set the cosmos turning. Painted as a pendant to The Geographer, the picture reflects Delft's culture of empirical inquiry, its still light and book-strewn study tuned to quiet concentration.

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: The Geographer
description: The companion piece to The Astronomer, showing a scholar with dividers paused over a chart, a terrestrial globe behind him. Vermeer drapes the figure in an exotic Japonse rok, framing seventeenth-century Dutch knowledge-making as a contemplative, light-filled pursuit.

title: The lacemaker
description: The smallest of Vermeer's surviving paintings, focusing tightly on a young woman bent over her bobbins and pins. Threads of red and white spill from a cushion in molten blurs that anticipate optical photography, while her concentrated face is rendered with the artist's signature stillness.

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: Santa Rosa de Lima
description: The first canonised saint of the Americas kneels in the white and black habit of a Dominican tertiary, gazing on the Christ Child seated on a cushion above her workbasket. Murillo presents the Limeña mystic with the gentle sweetness and silvery half-light that defined his late religious painting.
credit: FwHzDDlsnnrEmA at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Woman Writing a Letter
description: A seated lady drafts a letter at a draped table while her standing maid waits, glancing toward a sunlit window. Vermeer constructs the interior with his characteristic geometry of floor tiles, parallel walls, and a hanging picture, weaving the small domestic event into a quietly orchestrated study of light and waiting.
credit: Derived from National Gallery of Ireland

title: Still Life with Servant
artist: Paul de Vos / Possibly Cornelis de Vos
description: A monumental Flemish pronk still life heaped with fruit, game and silver, attended by a servant figure likely contributed by a collaborating figure painter. The composition exemplifies the Antwerp specialty of combining a Snyders-school animal painter with a portraitist for sumptuous decorative ensembles.
credit: 8gF_WAOn6cnSwg at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Mt. Heng, after Juran, from the Mustard Seed Garden Manual of Painting
description: China; Print; Prints
objectKey: collection-of-beauty/清_王槩_設計，李漁出版_芥子園畫傳_版本-Mt._Heng,_after_Juran_(active_ca._960–965),_from_the_Mustard_Seed_Garden_Manual_of_Painting_MET_DP-14004-001.jpg
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: The Laffite Family
description: A group portrait of the Laffite family by the leading court painter of Louis XIV. Rigaud's studio specialised in elaborate full-length likenesses of French nobility, with rich drapery, polished flesh tones and carefully posed sitters that established the visual vocabulary of Bourbon state portraiture.

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: Anatomy
description: Anatomy Iconographic Collections Keywords: Bernhard Siegfried Albinus; G. Scotin; Bernhard Siegfried 1697-1770. Albinus

title: Anatomy
description: Anatomy Iconographic Collections Keywords: Bernhard Siegfried Albinus; Simon Frangois Ravenet; Bernhard Siegfried 1697-1770. Albinus

title: Anatomy
description: Anatomy Iconographic Collections Keywords: Bernhard Siegfried Albinus; Charles Grignion; Bernhard Siegfried 1697-1770. Albinus

title: Self Portrait in a Turban
description: Rigaud, the great portraitist of Louis XIV's court, presents himself in an exotic turban, a studio prop conveying artistic vanity and learned cosmopolitanism. The lustrous fabrics and confident chiaroscuro foreshadow the polished grandeur he would soon bring to royal portraiture.

title: Diana and Her Dog
description: The huntress goddess pauses at rest, half-draped, with her hound pressed against her side and quiver beside her. Ricci handles the mythological subject with the airy palette and rococo grace that bridge late Venetian Baroque and the eighteenth century.
credit: DAH5bsUIX48KXQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Susannah and the Elders
description: The chaste Susanna twists away from two leering elders who corner her at her bath, the apocryphal subject a favorite vehicle for the female nude. Chiari, a pupil of Carlo Maratta, paints in a late-Roman Baroque idiom of cool flesh tones and theatrical gesture.
credit: Walters Art Museum : Home page Info about artwork

title: Portrait of Louis XIV
description: The Sun King at sixty-three, draped in ermine and blue velvet fleurs-de-lis, his bewigged head turned over a leg cocked in a courtly dance pose. Rigaud's state portrait set the template for European royal imagery, multiplied across embassies and palaces in countless studio replicas.

title: Kaigetsudo Ando - Standing Portrait of a Courtesan
description: A Yoshiwara courtesan stands alone in a richly patterned kimono, hip cocked in the languid S-curve that became the workshop's hallmark. As founder of the Kaigetsudō school, Ando concentrated entirely on monumental beauties, painted in fluid ink with broad swathes of color and gold on paper.

title: Matteo Ripa001 - Morning Glow on the Western Ridge
description: A copperplate view of the Kangxi Emperor's summer retreat at Chengde, with mist breaking over the western ridge in fine engraved hatching. Ripa, a Neapolitan missionary at the Qing court, produced the imperial album that introduced Western intaglio printing techniques to China.
credit: Scan from "New Visions at the Ch'ing Court"

title: KIYONOBU-Yamanaka-Ichikawa-1714
description: Two kabuki actors square off in a confrontational pose typical of aragoto bravura roles. Kiyonobu, founder of the Torii line of theatre artists, defined the early ukiyo-e print with bold contour lines and the hand-applied tan-e orange-red pigment characteristic of pre-polychrome woodblock work.

title: KorinsScreen
description: A pair of folding screens depicting the rocky pine-clad islets of Matsushima Bay, with stylised waves curling in gold-leaf and malachite green. Kōrin, leading master of the Rinpa school, treats the famed scenic site as flat decorative pattern rather than topographic record.
credit: Ogata Kōrin's Waves at Matsushima, public domain from the Smithsonian Museum of Asian Art

title: Venus and Satyr
description: A reclining Venus, attended by putti, is approached by a leering satyr in a confection of soft pinks, blues, and silver greys. Ricci's eroticized mythology helped translate the Venetian tradition of Veronese into a lighter rococo idiom.
credit: DQF-_OvOQfBgdw at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Pilgrimage to Cythera
description: Lovers prepare to leave the island of Venus in golden afternoon light, a melancholic departure rather than the arrival the title might suggest. Watteau's reception piece for the Académie royale invented the genre of the fête galante, with feathery brushwork drawn from his study of Rubens.
credit: Retouched from File:L'Embarquement pour Cythere, by Antoine Watteau, from C2RMF.jpg , originally C2RMF: Galerie de tableaux en très haute définition : image page

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: Nymphe de fontaine
description: A reclining nymph leans on her urn beside a flowing stream in a wooded glade. The drawing's soft contours and pastoral setting prefigure the Arcadian fete galante imagery for which Watteau would soon become known.
credit: 1. reproarte.com 2. Sotheby's New York, 26 January 2012, lot 63

title: Orchids
description: A literati-style study of orchids in ink on paper, drawn with the calligraphic spontaneity that defined Ike no Taiga's Nanga painting. The plant's sparse leaves and blossoms are set down in a few rapid strokes, a Confucian emblem of refined virtue rendered with Edo-period painterly freedom.

title: Portrait of Cardinal Guillaume Dubois
description: Dubois, tutor to the future Louis XV and chief minister under the Regent, sits enveloped in a voluminous wrap of scarlet silk topped by a short fur cape. Rigaud channels the swagger of Louis XIV-era state portraiture into a study of ecclesiastical and political power, the textiles handled with the sumptuous fluency that made him the Bourbon court's favoured face-maker.

title: Venus asks Vulcan for Weapons for his Son Aeneas
description: Venus leans toward Vulcan in his forge to plead for arms for her son Aeneas, the Cyclopes labouring at the anvil behind. Submitted as the painter's morceau de réception to the Académie Royale in 1734, the picture is a polished demonstration of the decorative Rococo mythology Natoire would soon deploy at Versailles.

title: The Triumph of Bacchus; oil on canvas
description: An exuberant Rococo procession of the wine god, attended by satyrs, maenads and tumbling putti amid garlands and overturned vessels. Natoire animates the mythological subject with feathery brushwork and pearled flesh tones characteristic of the French eighteenth century court taste.

title: Gio. Francesco II Brignole-Sale
credit: uAGQ6aZ0QZCs0A at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Rebuke of Adam and Eve
description: Adam and Eve are driven from Eden by an admonishing angel in this Rococo treatment of Genesis. Natoire softens the moralizing subject with flowing draperies, pastel colour and graceful attitudes drawn from his work as director of the French Academy in Rome.

title: Clayton Jones
description: An early Suffolk-period portrait of a young sitter, painted while Gainsborough was working in the manner of Hayman and the French rococo printmakers. The lightly built features and silvery palette already foreshadow the feathery brushwork of his mature society portraits.
credit: JgGJdiwSpAGoIA at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: A skeleton
description: Iconographic Collections

title: Skeleton with fewer muscles
description: A plate from Bernhard Siegfried Albinus's Tabulae sceleti et musculorum corporis humani (Leiden, 1747), engraved by Jan Wandelaar. The skeleton is set in a poetic landscape with a rhinoceros, marrying anatomical exactitude to Enlightenment-era pictorial staging.
credit: Historical Medical Books at the Claude Moore Health Sciences Library, University of Virginia

title: Skeleton with muscles
description: Another plate from Albinus's Tabulae sceleti et musculorum, in which Wandelaar layers the superficial muscles over the skeleton in a posed standing figure. The series was prized for its proportional accuracy, achieved with grids and double-perspective drafting unusual for medical illustration of the period.
credit: Historical Medical Books at the Claude Moore Health Sciences Library, University of Virginia

title: Cornard Wood
description: An early woodland scene from Gainsborough's Suffolk years, made before he turned full-time to portrait commissions in Bath and London. Drawing on Dutch landscape models, particularly Ruisdael, he threads a sandy track through scrubby oaks under a broken English sky.

title: Mr. and Mrs. Andrews
dateCreated: between 1748 and 1750 Romanticism (second half of 18 th century –first half of 19 th century )
description: A double portrait of a young Suffolk landowner and his bride seated at the edge of their estate, the rolling cornfields and oak deliberately presented as part of their identity. The crisp early style fuses French rococo elegance with topographical precision unusual in English portraiture.

title: Harunobu 2 Pers
description: A pair of figures in the slender, doll-like proportions Harunobu favoured, set within a softly muted interior. He was the pioneer of full-colour nishiki-e printing in 1760s Edo, shifting ukiyo-e away from hand-tinted limited palettes toward layered woodblock impressions.

title: Woman Admiring Plum Blossoms at Night
description: A young woman holds a paper lantern up to a flowering plum branch in the snow, the colour-printed surface tuned to soft greys and blacks. Harunobu was the first ukiyo-e artist to exploit the full nishiki-e palette, and the quiet nocturne is a showcase for it.

title: The Skating Minister
description: A Church of Scotland minister glides across a frozen Edinburgh loch in elegant profile, arms folded against the chill. The silhouetted black figure on pale ice and misty hills became an emblem of the Scottish Enlightenment and a touchstone of Scottish national painting.
credit: National Gallery of Scotland, Edinburgh, online collection

title: Ono Waterfall on the Kisokaidō
description: Travellers and packhorses pause on the Kisokaidō highway beside the Ono falls, dwarfed by a smooth blue sheet of water. From the Tour of the Waterfalls, the print pairs Hokusai's stylised, almost geometric treatment of cascades with the everyday traffic of one of Edo Japan's great post roads.

title: Suzuki Harunobu
description: From Harunobu's Eight Views of Ōmi, the print transposes the Chinese landscape topos onto Lake Biwa, here the returning sails at Yabase. Slender figures in a domestic interior stand in for the classical scenery, a witty mitate typical of his refined nishiki-e.
credit: Museum of Fine Arts, Boston, via [ukiyo-e.org ukiyo-e.org] ( Search archive )

title: Tenman Bridge at Settsu Province (Sesshū Tenmanbashi)
description: Japan; Woodblock print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: The Amida Falls in the Far Reaches of the Kisokaidō Road
description: From the celebrated series Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji, this woodblock print depicts the towering Amida Falls cascading from a circular pool said to resemble the head of the Buddha Amida. Hokusai pairs sweeping calligraphic line with flat planes of indigo and ochre, dwarfing tiny travellers beside the rushing water.

title: The Yoshitsune Horse-Washing Falls at Yoshino
description: Yoshitsune's retainers wash their horses beneath the Yoshino falls, the cascade splitting into braided ribbons of pale paper. The plate from the Waterfalls series fuses warrior legend with landscape, characteristic of how Hokusai layered narrative into his late topographical print sets.

title: Hilly Landscape with Figures Approaching a Bridge
description: A wooded river landscape in the Dutch-influenced manner of Gainsborough's middle years, with travellers descending toward a rustic bridge. Feathered foliage and a silvery palette establish the gentle, picturesque mood that defined his Bath-period landscapes.
credit: QQEt9B7ARFCb1A at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Beauty on a Veranda
description: An oversized nishiki-e showing a woman on a veranda outside a chamber where a party is underway, parodying the Muken-no-Kane scene from the play Hiragana Seisuiki. Harunobu's mitate updates classical drama into the language of the Edo pleasure quarters, with his characteristically delicate, almost weightless figures.

title: Gathering Bamboo Shoots
description: "Gathering Bamboo Shoots". Colored woodblock print

title: Harunobu Kanzan et Jittoku
description: A mitate of the Chinese Chan eccentrics Hanshan and Shide, Jittoku identifiable by his ever-present broom. Harunobu reimagines the Buddhist pair in the contemporary dress of Edo youths, the kind of layered classical-vernacular play that defined his refined nishiki-e output.

title: Young Man with Chrysanthemums
description: An unsigned chuban egoyomi, or pictorial calendar print, in which the days of the long and short months are concealed within the design. Harunobu produced these privately commissioned prints for poetry circles in the mid-1760s, and their experiments drove the development of full-colour brocade printing.

title: Road from Market
description: A rural landscape in which figures with laden horses return along a sunken track at the close of day. Gainsborough constructs the scene from imagined, idealised motifs, building a poetic vision of English country life in the Bath-period pastoral mode.
credit: ugG16d2TvlRyYw at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: An Experiment on a Bird in an Air Pump
description: A candle-lit demonstration of a vacuum pump, with a cockatoo gasping in its glass globe as a mixed audience reacts in awe, fascination, and dread. Wright transposes religious chiaroscuro onto the new theatre of Enlightenment science.

title: The Alchemist Discovering Phosphorus or The Alchemist in Search of the Philosophers Stone
description: An aged alchemist kneels before a glowing flask as phosphorus ignites in his laboratory, alluding to Hennig Brand's 1669 discovery. Wright stages the scene with the theatrical single-source illumination that defined his Enlightenment subjects, balancing scientific wonder against gothic shadow.

title: An Iron Forge
credit: 8QFaV4btMWcjBA at Google Cultural Institute , zoom level maximum

title: The Artist's Brother
description: An early portrait by the teenage Vigée Le Brun shows her younger brother Étienne in elegant attire, his open expression rendered with the precocious assurance that would soon win her royal commissions. The handling already displays the porcelain finish and Rococo grace of her mature manner.

title: The muscles of the human body
description: The muscles of the human body, fourth layer, seen from the front. Colour mezzotint by A. E.

title: The Rising Squall
description: An early oil view from the cliffs above the Avon Gorge as a sudden storm sweeps in over the Hot Wells spa. The seventeen-year-old Turner already orchestrates wind, rain and shifting light into a unified atmospheric event.
credit: Sotheby's, London, 2 July 2025, L25033, lot 28 ( 2000 × 1609 pixel / 1,40 MB ) — with frame

title: Joseph Vernet
description: A bust-length portrait of the marine painter Joseph Vernet, posed in a powdered wig and dark coat against a neutral ground. Vigée Le Brun renders the older painter with the soft modeling and warm flesh tones that defined her late-Ancien Régime studio practice, treating him as a colleague rather than a patron.

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: The Artist Moved by the Grandeur of Antique Fragments
description: Fuseli's draughtsman slumps against a colossal antique foot and hand, overwhelmed by the ruins of classical achievement. Drawn in red chalk and sepia wash during his Roman years, the sheet captures the Sublime sense of inadequacy before antiquity that shaped Anglo-Continental Romanticism.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"f/fuseli/02antiqu"

title: Portrait of Anna Magdalena Schweizer
description: A young Zurich sitter is shown half-length in soft chalk and wash, her gaze direct, her hair lightly powdered in late Rococo fashion. Fuseli's Swiss portrait drawings of friends and relatives are markedly more sober than his theatrical paintings, prizing physiognomic precision over the visionary excess of his London canvases.

title: Evening Snow on the Heater
description: A young woman beside a kotatsu, part of Harunobu's Eight Parlour Views series that transposes classical "Eight Views" landscape themes into intimate domestic scenes. The cotton padding draped over the heater is rendered in karazuri blind embossing — an unprinted relief technique that mimics the bulk of fresh snow.
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Kiyonaga bathhouse women-2
description: Half of a celebrated diptych depicting women in a public bathhouse, an unusually informal subject from the Tenmei era. Kiyonaga, head of the Torii school, sets his tall, calm figures in the careful spatial recession that reformed bijinga for the next generation.

title: Peace bringing back Prosperity
description: An allegorical pair: Peace, in classical drapery, gently leads forward Abundance, who carries a sheaf of wheat and a cornucopia. The painting was Vigée Le Brun's reception piece for the Académie Royale, accepted in 1783, and shows her combining Neoclassical subject matter with the warm flesh tones and silken finish of her portrait practice.

provenance.collection: Académie royale de peinture et de sculpture

title: The Garroted Man
description: An etching of a condemned prisoner slumped in the iron collar of the garrote, crucifix in hand. Goya pares the scene to a single lit body against deep aquatint shadow, prefiguring the political grimness of the Disasters of War.

title: The muscles of the hand
description: An anatomical engraving combining a flayed hand after Bernhard Siegfried Albinus with a foot after De Courcelles, the muscles, tendons and bones laid bare for study. Engraved by Robert Bénard for the late eighteenth-century editions of the Encyclopédie, the plate typifies the Enlightenment project of consolidating earlier anatomical illustration into a single compendium.

title: Thetis beweint den toten Achilleus
description: The sea-nymph Thetis bends in mourning over the corpse of her son Achilles, her grief amplified by the painter's sinuous neoclassical line. Fuseli reinterprets the Homeric scene through the proto-Romantic lens of sublime emotion, giving the figures attenuated, almost weightless poise.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Madame du Barry
description: Marie-Jeanne Bécu, the former royal mistress of Louis XV, is shown in a relaxed garden setting with loose curls and a simple white gown. The image belongs to a series of informal portraits painted at Louveciennes, exchanging court formality for the pastoral neoclassical mode then fashionable in Paris.
credit: The Corcoran Gallery of Art, Washington, D.C. [1]

title: Self Portrait in a Straw Hat
description: The artist holds palette and brushes beneath a wide plumed straw hat, smiling outward in a pose adapted from Rubens's Chapeau de Paille. Vigée Le Brun cast herself as both fashionable subject and confident professional, a self-promotional image she repeated.

title: Yolande-Martine-Gabrielle de Polastron
description: A portrait of Marie Antoinette's intimate favourite, shown in a soft straw hat and informal muslin dress holding a sheet of music. Vigée Le Brun's pearly flesh tones and feathery brushwork epitomise the late ancien régime ideal of natural elegance.

title: Admiral Rodney at the Battle of the Saintes
description: A swagger portrait of Admiral Lord Rodney, victor over the French at the 1782 Battle of the Saintes, the captured ensign of the Ville de Paris draped behind him. Gainsborough's feathery brushwork and silvery palette soften the formal demands of state portraiture.

title: Coastal Landscape with a Shepherd and His Flock
description: A late landscape in which a shepherd and flock cross a windswept shore beneath gathering clouds. Gainsborough loosens the topography into soft, tonal silhouettes, prefiguring the atmospheric coastal scenes that would influence British Romantic painters.
credit: uQExDZ9wUXdRhw at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Madame Grand
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Marie-Antoinette with the Rose
description: The queen in a simple white muslin gaulle holding a single rose, a deliberately informal court image the painter produced for the Salon. Public outrage at the chemise dress forced the picture's withdrawal and replacement with a more conventionally regal version.

title: Portrait of Charles-Alexandre de Calonne
description: The Controller-General of Finances under Louis XVI shown at his desk, gesturing to a state document. The portrait, lavishly painted in silks and gilt, became notorious as evidence at court of the painter's alleged closeness to her Versailles sitters.
credit: cQGHDF7RxrnPWQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Portrait of Marie Gabrielle de Gramont
description: The young duchesse sits in a relaxed pose, an open book in her lap and her powdered hair tumbling in fashionable disarray. Vigée Le Brun, official portraitist to Marie-Antoinette, favoured this informality of dress as a calculated marker of natural sensibility.
credit: _wHjP8UuQV3NbQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Dovedale by Moonlight
description: A silvered view of the Derbyshire valley of Dovedale under a full moon, its limestone crags and quiet river rendered in a cool, near-monochrome palette. Wright pursued nocturnes throughout his career, applying the dramatic chiaroscuro he developed for candlelit interiors to the open landscape.
credit: OwHAUYcn31UU7g at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Portrait of Mrs. Richard Brinsley Sheridan
dateCreated: between 1785 and 1787 Romanticism (second half of 18 th century –first half of 19 th century )
description: A full-length portrait of the singer and society beauty Elizabeth Linley, wife of the playwright Sheridan, posed against a windswept landscape. Gainsborough merges sitter and setting through fluttering drapery and feathery foliage in his late, freely brushed manner.
credit: National Gallery of Art pen Access Images Project. Link: https://www.nga.gov/content/ngaweb/Collection/art-object-page.99.html

title: Portrait of Madame Molé-Reymond
description: The actress of the Comédie-Italienne turns toward the viewer in a fur-trimmed coat and broad black hat, a muff held loosely at her side. Vigée Le Brun catches the sitter mid-movement with a fresh, animated brushwork that announces the informal, theatrical portraiture of the late ancien régime.

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: The Marquise de Pezay
description: A double portrait set out of doors, the Marquise de Pezay seated beside the Marquise de Rougé, whose two young sons press in beside her. Painted on the eve of the Revolution, the canvas blends the natural settings and tender maternal grouping that Vigée Le Brun had refined in her portraits of Marie Antoinette and her circle.
credit: 1. www.ne.jp 2. National Gallery of Art, Washington, DC

title: The Marsham Children
description: Four children of the Marsham family arranged in a wooded park, gathering nuts. Painted at the end of Gainsborough's career, it pairs the easy informality of his "fancy pictures" with the feathery, broken brushwork and silvery greens that define his late landscape backgrounds.

title: Portrait of Hubert Robert
description: The painter of picturesque ruins, Hubert Robert, is shown half-length in painter's smock, palette in hand, his unruly hair and quick-eyed glance conveying improvisatory energy. Vigée Le Brun's portrait of her colleague is a touchstone of late Ancien Régime artistic friendship, executed shortly before the Revolution dispersed both sitter and painter.

title: Portrait of Muhammad Dervish Khan
description: Tipu Sultan's ambassador to the court of Louis XVI stands full-length in robes of patterned silk, hand resting on a sabre. Painted in Paris in 1788 on the eve of revolution, the work remained in the artist's possession for fifty years as a record of her cosmopolitan clientele.

title: Thor's Battle with the Midgard Serpent
description: The Norse god Thor wrestles the Midgard Serpent in a churning sea, muscles knotted in the elongated, theatrical anatomy Fuseli favored. The Swiss-born painter mined Eddic and Miltonic mythology for charged subjects, exaggerating chiaroscuro and pose for sublime effect.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: A Moonlight with a Lighthouse
description: A Tyrrhenian lighthouse silhouetted against a cool, full moon over the Italian coast, recalled from Wright's 1773-75 sojourn near Naples. The painting exemplifies his fascination with competing light sources, here moonlight alone setting the spectral palette.

title: Comtesse de la Châtre
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Madame Vigée-Lebrun and Her Daughter
description: The painter is shown embracing her young daughter Julie in a Neoclassical interior, both wearing simple white chemise dresses. The intimate, sentimental staging was calculated to project natural maternal feeling for a Salon audience on the eve of revolution.
credit: own work, current photo taken by user Cybershot800i.

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: The Lictors Bring to Brutus the Bodies of His Sons
description: The Roman consul Brutus sits in shadow as lictors return the bodies of his sons, executed for treason on his order. David exhibited the picture at the Salon weeks after the storming of the Bastille, and Revolutionary audiences read its civic stoicism as immediate political instruction.

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: A View of the Archbishop's Palace
description: Submitted to the Royal Academy in April 1790, the month Turner turned fifteen, this was his exhibition debut. The watercolour shows a topographical precision learned from Thomas Malton, the architecture set at sharp angles to flaunt the teenager's grasp of perspective.

title: Countess Ekaterina Vassilievna Skavronskaia
description: The Russian countess reclines in a white empire-line gown trimmed with gauze, painted during Vigée Le Brun's emigration years among the European aristocracy. The picture's smooth porcelain finish and pearly tonality typify her courtly manner adapted to Saint Petersburg taste.

title: Fisherman's Cottage
description: A modest coastal cottage on the Kentish cliffs, rendered in the picturesque idiom Turner absorbed from Thomas Girtin and the watercolour tradition. The sheet dates from his teenage years working up topographical views for the print market.
credit: IAEH0C4fbQn60w at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Maria Luisa di Borbone
description: The Neapolitan princess, later Holy Roman Empress as wife of Francis II, painted during Vigée Le Brun's Italian sojourn after fleeing the French Revolution. The portraitist's flattering palette and soft, naturalistic informality made her a favourite of Europe's exiled royalty.

title: Portrait of Emma
description: Emma Hart, later Lady Hamilton, sits in three-quarter view with cascading dark hair and an open shawl, her gaze turned outward. The portrait reflects Vigée Le Brun's Italian period and her interest in the antique 'attitudes' for which Hamilton was already celebrated in Naples.

title: Portrait of Princess Maria Christina
description: A Bourbon princess of the Two Sicilies depicted with the soft, flushed informality Vigée Le Brun favoured for royal sitters. Painted during the artist's long European exile after fleeing Revolutionary France, the portrait carries her trademark blend of neoclassical poise and rococo warmth.

title: Self-portrait
description: Painted in Florence for the Uffizi's gallery of artists' self-portraits during her revolutionary exile, Vigée Le Brun shows herself at the easel sketching Marie Antoinette. The picture asserts both her professional standing as portraitist to the queen and her loyalty in the face of political upheaval.
credit: Art Renewal Center old image: from [1]

title: Titania and Bottom
description: The fairy queen, drugged by Oberon's love-juice, fawns over the ass-headed weaver from A Midsummer Night's Dream. Fuseli, a key contributor to Boydell's Shakespeare Gallery, exaggerates limbs and eyes into a mannerist dream-language drawn from Michelangelo and his own night terrors.

title: Portrait of Hyacinthe-Gabrielle Roland
description: The young Irish actress and future Marchioness Wellesley sits with a drift of sash and ribboned hair against a wooded ground. Vigée Le Brun's London exile period produced a string of such portraits in her airy, neoclassical mode of softened drapery and glowing skin.
credit: 1. ne.jp 2./3. Unknown source Unknown source 4. The AMICA Library

provenance.collection: Fine Arts Museums of San Francisco

title: Life Study of Lady Hamilton as the Cumaean Sybil
description: Emma Hamilton turns in the studio with a turbaned head and parted lips, posed as the prophetic Sibyl of Cumae. Vigée Le Brun carried the resulting composition through her exile from Naples to Vienna and Russia as a calling card of her Italian period.

title: Clare Hall and King’s College Chapel
description: An early topographical watercolour from Turner's Cambridge tour, viewing Clare Hall and the soaring Perpendicular pinnacles of King's College Chapel across the Cam. Drawn at eighteen, the sheet shows the precise architectural draftsmanship that earned him admission to the Royal Academy schools.
credit: jAEdez5qz_FbpQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Portrait of Countess Siemontkowsky Bystry
description: A society portrait from Vigée Le Brun's twelve-year exile after fleeing revolutionary Paris, when she travelled the courts of Italy, Vienna, and Saint Petersburg. Soft pastel-key flesh tones, a feathered hat, and a deliberately informal pose carry forward the Marie-Antoinette idiom that made her career.

title: Princess Karoline von Liechtenstein
description: The young princess is presented as Iris, draped in classicizing white muslin against a stormy sky, a length of rainbow gauze trailing from her shoulder. Vigée Le Brun painted her in Vienna during the émigré years that followed the artist's flight from revolutionary France.

title: Courtyard with Lunatics or Yard with Madmen
description: A small tin-plate painting from Goya's recovery after the illness that left him deaf, drawing on a violent scene he claimed to have witnessed in Zaragoza. Naked inmates wrestle under a barred opening while a warden flogs them, the cell's harsh chiaroscuro foreshadowing the Black Paintings.
credit: The Meadows Museum's Permanent Collection , accessed 2010-03-20.

title: Ebisu
description: An ukiyo-e print issued during Sharaku's brief, intense ten-month career, depicting a kabuki actor in the role of the smiling fisherman-deity Ebisu. The mica-grounded portrait style condenses a stage character into bold contour and exaggerated expression.
credit: Yamaguchi, Keizaburō (1994). Sharaku no Zenbō 写楽の全貌 (in Japanese). Tōkyō Shoseki. ISBN 978-4-487-79075-3 .

title: Odysseus in front of Scylla and Charybdis
description: Odysseus steers his vessel between the six-headed Scylla and the whirlpool Charybdis, his crew contorted in heroic dread. The composition belongs to Fuseli's lifelong cycle of literary illustrations, where storms of muscular figures stage classical and Miltonic catastrophe.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: The Angler
description: A solitary angler beside a wooded stream, painted in Turner's youthful picturesque manner. The watercolour reflects his early apprenticeship copying after Richard Wilson and the British landscape engravers.
credit: HAHWiWo1pknk2Q at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Takashima Ohisa Using Two Mirrors to Observe Her Coiffure: Night of the Asakusa Marketing Festival
description: Ohisa, a celebrated Edo teahouse beauty, inspects the back of her coiffure with paired mirrors during the Asakusa autumn festival. Utamaro's precise hairlines and luminous mica-dusted ground typify his bust-portrait okubi-e of fashionable contemporary women.

title: The Nude Maja
dateCreated: 1800 / between 1795 and 1800 Romanticism (second half of 18 th century –first half of 19 th century )
description: A reclining nude meets the viewer's gaze without mythological alibi, the first life-size unidealized female nude in Spanish painting. Commissioned by prime minister Manuel Godoy for a private cabinet, the picture later drew the Inquisition's attention to Goya himself.
credit: The Nude Maja . On-line gallery . Museo del Prado (2012). Archived from the original on 2010-01-03 . Retrieved on 2013-04-07 .

title: Alexandra and Elena Pavlovna of Russia
description: A double portrait of Grand Duchesses Alexandra and Elena Pavlovna, daughters of Paul I, painted soon after Vigée Le Brun's arrival at the Russian court in exile from revolutionary France. Soft pastel-like flesh and silvery silks display the courtly grace that made her Marie Antoinette's preferred portraitist.

title: Countess Anna Ivanovna Tolstaya
description: Painted during Vigée Le Brun's émigré years in Saint Petersburg, the Russian countess wears a soft white gown and a velvet wrap. The artist's Russian sitters helped sustain her career through the Revolution and account for many of her finest late portraits.

title: Fishermen at Sea
description: Turner's first oil exhibited at the Royal Academy, depicting a fishing boat tossed beneath a cold moon off the Needles, Isle of Wight. The contrast of silver lunar light and orange lantern glow announces a lifelong preoccupation with luminous opposition.
credit: Google Arts & Culture — UwG73jK8wqshlw Tate Images ( http://www.tate-images.com/results.asp?image=T01585&wwwflag=3&imagepos=2 )

title: Hairdresser
description: From the Twelve Types of Women's Handicraft series, an elegant ukiyo-e study of a kamiyui dressing a client's elaborate coiffure. Utamaro's signature bijinga close-up, pale ground and supple linework foreground the intimate gestures of female labour in the pleasure quarters.
credit: FwHe3o0UcP3NpA at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Portrait of Princess Anna Alexandrovna Galitzin
description: A Hermitage portrait of the Georgian-born Princess Galitzin, painted during Vigée Le Brun's long Russian exile after the Revolution. Pale silks and a soft sfumato face exemplify the flattering ancien-régime manner she carried across European courts.

title: The Sleep of Reason Produces Monsters
description: An artist slumps over his desk as bats and owls swarm from the dark behind him, plate forty-three of Goya's Caprichos. The etching with aquatint, issued in Madrid in 1799, became the central emblem of the series' attack on Spanish superstition and unreason.

title: Buttermere Lake
description: A rainbow arcs over Buttermere following a Lake District shower, the slate fells doubled in the still water below. Turner exhibited the picture at the Royal Academy aged twenty-three, after a Cumberland sketching tour. It is one of his earliest essays in the sublime mountain weather effects that would define him.

title: Shipping by a Breakwater
description: Merchant vessels ride the chop beside a stone breakwater in a dockside scene from Turner's early career, when he was building a reputation as a marine painter. The handling still leans on the Dutch seventeenth-century tradition of van de Velde.

title: Julie as Flora
description: The painter's daughter Julie posed as the Roman flower goddess, garlanded and seen against a soft pastoral ground. The mythological costume portrait belongs to the artist's exile years following the Revolution, when she worked across the European courts.

provenance.collection: Museum of Fine Arts, St. Petersburg, Florida, USA
provenance.location: Museum of Fine Arts, St. Petersburg, Florida, USA

title: Riders Attacked by a Giant Serpent
description: A horseman is overwhelmed by an enormous serpent that coils around him and his rearing mount. Fuseli specialised in nightmare iconography of this kind, drawing on Mannerist precedents to push human and animal anatomy into expressive distortion.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Snow Scene
description: Kunisada, the prolific Utagawa-school designer, sets a kabuki-style figure against falling snow on the banks of an Edo river. The plush patterned kimono and densely speckled white sky show his command of beauty prints and the expanded palette of nineteenth-century woodblock printing.
credit: Online Collection of Brooklyn Museum ; Photo: Brooklyn Museum, X632.2_IMLS_PS3.jpg

title: Academic study of a male torso
description: A youthful académie made in Jacques-Louis David's studio, drawn from a posed model with painstaking anatomical observation. Such exercises were the bedrock of Ingres's Neoclassical training and prefigure the sculptural torsos in his later history paintings.

title: Study of a Male Nude
description: An academic male nude executed during Ingres's training under David, when he was competing for the Prix de Rome. The figure's smooth modelling and elongated proportions already announce the linear classicism that would define his career.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"i/ingres/00ingres"

title: Fishermen Upon a Lee-Shore in Squally Weather
description: Fishermen struggle to bring a sailing vessel ashore as breaking surf and squall lines press against a steep coast. An early Royal Academy exhibit, the canvas sets Turner's lifelong concern with mariners' jeopardy in a darker, more Dutch-inflected idiom.

title: Grenoble Seen from the River Drac with Mont Blanc in the Distance
description: Grenoble lies between the Drac and the distant snowfields of Mont Blanc, drawn during Turner's first Continental tour after the Peace of Amiens reopened France. The 1802 Alpine campaign permanently reshaped his sense of geological scale. The picture's stretched horizontal format prefigures his later panoramic mountain views.

title: Ships bearing up for Anchorage
description: A squally Channel scene of merchantmen turning into the wind, indebted to the Dutch tradition of Willem van de Velde. Commissioned by the third Earl of Egremont, the canvas still hangs at Petworth House where Turner often painted.

title: The Tenth Plague of Egypt
description: Storm and divine wrath sweep over a darkened Egyptian city as the firstborn die. An early bid for the prestige of Old Master history painting, the picture leans openly on Poussin's biblical compositions.

title: Tummel Bridge
description: A Highland packhorse bridge over the Tummel, recorded on Turner's 1801 Scottish tour that supplied subjects for engraved series and Walter Scott illustrations. The watercolour balances rugged geology against an open, weather-shifting sky.
credit: GQHBAgj90T1exA at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Bonneville
description: An Alpine view from Turner's first Continental tour following the Peace of Amiens, recording the town of Bonneville against the Mont Blanc range. The classical compositional structure recalls Claude Lorrain, whom Turner studied closely as a young Royal Academician.

title: Calais Pier
description: Embarking passengers and fishwives crowd the French pier as a squall builds beyond the harbour mouth. Turner had himself crossed to Calais in rough weather in 1802, and he exhibited this large canvas to challenge the marine paintings of his French contemporaries.

title: The Pass of Saint Gotthard
description: A vertiginous Alpine gorge above the St Gotthard road, recorded on Turner's first Continental tour after the Peace of Amiens reopened Europe. The watercolour fed a series of Swiss subjects in which scale itself becomes the drama.

title: Arabian nights 3 by John Frederick Lewis
description: A finely worked Orientalist scene drawn from the Thousand and One Nights, set inside one of the tiled, latticed interiors Lewis studied during his decade in Cairo. He is best known for the jewel-bright watercolour technique that lets sunlight pick its way across carpets and costume.

title: Boats Carrying Out Anchors to the Dutch Men of War
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the National Gallery of Art . Please see the Gallery's Open Access Policy .

title: Newton
description: Blake's monoprint shows the natural philosopher hunched naked on a sea-floor rock, drawing geometric figures with a compass. The image reads as a critique of pure reason: Newton, absorbed in measurement, becomes blind to the imaginative cosmos beyond his diagram.

title: Fall of the Rhine at Schaffhausen
description: The thundering Rheinfall in Switzerland, its spray rising into a churning sky encountered on Turner's first Continental tour after the Peace of Amiens. The painting marks his early engagement with the Alpine sublime that would shape his Romantic vocabulary.

title: Philipp Otto Runge
description: A close study of a hound's head in chalk and lead, the breed's soft fur and alert eye observed with quiet precision. The drawing belongs to Runge's wider effort to ground his Romantic symbolism in patient natural observation.

title: Sunset on the River
description: An English river caught at the end of the day, with rushes and a low bank silhouetted against burning sky. The work belongs to the loose Thames oil sketches Turner made from his boat around Walton and Windsor.

title: The Deluge
description: An early biblical disaster scene depicting the universal flood, with massed bodies struggling against rising water beneath churning storm clouds. Turner builds the composition from a baroque diagonal of light, a precedent for his later sublime catastrophes.

title: The Hülsenbeck Children
dateCreated: between 1805 and 1806 Romanticism (second half of 18 th century –first half of 19 th century )
description: Three young siblings of the Hülsenbeck family stand and crouch in a Hamburg garden, the toddler clutching a sunflower leaf almost as large as himself. Runge invests the children with hieratic gravity, treating childhood as a metaphysical state.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: The Shipwreck
description: Survivors clinging to lifeboats amid mountainous green seas, the doomed vessel rolling beyond. Engraved by Charles Turner, this was the first of the artist's canvases reproduced as a mezzotint, spreading his fame as a painter of catastrophe.
