description: A dark-morph Red-tailed Hawk, which Audubon described as a distinct species he named Black Warrior in honour of an Alabama river, is shown in commanding profile. Now classified as Harlan's Hawk, the plate documents an important regional colour variant of the widespread raptor.

title: 87 Florida Jay
description: A pair of Florida Scrub-Jays perches on a flowering persimmon branch, the plate documenting a species endemic to the oak-scrub habitats of peninsular Florida. Hand-coloured aquatint engraved by Robert Havell Jr. after Audubon's watercolour from his Florida expedition.

title: 88 Autumnal Warbler
description: What Audubon described as the Autumnal Warbler is now recognized as the non-breeding plumage of the Bay-breasted Warbler, an instructive example of the seasonal variation that confused early American ornithology. Hand-coloured aquatint from the double-elephant folio.

title: 89 Nashville Warbler
description: Nashville Warblers move among the leaves and yellow blooms of a flowering plant, captured in the active foraging poses Audubon favoured over rigid museum-style profiles. Hand-coloured aquatint engraved in London by Robert Havell Jr.

title: 9 Selby's Flycatcher
description: What Audubon named Selby's Flycatcher in honour of British ornithologist Prideaux John Selby is now generally regarded as a Hooded Warbler. Hand-coloured aquatint from the double-elephant folio, recording one of several taxonomic redeterminations made after the work's initial publication.

title: 90 Black & White Creeper
description: Black-and-white Warblers ascend a tree trunk in the nuthatch-like fashion that gives the species its common name, with the bold zebra striping carefully rendered. Hand-coloured aquatint demonstrating Audubon's interest in characteristic posture as a diagnostic field mark.

title: 91 Broad-winged Hawk
description: An adult and juvenile Broad-winged Hawk perch together on a branch, paired to show the contrasting plumages of the small forest raptor known for its enormous autumnal kettling migrations. Hand-coloured aquatint engraving from the double-elephant folio.

title: 92 Pigeon Hawk
description: A Merlin, formerly called the Pigeon Hawk, grips a freshly killed Northern Bobwhite, the prey lying breast-up to display its plumage. The compact, agile falcon is shown in the predatory tableau Audubon used to convey both species' identity and ecological role.

title: 93 Sea-side Finch
description: Seaside Sparrows perch among the salt-marsh grasses that define their narrow coastal range from New England to the Gulf. Hand-coloured aquatint with the dingy olive-grey plumage and yellow lores rendered with the precision required for distinguishing closely related Ammospiza sparrows.

title: 94 Grass Finch or Bay-winged Bunting
description: A Vesper Sparrow stands among low grasses, the chestnut shoulder patch and white outer tail feathers carefully rendered as field marks. Hand-coloured aquatint from the double-elephant folio, executed by Robert Havell Jr. after Audubon's life-size watercolour.

title: 95 Yellow-poll Warbler
description: Yellow Warblers animate a flowering branch, with the male's chestnut breast streaks distinguishing him from the unstreaked female. Hand-coloured aquatint engraving in which the bird's lemon-yellow plumage is set against soft botanical illustration to enhance contrast.

title: 96 Columbia Jay
description: A pair of Black-throated Magpie-Jays, native to western Mexico, flaunts elongated tail feathers far exceeding their body length. The plate is unusual within Birds of America for depicting a species outside North American territory, drawn from skins rather than field encounter.

title: 97 Little Screech Owl
description: Three Eastern Screech-Owls illustrate the species' striking colour polymorphism, with red-morph and grey-morph individuals shown together on a single branch. Hand-coloured aquatint engraved by Robert Havell Jr., emphasizing the small owl's prominent ear tufts and yellow eyes.

title: 98 White-bellied Swallow
description: Tree Swallows wheel near a flowering plant, the iridescent blue-green dorsal plumage contrasted against the clean white underparts. Hand-coloured aquatint from the double-elephant folio, demonstrating Audubon's effort to depict aerial insectivores in characteristic flight rather than at rest.

title: 99 Cow-pen Bird
description: Brown-headed Cowbirds, the first North American brood parasite formally documented, are shown on a flowering branch. Hand-coloured aquatint engraving in which the male's warm brown head contrasts with the glossy black body, executed by Robert Havell Jr. in London.

title: A Ship Aground
description: A vessel keels over on the East Anglian sands at low tide, attended by figures in the wet shallows. The work belongs to a group of Yarmouth sample studies Turner produced for engravers as test pieces in a more vaporous late manner. It is now part of the Turner Bequest at the National Gallery.

title: Bathsheba
description: Hayez retells the Old Testament episode with a Venetian sensuousness, Bathsheba caught at her bath under King David's distant gaze. The smooth modelling and warm flesh tones reflect the Italian Romantic painter's debt to Titian.
credit: Christie's , LotFinder: entry 6510043 (sale 22691, lot 25, London, 3 December 2024)

title: Mortlake Terrace
description: A Thames-side terrace bathed in the dissolving glare of low evening sun, with a small black dog famously cut out and pasted onto the foreground lawn. The picture is among Turner's most radical experiments in painting directly into the light source.

title: Portrait of Auguste Strobl
description: A half-length portrait of Auguste Strobl, later Auguste Hilber, painted for King Ludwig I of Bavaria's Gallery of Beauties at Nymphenburg. Stieler's polished oil technique, soft modelling and restrained palette typify the Biedermeier court portraiture commissioned for that celebrated cycle of Munich women.

title: Shipping off East Cowes Headland
description: Sails crowd the Solent off East Cowes, where Turner stayed with the architect John Nash during the 1827 Royal Yacht Club regatta. The picture belongs to a series of studies generated by that summer visit.

title: Sketch for ‘East Cowes Castle
description: An oil sketch on canvas worked up while Turner was Nash's guest at East Cowes Castle. The yachts beating against the wind would be reorganised into the finished regatta picture exhibited at the Royal Academy in 1828.

title: Snowy Heron or White Egret
description: A snowy egret strides through shallows with golden feet outstretched, the long aigrette plumes that nearly drove the species to extinction cascading from nape and back. A small hunter crouches in the distance, an unusual narrative inclusion that links the bird to the plume trade beginning to thin its colonies.

title: The Apotheosis of Homer
description: Commissioned for a ceiling in the Louvre, this strictly symmetrical tableau enthrones the blind poet beneath a winged Victory while artists and writers from antiquity to the modern age gather as witnesses. Ingres treats the canvas almost as a bas-relief, marshalling Neoclassical contour and frieze-like balance to assert Homer's status as the wellspring of Western art.

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: The Lake
description: A small oil study made for Turner's Petworth series, painted while he stayed as a guest of the Earl of Egremont. The lake glows under a low sun in flat, almost abstract bands of pink, gold and lavender, anticipating the freedom of his late visionary landscapes.

title: Chichester Canal
description: The still water of the Sussex canal mirrors a tall-masted ship and a sky drenched in late sun. Turner painted the scene for his patron the Earl of Egremont, one of a set of canvases made for the panelled dining room at Petworth House and now in the Tate.

title: Hill Town on the Edge of the Campagna
description: An Italian hill town rises above the misted Campagna, drawn from sketches Turner made on his second visit to Rome. The thinly washed watercolour leaves the paper itself standing in for the bleached southern light he prized.

title: Hokusai
description: A cuckoo darts past a flowering azalea branch, its body sharply foreshortened in Hokusai's compact kachō-e mode. The pairing of a vocal summer bird with the seasonally apt blossom follows the conventions of haiku-illustrated prints, of which Hokusai was a leading designer.

title: Petworth Park: Tillington Church in the Distance
description: A long horizontal sweep of Egremont's deer park with Tillington's spire pinning the horizon. One of the studies made in the Petworth studio Egremont set aside for him, the picture is structured almost entirely by golden, grazing light.

title: Portrait of Cornelia Vetterlein
description: A portrait from Stieler's Munich practice, where he served as court painter to the Bavarian kings. The sitter is set against a smooth dark ground in the artist's signature manner, soft modelling of the face giving way to crisp, jewel-like attention at lace and ribbon.

title: The Defence of Saragossa
description: Wilkie commemorates the heroine Agustina de Aragón firing a cannon during the 1808 siege of Zaragoza, painted after his Spanish travels. The smoky, candlelit handling shows him reaching past genre toward the broader Romantic history mode of Géricault and Delacroix.

title: The Vale of Dedham
description: A panoramic view down the Stour Valley toward Dedham church and the Suffolk coast, the heart of Constable's native country. Painted late in his life, the picture distils decades of plein-air study into a richly worked oil, with broken touches of white — his signature "snow" — flickering across foliage and sky.
credit: 3wH6bKPjBycRxg at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: View of Orvieto
description: The cliff-set Umbrian town floats above its golden plain in a picture begun and finished during Turner's second Roman stay. Exhibited at his studio on the Quattro Fontane, it greeted Italian visitors as a deliberate answer to Claude.

title: Banks of the Loire
description: A French river view rendered in luminous gouache and watercolour on blue paper, part of Turner's projected 'Wanderings by the Loire' series. The chalky highlights catch a milky riverine light typical of his continental tour studies.

title: Lake Suwa in Shinano Province
description: Identificatie Titel(s): Het Suwa meer in de provincie Shinano. Shinshu Suwako (titel op object). 36 gezichten op de berg Fuji (serietitel).

title: Portrait of Anna Hillmayer
description: Anna Hillmayer, daughter of a Munich game merchant, sat for this picture as part of Ludwig I's Gallery of Beauties. Stieler departs from aristocratic convention to record a tradesman's daughter with the same idealised serenity and porcelain finish he gave his royal sitters.

title: A group of mountaineers
dateCreated: First publication: circa 1830 . This edition: circa 1930 .
description: A late plate from Hokusai's Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji, showing pilgrims roped along the rocky upper slopes of the sacred peak. Hokusai records the climb itself rather than the silhouette, using the famous bero-ai imported Prussian blue for the cold air at altitude.

title: A sketch of the Mitsui shop in Suruga in Edo
dateCreated: First publication: circa 1830 . This edition: circa 1930 .
description: From the Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji, Hokusai squeezes the snow-capped peak between the tiled roofs of the Mitsui dry-goods emporium. Kites, tradesmen, and a soaring eagle animate the bustling Edo street, the woodblock print pairing commerce and the sacred mountain in one frame.

title: Asakusa Hongan-ji temple in the Eastern capital
dateCreated: First publication: circa 1830 . This edition: circa 1930 .
description: A roofer kneels on the great tiled ridge of the Asakusa Hongan-ji while a kite drifts past and Mount Fuji rises in the distance. Plate four of the Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji turns the sacred peak into a small constant glimpsed across Edo's working rooftops.

title: At Sea off Kazusa (Kazusa no kairo)
description: Japan; Woodblock print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Basket-fishing in the Kinu River
description: From the Oceans of Wisdom series on traditional fishing methods, Hokusai shows villagers wading the Kinu with woven basket traps. The flattened riverbank and tight figural grouping demonstrate his mature handling of genre subjects in the 1830s.
credit: Original publication : Japan, 1830s Immediate source : https://ukiyo-e.org/image/mfa/sc145753

title: Carlyle Maclise Original
description: Thomas Carlyle leans against a pillar, top hat in hand, in a full-length character study by his fellow Scot Daniel Maclise. The portrait belongs to the celebrated Fraser's Magazine series of literary lions Maclise drew during the 1830s.

title: Ejiri in Suruga Province (Sunshū Ejiri)
description: Japan; Woodblock print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Enoshima in Sagami Province
dateCreated: First publication: circa 1830 . This edition: circa 1930 .
description: The tidal causeway to the sacred island of Enoshima opens before pilgrims as Mount Fuji rises in the distance. From Hokusai's Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji, the print exploits imported Prussian blue and the bold diagonal of the sandbar to organise its broad sea view.

title: Fuji—The Tama River, Musashi Province
description: Japan; Print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Fujimigahara in Owari Province (Bishū Fujimigahara)
description: Japan; Woodblock print; Prints
objectKey: collection-of-beauty/冨嶽三十六景_尾州不二見原-Fujimigahara_in_Owari_Province_(Bishū_Fujimigahara),_from_the_series_Thirty-six_Views_of_Mount_Fuji_(Fugaku_sanjūrokkei)_MET_DP141033.jpg
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Hakone Lake in Sagami Province
dateCreated: First publication: circa 1830 . This edition: circa 1930 .
description: From Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji, this print sets the still mirror of Lake Hakone in the foreground, with Fuji rising over wooded ridges beyond. Hokusai contrasts smooth bands of water with crisply outlined cedars and the modulated bokashi gradations of the sky.

title: Hodogaya on the Tōkaidō (Tōkaidō Hodogaya)
description: Japan; Woodblock print; Prints
objectKey: collection-of-beauty/冨嶽三十六景_東海道保土ケ谷-Hodogaya_on_the_Tōkaidō_(Tōkaidō_Hodogaya),_from_the_series_Thirty-six_Views_of_Mount_Fuji_(Fugaku_sanjūrokkei)_MET_DP141025.jpg
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: In the Mountains of Tōtomi Province (Tōtomi sanchū)
description: Japan; Woodblock print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Kajikazawa in Kai Province
dateCreated: First publication: circa 1830 . This edition: circa 1930 .
description: A lone fisherman casts his net from a jagged outcrop while Mount Fuji rises pale beyond the surf. From the Thirty-six Views, the design is among Hokusai's most pared-down, reducing landscape to interlocking triangles printed in the imported Prussian blue (bero-ai) that defines the series.

title: Koishikawa in the Morning after a Snowfall
description: From the Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji, Hokusai shows tea-house guests in Edo's Koishikawa district pausing over rice and sake to admire snow-clad Fuji at dawn. The white expanse and thin red sun exploit the limited palette of nishiki-e woodblock printing.

title: Liberty Leading the People
dateCreated: between October 1830 and December 1830 Romanticism (second half of 18 th century –first half of 19 th century )
description: An allegorical Liberty, bare-breasted and clutching the tricolore, leads insurgents over a barricade strewn with the dead during the July Revolution of 1830. Delacroix, the standard-bearer of French Romanticism, fuses contemporary reportage with classical personification in vivid, brushy oil.

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: Lower Meguro (Shimo Meguro)
description: Japan; Woodblock print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Mishima Pass in Kai Province (Kōshū Mishima goe)
description: Japan; Woodblock print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Mount Fuji seen from the tea plantation at Katakura in Suruga Province
dateCreated: First publication: circa 1830 . This edition: circa 1930 .
description: A woodblock print from the Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji series, framing the sacred peak through workers tending neat rows of tea bushes in Suruga Province. Hokusai integrates rural labor with landscape, the foreground figures pressing leaves into bundles while Fuji rises in cool, flat planes behind.

title: Nihonbashi in Edo (Edo Nihonbashi)
description: Japan; Woodblock print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Noboto Bay (Noboto no ura)
description: Japan; Woodblock print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Paddy fields in Ono
dateCreated: First publication: circa 1830 . This edition: circa 1930 .
description: From Hokusai's Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji, this print shows farmers tending paddy fields at Ōno in Suruga Province. The flattened planes of green rice terraces lead the eye toward Fuji's familiar silhouette in the distance.

title: Pleasure District at Senju
dateCreated: First publication: circa 1830 . This edition: circa 1930 .
description: From Hokusai's Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji, the print situates the sacred peak behind travelers and scenery in the provinces. Hokusai's signature flat planes of color and bold contour line organize the landscape into the layered recession the series made famous.

title: Reflection in Lake at Misaka in Kai Province (Kōshū Misaka suimen)
description: Japan; Woodblock print; Prints
objectKey: collection-of-beauty/冨嶽三十六景_甲州三坂水面-Reflection_in_Lake_at_Misaka_in_Kai_Province_(Kōshū_Misaka_suimen),_from_the_series_Thirty-six_Views_of_Mount_Fuji_(Fugaku_sanjūrokkei_MET_DP141064.jpg

title: Senju in Musashi Province (Bushū Senju)
description: Japan; Woodblock print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Shichirigahama in Sagami Province (Sōshū Shichirigahama)
description: Japan; Woodblock print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: South Wind
dateCreated: First publication: circa 1830 . This edition: circa 1930 .
description: Better known as Red Fuji, this woodblock print captures the volcano flushed warm by dawn light during the late summer southerlies. Banded clouds and a near-abstract triangular silhouette make it a touchstone of the Thirty-six Views series.

title: Sunset across the Ryōgoku bridge from the bank of the Sumida River at Onmayagashi
dateCreated: First publication: circa 1830 . This edition: circa 1930 .
description: Plate twelve of the Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji shows boatmen and stevedores along the Sumida embankment, the long span of Ryōgoku Bridge cutting the composition while Fuji rises faintly in the dusk. Hokusai keeps the celebrated peak modest in scale, ceding the foreground to working Edo.

title: Surugadai in Edo (Tōto Sundai)
description: Japan; Woodblock print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Tago Bay near Ejiri on the Tōkaidō (Tōkaidō Ejiri Tago no ura ryaku zu)
description: Japan; Woodblock print; Prints
objectKey: collection-of-beauty/冨嶽三十六景_東海道江尻田子の浦略図-Tago_Bay_near_Ejiri_on_the_Tōkaidō_(Tōkaidō_Ejiri_Tago_no_ura_ryaku_zu),_from_the_series_Thirty-six_Views_of_Mount_Fuji_(Fugaku_sanjūrokkei)_MET_DP141037.jpg
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: The back of the Fuji from the Minobu river
dateCreated: First publication: circa 1830 . This edition: circa 1930 .
description: A woodblock view from the series Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji, taken from the rear face of the mountain along the Minobu River. Hokusai contrasts the sinuous river bend and rocky banks with the broad, simplified silhouette of the peak, animated by tiny travelling figures.

title: The Circular Pine Trees of Aoyama
dateCreated: First publication: circa 1830 . This edition: circa 1930 .
description: A print from Hokusai's Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji, in which a clearing of pruned, circular pines frames the distant volcano. The flattened decorative pattern of the foliage demonstrates the artist's command of Edo-period graphic abstraction.

title: The Evening Star
description: A solitary boy with a dog wades along an empty Margate beach as Venus pricks the violet dusk. Pared back to bands of damp sand, sea and sky, the canvas distils Turner's late preoccupation with thresholds of light.

title: The Fuji seen from Kanaya on the Tokaido
dateCreated: First publication: circa 1830 . This edition: circa 1930 .
description: An additional view from the Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji, looking across the boulder-strewn Ōi River from the Kanaya post-station toward a snow-capped Fuji. Travellers and porters ford the shallows in the foreground, anchoring the landscape in everyday Tōkaidō life.

title: The Great Wave off Kanagawa
dateCreated: First publication: between circa 1830 and circa 1832 .
description: The opening sheet of Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji shows a towering offshore wave (okinami) breaking over fishing boats while Fuji stands small on the horizon. Hokusai's use of imported Prussian blue and his geometric treatment of foam made the print a touchstone of Edo design.

title: The Inume Pass in Kai Province (Kōshū Inume tōge)
description: Japan; Woodblock print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: The timberyard at Honjo
dateCreated: First publication: circa 1830 . This edition: circa 1930 .
description: From Hokusai's Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji, the print frames the sacred peak between stacks of squared timber on the Tatekawa canal. The geometric scaffolding of beams and ladders contrasts with workers and a kite drifting overhead, a quintessentially Edo conjunction of labour and landscape rendered in cool blue keylines.

title: The Waterwheel at Onden (Onden no suisha)
description: Japan; Woodblock print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Umezawa Manor in Sagami Province
dateCreated: First publication: circa 1830 . This edition: circa 1930 .
description: From the Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji, the volcano floats beyond a quiet manor pond ringed by cranes and pines. Hokusai marshals his celebrated bero-ai Prussian-blue gradients to balance domestic foreground against distant peak.

title: Under the Mannen Bridge at Fukagawa (Fukagawa Mannenbashi shita)
description: Japan; Woodblock print; Prints
objectKey: collection-of-beauty/冨嶽三十六景_深川万年橋下-Under_the_Mannen_Bridge_at_Fukagawa_(Fukagawa_Mannenbashi_shita),_from_the_series_Thirty-six_Views_of_Mount_Fuji_(Fugaku_sanjūrokkei)_MET_DP141017.jpg
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Ushibori in Hitachi Province (Jōshū Ushibori)
description: Japan; Woodblock print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Wasserdrachen
description: A coiling water dragon, or mizuchi, from a late ukiyo-e series devoted to birds, beasts, insects and fish. Eisen, better known for bijin-ga prints of Edo women, here adapts the colour woodblock to a more decorative, naturalist mode in keeping with kacho-e tradition.
credit: Museum of Art and Crafts Hamburg

title: Yoshida on the Tōkaidō (Tōkaidō Yoshida)
description: Japan; Woodblock print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Caligula's Palace and Bridge
credit: Google Arts & Culture — xQFxUjK0z9Pb_Q Tate Images ( http://www.tate-images.com/results.asp?image=N00512&wwwflag=3&imagepos=1 )

title: Lady Jane Elizabeth Digby
description: Painted for Ludwig I's Schönheitengalerie, the portrait shows the English aristocrat at twenty-four, before the marriages and travels that would carry her from Munich to a Bedouin tent near Damascus. Stieler's smooth, neoclassical finish lends the sitter the air of court decorum she would soon scandalously discard.

title: Marianna Marchesa Florenzi
description: The Italian noblewoman, translator and longtime confidante of Ludwig I of Bavaria, shown with the soft idealisation reserved for the king's Gallery of Beauties in Munich. Stieler emphasises her dark eyes and intellectual composure, signalling her standing as a scholar rather than mere society sitter.

title: Salisbury Cathedral from the Meadows
description: A late, emotionally charged view of Salisbury Cathedral seen across the water meadows under a rainbow, painted in the wake of Constable's wife's death. Flickering white highlights, the celebrated Constable snow, animate trees and sky in a picture that fused topographic affection with personal grief.

title: Storm Below the Summit
description: Thunderstorm Beneath the Summit, a Japanese woodblock print by Hokusai

title: The Refugees of Parga
description: Inhabitants of the Greek town of Parga gather their dead and their belongings before fleeing the 1819 Ottoman occupation, a contemporary tragedy embraced by Italian liberals. Hayez orchestrates the crowded shoreline as a Romantic tableau of exile, in line with his patriotic history paintings.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"h/hayez/2hayez"

title: Whaling off the Coast of the Goto Islands
description: Tiny whaling boats encircle a leviathan in choppy waters off Kyushu, their oarsmen dwarfed by the dark mass beneath. From Hokusai's Oceans of Wisdom series, the woodblock print pairs documentary subject with sweeping rhythmic line.

title: Calais Sands at Low Water: Poissards Collecting Bait
description: Local fisherwomen, the poissards, dig for lugworm bait on the broad expanse of Calais beach as the sun sinks behind them. Turner returned to the Channel coast repeatedly, treating Calais as the symbolic threshold between England and the Continent. The sky and wet sand dissolve into a single shimmering field of low light.

title: Childe Harold's Pilgrimage
description: Inspired by Byron's Mediterranean poem, Turner builds a luminous Italian campagna of crumbling arches, distant hills, and a glowing sky. The picture is part of the artist's long late dialogue with Claude Lorrain, dissolving classical landscape architecture into atmosphere.
credit: 1. abcgallery.com 2. Sotheby’s (not for sale, only as a reference image)

title: Futamigaura
description: Sunrise over the Wedded Rocks at Futamigaura, the Shinto pilgrimage site on Ise Bay where worshippers greet the dawn. Kunisada, the most prolific Utagawa-school designer of the late Edo period, frames the scene with his characteristic robust figural drawing and vivid bokashi sky.

title: Highland Hospitality
description: An early Scottish genre piece from before Lewis's Mediterranean and Egyptian years, showing crofters welcoming visitors at a Highland hearth. The picture belongs to a brief vogue for Walter Scott-inflected tartan subjects shared with Landseer. Lewis already shows his appetite for textured plaids, deer carcasses and rough stone interiors.
credit: vwF_I_gCRIKQcw at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Katsushika Hokusai
description: Cherry-blossom viewers gather on the bluff at Goten-yama overlooking Edo Bay, with Mount Fuji a faint cone on the horizon. Part of the Thirty-six Views, the print binds a popular hanami spot to the sacred mountain through Hokusai's careful balancing of human ritual and distant geology.

title: Portrait of Monsieur Bertin
description: The newspaper editor of the Journal des Débats sits forward, hands planted on his thighs, in a pose that became a touchstone for nineteenth-century bourgeois portraiture. Ingres's polished surface and tightly drawn contours register every wrinkle of Bertin's waistcoat without disturbing the granite stillness of the sitter.

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: Riders at the Tegernsee
description: Two riders pause on a height above the Tegernsee, the Bavarian Alps shimmering in clear morning air. Kobell, court painter in Munich, works in the crisp, glassy Biedermeier manner for which he is known, isolating sharply lit figures against a luminous, almost enamelled landscape.

title: Utagawa Kunisada I
description: Dawn breaks over the linked Wedded Rocks at Futami-ga-ura on the Ise coast, their sacred shimenawa rope strung between them. Kunisada renders the famous pilgrimage site in soft graded blues typical of early-1830s ukiyo-e landscape.

title: Yōrō Waterfall in Mino Province (Mino no Yōrō no taki)
description: Japan; Woodblock print; Prints
objectKey: collection-of-beauty/諸國瀧廻リ_美濃ノ国養老の滝-Yōrō_Waterfall_in_Mino_Province_(Mino_no_Yōrō_no_taki),_from_the_series_A_Tour_of_Waterfalls_in_Various_Provinces_(Shokoku_taki_meguri)_MET_DP141263.jpg
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Akasaka-juku
description: An Akasaka inn courtyard is shown in cross-section: maids preparing to receive guests on one side, bathing travellers on the other, separated by a clipped sago palm. The bird's-eye plan demonstrates the architectural curiosity Hiroshige brought to genre interiors.
credit: Database of the Museum of Fine Arts, Boston

title: Antwerp Van Goyen Looking Out for a Subject
description: An imagined homage in which the Dutch master Jan van Goyen sketches from a small boat in Antwerp harbor, surrounded by shipping under broken cloud. Turner pays tribute to the seventeenth-century landscape tradition while asserting his own atmospheric handling of sky and water.

title: Bridge of Sighs
description: Turner's Venetian capriccio assembles the Doge's Palace, the Bridge of Sighs and the Punta della Dogana along a sun-bleached lagoon. Architecture dissolves into luminous haze, foregrounding the Romantic interest in atmospheric light over topographic precision.

title: Chiryū-juku
description: Station Chiryū from the Tōkaidō series, depicting the early-summer horse fair on a grassy plain where merchants and tethered animals scatter under wide skies. Hiroshige uses subtle bokashi gradations along the horizon to evoke seasonal atmosphere; the Hoeidō publisher seal marks this as part of his career-making 1833 edition.
credit: Database of the Metropolitan Museum of Art, New York

title: Chôshi in Shimôsa Province
description: Chōshi in Shimōsa Province, from Hokusai's Oceans of Wisdom series, shows fishermen working their nets beneath a heaving wall of water. The aggressive curl and sprayed crest reprise, in horizontal format, the breaking-wave motif Hokusai had perfected a few years earlier in the Great Wave.

title: Ejiri-juku
description: Ejiri, set on Suruga Bay near the Miho pine grove, looks across a calm inlet dotted with sailing boats. Hiroshige flattens the distance into bands of graded color, a hallmark of his ukiyo-e landscape mode that influenced later Western printmakers.
credit: The fifty-stree stages of Tokaido (one of 53 prints, +2 for start and terminus)

title: Fuchū-juku
description: Travelers ford the Abe River at Fuchu, carried piggyback or on platforms by the station's licensed porters. The rhythmic figures across the shallow water exemplify Hiroshige's interest in the practical theater of travel along the Tōkaidō road.
credit: The fifty-stree stages of Tokaido (one of 53 prints, +2 for start and terminus)

title: Fujikawa-juku
description: Fujikawa shows a daimyō procession entering the station under banners, met by villagers prostrated in formal greeting. The print documents the parade etiquette of sankin-kōtai, the alternate-attendance system that drove Tōkaidō traffic in the Edo period.
credit: The fifty-stree stages of Tokaido (one of 53 prints, +2 for start and terminus)

title: Fukuroi-juku
description: At Fukuroi, travelers rest at a tea stand pitched beneath a leafy tree, a kettle steaming on the open fire. The genre vignette is set against a low, level landscape, demonstrating Hiroshige's gift for everyday observation within the Tōkaidō format.
credit: The fifty-stree stages of Tokaido (one of 53 prints, +2 for start and terminus)

title: Futagawa-juku
description: Travellers approach a thatched teahouse on the windswept Sarugababa heath outside Futagawa station. Hiroshige builds the scene from broad fields of muted ochre and pine green typical of the Hōeidō Tōkaidō's middle stretch.
credit: Database of the Museum of Fine Arts, Boston

title: Goyu-juku
description: Serving women aggressively haul reluctant travellers into an inn at Goyu, a stock comic episode of the Tōkaidō. The print is one of several in the series in which Hiroshige slips genre observation into what is nominally a landscape.
credit: Database of the Museum of Fine Arts, Boston

title: Hakone-juku
description: Hakone, the most mountainous station of the Tōkaidō, rises in jagged, faceted peaks shaded in flat planes of color. A small daimyō procession threads through the pass beside Lake Ashi, a composition Hiroshige used to dramatize the human scale against terrain.
credit: The fifty-stree stages of Tokaido (one of 53 prints, +2 for start and terminus)

title: Hamamatsu-juku
description: Hamamatsu features a great solitary pine on a windswept field, with figures gathered around a small fire at its base. The motif distills Hiroshige's recurring concern with weather and season, the bare branches and grey sky reading as winter on the road.
credit: The fifty-stree stages of Tokaido (one of 53 prints, +2 for start and terminus)

title: Hara-juku
description: Travellers cross the open plain at Hara station while Mount Fuji rises in pink-tinged morning light, breaking the sheet's upper border. The Hōeidō Tokaido is among the most successful print series of the Edo period, sold in tens of thousands of impressions.
credit: Database of the Museum of Fine Arts, Boston

title: Hayez
description: Lot's daughters ply their father with wine in the cave above the ruined Sodom, in Hayez's reading of the Genesis episode. The Milanese Romantic stages the scene like an opera, posing the figures within a sharply lit grotto and modelling flesh with the Neoclassical polish inherited from his teacher Canova.

title: Ishiyakushi-juku
description: Ishiyakushi takes its name from the Buddhist temple visible among trees behind the village fields. Hiroshige's quiet rural composition, with farmers at work and a stand of pines, exemplifies the lyrical pastoral mode he interleaved with the busier station scenes.
credit: The fifty-stree stages of Tokaido (one of 53 prints, +2 for start and terminus)

title: Kameyama-juku
description: Kameyama Castle rises above a snow-blanketed slope as a daimyō procession climbs toward the gate. The print is among the most admired snowscapes of the Hoeidō Tōkaidō, its diagonal trail and white expanse drawn with Hiroshige's restrained graphic economy.
credit: The fifty-stree stages of Tokaido (one of 53 prints, +2 for start and terminus)

title: Kanaya-juku
description: Kanaya sits on the western bank of the Ōi River, reached only by porters who carried travelers across its broad flats. Hiroshige looks down on the receding scene from a high vantage, ordering the mountains and water into clean horizontal registers.
credit: The fifty-stree stages of Tokaido (one of 53 prints, +2 for start and terminus)

title: Kanbara-juku
description: Three muffled figures trudge through deep night snow at Kanbara, the white sky bokashi-graded into a heavy grey. Long counted among Hiroshige's finest landscape prints, the design treats silence and stillness as subject in their own right.
credit: Database of the Museum of Fine Arts, Boston

title: Kusatsu-juku
description: The penultimate station of the Tōkaidō, Kusatsu was famed for its meibutsu tateba teahouse where porters rested with fast palanquins. Hiroshige captures the bustle of arriving travelers under banners, his linework crisp and his palette anchored by the deep indigo blues of the early Hoeidō printing.
credit: Database of the Museum of Fine Arts, Boston

title: Nichiren bezweert de golven bij Kakuda tijdens zijn verbanning naar Sado
description: The Buddhist reformer Nichiren stands in a pitching boat off Kakuda, calming the waves with the Lotus Sutra during his exile to Sado. Kuniyoshi's woodblock print sets red robes against churning blue surf in the dramatic warrior-print idiom of late Edo Utagawa school design.

title: Nihonbashi
description: A daimyō procession sets out across Edo's Nihonbashi bridge at dawn, opening Hiroshige's Tokaido Road series for the publisher Hōeidō. The print establishes the journey's ceremonial scale before the road's weather and incident take over.

title: Okabe-juku
description: The Okabe station turns inland through the gorge of Utsu Pass, where a stony stream cuts between steep wooded slopes. Hiroshige's compressed verticals and overlapping rocks give the print a closed, intimate atmosphere unusual for the series.
credit: The fifty-stree stages of Tokaido (one of 53 prints, +2 for start and terminus)

title: Okazaki-juku
description: The Yahagi Bridge at Okazaki, said to be the longest on the Tōkaidō, stretches across the river in a single shallow arc. Hiroshige aligns the procession crossing it with the distant castle, framing infrastructure and authority in one elegant span.
credit: The fifty-stree stages of Tokaido (one of 53 prints, +2 for start and terminus)

title: Rōben Waterfall at Ōyama in Sagami Province
description: Pilgrims bathe in the falls at Ōyama, where Rōben is said to have purified himself before founding the mountain's temple. From A Tour of the Waterfalls of the Provinces, Hokusai abstracts cascading water into ribbons of stylised line, treating each fall as a graphic emblem of place.

title: Sakanoshita-juku
description: Sakanoshita lies at the foot of the steep Suzuka pass, here dominated by the cliff known as Fudesute-yama, the 'brush-throwing mountain'. Travelers pause at a teahouse to contemplate the rock face, a scene that joins landscape and legend in Hiroshige's understated manner.
credit: The fifty-stree stages of Tokaido (one of 53 prints, +2 for start and terminus)

title: Shimada-juku
description: Shimada Station from Hiroshige's celebrated series The Fifty-three Stations of the Tōkaidō, showing travellers being carried across the unbridged Ōi River. The print is reproduced from a later miniature edition by the Takamizawa workshop in Tokyo.
credit: The fifty-stree stages of Tokaido (one of 53 prints, +2 for start and terminus)

title: Tsuchiyama-juku
description: Tsuchiyama is shown in heavy spring rain, a daimyō procession crossing a swollen stream beneath dripping trees. The slanting rain lines and saturated greens make this one of the most atmospheric prints in the Tōkaidō series.
credit: The fifty-stree stages of Tokaido (one of 53 prints, +2 for start and terminus)

title: Yokkaichi-juku
description: Station Yokkaichi shows a lone traveler bracing against the wind beside the Mie River, a hat tumbling away across the reeds. The sweeping diagonals and muted blues are characteristic of Hiroshige's Hoeidō Tōkaidō, a series that fixed the iconography of journey and weather in Edo print culture.
credit: Database of the Museum of Fine Arts, Boston

title: Yoshida-juku
description: Workmen scaffold the Toyokawa River bridge at Yoshida station while Yoshida castle rises beyond. Hiroshige uses the diagonal of the bridgework as a vehicle for his characteristic recession into atmospheric distance.
credit: Database of the Museum of Fine Arts, Boston

title: 960px-Hanagami Danjo no jo Arakage fighting a giant salamander
description: The warrior Hanagami Danjo grapples with a colossal salamander in the Tonta River of Izumo, a scene of legendary heroism. Kuniyoshi delights in muscular anatomy and reptilian texture, characteristic of the musha-e warrior prints that made his reputation.

title: Caroline von Holnstein from Fronberg
description: Countess Caroline von Holnstein, a Munich society figure, painted for Ludwig I's Schönheitengalerie. Stieler frames her in dark drapery and pearls, the smooth modeling and steady gaze typical of the court's gallery of beauties.

title: Hiroshige Fishing boats on a lake
description: Descending Geese at Katata, from the Eight Views of Ōmi, depicting boats and a flock settling onto Lake Biwa at dusk. Hiroshige distils the classical Chinese-derived theme into broad bands of graded indigo bokashi, a printing technique that became his signature.

title: Hiroshige Full moon over a mountain landscape
description: The Autumn Moon at Ishiyama Temple, from Eight Views of Ōmi, shows the bright disc rising over Lake Biwa behind the temple's wooded bluff. Hiroshige draws on the classical meisho tradition, pairing a literary scenic theme with his characteristic graded blue skies.

title: Hiroshige Heavy rain on a pine tree 2
description: Night Rain at Karasaki, from Eight Views of Ōmi, isolates the great pine of Karasaki shrine as wind and rain lash its dark canopy. The near-monochrome palette and dense parallel lines give the print the hush of a literary classical theme treated in late ukiyo-e.

title: Hiroshige View of a long bridge across a lake
description: Evening Glow at Seta, from Eight Views of Ōmi, depicts the Karahashi bridge stretching across the southern reaches of Lake Biwa under a flushed sky. Hiroshige uses bokashi gradations to model dusk, a printing technique that gives the series its meditative tonal character.

title: Life stages
description: Five figures on a Baltic shore correspond to five sailing vessels at varying distances — an allegory of the ages of human life rendered in Friedrich's late, hushed palette. The painting was made near the artist's birthplace of Greifswald and is now in Leipzig's Museum der bildenden Künste.

title: Peonies and Canary
description: A canary alights on a bowed peony stem against a deep blue sky, accompanied by a haiku. From Hokusai's Large Flowers series for the publisher Eijudō, the print compresses the kachō-e bird-and-flower tradition into a tight diagonal, exploiting the new Prussian blue pigment for luminous backgrounds.
credit: wgHj0SmNQW7_UQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Burning of the Houses of Lords and Commons
description: Westminster's old Palace blazes against the night sky on 16 October 1834, watched by a crowd massed on the Thames. Turner sketched the fire from a hired boat and produced two oil versions, of which this is the Philadelphia canvas. The conflagration let him paint exactly the kind of incandescent yellow-and-red sky he had been pursuing for decades.
credit: UwEixmBDiZjk7A at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Burning of the Houses of Parliament
description: An eyewitness response to the conflagration that destroyed the medieval Palace of Westminster, watched by crowds along the Thames. Turner sketched the blaze from a hired boat and translated it into pyrotechnic yellows and reds against the cool Westminster Bridge.

title: The Women of Algiers
description: Three women lounge with a servant in a tiled Algerian harem chamber, surrounded by hookah, slippers and richly patterned textiles. Painted after Delacroix gained rare access to a domestic interior in 1832, the Louvre canvas became a foundational source of the nineteenth-century Orientalist imagination.

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: Venice - The Dogana and San Giorgio Maggiore
credit: National Gallery of Art Washington DC

title: 1849), Kirifuri waterval bij de Kurokami berg
description: The Kirifuri ("falling mist") cascade on Mount Kurokami breaks into four white tendrils against dark cliffs, with tiny travellers gazing up from below. Hokusai's Waterfalls series treats Japan's sacred falls as near-abstract patterns, the water carved as flat decorative line rather than naturalistic spray.

title: Head of a Person Asleep
description: A small, intimate study of a figure asleep, head sunk against bedding, kept in Turner's studio and bequeathed to the nation. The looseness of touch is closer to the colour-beginnings of his late watercolours than to anything he exhibited. It belongs to a private corpus of figurative sketches now in the Tate's Turner Bequest.

title: Keelmen Heaving in Coals by Moonlight
description: Tyneside coal-heavers loading colliers under a full moon at Shields, painted as a pendant to a Venetian scene to contrast industrial north and southern lagoon. The torchlit smoke and silvered water typify Turner's late nocturnes.
credit: 5AHnYBRd8dD08g at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Line Fishing
description: Fishing boats work the swell off Hastings under a turbulent sky, the figures and rigging set against pale sea-light. Turner treats the everyday coastal labor with the atmospheric breadth of his mature marine paintings.
credit: -wG8RNgzDMZgIA at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Stonehenge
description: A late watercolour showing the prehistoric stones beneath a roiling sky split by a double rainbow. Constable, working in his final years, treats the monument as a meditation on time and weather, the broken trilithons set against the moving drama of the English heavens.
credit: QwG39cxEne2qGQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Stormy Sea with Blazing Wreck
description: A late Turner storm piece in which a burning ship is almost swallowed by towering surf and smoke. Paint is scrubbed and scraped into vortices of orange and lead grey, the catastrophe rendered as pure elemental force, a hallmark of his final Tate Bequest oils.

title: The Bright Stone of Honour
description: The Rhine fortress of Ehrenbreitstein rises in golden haze above the confluence with the Moselle, paired with the riverside tomb of the French general Marceau. Turner illustrates a passage from Byron's Childe Harold, fusing topography with literary elegy.

title: The Grand Canal - Venice
description: A view down the Grand Canal toward the domes of Santa Maria della Salute, gondolas dispersed across luminous water. Turner dissolves the architecture of Venice into pale light and reflected color, characteristic of his late Romantic vedute.

title: The Natchez
description: A Natchez couple cradles their newborn on the banks of the Mississippi, fugitives from a colonial massacre, after Chateaubriand's 1801 novel 'Atala'. Delacroix turns the literary source into a sombre meditation on indigenous extinction, exhibited at the Salon of 1835 and now in the Metropolitan Museum.
credit: 1. Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"/d/delacroi/2/212delac" 2. Metropolitan Museum of Art, online collection (The Met object ID 110000620)

title: Palikare Return Home
description: A Berlin Biedermeier scene in Philhellenist key: a palikar, one of the irregular fighters of the Greek War of Independence, returns to his family. Magnus, a portraitist trained under Karl Begas, treats the costume and embroidery with ethnographic precision while keeping the emotional register restrained.

title: Rome
description: A panoramic view of the Eternal City bathed in golden Mediterranean haze, with the Tiber winding past domes and ruins toward distant hills. Turner exhibited this canvas after his Italian travels, dissolving topographical detail into atmospheric light worthy of Claude Lorrain.
credit: dailymail.co.uk the second upload thehistoryblog.com the third upload thehistoryblog.com

title: Valley of Aosta: Snowstorm
description: Storm, avalanche and lightning converge over the Italian Val d'Aosta, tiny figures swept along the ravine floor. Drawing on Alpine sketches made with Hugh Munro of Novar, Turner orchestrates Romantic catastrophe through swirling diagonals of paint.

title: Ancient Rome; Agrippina Landing with the Ashes of Germanicus
Eugene delacroix
description: A self-portrait from the artist's late thirties, the painter facing the viewer with steady, somewhat melancholy eyes and a black coat that dissolves into shadow. Delacroix here adopts the sober, unidealised register of seventeenth-century Dutch self-portraiture rather than Romantic flourish.

title: Fishing Boats with Hucksters Bargaining for Fish
Karl Friedrich Schinkel Allegorie auf Beuth
description: An allegorical tribute to the Prussian industrial reformer Peter Beuth, shown astride Pegasus as he soars over a panorama of human civilisation and progress. The composition fuses Romantic landscape with Enlightenment allegory, celebrating Beuth's role in the modernisation of the Prussian state.

title: The Fanatics of Tangier
description: Delacroix records the ecstatic Aissaoua brotherhood spilling through the streets of Tangier, a procession he had witnessed during his 1832 Moroccan trip. The swirling crowd and sun-bleached architecture exemplify his lifelong fascination with North African religious spectacle.
credit: 1. Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"/d/delacroi/3/314delac" 2. [1]

title: The Parting of Hero and Leander
description: Het afscheid van Hero en leander door J. M. W.

title: The Story of Apollo and Daphne
description: A late mythological landscape by Turner, the Ovidian chase staged across a golden Italianate valley bathed in the swirling light he had brought back from his Roman travels. Figures dwindle beside vast trees and rivers, drama dissolved into atmosphere in the manner of his ageing Claude homages.

title: Hiroshige
description: Two mandarin ducks float together among reeds, the drake's plumage rendered in patterned blocks of vermilion, ochre, and indigo. Paired oshidori were a long-standing kachō-e symbol of marital devotion; Hiroshige treats the subject with the calligraphic economy of his late bird-and-flower work.

title: The Last Meeting between Jacopo Foscari and his Family Before Being Sent into Exile
description: The painting was commissioned in 1838 by Emperor Ferdinand I , who had arrived in Milan for his coronation as king of Lombardy and Veneto , and it was destined for the Belvedere Gallery in Vienna. On its completion in 1840 the work was valued at the extremely high price of five thousand florins by the Commissione Permanente di Pittura dell’Accademia di Brera , which thus recognized its exceptional quality.

title: Wilhelmine Sulzer
description: A young woman gazes outward in a richly trimmed dress and lace collar. Painted for Ludwig I's Schonheitengalerie in Munich, the portrait shows Stieler's polished court manner: porcelain skin, unblemished surface, and discreet idealisation.

title: General Sir David Baird Discovering the Body of Sultan Tippoo Sahib
description: A vast late history painting commemorating the British general's discovery of Tipu Sultan's body after the storming of Seringapatam in 1799. Wilkie staged the torch-lit scene with theatrical chiaroscuro, working in the grand manner he adopted following his travels in Spain.

title: Jewish Wedding in Morocco
description: Guests crowd a sunlit Tangier courtyard around dancers and musicians at a Sephardi wedding feast. Drawing on sketches from his 1832 North African journey with the Comte de Mornay, Delacroix translated direct observation into a Romantic ethnography rich in textile color and architectural detail.

title: Modern Rome - Campo Vaccino
credit: 9wG1GfGh9530qg at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Odalisque with Slave
description: An odalisque reclines on a divan while a slave plays the tanbur and a black eunuch keeps watch in a tiled harem chamber. Now at the Harvard Art Museums, this version repeats with new richness Ingres's lifelong fascination with the boneless, sinuous female nude in a faux-Ottoman setting.

title: The Fighting Temeraire tugged to her last berth to be broken up
description: The 98-gun veteran of Trafalgar is towed by a black steam tug along the Thames toward the breaker's yard, set against a smouldering sunset. Turner contrasts the pale, ghostly hulk with the squat industrial tug, mourning the passing of the age of sail.

title: The poor poet
description: A penniless poet huddles under an umbrella in his garret bed, scribbling verses while the roof leaks. Spitzweg's most famous Biedermeier image, now in Munich's Neue Pinakothek, distils the era's fondness for affectionate caricature of the eccentric outsider.
credit: 1. The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 . (the version fom Berlin, Nationalgalerie) 2. Wichmann, Siegfried: Carl Spitzweg , München 1990, S. 57 ISBN 3-7654-2306-8 3. Cybershot800i , Own work , Taken in 17 June 2011

title: Bouquet de glaïeuls
description: A bouquet of gladioli, from the brief stretch of still life Monet produced after settling at Vetheuil. The blossoms are massed in vertical accents of red and pink, paint laid on thickly to register petal against petal.

title: Camille sitting on the beach at Trouville
description: Camille Doncieux on the beach at Trouville during the family's stay in the summer of 1870, painted in the open air with sand reportedly still embedded in the paint. The handling is rapid and sketch-like, capturing wind, light and the moment of leisure.

title: Evening at Argenteuil
description: Evening at Argenteuil, in which the Seine carries the warm pinks and lilacs of a fading sky. Sailboats are noted with a few economical strokes, the picture organised entirely by horizontal bands of reflected light.

title: Frost at Giverny
description: A field at Giverny rimed with hoarfrost, painted soon after Monet settled at the Norman village that would consume the rest of his career. Broken touches of pale violet, rose, and silvery blue register the chill morning light without recourse to white.

title: Monet w109
description: An early work from the period before Monet settled into the Impressionist idiom, when he was still absorbing lessons from Boudin and the Barbizon school. The handling is firmer and the tonal range narrower than in his mature plein-air practice.

title: Monet w148
description: An early work from Monet's apprentice years, when his practice was still indebted to plein-air predecessors like Eugène Boudin. The brushwork is tighter and the subject more conventionally framed than in the mature Impressionist canvases.

title: Monet w1725
description: A late canvas from Monet's Giverny years, one of the water-garden compositions in which the pond surface dissolves into a near-abstract field of reflected sky and floating blooms. Loose, layered brushwork builds colour without a fixed horizon, anticipating the immersive Grandes Decorations.

title: Monet w1781
description: A Giverny pond study built up in long, weighted strokes of violet, green and pink. Monet works without a horizon line, letting reflected light and lily pads alone organise the picture plane.

title: Monet w1786
description: Part of the sustained water-lily project that occupied Monet's final two decades. The handling is broader and more gestural than his earlier Impressionist work, the pigment scrubbed and dragged to evoke shifting water and weather.

title: Monet w182
description: An early-1870s landscape from Monet's Argenteuil period, when he settled on the Seine and refined the broken-stroke technique that would define Impressionism. Light is registered in short touches of unmixed colour laid wet-in-wet.

title: Monet w1884
description: A late Giverny study in which the lily pond becomes a near-flat tapestry of floating discs and submerged shadow. Monet builds the surface in restless, overlapping touches that carry no firm contour.

title: Monet w197
description: An Argenteuil-era landscape from the years Monet shared the riverbank with Manet and Renoir. The brushwork is quick and divisible, the palette tuned to outdoor daylight rather than studio convention.

title: Monet w222
description: An Argenteuil scene from the year of the first Impressionist exhibition's gestation. Monet works en plein air, registering reflections on water and the play of leaves with rapid, divided touches.

title: Monet w265
description: A landscape from the years Monet rented the house at Argenteuil that became a hub for his circle. Light is treated as the true subject, with grass, water and sky fused by a single weather and hour.

title: Monet w327
description: A mid-1870s landscape from the Argenteuil years, painted as Monet pushed his palette toward purer, lighter chromatic notes. The brushwork is short and patchy, more interested in vibration than in contour.

title: Monet w357
description: An outdoor canvas from 1875, when Monet was at the technical heart of his Argenteuil period. Forms are dissolved into juxtaposed strokes of warm and cool, with shadow rendered in colour rather than black.

title: Monet w368
description: A landscape made the year Monet's family was forced from their Argenteuil house by mounting debts. The picture nevertheless shows the confident high-key palette he had perfected on the Seine.

title: Monet w386
description: A garden or riverbank motif from 1875, executed in the loose, sun-flecked manner Monet had refined at Argenteuil. Pigment is laid in short directional strokes that read as foliage at viewing distance.

title: Monet w397
description: A canvas from the year of the second Impressionist exhibition, where Monet showed eighteen works. The brushwork is confidently shorthand, atmosphere carried by colour temperature rather than line.

title: Monet w419
description: The Pond at Montgeron, painted at the country estate of the collector Ernest Hoschede, who commissioned a group of decorative panels from Monet. Reflections on still water already prefigure the Giverny obsession to come.

title: Monet w502
description: A landscape from the Vetheuil years, when Monet, recently widowed, painted the small Seine village and its surrounding fields in all weathers. The handling is rougher and more searching than his Argenteuil work.

title: Monet w58
description: An early landscape from Monet's pre-Impressionist years, before the broken-colour technique fully emerged. The handling still carries traces of Boudin and the Barbizon plein-air tradition that shaped his apprenticeship along the Normandy coast.

title: Monet w601
description: A landscape from Monet's Vetheuil period, when he worked obsessively along the Seine's bends. The brushwork is rapid and atmospheric, recording weather rather than topography in the manner that defines his mature outdoor practice.

title: Monet w649
description: A coastal or riverside study from 1881, the year Monet divided his time between Vetheuil and the Normandy cliffs. Quick wet-on-wet strokes capture transient light, with no preparatory drawing visible beneath the painted surface.

title: Monet w653
description: A landscape from Monet's intensely productive 1881 season, when he worked between Vetheuil and the coast. The picture relies on tonal harmonies rather than line, the surface built up from short, dragged strokes of broken colour.

title: Monet w658
description: A landscape from 1881, the year Monet was preparing to leave Vetheuil for Poissy. The brushwork remains feathery and fragmented, recording the play of light over foliage and water in the manner he had refined through the previous decade.

title: Monet w662
description: An outdoor study from Monet's 1881 campaign, painted with the rapid, broken touch he had perfected at Argenteuil and Vetheuil. Saturated greens and lavender shadows show his consistent attention to the temperature of light.

title: Monet w663
description: A landscape painted during Monet's last summer at Vetheuil, before the move to Poissy. Loose vertical brushwork describes trees and reflections together, characteristic of his interest in dissolving form into atmosphere.

title: Monet w664
description: A Vetheuil-era landscape worked entirely en plein air. Monet builds the scene from short, parallel strokes of broken colour, layering complementary hues to register the vibration of sunlight rather than to outline objects.

title: Monet w668
description: A landscape from the early 1880s, when Monet was still working the Seine valley around Vetheuil. The handling favours atmospheric unity over topographic detail, with soft transitions between sky, foliage and water.

title: Monet w679
description: An outdoor landscape from Monet's Vetheuil years. The picture is constructed from short, parallel strokes that knit foliage, sky and water into a single shimmering field, a technique central to Impressionist landscape practice.

title: Monet w683
description: A landscape from 1881, when Monet was producing canvases at high speed for the dealer Durand-Ruel. The handling is quick and confident, with thin scumbles allowing the light ground to glow through the upper layers of paint.

title: Monet w684
description: A landscape from Monet's final Vetheuil season, painted with the rapid, fragmented touch he had honed throughout the 1870s. The surface registers atmosphere as a single tonal envelope rather than a sequence of described objects.

title: Monet w691
description: A landscape from 1881, the year Monet worked between the Seine valley and the Normandy coast. Broken brushwork and a high-keyed palette dissolve the motif into pure light, in the spirit of the earlier Argenteuil pictures.

title: Monet w694
description: An outdoor study from 1881, made during one of Monet's most prolific years. The handling shows the mature Impressionist economy: no preliminary drawing, only short, directional strokes of complementary colour worked wet into wet.

title: Monet w706
description: A landscape from 1882, when Monet had relocated to Poissy and was making frequent trips to the Normandy coast. The brushwork remains rapid and fragmentary, the palette tuned to the silver-grey light of the lower Seine.

title: Monet w717
description: A coastal scene from Monet's 1882 Normandy campaign at Pourville and Varengeville. Sea, sky and cliff are unified by a silvery palette and short, directional strokes, evidence of his close attention to the changing light of the Channel.

title: Monet w724
description: A landscape from the 1882 Normandy season, when Monet was shuttling between Pourville and his rented house at Poissy. Cliff and sea are simplified to broad zones of broken colour, the handling more atmospheric than descriptive.

title: Monet w736
description: A coastal subject from 1882, the year of Monet's intensive Pourville and Varengeville campaigns. Broken brushwork and a cool, salt-bleached palette register the particular light of the Normandy cliffs above the Channel.

title: Monet w752
description: A landscape from 1882, painted with the swift, confident touch Monet had refined over a decade of plein-air work. The surface is built up from short directional strokes that suggest weather and time of day before they describe place.

title: Monet w753
description: A coastal study from Monet's prolific Normandy campaign, executed with the broken-touch brushwork that defines his early-1880s plein-air practice. Cliffs, sea, and weather are reduced to chromatic vibrations, the palette tilted toward cool greens and pearly greys.

title: Monet w757
description: Painted during Monet's repeated stays on the Channel coast, the canvas tracks the play of light across rock and water with rapid, scumbled strokes. Form gives way to atmosphere, an approach that anticipates the artist's later serial investigations.

title: Monet w759
description: One of dozens of seascapes Monet produced from rented quarters along the Normandy shore, where weather shifts dictated his working day. The composition relies on a high horizon and a textured, comma-stroke sea typical of his Pourville and Étretat campaigns.

title: Monet w764
description: A Channel-coast motif handled with the loose, weather-driven facture Monet refined in the early 1880s. Sky and sea are knit together by repeated short strokes, the pigment kept high-keyed to register passing light.

title: Monet w767
description: From Monet's Normandy production, the scene captures the headlands and shifting maritime light he chased obsessively that decade. The handling is brisk and granular, with chalky lilacs and greens laid wet-into-wet.

title: Monet w772
description: Another Channel-coast view from Monet's 1882 campaign, with cliffs cropped tight and the sea pushed nearly flat against the picture plane. The brushwork remains unblended, each stroke registering a separate observation of colour and weather.

title: Monet w776
description: A coastal subject from Monet's intensive Normandy season, painted in the open air against quickly changing conditions. Greens, mauves, and cream whites are dragged across the surface in the loose impasto he had by then made his signature.

title: Monet w778a
description: Painted on the Normandy shore, the work belongs to a string of cliff and sea studies in which Monet tested how far light could dissolve solid form. The stroke is rapid, the palette cool and slightly chalky.

title: Monet w787
description: From Monet's Pourville-Varengeville orbit, where he repeatedly framed the Channel through cliffs and scrubby vegetation. The surface is built up in short, directional strokes that encode wind, haze, and tide.

title: Monet w807
description: A Normandy seascape carrying the high-keyed, broken-touch language Monet had perfected by the early 1880s. Distance is implied not by linear perspective but by graded warmth and saturation across the brushwork.

title: Monet w81
description: An early canvas predating Impressionism's public arrival, in which Monet is still negotiating his debt to Boudin and Jongkind. Already evident is his appetite for outdoor light and a loosened, atmospheric handling of sky and water.

title: Monet w835
description: From Monet's continued exploration of the Channel coast in 1883, the work pushes light and atmosphere over topography. Strokes remain short and unblended, the palette pitched to the silvery weather of Normandy.

title: Monet w844
description: Painted during one of Monet's working trips along the Normandy shore, where he developed his habit of carrying multiple canvases for changing conditions. The handling is rapid and additive, building form from layered colour rather than line.

title: Monet w867
description: A landscape from Monet's mid-1880s output, when he moved freely between the Normandy coast and the Mediterranean. The surface is densely worked in short strokes that hold light without resolving into precise contour.

title: Monet w868
description: Painted in a year split between Étretat and the Riviera, the canvas carries the loose, weather-attuned facture Monet trusted in any setting. Form is sketched only as far as light requires, the rest given to colour and texture.

title: Monet w885
description: From Monet's Bordighera and Cap Martin campaigns, the canvas pursues the strong colour and clarity of the Mediterranean coast. Pigment is laid in vivid, separated strokes, a deliberate departure from the cooler Norman palette.

title: Monet w891a
description: A Riviera subject from Monet's 1884 sojourn in Bordighera and Menton, painted in heightened colour to register subtropical light. The brushwork is brisk and the palette unusually saturated for the artist, full of pinks, oranges, and intense blues.

title: Monet w891b
description: Companion to its near twin, this Riviera canvas chases the same southern light with the comma strokes Monet relied on in the field. Foliage and rock are dissolved into vibrating colour with little tonal modelling.

title: Monet w892
description: A southern landscape from Monet's Mediterranean campaign, where he tested how vivid he could push his palette without losing atmospheric truth. Strokes are short and directional, building form through colour contrast rather than line.

title: Shipwreck of Don Juan
description: Survivors of a foundered ship draw lots for cannibalism in an open boat, an episode adapted from Byron's poem. Delacroix turns the literary Romantic subject into a study of taut, sea-tossed bodies and overcast Channel light in the wake of Géricault's Raft of the Medusa.

provenance.collection: Department of Paintings of the Louvre

title: Sun Setting over a Lake
description: Form is reduced to bands of gold, rose and slate water in a late, near-abstract evocation of dusk. Pictures of this kind, left in his studio at his death, anticipate by half a century the dissolutions of Impressionism.

title: The Butterfly Hunter
description: A frock-coated naturalist tiptoes through tall grass with raised butterfly net, lost in Linnaean reverie. The collector-eccentric, beloved subject of Spitzweg's Biedermeier comedies, is here painted on a vertical panel that exaggerates the man's spindly absurdity.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: The New Moon; or
description: A coastal genre piece whose long, quarrelsome title points to a children's seaside dispute beneath the new moon. Turner places small contesting figures against a vast, luminous estuary, the tiny human anecdote dwarfed by his characteristic sweep of sea and silvery sky.

title: The Slave Ship
description: A slaver flings the dead and dying from its hold as a typhoon rises over a blood-orange sea, painted in response to the Zong massacre and abolitionist debate. Turner dissolves moral horror into dazzling atmosphere, the figures and chains barely surfacing through churning paint.

title: The Water-Lily Pond
description: A canvas from the Japanese-bridge and water-garden series of Monet's last years, painted as his cataracts deepened and his palette warmed toward reds and oranges. Form gives way almost entirely to colour and reflected light.

title: Water Lilies with Reflections of Tall Grass
description: From the late Nymphéas cycle, the canvas dissolves the lily pond into floating pads, drowned grasses, and reflected sky. Working at monumental scale in his Giverny studio, Monet abandoned the horizon altogether to let surface and depth fuse into a single chromatic field.

title: Waves Breaking against the Wind
description: A pure marine, the canvas is given entirely to a churning sea raked by spray. Turner reportedly had himself lashed to a mast in storms; the picture's loose, scumbled paint claims that immediacy.

title: Hatsuhana doing penance under the Tonosawa waterfall
description: From the series Lives of Wise Women and Virtuous Wives, Hatsuhana of the joruri play Hakone Reigen prays under the icy Tonosawa falls to obtain her crippled husband's revenge. Kuniyoshi treats the ascetic subject with characteristic dynamism, the cascading water rendered in steep blue parallel lines.

title: His Highness Muhemed Ali
description: The Albanian-born ruler of Egypt sits in a European armchair in turban and richly embroidered Ottoman dress, painted on Wilkie's late tour to the Near East. The Scottish painter died at sea on the return voyage from this commission, and J.M.W. Turner commemorated his burial in 'Peace - Burial at Sea'.

title: Karolina Lizius
description: The shoemaker's daughter Karolina Lizius gazes outward in modest dress, painted as one of the bourgeois sitters Ludwig I added to his Schönheitengalerie. Stieler, court painter in Munich, specialised in this restrained Biedermeier portrait formula: smooth finish, neutral ground, demure psychology.

title: Katerina Rosa Botsaris
description: The young daughter of a Greek War of Independence hero is shown in embroidered Greek costume, her dark hair loose beneath a tasselled cap. Stieler painted the portrait for Ludwig I of Bavaria's Schönheitengalerie, the Munich gallery of beauties in which the king collected idealised female likenesses.

title: Lake Lucerne: the Bay of Uri from above Brunnen
description: One of the watercolour studies from Turner's late Swiss tours, when he returned again and again to Lake Lucerne. From the heights above Brunnen the lake opens in pale washes of blue and pearl, mountains dissolving into the atmospheric haze that defined his final manner.

title: Portrait of Alessandro Manzoni
description: The novelist Alessandro Manzoni, author of I Promessi Sposi, sits in sober dark dress against a neutral ground, fixing the viewer with a measured gaze. Hayez treats the literary patriot of the Risorgimento with the restrained likeness and crisp drawing of the Italian Romantic portrait tradition.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Sir David Wilkie's Residence in Kensington
description: Sir David Wilkie's residence in Kensington London, by William Collins 1841 (painted just after Wilkie's death)

title: The Sunday Stroll
description: A bourgeois family in their Sunday best parades down a country path under a parasol, the children straggling behind. Spitzweg captures the small comedies of Biedermeier ritual with affectionate, miniaturist precision.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Campo Santo
description: A tall-masted barge drifts past the island cemetery of San Michele in the Venetian lagoon. Painted alongside its Royal Academy companion the Dogano, the work converts a place of burial into a study of pearled morning light.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"t/turner/2/2041turn"

title: Kuniyoshi Utagawa
description: A tiger paces against a wind-blown ground, its striped coat picked out in dense keyblock lines. Kuniyoshi, never having seen a live tiger, drew on imported Chinese painting models and his own taste for muscular animal subjects in the warrior-print tradition.

title: Odalisque with Slave
description: Commissioned by King Wilhelm I of Württemberg, the picture reclines an odalisque before a garden vista while a servant plays the lute. Ingres designed and largely painted it, with the landscape backdrop entrusted to his pupil Paul Flandrin.
credit: Walters Art Museum : Home page Info about artwork

title: Peace - Burial at Sea
description: Commemorates the sea burial of the painter David Wilkie off Gibraltar, the steamer's silhouette and inky black sails set against a flaring sun. Exhibited as a pendant to Turner's fiery 'War,' the picture pairs cool nocturnal calm with the warmth of mourning.

title: Samson and the lion
description: Samson straddles the lion and pries open its jaws, his musculature lit with the academic clarity Hayez absorbed from study after the antique. The Old Testament hero is staged as a single isolated figure, drawing on the sculptural Romanticism that linked the artist to Canova's circle.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Snow Storm: Steam-Boat off a Harbour's Mouth
description: A paddle-steamer engulfed by a vortex of snow, smoke and sea, exhibited with Turner's claim that he had been lashed to the mast to observe it. Critics dismissed the canvas as 'soapsuds and whitewash', but it remains his definitive statement on the elemental sublime.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"t/turner/2/2040turn"

title: Collision of Moorish Horsemen
description: Delacroix drew on his 1832 North African journey with Count de Mornay's diplomatic mission to evoke the violent equestrian games of the Moroccan court. The clashing horses and flying robes display the saturated, almost vibrating palette he developed after seeing Maghreb light at first hand.
credit: Walters Art Museum : Home page Info about artwork

title: In the Water by Eugen von Blaas
description: Venetian girls hike up their skirts to wade in the lagoon shallows, a favourite genre subject of Eugen von Blaas. The Austrian-Italian academician built his career on these sun-warmed scenes of Adriatic peasant life, rendered with porcelain finish and bright local colour.

title: Light and Colour
description: A vortex of golden light spirals around the figure of Moses inscribing Genesis, with a serpent and submerged figures at its rim. Turner conceived the picture as a pendant illustrating Goethe's theory of positive, light-giving colour.

title: The Desperate Man
description: A wide-eyed self-portrait, the young Courbet clutching his hair and staring directly out at the viewer. Painted in his early twenties, it announces the confrontational self-fashioning that would define his career and the Realist project.

title: The Evening of the Deluge
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the National Gallery of Art . Please see the Gallery's Open Access Policy .

title: The Sun of Venice Going to Sea
credit: Google Arts & Culture — dwEYd5yKW43wWQ Tate Images ( http://www.tate-images.com/results.asp?image=N00535&wwwflag=3&imagepos=5 )

title: Fishing Boats Bringing a Disabled Ship into Port Ruysdael
description: Boats tow a damaged sailing ship into the imaginary Dutch harbour of Port Ruysdael, Turner's homage to Jacob van Ruisdael. Painted late in his career, the canvas dissolves rigging and breaking sea into broad sweeps of grey, white and salt. It is one of several pictures in which Turner used invented Dutch place-names as a critical tribute.
