title: Last Words of the Emperor Marcus Aurelius
description: The dying emperor Marcus Aurelius commends his unworthy son Commodus to a grave circle of Stoic counsellors. Delacroix exhibited the picture at the 1845 Salon, where Baudelaire singled out its sombre violet and ochre palette as the chromatic distillation of Romantic history painting.
credit: Œuvre appartenant au musée des Beaux-Arts de Lyon Consulter cette image en très haute définition (+1 milliard de pixels)

title: Meeting of Job and Esau
description: The painting illustrates Jacob's return to his homeland, asking forgiveness from his brother Esau for having deceived his birthright. Biblical subjects had a lot of luck around the mid-nineteenth century, as the suffering of the Jewish people recalled the Italian political situation.

title: Rain
description: A Great Western Railway locomotive races across Maidenhead Bridge through curtains of rain, the iron viaduct dissolving into vapour and golden light. The picture is a defining statement of the modern industrial sublime, painted with palette knife and finger as much as brush.

title: Takiyasha the Witch and the Skeleton Spectre
artist: Takiyasha_the_Witch_and_the_Skeleton_Spectre_1.jpg : Utagawa Kuniyoshi Takiyasha_the_Witch_and_the_Skeleton_Spectre_2.jpg : Utagawa Kuniyoshi Takiyasha_the_Witch_and_the_Skeleton_Spectre_3.jpg : Utagawa Kuniyoshi derivative work: Crisco 1492
description: A nishiki-e triptych staging the sorceress Takiyasha as she summons a colossal skeleton against the warrior Mitsukuni in the ruined Soma palace. Kuniyoshi compresses three full sheets into one continuous, theatrically lit scene drawn from the gothic literature of late Edo.

title: Van Tromp
description: The Dutch admiral Maarten Tromp puts his ship about in heavy weather, a subject Turner returned to repeatedly in his last decade. The picture extends his admiration for the seventeenth-century Dutch marine tradition into a high-key, Romantic key.
credit: OwF7vTnPbV9UwQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Venice Quay
description: The Doge's Palace and the Riva degli Schiavoni shimmer through lagoon light in a late Venetian view. By his third trip to Venice in 1840 Turner was treating the city's stones as little more than coloured vapour.

title: Feminine Wave
description: A graceful, curling wave breaks in undulating tendrils against a void, conceived as the gentler counterpart to Hokusai's better-known 'Masculine Wave'. The print belongs to the artist's late mature exploration of water as pure pattern, where ukiyo-e line approaches abstraction.

title: Norham Castle
description: The Northumbrian border keep dissolving into rose and gold mist above the Tweed, a motif Turner painted repeatedly across his career. The late oil reduces architecture and cattle to translucent veils of pigment, anticipating modern abstraction.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"t/turner/2/206turne" http://www.tate.org.uk/art/artworks/turner-norham-castle-sunrise-n01981

title: Portrait of Alexandra Amalie von Bayern
dateCreated: 1845, replica of a portrait painted in 1838
description: A Schönheitengalerie portrait of Princess Alexandra of Bavaria, daughter of Ludwig I, rendered in Stieler's characteristic porcelain finish. The sitter is shown with the soft idealization the Munich court demanded of its gallery of beauties.
credit: originally uploaded to de.wikipedia by Thyra ( talk · contribs ) on 20 December 2007, 19:16 under the file name Stieler - Alexandra, Prinzessin von Bayern (Schönheitengalerie).jpg . ( +/− )

title: Portrait of Comtesse d'Haussonville
description: A society portrait of Louise de Broglie, comtesse d'Haussonville, that Ingres laboured over for three years and that now hangs in the Frick Collection. Mirror, ribboned slippers and porcelain are arranged with linear precision, while the sitter's improbably elongated arm exemplifies the artist's controlled anatomical license.

title: Portrait of Friederike Freiin von Gumppenberg
description: A late entry in the Schönheitengalerie, depicting the Bavarian baroness with the soft chiaroscuro and idealized features Stieler used throughout the cycle. The portrait belongs to the long Munich project that gathered thirty-six women from across the social spectrum at Ludwig I's command.

title: The Balcony Room
description: An empty room, white curtains billowing at the open balcony door, a sofa half-glimpsed in a wall mirror. Menzel made the small oil for himself, capturing transient sunlight with the immediacy of a snapshot decades before plein-air Impressionism reached Berlin.

title: Building Site with Willows
description: Menzel turns a humble Berlin construction site, fringed by drooping willows, into a study of weather and labour. The unposed subject and freshly observed light anticipate the realist vein the Prussian painter would pursue across decades of obsessive sketching from life.

title: Pizarro Seizing the Inca of Peru
description: Painted when Millais was barely seventeen and exhibited at the Royal Academy, this dramatic history scene shows the conquistador wresting Atahualpa from his throne amid scattering attendants. The crowded composition and bright local colour announce the precocious draughtsmanship he would soon channel into the Pre-Raphaelite Brotherhood.
credit: ZgEnAhLJz5AJNA at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Undine Giving the Ring to Massaniello
description: A fantastical pairing of the water-nymph Undine, drawn from de la Motte Fouqué's novella, with the Neapolitan fisherman-rebel Masaniello. Painted at seventy-one, the canvas dissolves narrative into a swirl of aquatic colour typical of Turner's late square-format mythologies.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"t/turner/2/208turne"

title: Accusa segreta
description: A Venetian noblewoman pauses by the bocca di leone, the lion's-mouth letterbox into which secret denunciations were dropped under the Republic. Hayez, leader of Italian Romantic painting, often coded contemporary Risorgimento politics into such historical scenes, here rendered with his porcelain-smooth flesh and rich brocades.

title: At first glance he looks fierce, but he's really a nice person
description: A glowering giant's face is, on closer look, made up entirely of squirming naked men — a sly anatomical pun on the proverb in the title. Kuniyoshi produced such jigsaw figures to slip past Tempō-era censorship that restricted depictions of named actors and courtesans.

title: Lola Montez
description: The Irish-born dancer who styled herself a Spanish noblewoman, painted at the height of her hold over Ludwig I of Bavaria. Her place in the Schönheitengalerie was itself a small political event, the affair contributing to the king's abdication the following year.

title: Tamar
description: The biblical Tamar, daughter-in-law of Judah, is rendered as a brooding half-length figure veiled in shadowed reds. Hayez, the central painter of Italian Romanticism, treats Old Testament heroines with the same charged sensuality and theatrical chiaroscuro he brought to his patriotic allegories.

title: hanging scroll
description: A hanging scroll depicting the arhat Handaka Sonja, shaven-headed and large-eared, summoning a dragon from his begging bowl amid a coiling rain cloud. Kuniyoshi handles the Buddhist iconography in ink and pale colour with the muscular drawing he refined in his warrior prints.

title: Menzel's sister Emilie
description: A small, intimate study of the painter's sister Emilie asleep in a chair, her head fallen to one shoulder beneath a lamp. Menzel's private domestic oils, made off the public radar, anticipate Impressionism in their sketchy handling and unsentimental observation of momentary light.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Self-Portrait with Pipe
description: An early self-portrait from Courbet's Romantic-tinged youth, the painter shown smoking a long pipe with hooded eyes and a tilted head. Strong dark-on-dark modelling and a deliberately bohemian air announce the self-mythologising persona he would build through the 1850s.

title: In the Attic Room
description: The Garret or The Attic (c. 1840s). Private collection

title: Kagesue
description: Two mounted samurai surge through the rapids of the Uji in their famed race to lead the charge during the Genpei War. Kuniyoshi packs the triptych with churning water and twisting horses, exemplifying the warrior-print genre (musha-e) he revived in the late Edo period.

title: Studio Interior
description: William Merritt Chase (American, 1849-1916). Studio Interior, ca. 1882.

title: The Haunted Manor
description: An ivy-choked manor stands beyond a dark woodland pool that catches a livid sunset. Painted early in Hunt's career, the eerie stillness and minutely studied foliage signal the emerging Pre-Raphaelite commitment to truth-to-nature charged with literary mood.

title: The Wreck Buoy
description: A scarlet buoy bobs above a foundered hull as a low rainbow arcs over a ruffled sea. One of Turner's last completed oils, the picture reworks an earlier seascape into a meditation on hazard and salvage.

title: A colored version of the Big wave from 100 views of the Fuji
description: A reworked echo of the famous Kanagawa wave, drawn from the later One Hundred Views of Mount Fuji. Hokusai mirrors the composition, removes the fishing boats, and turns the spume into a flock of birds — a meditation on transformation rendered in his late, more graphic woodblock manner.

title: Boar Lane
description: A wet Leeds thoroughfare at dusk, gas lamps haloed in mist and shopfronts mirrored on the slick pavement. Grimshaw built a career on these meticulously observed urban nocturnes, balancing Pre-Raphaelite detail with a Whistlerian feel for atmospheric blue-greens.

title: Cats Suggested as The Fifty-three Stations of the Tōkaidō
description: Kuniyoshi, a noted cat lover, replaces every post town on the Tōkaidō highway with a cat whose pose or attribute puns on the station's name. The triptych is a tour de force of Edo wordplay (mitate) and ukiyo-e woodblock printing, made when actor prints had been restricted by Tenpō reform censorship.

title: Frederick the Great Playing the Flute at Sanssouci
description: Menzel reconstructs an evening concert at Sanssouci, with Frederick the Great playing flute beside C.P.E. Bach at the harpsichord and Quantz, Benda, and the Margravine Wilhelmine listening. Painted under Friedrich Wilhelm IV, it crowns the artist's long Prussian historical project.

title: Hokusai Manga 01
description: A page from the first volume of the Hokusai Manga, the artist's sprawling printed sketchbooks that catalogue figures, animals, landscapes, and curiosities. Issued from 1814, the volumes circulated widely as drawing manuals and later became a key conduit of Japanese form into European art.
credit: Michener, James A. (1958) Hokusai Sketchbooks: Selections from the Manga , Rutland, Vermont & Tokyo: Charles E. Tuttle Company

title: Hokusai Manga 02
description: Another page from the Hokusai Manga, the encyclopedic woodblock sketch compendium that eventually ran to fifteen volumes. The loose, observational lines were intended as study material for students, though they read today as a portrait of Edo's everyday visual world.
credit: Michener, James A. (1958) Hokusai Sketchbooks: Selections from the Manga , Rutland, Vermont & Tokyo: Charles E. Tuttle Company

title: Susanna at Her Bath
description: The biblical Susanna twists modestly at her bath, surprised by the elders whose presence is implied beyond the frame. Hayez handles the polished nude in the National Gallery, London, with a sculptural finish indebted to Canova and the warm tonalities of his Venetian training.
credit: 1. the second upload from File:Francesco Hayez 054.jpg 2. - 5. National Gallery, London - online catalogue .

title: The Church at Vétheuil
description: The Church Vétheuil

title: The Painter Resting Under an Umbrella in a Forest Clearing
description: A plein-air painter has stretched out on his back beneath a propped umbrella in a forest clearing, paint box at his feet. The compact panel is a gentle self-mockery of the Romantic ideal of the artist communing with nature.
credit: Joachim Nagel: Carl Spitzweg. Belser, Stuttgart 2008, S. 11

title: Mariana
description: Drawn from Tennyson's poem after the Shakespearean character, Mariana stretches wearily at her embroidery beside a stained-glass window, autumn leaves drifting onto the floor. Millais paints the heraldic glass and velvet gown with founding Pre-Raphaelite precision, every fibre and pane separately observed.
credit: Google Arts & Culture — 6wGvnPEiyH8RFA Also available at http://www.tate-images.com/results.asp?image=T07553&wwwflag=3&imagepos=41 .

title: Monet w1050
description: A Belle-Île seascape from Monet's 1886 Brittany campaign, where he confronted the Atlantic's dark, jagged cliffs under shifting weather. The palette tightens to slate, violet and bottle-green, with broken brushwork registering the swell against rock.

title: Monet w1054
description: Painted during the artist's ten-week stay on Belle-Île-en-Mer, the canvas wrestles with pyramidal sea-stacks and pounding surf. Monet worked in oilskins on the cliffs, layering short, scumbled strokes to capture spray and the heavy Atlantic light.

title: Monet w1056
description: Another study from the Belle-Île series, in which Monet returned again and again to the same outcrops to track changing weather. Cool blues and bruised purples dominate, the sea built up in dense, choppy strokes that mimic the swell itself.

title: Monet w1065
description: A motif drawn from Monet's Belle-Île campaign on Brittany's wild south coast, where granite needles meet a relentless sea. The painter mixed lead white into deep ultramarines to render foam, working in the open air against squalls that often interrupted the sittings.

title: Monet w1075
description: From the artist's mid-1880s output, this canvas shows the loosened, near-abstract handling Monet was developing on the Channel coast. Surface texture takes priority over contour, with the brushstroke itself conveying wind, weather and tide.

title: Monet w1089
description: A landscape from Monet's most prolific decade at Giverny, where the garden and surrounding meadows became his open-air laboratory. Light is registered in feathered, comma-shaped strokes that trade modeling for vibration of colour.

title: Monet w1120
description: Painted in the late 1880s, the canvas belongs to Monet's increasing tendency to dissolve subject into atmosphere. Broken touches of complementary colour set the picture humming, anticipating the serial method of the haystacks and poplars.

title: Monet w1155
description: A Giverny landscape from the years when Monet was systematizing his serial practice, returning to the same view under successive lights. Foliage is broken into flickering touches that read as foliage only at a distance.

title: Ophelia
description: Shakespeare's drowning Ophelia drifts among meadow flowers along the Hogsmill River in Surrey. Millais painted the riverbank botany on site over five months, then posed Elizabeth Siddal in a heated bathtub for the figure. Each bloom carries Victorian floral symbolism for grief, innocence and madness.

title: Poplars in the Sun
description: Part of Monet's poplar series on the banks of the Epte, painted from a flat-bottomed boat moored among the trees. The verticals march across the canvas in a near-decorative rhythm, sunlight filtered into juxtaposed greens, lavenders and rose.

title: Portrait of Madame Moitessier
description: Madame Moitessier stands in black velvet and lace, a heavy gold bracelet anchoring her gloved wrist. Ingres laboured on his Moitessier portraits across more than a decade, prizing the linear contour and porcelain finish that define his late society style; this version belongs to the National Gallery of Art in Washington.
credit: National Gallery of Art, Washington, D. C., online collection

title: Portrait of Princess Albert de Broglie
description: Pauline de Broglie is set against a tapestry-hung wall, her blue satin gown rendered with Ingres's near-fanatical attention to fabric. The portrait was painted late in his career and is now a centrepiece of the Robert Lehman Collection at the Metropolitan Museum.
credit: This file was derived from: Jean auguste dominique ingres princesse albert de broglie.jpg :

title: An Idyll
description: An early pastoral by Gérôme in the vein of his Néo-Grec circle, reaching back to Anacreontic and Pompeian models. Slender nudes, shallow stage-like space and porcelain-cool color place the picture closer to his Greek Interior than to his later Orientalist scenes.

title: Back View of Fuji from Dream Mtn in Kai Province
description: Hiroshige reverses the conventional southern view of Fuji, glimpsing the sacred peak from Kai Province on the mountain's far side. Part of his first Thirty-six Views series, the design uses graduated bokashi printing to layer mist, peak, and foreground.

title: Sokokura by Hiroshige1
description: A woodblock view of Sokokura, one of the seven hot springs of Hakone, with bathers and travelers gathered amid the steep wooded gorge. The print belongs to Hiroshige's series Hakone shichiyu zue, devoted to the spa villages prized by Edo-period travelers along the Tōkaidō.

title: Café Terrace at Night
description: A nocturnal view of a yellow-awninged café on the Place du Forum in Arles, with cobblestones receding under a sky of large stars. Van Gogh painted it without using black, building the night entirely from saturated yellows, blues and greens — an early experiment in his ambition to render the south at night in pure colour.
credit: picture taken and uploaded by Paul Hermans

title: The Awakening Conscience
description: A young woman starts up from her lover's lap in a cluttered London parlour, her face caught at the instant of moral recognition. Hunt loaded the picture with Pre-Raphaelite symbolism, every object on the patterned wallpaper, the discarded glove, the cat at the table chosen to spell out the cost of a kept woman's situation.

title: The Order of Release 1746
description: A Highland wife presents a release order to a soldier guarding her wounded Jacobite husband, who collapses against her with their child asleep on his shoulder. Effie Ruskin posed for the wife — the sittings precipitated her separation from John Ruskin and eventual marriage to Millais.
credit: Transferred from en.wikipedia to Commons by Mattis . Original uploader was Paul Barlow

title: The Sleeping Spinner
description: A young spinner has fallen asleep at her wheel, distaff drooping in her lap. Courbet treats the rural genre subject with monumental gravity, applying paint thickly with knife and brush so that the homely interior carries the weight of a Salon picture.

title: Roman Pilgrims
description: A group of Italian pilgrims rests on the road, products of Lewis's residence in Rome during the late 1830s before he settled in Cairo. The picture catalogues regional costume with the same ethnographic precision he would later turn on the Ottoman world. It marks his transition from Spanish to Mediterranean genre subjects.
credit: YQEyub_NfGMHxg at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Finding of the Saviour in the Temple
credit: qQEKNsHatzrphQ at Google Cultural Institute , zoom level maximum

title: The Painter's Studio
description: A monumental allegory of seven years of Courbet's artistic life, gathering critics, patrons, and anonymous figures around the painter at his easel, with a nude muse and small boy looking on. Rejected from the 1855 Exposition Universelle, it became the centrepiece of his self-organised Pavilion of Realism in Paris.

title: The Scapegoat
description: A goat staggers across the salt flats of the Dead Sea, its horns bound with the red wool of the Yom Kippur ritual. Hunt painted the scene on site in 1854, hauling his easel to the shore of Oosdoom in pursuit of the Pre-Raphaelite ideal of truth to nature, sharpened by Christian symbolism.

title: The Serenade
description: A hopeful suitor stands beneath a balcony serenading an unseen sweetheart, while neighbours watch from their windows. Spitzweg returned often to this nocturnal Ständchen subject, savouring the gap between Romantic gesture and small-town audience.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Arab saddling his horse
description: An Orientalist subject typical of Delacroix's late career, depicting a North African horseman tending to his mount. The painter draws on sketches from his 1832 Moroccan journey, deploying loose, energetic brushwork and a warm, dust-toned palette to evoke movement and Mediterranean light.
credit: 1. The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 . 2. artclon.com , image 3. The Hermitage, St. Petersburg

title: Awa Province : Naruto Whirlpools
description: A vertical ōban from the Famous Places in the Sixty-odd Provinces series, looking down on the swirling Naruto whirlpools that churn between Awa and Awaji. Hiroshige flattens the sea into concentric arcs of bokashi blue, framed by a steep cliff at left.
credit: Museum of Fine Arts, Boston, online database

title: In the Harem
description: An odalisque reclines under fretwork screens in a Munich painter's idea of a Turkish harem, the orientalist staffage rendered with Spitzweg's trademark twinkling humour. The artist drew his exotic detail entirely from prints and travel literature, never having visited the East.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Rocks at Mouthier by Gustave Courbet
description: A close-up view of weathered limestone cliffs near the artist's native Franche-Comté, the river snaking through a narrow valley below. Courbet builds the rock face with palette-knife slabs of paint, asserting landscape as raw geological matter rather than picturesque scenery.

title: The intercepted love letter
description: A pair of conspirators in a courtyard puzzle over a love letter snatched from its addressee. The narrative anecdote, the costumed protagonists, and the pinpoint architectural detail are textbook Spitzweg, drawn from a stage tradition rather than direct observation.
credit: 1. The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 . 2. Wichmann, Siegfried; Carl Spitzweg , München 1990, S. 114

title: A Frank Encampment in the Desert of Mount Sinai
description: A British traveller's tent camp pitched in the Sinai wilderness, with St Catherine's monastery faintly visible in the distance. Lewis drew on a decade of life in Cairo to render carpets, tasselled saddles and Bedouin retainers with miniaturist exactitude. The watercolour fuses Orientalist reportage with the topographical tradition.
credit: DgFnh3a4-_8sAw at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Male Nude
description: Male Nude Artist: Edgar Degas (French, Paris 1834–1917 Paris) Date: 1856 Medium: Oil on canvas Dimensions: 13 3/4 x 24 1/4 in. (34. 9 x 61.

title: Monet w1001
description: From the mid-1880s, when Monet was extending his repertoire of coastal and rural motifs after settling at Giverny. The brushwork is densely woven, building landscape from short, directional strokes of contrasting hue.

title: Monet w1003
description: A mid-1880s landscape from Monet's expanded raisonné, painted as he consolidated the rapid, plein-air manner into a more deliberate decorative surface. Form yields to a tapestry of small coloured touches.

title: Monet w1016
description: A landscape entry in Monet's catalogue raisonné showing his characteristic divisionist-leaning brushwork of the period. Local colour is broken across complementary touches so the eye reassembles the scene at viewing distance.

title: Monet w1035
description: From Monet's Étretat campaigns of the 1880s, when he braved cliff ledges and rising tides to capture the chalk arches in changing weather. The handling marries graphic structure to looser, atmospheric water.

title: Monet w1042
description: A Normandy coast subject from Monet's prolific Étretat-era output, where rock, sea, and sky are unified by a high-keyed palette of blue, lilac, and creamy white. Brushwork follows the contours of swell and stone.

title: Monet w1179
description: An earlier landscape predating the high Impressionist style, with a more traditional, tonal handling than the artist's later work. Drawing remains visible beneath the paint, and the palette stays close to a Barbizon range of earths and greens.

title: Monet w1296
description: An early landscape from Monet's formative years, before the Impressionist breakthrough of the 1870s. The composition retains traditional perspectival depth, with paint laid more thinly than in the textured works of his maturity.

title: Monet w1563
description: A landscape held within Monet's Impressionist repertoire, where the optical mixing of small coloured strokes carries the work. Atmosphere takes precedence over drawing, the eye assembling the scene from a tissue of brushmarks.

title: Monet w1636
description: From Monet's later Giverny output, where landscape gives way to immersive fields of colour and reflection. The painter's brushwork loosens further, sacrificing legibility of motif to a unified surface vibration.

title: Monet w948
description: A very early work from Monet's teenage years in Le Havre, predating his formal training under Boudin and his move toward Impressionism. The handling is more conventional than his later production but already shows an interest in outdoor observation.

title: Monet w961
description: An adolescent piece from Monet's Le Havre period, made before he had committed to landscape painting in the open air. Drawing and tonal modelling still carry the work, with only hints of the looser facture he would soon develop.

title: Monet w962
description: An early Monet landscape painted while the artist was still a teenager working from nature in Normandy. The brushwork shows the influence of Boudin and the Barbizon school, foreshadowing the loose handling that would later define Impressionism.

title: Sumida River
description: From One Hundred Famous Views of Edo, the print frames the Sumida River through the trees of the Suijin shrine grove at Masaki. Hiroshige uses the steep foreground branches and high horizon characteristic of the late series to fold the viewer into the riverside scene.
credit: Image: http://collections.lacma.org/sites/default/files/remote_images/piction/ma-18199982-O3.jpg Gallery: http://collections.lacma.org/node/190172 archive copy at the Wayback Machine

title: Yoro Waterfall in Mino Province
description: From the late series Famous Views of the Sixty-odd Provinces, this vertical print shows the Yōrō Falls of Mino plunging through a wooded ravine. Hiroshige exploits the tall format to stretch the cataract into a single white ribbon against deep green cliffs, with figures dwarfed at its base.

title: Bamboo Yards
description: An Autumn print from Hiroshige's One Hundred Famous Views of Edo, showing the bamboo yards along the Kanda River where poles were stacked vertically into stark, near-abstract patterns. The diagonal viewpoint and reduced palette typify the formal boldness of his final great series.

title: Egyptian Recruits Crossing the Desert
description: Painted after Gérôme's first journey up the Nile, the canvas shows conscripts being driven across the sand under guard. The high horizon, sharp shadows and ethnographic costume detail are characteristic of his fastidious Orientalist manner.

title: Horikiri Iris Garden
description: Tall purple irises crowd the foreground of the celebrated Horikiri garden east of Edo, dwarfing the strolling visitors beyond. The radical close-up viewpoint, from One Hundred Famous Views of Edo, would be admired and copied by Van Gogh.
credit: qgFCjAmgF1vaxA at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Justitia
description: A weathered statue of blindfolded Lady Justice stands with broken scales while a soldier loiters in shadow behind her, a wry Biedermeier comment on the gap between law and power. The panel was sold under Nazi duress by Jewish collector Leo Bendel and only restituted to his heirs in 2019.

title: Plum Park at Kameido
description: A celebrated plum tree in the Kameido garden east of Edo, its blossoming branch thrust across the foreground while visitors stroll behind in a red-suffused dusk. Hiroshige's print from One Hundred Famous Views of Edo was later copied in oil by Van Gogh.

title: Sudden Shower Over Shin-Ohashi Bridge and Atake
description: From One Hundred Famous Views of Edo, figures hurry across the Shin-Ōhashi bridge as a sudden summer downpour falls in fine engraved lines over the Sumida. The compressed perspective and slanting rain made the print a touchstone for European Japonisme; Van Gogh later copied it in oil.

title: Suido Bridge and Surugadai
description: From One Hundred Famous Views of Edo, Hiroshige's late masterwork-series, this Summer sheet looks down from the Suidobashi bridge over the Kanda aqueduct toward the scholar district of Surugadai. A giant carp streamer for Boys' Day floats in the foreground, a hallmark of his daring close-up framing.

title: The Bridge at Ambrussum
description: A small landscape of the Roman bridge over the Vidourle in Languedoc, oil on paper later mounted on panel. Courbet builds the stonework and riverbank with the heavy palette knife and earthy tonal range that defined his realist treatment of provincial French scenery.

title: The Coffee Bearer
description: A young Egyptian woman carries a brass coffee tray through a marble courtyard, ducking below the lintel of a sunlit doorway. Lewis stages the scene to showcase the architectural light effects he studied during his Cairo years. The metalwork, sash and tilework are executed with miniaturist watercolour precision in oils.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: The Duel After the Masquerade
description: Dawn in the Bois de Boulogne: a wounded Pierrot collapses into the arms of the Duc de Guise while a Venetian doge inspects the injury and a black-cloaked Domino reels away. Gérôme's tightly finished, stage-like composition, derived from a version made for the Duc d'Aumale, exemplifies his blend of academic precision and theatrical narrative.
credit: Walters Art Museum : Home page Info about artwork

title: Utagawa Hiroshige
description: A vertical triptych charts the famed Naruto whirlpools off Awa province, the churning indigo eddies set beneath a banded sky. Hiroshige stretches the ooban format to give the tide its full vertiginous scale.

title: Utagawa Kunisada
description: From the series Modern Parodies of the Four Classes, this sheet personifies the artisan estate as a fashionable beauty. Kunisada combines bijin-ga portraiture with the Confucian shi-no-ko-sho social hierarchy.

title: 13. The Tama River
description: The Tama River flows beneath a pale Fuji in this thirteenth design from Hiroshige's Thirty-six Views. Rafts and figures animate the middle distance, while bokashi gradients carry the eye from green water to blue mountain.

title: 20. The Entrance Gate In Enoshima
description: Plate twenty of Hiroshige's Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji, showing pilgrims passing through the bronze torii at Enoshima with Fuji floating across Sagami Bay. The composition is built from flat overlapping silhouettes, a hallmark of late-Edo ukiyo-e landscape design.

title: 21. Lake In Hakone
description: Plate twenty-one of the Thirty-six Views, with the dark glassy surface of Lake Ashi at Hakone reflecting Mount Fuji and the surrounding peaks. Hiroshige was a master of these mirror-symmetry compositions, splitting the sheet between solid landscape and its watery double.

title: 26. The Oi River Between Suruga and Totomi Provinces
description: Porters ford the broad shallows of the Oi River, with Fuji's cone framed beyond. Hiroshige drew the design for the Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji issued by Tsutaya Kichizo, deploying the bokashi gradient technique to suggest mist rising off the water.

title: 32. Dog Eye Pass
description: Hiroshige's view from Inume Pass in Kai Province looks down on a sea of cloud broken by Fuji's summit, plate 32 of his Thirty-six Views series. The composition compresses foreground rocks and distant peak into a single graphic plane, a hallmark of late-Edo landscape design.

title: 34. Kuroto Bay
description: Boats drift in Kuroto Bay with Fuji visible across the water, plate 34 of Hiroshige's Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji. The flat blue expanses and economical drawing of sails reflect the woodblock vocabulary that would soon enthral European Japonisme.

title: 36 Views of Mount Fuji - #3. Sukiyagashi
description: The Sukiyagashi district of Edo with Fuji rising beyond rooftops, plate three of Hiroshige's Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji. The print balances the dense urban foreground against the calm geometric mountain that recurs throughout the series.

title: 36 Views of Mount Fuji - 18. The Sagami River
description: Plate eighteen of Hiroshige's late Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji, with the Sagami River winding through Kanagawa Province toward the snow-capped peak. The vertical ōban format compresses foreground rapids and distant cone into a single, telescoped view.

title: 36 Views of Mount Fuji - 19. The Seven-Ri Beach
description: Plate nineteen of the Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji, depicting Shichirigahama, the seven-ri stretch of coast in Sagami Province. Enoshima crouches on the horizon while Fuji floats beyond, the printer using bokashi gradient to fade sea into sky.

title: 36 Views of Mount Fuji - 33. Kogane Plain
description: A wide view across the Kogane plain in Shimosa, with Fuji blue on the horizon and grasses sketched in the foreground. From Hiroshige's Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji, the print uses the bokashi gradation to soften sky and distance.

title: 36 Views of Mount Fuji- 23. The Sea off Satta
description: Plate twenty-three of the Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji. Big stylised waves curl in the foreground off Satta Point on the Tōkaidō road, with Fuji rising serenely behind, an answer to Hokusai's earlier Great Wave composition.

title: 36 Views of Mount Fuji' - 36. The Hoda Coast
description: The closing print of Hiroshige's Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji shows the rocky Hoda coast in Awa Province, Fuji floating across Edo Bay. Crisp keyblock outlines and indigo seas give the design the graphic clarity that defined his late style.

title: At Prayer
description: A solitary Muslim worshipper kneels on a prayer rug, the figure isolated against an austere architectural ground. The work belongs to Gérôme's earliest Orientalist forays, marked by his crisp draftsmanship and the smooth, almost lacquered surface he favoured throughout his career.

title: Frederick the Great at Lissa: Bonsoir
description: Frederick II strides into a candlelit Silesian hall after the battle of Leuthen with a casual greeting to startled Austrian officers billeted there. Menzel built his career as the great visual chronicler of the Prussian king, working from meticulous reconstructions of period uniforms and interiors.
credit: Photographer and uploader was Hajotthu at de.wikipedia

title: Fuji on the Left of Tokaido Road
description: Travellers move along the Tokaido highway as Mount Fuji rises to the left, a print from Hiroshige's posthumously published Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji. The composition layers near figures against a flattened distance, exploiting the woodblock medium's graded skies and crisp silhouettes.

title: Fuji sanjūrokkei Tōto ikkokubashi
description: Sheet from Hiroshige's late series Thirty-Six Views of Mount Fuji, showing Edo's Ikkoku Bridge with the sacred peak rising behind the city's tiled rooftops. The print uses the deep cobalt bokashi gradation Hiroshige favoured for sky and water, contrasting urban bustle below with serene distance.
credit: print scan original at Library of Congress

title: Hiroshige
description: Red maples frame a view of Fuji from the slopes of Meguro at sunset, plate ten of Hiroshige's Thirty-six Views. Printed by Tsutaya Kichizo, the design uses heavy bokashi in the sky to evoke the dying autumn light.

title: Ise Futamigaura
description: The wedded rocks of Meoto Iwa stand off the Futami shore, joined by the sacred shimenawa rope, with Fuji visible across the bay. Plate twenty-seven of the Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji, the design pairs Shinto pilgrimage iconography with Hiroshige's signature distant cone.

title: Misaka Pass in Kai Province
description: From the Misaka Pass in Kai Province, Fuji is doubled by its reflection in Lake Kawaguchi below. Hiroshige's Thirty-six Views design exploits the symmetry as a graphic device, layering pine-clad slopes against the still water.

title: Self-Portrait
description: An early self-portrait by the painter best known for his hushed flower pieces and group portraits of the Parisian avant-garde. The dark ground and concentrated gaze owe much to his close study of seventeenth-century Dutch masters in the Louvre.

title: The courier in the Rosenthal
description: A village postman trudges along a sun-drenched lane in the Rosenthal, satchel slung at his hip. Spitzweg, a trained pharmacist who painted in his spare hours, made a career of these affectionately ironic small-town vignettes.
credit: Joachim Nagel: Carl Spitzweg. Belser, Stuttgart 2008, S. 31

provenance.collection: Museum für Kunst und Kulturgeschichte der Philipps-Universität Marburg
provenance.location: Museum für Kunst und Kulturgeschichte der Philipps-Universität Marburg

title: The lute player
description: A single seated figure tunes a long-necked lute in a dim Cairo interior, the strings catching a shaft of light. Gérôme uses the musician as a pretext for a study in muted tonality and meticulous costume detail.

title: The Otsuki Plain
description: Travellers cross the open plain of Otsuki with Fuji set against a pale sky, plate 31 of the Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji. Hiroshige favoured such low horizons to let the mountain dominate, balanced by the human-scale activity of road and field.

title: The Pine Forest of Mio
description: From Hiroshige's late Mount Fuji series, the spit of pines at Miho-no-Matsubara curls into Suruga Bay with Fuji on the far shore. The site is bound up with the noh play Hagoromo, and Hiroshige uses bokashi blues and the dense black of pine needles to set its quiet mood.

title: The Sea off the Miura Peninsula
description: From Hiroshige's late Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji series, published shortly before his death. A foreground of dark, choppy water frames the distant cone of Fuji across the bay; the woodblock printer's bokashi gradients soften sky and sea into bands of graded blue.

title: The Teahouse with the View of Mt. Fuji
description: From Hiroshige's Thirty-Six Views of Mount Fuji, travellers pause at a roadside teahouse while the snow-tipped volcano floats beyond. The graded blue sky and crisp foreground figures show the woodblock master's command of bokashi printing and his eye for the everyday rhythms of Edo travel.

title: The Vale of Rest
description: Two nuns dig a grave at twilight in a walled cemetery, one resting on her spade as a coffin-shaped cloud drifts overhead. Begun outdoors at Kinnoull near Perth, the painting marks Millais's shift from minute Pre-Raphaelite finish toward broader, mood-saturated landscape.

title: View At Rouelles
description: A youthful landscape of Rouelles in the Le Havre hinterland, often cited as Monet's earliest surviving oil. The careful, almost Corot-like handling of trees and stream marks the threshold before his conversion to outdoor, sketch-based painting.

title: Wild Goose Hill and the Tone River
description: From Hiroshige's late Thirty-Six Views of Mount Fuji, the Tone River winds toward the distant snow cone past Wild Goose Hill. Migrating geese punctuate a sky printed in deep bokashi blue, the woodblock gradation that became the artist's signature atmospheric device.

title: Hiroshige II Suō Iwakuni
description: The Kintai Bridge arches in five wooden spans over the Nishiki at Iwakuni, a celebrated feat of Edo carpentry. The print belongs to Hiroshige II's One Hundred Famous Views of the Provinces, continuing the meisho tradition his teacher had popularised.

title: Ingres Etude Bain Turc Louvre 1859
description: A preparatory drawing in the Louvre's Cabinet des dessins for Le Bain turc, the circular canvas Ingres completed at age 82. The sheet shows him reworking the languid female nudes he had drawn from life decades earlier into a single dense Orientalist tableau.

title: King Candaules
description: Drawn from Herodotus, the canvas catches the moment Gyges glimpses the queen of Lydia undressing while King Candaules watches from concealment. Gérôme stages the voyeuristic episode as a brightly lit interior, turning ancient text into theatrical tableau.
credit: Museo de Arte de Ponce online – click the right arrow by the bottom series of images several (currently 6) times, then its image, to see details about this painting.

title: Student Torchlight Procession
description: Menzel records a Berlin student torchlight procession with his characteristic reportorial eye, the crowd compacted into a flickering frieze of faces and smoking brands. He was the great chronicler of Prussian public life, equally at ease with court ceremonies and street gatherings.

title: The Death of Caesar
description: Gérôme avoids the assassination itself, instead showing senators streaming from the curia while Caesar's body lies abandoned in the foreground. The cool, measured composition and almost photographic finish drew contemporary comparison to a daguerreotype of the ancient world.
credit: Walters Art Museum : Home page Info about artwork

title: The Kiss
description: A young couple embrace on a stone staircase, the woman's blue gown and the man's red tights forming the colours of the French and Italian flags. Hayez's most popular canvas became a coded emblem of the Risorgimento and the alliance behind Italian unification.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"s-e.html?/html/h/hayez/6hayez"

title: Diogenes
description: The Cynic philosopher squats inside his earthenware tub, lighting a lamp by daylight to search Athens for an honest man. Gérôme surrounds him with the dogs that gave Cynicism its name, treating the anecdote with the archaeological literalism typical of his Greek subjects.
credit: Walters Art Museum : Home page Info about artwork

title: Fox in the Snow
description: A red fox crouches over its prey in a frozen field, painted with the palette knife's broad, material slabs that Courbet championed in his Realist landscapes. The unsentimental subject and the granular, scraped whites of the snow strip the hunt scene of Romantic glamour.
credit: 1. dallasmuseumofart.org 2. The AMICA Library 3./4. Dallas Museum of Art, online collection

title: James Abbott McNeill Whistler
description: Plate from the Thames Set, made when Whistler took rooms at the Angel Inn to study London's working riverfront. A balcony view crowded with rigging, mooring posts and a pair of seamen, etched with the dense, descriptive line he later abandoned for atmospheric suggestion.

title: Schmetterlinge
description: A swirl of butterflies fills the sheet, drawn with finely observed wing markings and a decorative arabesque rhythm. Kaulbach, best known as a history painter, here turns to natural history as ornamental composition.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: The Black Brunswicker
description: A young Brunswick cavalry officer takes leave of his sweetheart on the eve of Waterloo, her hand braced against the door he is determined to open. Millais paired the black death's-head hussar uniform against shimmering ivory silk to heighten the parting. Charles Dickens's daughter Kate modelled for the woman.

title: The Oceanids
description: A wood-engraved fantasy of the Oceanid sea nymphs rising through dark surf. Doré, working largely as a print designer, deploys his trademark high-contrast hatching to model luminous bodies against deep velvety blacks, building atmosphere from tonal extremes rather than colour.

title: The Thames in Ice
description: Painted during the great freeze that gripped London in the winter of 1860, when Whistler was lodging at Wapping. A merchant ship sits locked among broken floes, its rigging set against a smoke-grey sky in the muted, almost monochrome palette he was developing for the river.

title: Zeitungsleser im Hausgarten
description: A Biedermeier gentleman absorbs himself in his newspaper amid the trellised greenery of a small private garden. Spitzweg specialized in such gently humorous bourgeois vignettes, painted in rich enamel-like color, that catalogued the introspective civic culture of mid-nineteenth-century Munich.

title: Phryne before the Areopagus
description: Gérôme stages the legendary trial of the courtesan Phryne, disrobed by her advocate Hypereides to sway the Athenian judges. The single sweep of light on her body and the recoiling line of bearded jurors make a deliberately theatrical machine of his archaeological reconstruction.

title: Symphony in White
description: Joanna Hiffernan stands full-length in a flowing white dress on a white drape, holding a single lily. Whistler's emphasis on subtle gradations of cream, ivory and pale flesh tones over storytelling helped lay the ground for Aestheticism's art-for-art's-sake credo.
credit: National Gallery of Art, Washington, D. C., online collection

title: Music in the Tuileries
description: A fashionable crowd in top hats and crinolines listens to a band in the Tuileries gardens, the canvas crowded with portraits of Manet's circle, including Baudelaire, Gautier and Fantin-Latour. The flat patches of black coats and abrupt cropping mark Manet's first decisive turn toward painting modern Parisian life.

title: Shipwreck on the Coast
description: A small craft is overwhelmed by churning surf beneath a leaden sky, figures barely visible amid the spray. The late Delacroix returns again to the shipwreck motif as an emblem of human helplessness before nature, treated with a freer, almost atmospheric brush.
credit: 7QEvX2VgBvI66Q at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Turkish bath
description: A circular tondo crowded with bathing women, drawn together from poses Ingres had refined across half a century of nudes. Painted in his eighties for Prince Napoléon, the work distils his lifelong fascination with sinuous contour and the Orientalist harem trope into a single hothouse composition.
credit: This file was derived from: Le Bain Turc, by Jean Auguste Dominique Ingres, from C2RMF.jpg :

title: Dance of the Almeh
description: A solo dancer raises her arms before seated Ottoman soldiers in a smoky tavern, the firelight glinting off brass and embroidered cloth. The almeh -- an Egyptian singing-dancer -- was one of Gérôme's recurring Orientalist motifs of the 1860s.

title: Don Quijote illustrated by Gustav Dore III
description: Don Quixote rides out across the Spanish meseta with Sancho Panza behind, an illustration from Doré's monumental 1863 edition of Cervantes. The wood-engraved plates, cut by a battery of Parisian engravers, fixed the public image of the knight-errant for the next century.

title: Eve of St Agnes
description: Drawn from Keats's poem, Madeline undresses by moonlight on St Agnes's Eve in the hope of dreaming of her lover. Millais painted the scene by night at Knole House using his wife Effie as model, working in the cool silvered palette characteristic of his later, looser Pre-Raphaelite manner.

title: Napoleon in Egypt
description: Napoleon on horseback before the Sphinx and pyramids, a meditation on power and antiquity tied to the 1798 Egyptian campaign. Gerome's polished academic finish and ethnographic eye made him the leading French Orientalist of the Second Empire.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Olympia
description: A reclining nude courtesan meets the viewer's gaze with cool defiance, attended by a Black maid bearing flowers from a client. Manet flattens form with stark lighting and sharp tonal contrasts, scandalising the 1865 Salon by transposing Titian's Venus into contemporary Parisian demi-monde.
credit: Google Art Project: Home - pic Maximum resolution. Colours edited by uploader

title: The Christian Martyrs' Last Prayer
description: A Roman arena scene in which kneeling Christians pray as lions are released and crucified figures burn against the stadium walls. Gerome combines archaeological detail of the Circus Maximus with the polished academic finish typical of his Salon machines.
credit: Walters Art Museum : Home page Info about artwork

title: The Princess from the Land of Porcelain
description: Painted in London during Whistler's japonisme phase, the canvas dresses Christine Spartali in a loose kimono among Asian carpets and a folding screen. Frederick Leyland later acquired it and hung it in the dining room that became the Peacock Room, now reinstalled at the Freer Gallery.
credit: This file was derived from: James McNeill Whistler - La Princesse du pays de la porcelaine - Google Art Project.jpg :

title: A Seascape
description: A nocturnal Channel scene with moonlight breaking through cloud over distant sailing vessels. Made during Monet's formative summers on the Normandy coast, the picture follows the marines of Boudin and Jongkind, with feathery brushwork capturing reflected silver on dark water.
credit: IAGkIdZveew5CQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Monet w 42
description: An early landscape from Monet's mid-twenties, when he was painting outdoors in the forest of Fontainebleau and along the Norman coast. The dark tonal range and tighter handling reflect his Barbizon-school roots before the lighter Impressionist palette emerged.

title: Prayer in the Desert
description: An Albanian-Ottoman irregular has dismounted from his caravan and laid a cape on the sand, prostrating himself toward Mecca in the desert's emptiness. Gérôme isolates the figure against vast horizon, a compositional habit that recurs throughout his prayer pictures.

title: Preparation for the exam
description: An early academic study from Repin's student years, depicting young men absorbed in their books before an examination. The careful tonal modelling and observed gestures already show the eye for everyday human drama that would shape his later realist canvases.

title: Romaine Lacaux
description: A poised portrait of a Parisian ceramic-maker's young daughter, seated in a stiff white dress with a flower in her hand. Among Renoir's earliest signed works, it still carries the smooth finish of academic portraiture but already shows his sensitivity to reflected light on translucent fabric.

title: Spring Flowers
description: Peonies, hydrangeas, and lilacs spill from basket and pot against a darkened ground, painted with an almost botanical attentiveness. An early still life, it shows the young Monet weighing studio precision against the looser, light-driven handling that would soon define his work.

title: Symphony in White
description: Whistler's full-length portrait of his model and lover Joanna Hiffernan in a white muslin dress, holding a lily. Conceived as a study in tonal harmony rather than narrative, the picture became a touchstone for the Aesthetic movement's pursuit of art for art's sake.
credit: Whistler Symphony in White no 2 (The Little White Girl)

title: The Battle of the Kearsarge and the Alabama
description: The Confederate raider Alabama lists and burns under fire from the Union sloop Kearsarge off Cherbourg, smoke staining a blue Channel sea. Manet, who was reportedly close enough to watch French pilot boats return from the engagement, treats this American Civil War moment with a high horizon and broadly brushed water that anticipates marine Impressionism.
credit: 1. From en.wiki 2. The Philadelphia Museum of Art

title: The Dead Christ with Angels
description: Two angels support the slumped body of the dead Christ, his pallid torso lit against shadow. Manet borrows a traditional Lamentation type but flattens modeling and exposes brushwork in a way that scandalized the 1864 Salon, where critics also faulted the placement of the side wound on the wrong flank.

title: The Pointe de la Hève
description: An early Channel-coast view of the headland at Sainte-Adresse, painted when Monet was still working through the lessons of Boudin and the Barbizon school. Wide sky and tide-flat foreground show his lifelong fascination with the meeting of sea and weather.

title: A Woman Seated beside a Vase of Flowers
description: Madame Valpinçon glances aside while a riotous bouquet dominates the canvas, the figure pushed to the edge in a deliberate inversion of conventional portrait hierarchy. Degas treats the flowers with thick impasto, the sitter with restrained drawing-led brushwork.

title: Arnaut fumant
description: A standing Albanian irregular smokes a long-stemmed pipe, his costume bristling with daggers and embroidered braid. Gérôme treats the bashi-bazouk as a costume study, the kind of single-figure ethnographic exercise he repeated through the 1860s.

title: Bazille and Camille
description: A figure study made for Monet's lost large-scale Dejeuner sur l'herbe, showing the painter Frederic Bazille and his future wife Camille Doncieux among forest light. The brisk plein-air handling already announces the broken touch that would define Impressionism.

title: Destruction of Leviathan
description: God smites the sea-monster Leviathan with a sword, the beast writhing in the deep amid lightning-lit clouds. Doré's wood-engraved illustration appeared in his folio English Bible, blending Romantic sublimity with the dense cross-hatching that defined his commercial print style.

title: Evening Prayer
description: Worshippers prostrate themselves on the flat roof of a Cairo mosque as the day's last light slants across the architecture. The picture belongs to Gérôme's prayer series of the 1860s, in which he applied his polished panel technique to ethnographic studies gathered on his Egyptian journeys.

title: Gustave Courbet 029
description: A nude study in Courbet's signature register, modelled with the dense, kneaded paint and warm flesh tones of his mid-1860s work. The treatment privileges the tactile reality of the body over idealised form, characteristic of the Realist's frank handling of the figure.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Still Life with a Carafe, Flowers and Fruit
description: A glass carafe, scattered fruit and a small bouquet are composed on a dark cloth in disciplined Salon order. Among Fantin-Latour's earliest mature still lifes, it shows the precise tonal modelling that drew Whistler's circle to his work.
credit: PgHDLISlX3RxkA at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Artist in His Studio
description: An oil sketch on wood for an unrealised group portrait, showing Whistler at his easel with the models Joanna Hiffernan and Fumette behind him. The thinly brushed greys and creams demonstrate the tonal economy he was beginning to formulate as a programme.

title: The Muezzin
description: A solitary cleric calls the faithful to prayer from a high minaret balcony, the city of Cairo spreading out far below. The vertiginous viewpoint and crisp morning light are typical of Gérôme's Egyptian travel subjects.

title: The Pointe de la Hève at Low Tide
description: Wagons and figures cross the wet sand below the headland of La Hève near Le Havre, Monet's home town. Shown at the Salon of 1865 — his official debut — the canvas owes much to the marine realism of Boudin and Courbet.

title: The Ragpicker
description: A life-size Spanish-inspired portrait of a Paris chiffonnier propped on his stick, set against a neutral grey ground. Manet borrows the format of Velázquez's philosopher portraits to dignify a marginal modern Parisian.
credit: 1. The Athenaeum: Home - info - pic 2. The Norton Simon Museum

title: Young Greeks in the Mosque
description: Boys at prayer kneel beneath the painted vaults of an Ottoman mosque, the marble floor catching reflected light. Gérôme paints the interior with archaeological care, a habit cultivated on his repeated journeys through Cairo and Istanbul.

title: Cleopatra and Caesar
description: Cleopatra steps from the unrolled carpet to confront Caesar at his writing table, the ancient anecdote staged with the polished historicism Gérôme made his trademark. Smooth porcelain finish, archaeologically researched props, and a controlled tonal palette anchor the picture in academic theatre.

title: Flowers and Fruit
description: Roses, peaches and grapes are arrayed on a plain tabletop against a neutral ground. Fantin-Latour's still lifes, prized in England via his dealer Edwin Edwards, distil the Dutch tradition into quiet, almost photographic studies of bloom and bloom-bearing skin.
credit: TwGqoeTbR6edeQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Garden at Sainte-Adresse
description: An ornamental garden at the Norman seaside resort where Monet's aunt and family summered. The bright flower beds, brisk handling and high horizon look forward to the celebrated Terrace at Sainte-Adresse painted the following summer at the same property.

title: Gustave Doré - The Holy Bible - Plate I
dateCreated: The first edition was 1866. This edition is probably from about that time - may well be a first edition - but in any case is certainly not later than 1900 or so.
description: The frontispiece to Doré's 1866 illustrated Bible: humans and a tigress alike struggle to save their young as the floodwaters rise. Engraved in dense black-and-white from Doré's design, the plate established the visual template for the Deluge in popular imagination for generations.
credit: The Holy Bible: Containing the Old and New Testiments, According to the Authorised Version. With Illusrations by Gustave Doré. ( Cassell / Company , Limited: London, Paris & Melbourne)

title: In the Forest of Fontainebleau
description: Figures rest in the dappled clearing of the Forest of Fontainebleau, the wooded preserve south of Paris that drew the Barbizon and early Impressionist painters. Renoir's youthful palette is still rooted in earthy Courbet-like tonality before his shift to brighter spectral colour.

title: Jo
description: A portrait of Joanna Hiffernan, model and lover of Whistler, gazing into a hand mirror as she draws her copper-red hair through her fingers. Courbet painted at least four versions, the dense impasto and earth-toned palette characteristic of his Realist studio practice.
credit: 6wGkbiqb-8Vy9A at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Odalisque reading
description: A reclining harem figure pauses over a book, her bared shoulders and embroidered cushions rendered with the polished surfaces and erotic restraint Hayez brought to his Orientalist subjects. The smooth modelling and warm half-light reflect his long career as Milan's leading Romantic portraitist.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Roland
description: A wood engraving from Quatrelles' Légende de Croquemitaine, depicting the Carolingian hero Roland in full armour. Doré's chivalric illustration trades on the same dense crosshatching and theatrical lighting that made his Don Quixote and Bible plates ubiquitous in nineteenth-century print culture.
credit: https://archive.org/details/daysofchivalryor00quat/page/136/mode/2up Days of Chivalry https://archive.org/details/AI156/page/n101/mode/2up Sagrada Biblia T1 https://archive.org/details/AI159/page/n331/mode/2up Sagrada Biblia T4

title: Spring Bouquet
description: An early still life of garden flowers crowded into a white porcelain vase, painted under the influence of Courbet and Monet. The dense, dark ground and weighty paint surface show Renoir before his Impressionist palette had fully lightened.

title: The Sleepers
description: Two nude women lie entwined in dishevelled bedding, jewellery and a broken pearl strand scattered on the sheet. Commissioned by the Turkish diplomat Khalil-Bey, the canvas treats lesbian love with a frank realism that scandalised the Second Empire.

title: The Vision of The Valley of The Dry Bones
description: An engraving from Dore's illustrated Bible, depicting Ezekiel's vision in which scattered bones reanimate as a vast army. The artist exploits dense crosshatching and dramatic chiaroscuro to give the desolate plain a haunted, otherworldly atmosphere.

title: Valparaiso Harbor
description: Made during Whistler's eight-month visit to Chile, where he had travelled to observe the Spanish bombardment of the port. Warships ride on a flat blue bay beneath a softly graded sky, a dusk study that anticipates his Thames Nocturnes by a decade.
credit: SAHIU1kIf-Bfhw at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Woman with a Parrot
description: A reclining nude exhibited at the Salon of 1866, where Courbet's frank naturalism, tousled hair and unidealised flesh, scandalised critics expecting an academic Venus. The bird perched on her outstretched hand provides the slim mythological pretext. Now in the Metropolitan Museum of Art.
credit: Metropolitan Museum of Art, online collection (accession number 29.100.57)

title: Adolphe Monet in the Garden of Le Coteau at Sainte-Adresse
description: The painter's father sits in a straw hat among flowering plants in the family's seaside garden at Sainte-Adresse. Painted during the same productive Norman summer as the Regatta, the picture combines a tender domestic portrait with Monet's emerging interest in dappled outdoor light.

title: Garden of the Princess
description: From a high window of the Louvre, Monet looked across the Pont des Arts to the dome of the Institut and the formal parterre of the Garden of the Princess. One of three Paris views painted from the same elevated vantage in 1867, it shows top-hatted strollers and carriages reduced to crisp coloured marks.
credit: 1. repro from art book 2. posted to Flickr as monet_garden_princess_1867 , by Flickr user ErgsArt

title: Idylls of the King 3
description: A wood-engraved illustration to Tennyson's Arthurian cycle, showing Edyrn travelling to Arthur's court with his lady and dwarf. Doré builds the scene from his characteristic stagecraft of looming forest and theatrical light, the dense black tonalities suited to the poem's medieval atmosphere.
credit: Enid , by Lord Alfred Tennyson . London: Edward Moxon & Co., 1868.

title: Jeanne-Marguerite Lecadre in the Garden Sainte-Adresse
description: Jeanne-Marguerite Lecadre, a relative, stands among flowering beds in the family garden at Sainte-Adresse near Le Havre. The picture's bright local colour and crisp shadows show Monet absorbing the lessons of Manet and Japanese prints.

title: Jerusalem
description: Now at the Musée d'Orsay, the canvas pictures the three crosses of Calvary as long shadows cast across Jerusalem at the moment of Christ's death. Gérôme adopts a striking near-aerial viewpoint, abstracting the Crucifixion to silhouette and shadow.

title: Little Red Riding Hood
description: From Doré's celebrated illustrations to Perrault's Contes, the wood engraving stages the child's fateful meeting with the wolf among forest shadows. Doré pioneered cinematic tonal staging in printed fairy tales, and these designs shaped the visual imagination of the genre across Europe.

title: On the Desert
description: Commissioned by Baltimore collector William T. Walters, the small panel pictures a Nubian handler walking two slim sighthounds through a windswept desert. The trim silhouettes suggest Pharaoh hounds, a subject Gérôme studied during his Egyptian travels of the 1860s.
credit: Walters Art Museum : Home page Info about artwork

title: Pierre-Auguste Renoir - Frédéric Bazille
description: Renoir portrays his fellow Impressionist Frédéric Bazille at work before an easel, brush poised over a still life of a heron. The picture is a touching record of the early Batignolles circle, painted shortly before Bazille's death in the Franco-Prussian War.
credit: Renoir, peintre du bonheur : 1841-1919 , de Gilles Néret, Köln, Taschen, 2001, p. 14. ISBN 9783822857410

title: Portrait of Édouard Manet
description: Manet stands three-quarter length in dark coat and top hat, gloves and cane in hand, framed against an austere studio ground. Fantin-Latour's portrait, the basis for his role in the group canvas Hommage à Manet, presents the Impressionist forerunner with the dandified self-possession his contemporaries described.

title: Promenade sur la falaise de Sainte-Adresse
description: Strolling figures top a grassy clifftop above the Channel near Le Havre, with sailing vessels scattered across a breezy sea. Painted at his family's seaside resort, the picture marks Monet's emergence from Boudin's tutelage toward his own brisk plein-air idiom.

title: Quai du Louvre
description: An early Parisian cityscape, painted from a balcony of the Louvre overlooking the Seine. Bustling figures, carriages, and the dome of the Pantheon are rendered with the high vantage and bright daylight that anticipate Monet's mature Impressionist manner.

title: Regatta at Sainte-Adresse
description: Sailboats and onlookers crowd the beach at Sainte-Adresse, the Norman resort where Monet spent the summer with relatives. The crisp horizontals and bright local colour are still indebted to Boudin and Jongkind, but already announce the open-air manner that Impressionism would soon codify.

title: Self-portrait
description: The artist meets the viewer's eye in a fur-collared coat, painted with the meticulous, hard-edged detail he championed. Hunt was a founding member of the Pre-Raphaelite Brotherhood, and the picture's sober concentration befits his role as its theoretical anchor.

title: Street in Sainte-Adresse
description: An early view of the seaside resort north of Le Havre, where Monet stayed with his aunt during a formative summer. Architecture is still drawn with relatively firm contour, but the bright Norman light and high-keyed palette already point toward Impressionist practice.

title: The Abduction
description: An early figural composition treating the rape of Persephone by Pluto in dark, churning paint and a fevered ochre-and-black palette. Cézanne dedicated the canvas to his friend Émile Zola; it dates from the violent, romantic phase that preceded his Impressionist conversion at Pontoise.

title: The Destruction of the Temple of Jerusalem
description: Hayez stages the Roman sack of Herod's Temple as operatic Romantic history painting: collapsing columns, fleeing priests and a smoke-darkened sky. The vast canvas, now in the Gallerie dell'Accademia, was conceived as a meditation on tyranny that resonated with Italian audiences under Austrian rule.

title: The Horse Market
description: A bustling North African market scene rendered with Gérôme's characteristic ethnographic precision: tethered Arabian horses, robed traders, and sun-bleached architecture under a hot, even light. The picture belongs to the Haggin Museum's deep Gérôme holding, assembled by California financier James Ben Ali Haggin.

title: The kill of deer
description: A vast hunting picture, over five metres wide, in which hounds bring down a stag in deep snow under the eye of a mounted huntsman. Courbet draws on his Franche-Comté upbringing and the Flemish hunt tradition, scaling a popular subject up to the format of history painting in keeping with his Realist programme.

provenance.collection: Musée des Beaux-Arts et d'Archéologie de Besançon
provenance.location: Musée des Beaux-Arts et d'Archéologie de Besançon

title: Афганец
description: Victorious Central Asian troops stand among severed heads and abandoned weapons after the action, the celebration unsettling rather than triumphant. Paired with 'After a failure', the picture insists that battlefield outcomes share a single moral landscape of slaughter.

title: Artists Sketching in the White Mountains
description: Three painters perch on a New Hampshire ridge, easels braced against the wind as they survey the valley below. The wry small canvas comments on the Hudson River School fashion for plein-air landscape, with Homer himself among the figures.
credit: 1. Unknown source Unknown source 2. antiquesandfineart.com 3. Portland Museum of Art

title: Bashi-Bazouk
description: A studio portrait of an Ottoman irregular soldier, painted from a model in Paris using costumes Gérôme had collected on his travels. Now in the Metropolitan Museum, the canvas is admired for the embroidered silk turban and the jewel-bright contrast against a dark ground.

title: In the Summer
description: A dark-eyed young woman sits outdoors in a loose white blouse, her hair undone — the painter's model and lover Lise Tréhot cast as a summer bohémienne. The picture predates Renoir's full Impressionist break and still owes a debt to Courbet's full-bodied realism.

title: Interior
description: A lamp-lit domestic interior from Monet's youth, with figures gathered after a meal. Indoor subjects are unusual in his work; here the warm artificial light and dark-keyed palette betray a debt to the realism of Manet and Bazille more than to his later high-colour outdoor practice.

title: La Siesta, Memory of Spain
description: A recollection of Doré's Spanish travels, figures resting under midday heat in a sun-bleached landscape. Better known as a wood-engraver, Doré painted ambitious oils late in his career, here applying the dramatic chiaroscuro of his book illustrations to a Romantic genre subject.
credit: GwHIUBydu4m7Ig at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Monet w 106
description: A coastal scene from Monet's formative years on the Channel, when he was working alongside Boudin and Jongkind. The handling is broader than academic landscape of the period and already shows his interest in transient light over fixed topography.

title: Monet W108
description: A tender domestic study of the artist's infant son Jean asleep in his cradle, dating from the precarious early years of Monet's family life. The handling is broad and affectionate, sheets and shadow simplified into planes of cool grey.

title: The Birthday
description: Hunt's young wife Edith is portrayed in profile against a deep ground, adorned with elaborate jewellery and patterned fabrics gathered during the painter's eastern travels. The exacting detail of ornament shows his characteristic Pre-Raphaelite finish in an intimate domestic register.

title: The Execution of Marshal Ney
description: A cold December morning at the Luxembourg gardens: Napoleon's marshal lies face down in the mud as the firing squad turns away, posters of his condemnation peeling on the wall behind. Gérôme uses an austere, almost reportorial composition to deliver a quiet indictment of Bourbon vengeance.

title: The Luncheon
description: An ambitious early figure piece showing Monet's companion Camille and their son Jean at a sunlit table, with a visitor lingering at the garden's edge. Submitted to and rejected by the 1870 Salon, it announces the everyday subjects that would define Impressionism.

title: The Magpie
description: A single black bird perches on a wattle gate in a snowbound Norman garden, casting a faint blue shadow on the drift. One of Monet's earliest essays in coloured shadow, the picture was rejected by the 1869 Salon and now hangs in the Musée d'Orsay.
credit: rQGnadHwK8lSmg at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Porte d'Amont
description: The Upstream Gate, Etretat

title: The Sea at Le Havre
description: A grey Channel seascape painted from the harbour Monet had known since childhood, with sailing vessels riding a low swell beneath broken cloud. The high horizon and economical palette anticipate the marines of the following decade.
credit: Wikipedia Loves Art at the Carnegie Museum of Art This photo of item # 53.22 at the Carnegie Museum of Art was contributed under the team name " dewey_decimals " as part of the Wikipedia Loves Art project in February 2009. Carnegie Museum of Art The original photograph on Flickr was taken by Moira Burke — please add a comment to the original Flickr page whenever a use has been made on Wikipedia or another project. Project galleries on Flickr: this institution , this team

title: The Triumph of Christianity over Paganism
description: A vast allegorical canvas in which a cross-bearing Christ, ringed by sword-wielding angels, sweeps above the toppling deities of antiquity. Dore stages the contest as a vertiginous airborne procession, exploiting the canvas's monumental scale to push the pagan host into chaotic free-fall below.

title: A Garden gate in Chuguchak
description: A Garden gate in Chuguchak

title: At the Races in the Countryside
description: A landau parked at a country racecourse, the Valpinçon family attending to their newborn son under the shade of a parasol. Degas crops the carriage and horses with photographic abruptness, turning a polite outing into a study of asymmetric modern composition.
credit: fAG5E-YLY4Q57g at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Bashi-Bazouk
description: A study of an Ottoman irregular soldier, painted after Gérôme's travels through Egypt and the Levant. The figure's embroidered silks and weaponry are rendered with the polished, almost photographic finish that defined French academic Orientalism, set against a neutral studio ground that isolates costume and pose.
credit: Metropolitan Museum of Art, online collection (The Met object ID 440723)

title: Boote
description: A study of moored rowing boats, the kind of riverside motif Monet repeatedly mined along the Seine. Water is rendered in horizontal slabs of broken colour, the hulls anchoring an otherwise nearly abstract surface.

title: Dervishes in Festive Attire
description: Two dervishes pose in vivid ceremonial garments studded with shells, beads, and embroidery. The careful ethnographic detail reflects Vereshchagin's Central Asian travels and his reportorial approach to Russian Orientalist subjects.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Gur Emir mausoleum. Samarkand
description: Gur Emir mausoleum. Samarkand

title: Inside the Tent of a Rich Kirghiz
description: Внутренность юрты богатого киргиза

title: Long Branch
description: Two fashionable women in bustled dresses pause on a cliff at the Jersey Shore resort, parasols snapping in the Atlantic wind. Homer's bright, almost Impressionist palette captures the new postwar leisure culture with magazine-illustrator clarity.
credit: 1. The AMICA Library 2. Museum of Fine Arts, Boston, online database

title: Nomadic road in the Alatau Mountains
description: Nomadic road in the Alatau Mountains

title: On the Beach
description: Children and bathers cluster on a sunlit Atlantic strand, the composition organized in horizontal bands of sand, surf and sky. Homer's interest in the new American leisure shore renders the figures with quick, illustrational economy.

title: Ruins of Chinese sanctuary. Ak-Kent
description: Ruins of Chinese sanctuary. Ak-Kent

title: Self-Portrait at the Age of 78
description: A late self-portrait at age seventy-eight, the Milanese master appraising himself with calm, unflattering directness. The restrained palette and tightly painted head reflect the academic rigour Hayez upheld at the Brera even as Romanticism gave way to verismo.

title: Still Life with Flowers and Fruit
description: An early still life arranging garden blooms alongside ripe pears and grapes on a draped surface. Painted before Monet committed fully to plein-air landscape, the canvas shows him testing dense impasto and saturated colour against the conventions of Salon still-life painting.
credit: cAEQBxyETd21jQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Still Life With Watermelon
description: An early student work made during Chase's Indianapolis years, the cut watermelon is laid out with the dark ground and dramatic highlights of the Munich-school tradition he would soon study at first hand. The picture predates his shift toward looser, brighter American Impressionism.

title: The Bridge at Bougival
description: Painted during Monet's lean Bougival period alongside Renoir, the composition hinges on the wooden river bridge cutting across the Seine. The handling is still relatively tight, but already shows the rapid notation of reflections that would soon define Impressionism.

title: The Calm Sea
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: The Frog Pond
description: Bathers and rowboats cluster around the floating café on the Seine at Bougival, the spot where Renoir and Monet spent the summer of 1869 painting side by side. Broken touches of opaque colour on the water mark one of the foundational moments of Impressionism.
credit: HgHrOyFUfcFtLw at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Frog Pond
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: The Sailboat
description: The Sailboat (Seascape)

title: The Seine at Bougival
description: Painted from the riverbank at the village that drew Monet, Renoir and Pissarro alike, the scene catches a row of moored boats and the mirror of the Seine. Quick, comma-shaped strokes record passing reflections rather than fixed contours.

title: The Wave
description: One of a series of stormy seascapes Courbet painted at Étretat in 1869, the breaking wave thickly built up with the palette knife. He treated water as solid matter, layering grey-green pigment against a leaden sky to register the weight rather than the glitter of the Channel.

title: The Wave
description: A breaking wave rears in heavy impasto under a brooding Channel sky, the horizon pushed high and the surf laid on with palette knife. Courbet painted dozens of these studies on the Normandy coast at Étretat in 1869, treating seascape as a study of matter rather than picturesque incident.

title: Афганец
description: A Kyrgyz family is on the move across the steppe with camels, horses, and bundled yurt poles, the procession strung out under a wide sky. Vereshchagin records the seasonal migration with documentary attention to harness, load, and the choreography of nomadic transport.
