title: Дети племени солонов
description: Дети племени солонов

title: Киргизские кибитки на реке Чу
description: Киргизские кибитки на реке Чу

title: Китайская палатка
description: Китайская палатка

title: Мавзолей Шах-и-Зинда в Самарканде
description: Мавзолей Шах-и-Зинда в Самарканде

title: Медресе Шир-Дор на площади Регистан в Самарканде
credit: former image source picture.art-catalog.ru ; current image source [1]

title: Проход Барскаун
description: Проход Барскаун

title: Barge Haulers on the Volga
description: Eleven harnessed labourers heave a barge upstream across a vast, sun-bleached Volga sandbar, their faces individuated portraits of exhaustion and stoicism. Repin's early masterpiece announced the social conscience of the Peredvizhniki, marrying academic figure construction to a panoramic Russian landscape.

title: Chill October
description: An autumnal Perthshire backwater under leaden skies, painted from a sandbank on the Tay near Murthly. Millais himself called this his first true landscape, and its damp, unromantic mood anticipates the meditative late landscapes that occupied his final decades.

title: Eagle Head
description: Three young women emerge from the surf onto the Cape Ann shore, their wet bathing dresses clinging in a way that scandalized contemporary critics. Homer treats the scene as a study of weight, wind and waterlogged fabric rather than narrative incident.
credit: Metropolitan Museum of Art, online collection (The Met object ID 20011474)

title: en
description: Boats crowd the harbour mouth of the Norman resort of Trouville, where Monet honeymooned with Camille in the summer of 1870. The pictures from that holiday — sand still embedded in some of the canvases — were the last he made before fleeing the war to London.

title: Hôtel des Roches Noires
description: Holiday-makers parade beneath a row of fluttering tricolours along the boardwalk of Trouville's grandest hotel. Painted on the eve of the Franco-Prussian War, the picture is a brisk exercise in plein-air reportage, with sand still trapped in the paint surface.
credit: lAEswCWla3bwcw at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Moorish Bath
description: A bather is attended by a dark-skinned servant in the steam of a hammam, the marble basin and brass ewer rendered with Gérôme's ethnographic precision. The subject became a recurring vehicle for him, combining Orientalist setting with academic study of the female nude.

title: Riders Crossing The Desert
description: A small file of riders pushes across the open Egyptian sand under a hard noon sun, the line of barren hills receding behind them. The intimate panel format and tightly drawn silhouettes are characteristic of Gérôme's traveller scenes from the early 1870s.

title: Still Life with Hummingbird
description: A hummingbird hovers above a cluster of cut flowers and fruit on a dark ground. The dense Munich-trained shadows and tightly observed wings reflect Chase's early academic still-life manner before his later turn to airy Impressionist plein-airs.
credit: IAHM9vQlxjlkuQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Beach at Trouville
description: Painted on the Normandy coast during Monet's honeymoon summer, the canvas shows two seated women — likely Camille Monet and a companion — under parasols on the windswept sand. Grains of beach are embedded in the paint, a documentary trace of the open-air method.

title: The boardwalk on the beach at Trouville
description: A breezy Norman seaside view from Monet's 1870 Trouville stay with Camille, days before the Franco-Prussian War sent him into London exile. Sand grains are reportedly embedded in the paint, evidence of true plein-air execution.

title: The Harem in the Kiosk
description: A Black eunuch stands sentinel at the lattice of a waterfront kiosk while veiled women look out toward the Bosphorus. Gérôme's love of Ottoman tilework and his crisp architectural perspective shape the picture's mood of guarded retreat.

title: The Orchestra at the Opera
description: A view of the Paris Opera pit dominated by the bassoonist Désiré Dihau, with the legs and tutus of dancers cropped above the proscenium. Degas pioneers the abrupt foreground/background split and the off-axis viewpoint that would shape his ballet pictures for the next two decades.
credit: Google Art Project: Home - pic Maximum resolution.

title: The Shadow of Death
description: A Pre-Raphaelite vision of Christ as a young carpenter stretching after labor, his outflung arms casting a cruciform shadow across the workshop wall. Hunt painted the scene in Jerusalem to ground every tool, shaving and textile in archaeological accuracy.
credit: Originally from en.wikipedia ; description page is/was here . 2007-01-04 (original upload date). Original uploader was Paul Barlow at en.wikipedia

title: Политики в опиумной лавочке. Ташкент
description: Polititians in opium shop. Tashkent

title: A rich Kyrgyz hunter with a falcon
Apotheosis of war
description: A pyramid of human skulls rises on a scorched plain before a ruined city, crows wheeling overhead. Vereshchagin, a veteran of the Russian campaigns in Turkestan, dedicated the canvas "to all conquerors, past, present and to come"; it is the closing image of his Turkestan series.

title: By the Fortress Wall. "Let Them Enter
description: By the Fortress Wall. "Let Them Enter" (1871) by Vasili Vereshchagin. Painting commemorating the capture of Samarkand (in present-day Uzbekistan ) by Russian Imperial troops.

title: Houses by the Bank of the River
description: Painted during Monet's Dutch sojourn after the Franco-Prussian War, the canvas shows a row of green-shuttered houses reflected in the Zaan. Long horizontal brushstrokes mirror the calm of the Holland waterways that fascinated him.

title: In the Alpine High Valley
description: A small group winds along a high alpine track with the Wendelstein massif rising behind them. Late in his career Spitzweg loosened his touch under Barbizon influence, and the airy mountain palette here is closer to plein-air observation than to his earlier studio finish.
credit: ywEgUz_pmN9_IA at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: LE PORT DE ZAANDAM
description: The Dutch industrial port at Zaandam painted during Monet's exile from the Franco-Prussian War, with windmills and timber yards reflected in the Zaan. The shifting greys and broken brushwork mark his decisive move toward fully Impressionist landscape after the London canvases.

title: Meditation
description: Camille Doncieux, the painter's first wife, reclining on a sofa in quiet thought. The intimate domestic subject and muted tonal range belong to Monet's early-1870s figure painting, before his work shifted decisively outdoors toward pure landscape and atmospheric study.

title: Nocturne: Grey and Gold
description: Westminster Bridge and the Thames vanish into a haze of grey-blue twilight, pierced by scattered gold lamplight. The painting belongs to Whistler's Nocturne series, in which musical titles signalled the priority of tonal harmony over narrative.

title: Portrait of the Artist's Mother
description: Anna McNeill Whistler sits in profile in a London interior, bonneted and dressed in black against a pale grey wall hung with one of her son's etchings. The strict horizontal-vertical geometry and muted palette underscore Whistler's framing of a familial portrait as a tonal arrangement.

title: Prayer in the Mosque Alternative title
description: Worshippers prostrate themselves in long rows beneath the soaring arches of Cairo's Mosque of Amr, sunlight slanting across the columns and prayer mats. Gérôme's enamel-smooth Orientalism rests on direct study of Egyptian architecture from his repeated travels in the country.

title: Still life with fruit
description: Grapes, peaches and other fruit are heaped on a draped tabletop in a darkened interior. The Munich-school chiaroscuro and confident, loaded brushwork mark the young Chase's training in 1870s Bavaria.

title: The Champion Single Sculls
description: Eakins's friend Max Schmitt rests on his oars on the Schuylkill River at twilight, with the painter himself rowing in the middle distance. The composition is built from rigorous perspective studies of bridges and ripples, reflecting Eakins's Philadelphia commitment to anatomical and optical truth.

title: The Slave Market
description: A buyer inspects the teeth of a stripped female captive in a North African market, surrounded by impassive merchants. Now at the Clark, the work is one of several slave-market scenes Gérôme staged across both ancient Roman and contemporary Ottoman settings.
credit: CQHyY347a4a2Vw at Google Cultural Institute , zoom level maximum

title: The Thames below Westminster
description: A view across the Thames toward the Houses of Parliament and Westminster Bridge, dissolved in river mist. Painted during Monet's wartime exile in London, the canvas anticipates by three decades his later Thames series, already setting silhouetted architecture against a pearly, fog-bound sky.

title: The Zaan at Zaandam
description: Refugee from the Franco-Prussian war, Monet found in the Dutch waterways at Zaandam a flat horizon punctuated by sails and clapboard houses. Cool blues and ochre dominate the four-month Holland sojourn, in which he painted some two dozen views along the Zaan.

title: Variations in Pink and Grey: Chelsea
description: A Thames-side view from the years when Whistler lived on Lindsey Row and was reformulating landscape as colour harmony. Fishermen and barge-poles are reduced to slim vertical accents on a horizontal field of pearly grey water and dusty pink shore.

title: Windmill and Boats near Zaandam
description: Painted during Monet's residence in the Dutch town of Zaandam, the canvas registers a working waterway under low northern light, with a windmill anchoring the moored barges. Quick, broken touches across the water anticipate the Impressionist breakthroughs of the next few years.

title: Windmills Near Zaandam
description: A girl walks the dyke path between Oostzaan and Zaandam, windmill sails turning over flat polder land. Painted during Monet's 1871 Dutch sojourn after his English exile, the picture's narrow tonal range catches a typical overcast Holland sky.
credit: Walters Art Museum : Home page Info about artwork

title: “And the Prayer of Faith Shall Save the Sick”
description: A late biblical genre piece taking its title from the Epistle of James, set within a sunlit Eastern interior. Lewis casts the scriptural episode in the Cairene domestic vocabulary he had perfected over decades. Mashrabiya light falls across patterned cushions and the bedside attendants in saturated colour.
credit: 1gEp7ztczN5A8w at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Argenteuil
description: Sailboats lie along the bank of the Seine at Argenteuil, the suburban boating town where Monet settled after the Franco-Prussian War. Quick comma-strokes break the water and sky into the broken, prismatic colour that became the signature of early Impressionism.

title: Argenteuil
description: A view of the Seine at Argenteuil, the suburban boating town where Monet settled after the Franco-Prussian War. Sailboats and reflections on calm water became his central subject here, painted from a floating studio he converted from a small skiff.

title: Arrangement in Gray: Portrait of the Painter
description: A self-portrait built from narrow tonal intervals of grey, the painter emerging only as a darkened silhouette and pale face from a near-uniform field. The careful tuning of values exemplifies Whistler's musical analogies for picture-making.

title: Hope
description: An allegorical female nude personifying Hope sits on a barren battlefield, holding an olive branch as ruins and grave markers recede behind her. Painted in the aftermath of the Franco-Prussian War, the muted palette and frieze-like simplicity exemplify Puvis de Chavannes's pale, fresco-inspired symbolism.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Impression
description: The harbour of Le Havre at dawn dissolves into vapour, masts and cranes ghosted behind an orange sun whose reflections crackle across the water. Exhibited at the first independent show of 1874, its title supplied a hostile critic with the term 'Impressionism' that would name the movement.

title: Monet w 208
description: Painted in the early 1870s as Monet established the visual language of Impressionism, the picture trades finish for immediacy, applying paint in mosaic-like touches. Works of these years preceded and prepared the 1874 group exhibition that gave the movement its name.

title: Monet w 235
description: A river or harbour subject from 1872, the year of the famous Impression, Sunrise, sharing its concern with reflections, vapour, and the dissolution of form on water. The vibrating shorthand of small coloured strokes marks Monet's break with conventional finish.

title: Monet w199
description: Springtime at Argenteuil, painted soon after Monet moved downriver from Paris in 1871. Blossoming trees and sunlit grass are caught in flickering strokes, the figures held lightly within an atmosphere of dappled spring light.

title: Nocturne in Blue and Gold: Old Battersea Bridge
description: Old Battersea Bridge looms in silhouette over the Thames at dusk, its piers stretched into a near-Japanese vertical. Whistler reduced river, sky, and timber to a few veils of blue and gold, treating the cityscape as an arrangement of tones in line with his Aesthetic credo.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: On the Beach -- Two Are Company
description: A wood engraving after Winslow Homer published in Harper's Weekly, depicting a flirtation interrupted on a New England beach. Homer's Reconstruction-era illustrations chronicled American leisure with a wry observational eye.

title: Pollice Verso
description: A staged Roman amphitheatre scene in which a victorious mirmillo stands over his fallen opponent and looks up at the Vestals' thumbs-down verdict. Gérôme drew on his own collection of antique armour for the gladiators' helmets and helped fix Hollywood's mental image of the arena.

title: Pont-Neuf
description: Pedestrians and carriages cross Paris's oldest bridge under a clear summer sky, the Seine glittering between embankments. Renoir's brother Edmond reportedly delayed passersby in conversation so the painter could capture them in rapid, sun-drenched touches.

title: Presentation of the trophies
description: Part of the Turkestan series, the painting shows the Emir of Bukhara receiving a heap of severed Russian heads as battle trophies in a tiled courtyard. Vereshchagin's archaeological precision in the architecture sharpens, rather than softens, the indictment of war.

title: Regattas at Argenteuil
description: Sails crowd the Seine basin at Argenteuil, doubled in vivid downward strokes that read almost as flames on the water. The painting's loose vertical reflections were among the works that helped define the Impressionist handling of moving light on water.

title: Rider and his Steed in the Desert
description: A horseman rests against the flank of his exhausted mount in the brassy heat of the desert afternoon, barren hills behind. Gérôme exploits a low horizon and brushed-smooth surface to evoke the still, shimmering air of his North African travels.

title: Selling a child-slave
description: A rich Turkish man examines a naked boy, before buying him.

title: Springtime
description: A young woman, almost certainly Camille, sits reading in dappled shade beneath flowering trees. Pink-white blossoms and green foliage are dissolved into a single shimmering screen of broken touch, an early statement of Monet's plein-air figure painting.
credit: pAEsabNHoa1naA at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Still Life
description: An early domestic still life from Monet's Argenteuil years, when he briefly turned from landscape to tabletop subjects. A lacquered tray, porcelain cups and a polished kettle are arranged with Japoniste restraint, the gleaming surfaces reflecting his admiration for Manet and for Edo lacquerware.

title: The Highway Bridge under repair
description: The road bridge at Argenteuil propped on temporary timber scaffolding after wartime damage. Monet treats the construction site as a fully serious motif, the geometry of beams and trestles balanced against the soft Seine below.

title: The Petit Bras of the Seine at Argenteuil
description: A quiet backwater of the Seine fringed by bare poplars and reflected on a glassy surface. Painted in the first weeks of Monet's settlement at Argenteuil, the canvas balances delicate atmospheric grey-blues against incipient Impressionist touch.

title: They are triumphant
description: Set in Samarkand's Registan square, a victory ceremony unfolds before the great madrasa portals while Russian soldiers' heads stand impaled on poles in the foreground. Vereshchagin pairs the splendour of Timurid tilework with documentary atrocity, a recurring strategy of his Turkestan cycle.

title: A Lady Receiving Visitors
description: A harem interior in Cairo, where a seated lady greets her guests amid mashrabiya screens and a sun-dappled mosaic floor. Lewis lived a decade in an Ottoman house in the city and made the filtered light through latticed windows his signature subject. Every textile, slipper and tile is rendered in jewel-bright watercolour clarity.
credit: 3gFFY2gmy4bgYg at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: A Walk in the Meadows at Argenteuil
description: Figures stroll through tall summer grass beside the Seine at Argenteuil, the village where Monet lived from 1871 to 1878. The high horizon and broken touch dissolve foreground wildflowers into a tapestry of pure colour, a defining experiment of his Argenteuil years.

title: Autumn on the Seine
description: Riverside trees in russet and gold are doubled in the still surface of the Seine. Painted from the bank at Argenteuil, the picture exploits Monet's preferred device of mirrored reflection, the upper and lower halves of the canvas answering one another in horizontal bands.

title: Ballettprobe
description: Dancers warming up in the rehearsal room, observed from a raked corner viewpoint that pushes the orchestra rail into the foreground. Degas favoured these unguarded moments of preparation over performance, where artificial light, pastel-keyed tones and oblique geometry could be studied at leisure.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Boys in a Dory
description: Watercolor; Drawings
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Breezing Up
description: A catboat heels in stiff wind off the Massachusetts coast, three boys and a fisherman crowded along the windward rail. Homer reworked the composition over two years, settling on the diagonal mast that turns a leisure scene into an emblem of post-Civil-War optimism.

title: Confusion of Tongues
description: An illustration to Genesis 11 from Doré's English Bible, depicting the workers scattering as the Tower of Babel founders mid-construction. Doré stages the scene with vertiginous architecture and a gulf of stormy sky, exploiting the dense black hatching of wood-engraving to dramatise divine judgement.

title: Cow Boys
description: Two young cattle drovers in straw hats lean on a rail fence, watching their herd from a sun-soaked pasture. Often catalogued as Cow Boys, the canvas typifies Homer's Gloucester-era interest in agrarian boyhood as an emblem of postwar American innocence.
credit: Ball State University Libraries: Digital Media Repository

provenance.collection: David Owsley Museum of Art Ball State University

title: Dad's Coming!
description: A boy perched on an overturned dory watches the horizon while his mother and infant sister wait beside him on the beach. The hopeful title masks Homer's familiar coastal anxiety, as the absent father's sail is nowhere yet visible.
credit: National Gallery of Art, Washington, D. C., online collection

title: Girl in the Hammock
description: A woman reclines in a slung hammock with a book, her white dress catching dappled light through the canopy. Homer's flattened design and decorative pattern of leaves reflect his exposure to contemporary Japanese prints.

title: Houses at Argenteuil
description: Painted in the Paris suburb where Monet lived from 1871 to 1878, the heart of the early Impressionist project. A row of villas behind a flowering garden is rendered in broken touches of green, rose and violet, light flickering across stucco walls.

title: Jacob Wrestles with the Angel
description: A wood-engraved illustration from Doré's English Bible (1866), depicting Genesis 32 — Jacob struggling through the night with a divine adversary at the ford of the Jabbok. The dense black hatching and theatrical lighting are hallmarks of Doré's Romantic graphic style.

title: Masked Ball at the Opera
description: Top-hatted men press around costumed women in dominoes beneath the colonnaded foyer of the old Paris Opéra on rue Le Peletier, painted only months before the building burnt down. Manet compresses the crowd into a frieze of black coats, reading the bourgeois carnival as a study in modern social ritual.

title: Monet w 272 Apple Trees in Bloom
description: Apple trees in full blossom, their pinkish-white canopies set against fresh spring green and dabbed in short broken strokes. The subject was a favourite of the early Impressionists, who valued orchards as outdoor studios where colour and light could be studied together.

title: Mullah Rahim and Mullah Kerim on his way to the bazaar are quarreling
description: Mullah Rahim and Mullah Kerim on his way to the bazaar are quarreling

title: Naked Woman Lying on a Couch
description: An unidealised nude sprawls across a rumpled divan, her clothing tossed over the chair beside her. Painted early in Caillebotte's career, the canvas treats the female body with a candid, almost photographic objectivity that was startling to Salon-trained eyes.

title: Railroad Bridge
description: A modern iron rail bridge strides across the Seine on concrete piers, a small steam train trailing white smoke above sailing boats. Monet repeatedly painted this junction of leisure and industry during his Argenteuil years.

title: Ships in a Harbor
description: Ships in a Harbor

title: Spring
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: The Artist's Garden in Argenteuil
description: A corner of Monet's Argenteuil garden ablaze with dahlias, with the painter's house glimpsed beyond a wooden fence. The canvas — long associated with a similar work by Renoir painted alongside Monet — applies broken Impressionist touches to translate the heat of late summer.

title: The Four Leaf Clover
description: A young woman in a wide-brimmed hat searches a meadow for the proverbial lucky clover, her downturned head shaded by the brim. Homer turns a slight pastoral conceit into a study of summer light filtered through straw.
credit: 1./2. Bridgeman Art Library : Object 241624 3./4. Detroit Institute of Arts

title: The Grey Eminence
description: Capuchin friar François Leclerc du Tremblay descends a staircase at the Palais-Cardinal, courtiers bowing to the grey habit while ignoring the man inside it. Gérôme stages the scene as silent political theatre, contrasting the friar's coarse wool against polished marble and gilded livery.
credit: Scan from the original work : Richelieu: Art and Power , 2002 exhibition catalog, edited by Hilliard Todd Goldfarb, p. 381. Montreal : Montreal Museum of Fine Arts ; Cologne : Wallraf-Richartz-Museum – Fondation Corboud ; Ghent, Belgium : Snoeck-Ducaju & Zoon. ISBN 9789053494073 .

title: The Luncheon
description: An intimate domestic interior showing the remains of a meal at the Monets' Argenteuil house, with the artist's first wife Camille and son Jean implied in the staging. Light pours across linen, glassware, and fruit in a quietly Manet-inflected still life.

title: The Railway Bridge in Argenteuil
description: The iron railway bridge at Argenteuil cuts across the Seine in flat planes of pale stone, a steam train trailing white smoke above. Monet treats modern infrastructure as a legitimate motif of plein-air painting, balancing its industrial geometry against the soft river bank.

title: Wild Poppies Near Argenteuil
description: Camille and the young Jean Monet wade through high summer grass at Argenteuil, scarlet poppies dotted across the field in dabs of vermilion. A second figure-pair higher on the slope mirrors the first, knitting two moments into one composition.

title: Парламентёры
description: Парламентёры. "Сдавайся! " — "Убирайся к чёрту!

title: Boating
description: A boater in straw hat and crisp whites holds the tiller while his companion lounges in the prow against a wall of saturated blue water. The radically cropped composition and high horizon reveal Manet's debt to Japanese prints and his summer at Argenteuil with Monet.

title: Buddhist Temple in Darjiling. Sikkim
description: Buddhist Temple in Darjiling. Sikkim.

title: Canoeists near Argenteuil
description: Sailboats and rowers drift on the Seine at Argenteuil, the Parisian suburb where Monet lived through the early 1870s and turned regatta-day leisure into a recurring subject. Quick comma-strokes describe water and rigging with equal economy.
credit: L'Évasion des Sens : MONET_Canotiers à Argenteuil

title: Embankment in Le Havre
description: A working harbour view of Monet's home port, painted the year of the first Impressionist exhibition. Masts, smokestacks and reflections dissolve into rapid, comma-like strokes, the artist treating modern dockside bustle with the same atmospheric urgency he had just brought to Impression, Sunrise.

title: Fishing boats leaving the harbor
description: Sail-rigged fishing boats slip out of Le Havre on a misty morning, the harbour walls and shipping reduced to muted silhouettes. Monet painted the port of his boyhood repeatedly in the early 1870s, and works of this kind directly preceded Impression, Sunrise of 1872.

title: Monet w 338
description: Painted during the year of the first Impressionist exhibition, this work shows Monet's mature open-air method fully formed. Pigment is laid down in distinct touches that resolve into atmosphere only at viewing distance, refusing the smooth surface prized by the Salon.

title: Moonlight
description: A study of figures on a darkened shore under a low moon, painted in oil with the heavy tonal contrasts Homer favored after the Civil War. Silvered light on water dominates the otherwise nearly silhouetted forms.
credit: 1. The Athenaeum: Home - info - pic 2. Fenimore Art Museum

title: Pont Argenteuil Monet 1
description: Sailing boats line the basin at Argenteuil beneath the highway bridge, their masts reflected in the broken blue water of the Seine. The picture belongs to the suburban riverscapes that defined the early Impressionist summers of 1872 to 1874.

title: Snow at Argenteuil
description: A muffled village street under fresh snow, part of Monet's sustained study of Argenteuil winters. The white ground is laid in with cool blues and lilacs rather than pure white, a decisive Impressionist break from academic monochrome.
credit: Google Art Project works from the Nelson-Atkins Museum of Art

title: Summer
description: Camille and Jean lie among long grass and poppies under a high summer sky. The picture belongs to a small group of figure-in-meadow compositions Monet made at Argenteuil, treating the family as colour incidents within a continuous field of warm growth.

title: The Bassin du Commerce
description: A canvas of Le Havre's commercial basin, the harbour from which Monet had launched Impression, Sunrise two years earlier. Painted with rapid broken touches, the work continues his investigation of Channel-port atmospheres, where masts, smoke and reflected light become almost interchangeable.

title: The Bridge at Argenteuil
description: The road bridge at Argenteuil, rebuilt after the Franco-Prussian War, frames moored sailboats on the Seine. Monet used the same bridge in several canvases of 1874, varying the viewpoint and weather to test how a single motif behaves under changing light.
credit: zAGt_suewfK9Qw at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Geese
description: Geese gather at a farmyard pond in this rural scene from Monet's Argenteuil years, painted at the moment Impressionism crystallised around the first group exhibition. The whitewashed wall and reflecting water carry the picture's main optical work, dappled with quick, flickering touches.
credit: 1. Unknown source Unknown source 2. Clark Art Institute

title: The North-West Passage
description: An old sea captain sits stiffly by a window while his daughter reads aloud from a logbook, a Union Jack draped across his knees. Millais distilled Britain's Arctic ambitions into domestic drama; the canvas became one of the most reproduced images of Victorian patriotic sentiment.

title: The Windmill on the Onbekende Gracht
description: A brick polder mill stands across one of Amsterdam's quieter canals, its sails set against a wind-swept Dutch sky. Monet painted it during his autumn 1874 Dutch trip, a return to the country he had first sketched while waiting out the Franco-Prussian War.
credit: 1. Bridgeman Art Library : Object 129973 2. Museum of Fine Arts, Houston, online collection

title: The Zuiderkerk
description: A view up the Groenburgwal canal toward the spire of the Zuiderkerk, painted during Monet's brief Dutch sojourn. The canvas already carries the rapid, broken brushwork of nascent Impressionism, dissolving brick facades and water into vibrating coloured strokes.

title: A Corner of the Apartment
description: An interior view of the Monet family's Argenteuil home, with young Jean standing at the end of a darkened hallway between hanging plants and curtained light. The painting marks one of Monet's rare domestic interiors, balancing silhouetted shadow against a glowing window.

title: Camille at the Loom
description: An intimate domestic scene of Monet's first wife, Camille Doncieux, seated at an embroidery frame in their Argenteuil home. The painter treats the patterned tapestry, dress, and wall hangings as a single woven field of colour, dissolving figure and interior into a continuous surface of touches.

title: Camille Monet and a Child in the Artist's Garden in Argenteuil
description: Camille Monet and a Child in the Artist's Garden in Argenteuil

title: Meadow with Poplars
description: Poplars near Argenteuil, painted in the suburban Seine landscape that nourished Monet's early Impressionist breakthrough. Vertical trunks divide the field into rhythmic intervals, a compositional device he would push further in the 1891 Poplars series.

title: Mr and Mrs Edwin Edwards
description: The English etcher and printer Edwin Edwards sits at his desk while his wife Ruth stands behind him, both rendered in Fantin's habitual sober blacks. The Edwardses were the artist's closest patrons, channelling his still lifes into the lucrative London market.

title: Nocturne
description: Part of Whistler's celebrated Nocturne series, in which the painter pared the Thames down to washes of dusky blue and pewter. The work treats landscape as a tonal arrangement, anticipating the abstract sensibilities Whistler championed in his Aesthetic writings.

title: Nocturne in Black and Gold: The Falling Rocket
description: Showers of golden sparks fall through a smoky night sky above the Cremorne Gardens fireworks. The thinly washed, near-monochrome surface so provoked John Ruskin that his attack triggered the famous 1878 libel trial.

title: Poppy Fields near Argenteuil
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Portrait of Dr. Samuel D. Gross
description: A monumental scene of surgeon Samuel Gross presiding over an operation before his medical students at Jefferson College. Eakins records the bloodied hand, the chloroformed patient and the rapt amphitheatre with uncompromising realism, anchoring American painting in the lecture-hall light of modern science.
credit: tQFcdDEg20osqg at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Red Boats
description: Sailing skiffs with vermilion hulls cluster against the willow-lined bank of the Seine at Argenteuil, where Monet kept his studio boat. The flickering reflections and high-keyed palette typify the river scenes he painted alongside Renoir and Manet during the suburb's pivotal Impressionist summers.

title: Renoirgarden
description: A woman shaded by a parasol stands amid blossoming flowerbeds, her figure dissolving into the surrounding foliage. Renoir uses fragmented, comma-like brushstrokes and unmodulated touches of pure colour to fuse figure and garden into a single shimmering field of sunlight.

title: Summer
description: A bank of red poppies climbs a sunlit slope above figures lost in the grass. The canvas is one of several Monet devoted to the motif of wildflower meadows, the bright dabs of red functioning as both botanical record and pure chromatic accent.

title: The Bridge at Argenteuil
description: A view of the road bridge at Argenteuil, the Seine town that became the Impressionist headquarters in the early 1870s. Monet records the pleasure boats and tree-lined bank with quick comma-strokes, the rebuilt iron-and-stone span symbolising the post-war modernity of suburban leisure.
credit: MNBA logo This file, part of the Collection of the Fine Arts Museum, was provided to Wikimedia Commons thanks to an agreement between the Museo Nacional de Bellas Artes and Wikimedia Argentina .

provenance.collection: National Museum of Fine Arts, Argentina
provenance.location: National Museum of Fine Arts, Argentina

title: The grand canal of Venice
description: Painted on a Venetian visit alongside Monet, the canvas captures the Grand Canal in shimmering broken touches of blue, white and ochre. Striped mooring posts cut vertical accents through the rippling water, marking Manet's most overtly Impressionist experiment.

title: The Iron Rolling Mill
description: A grimy panorama of the Königshütte rolling mill in Upper Silesia, with workers wrestling glowing iron under shafts of furnace light. Menzel spent months on site making hundreds of drawings, producing one of the first ambitious European paintings to take heavy industry as its subject.

title: The Skiff
description: Two young women row a vivid orange skiff on the Seine at Chatou, the river populated by sailboats and a distant railway bridge. Renoir's complementary contrast of cobalt water and chrome-orange hull, applied in feathered touches, captures the leisurely Impressionist suburb of the 1870s.

title: The Studio Boat
description: Monet's floating studio, a converted boat moored on the Seine that let him paint from the water itself. Manet had recorded him at work in it; here the vessel becomes the subject, half-shaded under a bank of trees.

title: The Yerres
credit: Sidney and Lois Eskenazi Museum of Art Provenance Project

title: Train in the Snow
description: A locomotive idles at the Argenteuil station, its dark bulk and red signal lamps set against snow and bare trees. Monet returned often to the new railway as a subject, treating engine smoke and winter light as kindred atmospheric phenomena; the canvas is now at the Musée Marmottan Monet, Paris.

title: View of Argenteuil - Snow
description: Snow lies along the riverbank at Argenteuil, with the white plain crossed by a lone walker and faint footprints. Monet's winter pictures from this period set warm rooftops and clothed figures against muted blue-grey snow.
credit: The Nelson-Atkins Museum of Art, Kansas City

title: Woman with a Parasol - Madame Monet and Her Son
description: Camille turns under a green parasol while young Jean stands among grasses on a windy bluff, painted from below so the figures rise into a billowing sky. The picture, now in Washington's National Gallery, is Monet's bid to handle the figure outdoors with full plein-air conviction.

title: A Parisian Lady
description: A near-life-size portrait of the actress Ellen Andrée in fashionable street dress, her black silhouette set against a flat ground in Manet's signature manner. The picture pursues the figure of the modern Parisienne championed by Baudelaire rather than a sitter's likeness.

title: Bal du moulin de la Galette
description: A bustling Sunday afternoon at a Montmartre dance garden, where dappled sunlight filters through the trees onto crowds of working-class Parisians. Renoir's loose, broken brushwork captures shifting light and lively conversation, treating modern leisure as a worthy subject for monumental painting.

title: Chariot Race
description: A reconstruction of a race in the Circus Maximus, with quadrigas thundering past the spina under the gaze of a packed Roman crowd. Gérôme drew on archaeological sources to render every harness, obelisk, and statue with the meticulous polish that made him a star of Salon history painting.

title: Dans la prairie
description: Camille Monet reads in a meadow strewn with poppies and daisies, her parasol propped beside her. Monet's broken brushwork dissolves figure and field into a single screen of high-keyed colour, a Giverny-era exercise in the Impressionist treatment of figures outdoors.

title: Dore-I had done a hellish thing
description: A wood-engraving from Doré's late illustrated edition of Coleridge's Rime of the Ancient Mariner, showing the sailor clinging to the rigging after slaying the albatross. Dense parallel hatching builds the storm-charged sea and the cramped vertigo of the crow's nest.

title: Entry of Crown Prince Friedrich Wilhelm of Prussia into Jerusalem
description: Crown Prince Friedrich Wilhelm of Prussia enters Jerusalem in 1869

title: Flowers in a Rococo Vase
description: An exuberant bouquet spills from an ornate Rococo vase, painted with the broken touches and shifting planes that mark Cézanne's early move beyond Impressionism. The composition tilts the vessel forward, pressing pigment-laden blossoms against a darkened ground in dense, sculptural facets.

title: Google Art Project levels adjustment 2
description: Repin's graduation piece for the Imperial Academy of Arts depicts the Novgorod merchant-hero Sadko in the underwater kingdom of the Sea Tsar, choosing a bride from a procession of foreign maidens. Painted in Paris, it earned him a gold medal and signalled an early turn toward Russian folkloric subject matter.
credit: PgFUvF6cbe8PYQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Hunting
description: A hunting scene from 1876, an unusual subject for Monet, who almost never painted narrative motifs. It belongs to the decorative panels commissioned by Ernest Hoschede for the chateau de Rottembourg at Montgeron.

title: On the Banks of the Nile
description: A late riverside scene from Upper Egypt, with figures and pack animals drawn up on a sunlit bank above the Nile. Painted long after Lewis's return to England, it draws on the cache of Cairo sketchbooks he mined for the rest of his career. The high horizon and clear ochre palette typify his desert manner.
credit: nQGSS6UcbBlXUQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Pool in a Harem
description: Two nudes loll beside the marble pool of a tiled harem interior while a guard withdraws into shadow. Gérôme paints the architecture with a near-photographic finish, building atmosphere from the contrast of cool stone and warm flesh.

title: Portraits in the Countryside
description: Caillebotte's mother, aunt, cousin, and a family friend sit reading and sewing on the lawn at the family's Yerres estate, viewed from above through a steep garden perspective. The bourgeois leisure scene combines Impressionist plein-air light with Caillebotte's almost geometric staging of figures in space.

provenance.collection: Museum of Art and History Baron Gérard
provenance.location: Museum of Art and History Baron Gérard

title: Song of the Lark
description: A young farmer pauses with his scythe at twilight, head tilted toward the unseen call of a lark. Painted shortly after the Civil War, the work distills Homer's interest in rural American labor and the silent intervals that punctuate it, rendered in a low-keyed palette of dusk.
credit: 1. The Athenaeum: Home - info - pic 2. Chrysler Museum of Art, search: 83.590 3. Google Art Project works from the Chrysler Museum of Art

title: The Standing Bearer
description: A standard-bearer in Cairo unfurls the green banner of the Prophet before a mosque facade, surrounded by attentive figures. Gérôme stages the ceremony like a frieze, using crisp profile poses and tile patterns rendered with near-photographic care.

title: The Studio Boat
description: The floating studio Monet had built at Argenteuil, a small cabined skiff that let him paint directly on the Seine. Reflections double the boat in rippling vertical strokes, an image of the artist's working method as much as a landscape.

title: The Studio Boat
description: Monet's floating studio, the converted boat he commissioned in Argenteuil so he could paint the Seine from on the water itself. Reflections of hull, mast, and bankside trees fragment across the surface in short broken strokes, the picture as much about looking from the boat as at it.

title: turf bench
description: A relaxed open-air group portrait of Repin's wife, daughters, and in-laws gathered on a turf bench in the family garden. Painted during his summers in Krasnoye Selo, it shows him adapting Russian realism to a freer, sun-warmed plein-air idiom.

title: Ukrainian woman
description: Украинка

title: Veiled Circassian Beauty
description: A bust-length study of a young Circassian woman whose features are nearly hidden by a fine white veil, her dark eyes the only direct address to the viewer. Gérôme exploits the contrast between hyper-fine gauze and the heavy embroidered cap to flaunt his control of texture.

title: Arrival of the Normandy Train
description: Steam billows beneath the iron-and-glass shed of the Gare Saint-Lazare as a locomotive pulls in from the Normandy line. One of twelve canvases Monet made of the station in 1877, the series treated industrial vapour as a modern equivalent of mist on the Seine.

title: Artlib gallery-13875-o
description: A requiem on a frozen plain: a priest swings a censer over a field of fallen Russian soldiers after the Russo-Turkish War. Vereshchagin painted the scene from direct experience as a war correspondent, part of his unsparing campaign against martial glamour.

title: Boating on the Yerres
description: A solitary oarsman pulls down the Yerres river near the artist's family estate, the viewpoint set startlingly low and close to the rower's straining body. Caillebotte's boating scenes treat leisure with the engineer's eye for mechanism, anticipating the cropped framing of modern photography.

title: Danceringreen
description: A ballet dancer in a green tutu adjusts her costume in the wings, captured from Degas' habitual cropped, off-axis vantage. Pastel hatching layers powdery greens and pinks, a chromatic shorthand the artist refined throughout his Opéra de Paris series.

title: Exterior of the Gare Saint-Lazare
description: Part of Monet's 1877 Gare Saint-Lazare series, the first concerted Impressionist meditation on industrial modernity. Steam, sunlight, and iron architecture dissolve into one another, the locomotive yard reframed as a study in coloured vapour.

title: Flowered Riverbank
description: A bank of the Seine near Argenteuil shimmers under summer foliage and scattered blossom. The high-keyed greens and broken touch are characteristic of Monet's most concentrated Impressionist years, when he and Renoir painted side by side along the river.

title: Market at Jaffa
description: A bustling market square in Ottoman Jaffa rendered with Bauernfeind's near-photographic precision, every robe, awning and stone of the citadel sharply observed. A trained architect turned Orientalist painter, he based such scenes on extensive study trips and on-site sketches in the Levant.

title: Paris Street in Rainy Weather
description: A wet-cobbled Place de Dublin in Haussmann's rebuilt Paris, viewed from Rue de Moscou. Caillebotte, the Impressionists' patron and engineer, here departs from their feathery touch for a cool, photographic precision, deploying steep perspective and a near-life-size foreground couple.
credit: 5wEUCOlEf-EaVQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Pond at Montgeron
description: Painted at the Hoschedé estate of Rottembourg, where Monet was commissioned to produce four large decorative panels, a still pond mirrors poplars and a strolling figure in flickering verticals of green. The Montgeron sojourn deepened the relationship that would, after Camille's death, lead to his second marriage with Alice Hoschedé.

title: Portrait of Jeanne Samary
description: The Comédie-Française actress Jeanne Samary leans forward with parted lips and luminous eyes, her pink dress dissolving into the rosy ground. Renoir's brush builds the portrait almost entirely from warm, transparent layers of colour.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: The Gare Saint-Lazare; Arrival of a Train - 1877
description: A locomotive pulls under the iron-and-glass shed of the Gare Saint-Lazare, steam rolling up to merge with the smoke-stained light. Part of a twelve-canvas series for which Monet secured permission to set up his easel inside the working terminus.

title: The Plum
description: A young woman sits alone at a café table, an unlit cigarette between her fingers and a brandy-soaked plum before her. Manet captures the suspended boredom of modern Parisian leisure with loose brushwork and a muted palette of pinks, creams and pale lilacs.

title: Battlefield at the Shipka Pass
description: From Vereshchagin's Balkan series, prompted by his service as an aide to General Skobelev in the Russo-Turkish War of 1877-78. The Shipka Pass, scene of brutal winter fighting, is shown after the battle as a thin scatter of corpses under a white sky.

title: Landscape at Saint-Ouen
description: An early landscape from the industrial outskirts north of Paris, painted before Seurat formulated his pointillist method. Loose, almost Corot-like brushwork registers river, sky and bank in muted tones, a quiet rehearsal for the rigorous technique to come.

title: Landscape in Scotland
description: A Highland glen under turbulent skies, painted after Doré's salmon-fishing expeditions to Scotland in the 1870s. As an Alsatian outside the Parisian academy, he turned to landscape oils late, importing the grand vistas and atmospheric drama of an earlier Romantic generation.
credit: Walters Art Museum : Home page Info about artwork

title: On the Stile
description: A young woman pauses at a wooden stile separating two pasture fields, her gaze turned away from the viewer. The painting belongs to Homer's Houghton Farm idylls, where rustic labor is filtered through the conventions of the genteel illustration.
credit: National Gallery of Art, Washington, D. C., online collection

title: Pornocrates
description: A blindfolded woman in stockings is led by a pig over a frieze of allegorical figures, while putti tumble overhead. Rops's notorious Symbolist etching, executed on coloured ground, indicts bourgeois hypocrisy by personifying carnal power as a queen guided by base instinct.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Reception of the Grand Condé at Versailles
description: Louis XIV welcomes the Prince de Condé on the grand staircase at Versailles, the victorious general bowing as courtiers look on. Gérôme reconstructs the seventeenth-century scene with archaeological precision, marshalling marble, brocade and ceremonial hierarchy in the polished academic style he made famous.

title: Setting Out to Fish
description: Breton women and children file across the tidal flats of Cancale at low water, baskets balanced on shoulders. The Salon-scale picture established the twenty-two-year-old Sargent in Paris and won a second-class medal in 1878. The wet sand mirrors a high pearly sky in long horizontal bands of grey-blue.
credit: National Gallery of Art, Washington, D. C., online collection

title: Stilleben mit Chrysanthemen
description: A still life of chrysanthemums in a glazed pot, painted at Argenteuil during a season when Monet turned indoors to floral subjects. Loose, prismatic touches break the petals into shimmering points of colour, treating the bouquet with the same outdoor optics he brought to his river views.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: The Green Hill
description: A small group of figures crosses the slope of a sunlit New England hillside, the composition reduced to broad bands of grass, sky and shadow. The work belongs to Homer's late-1870s rural idylls, balancing illustration and quiet landscape observation.
credit: National Gallery of Art, Washington, D. C., online collection

title: The Milk Maid
description: A young woman in a bonnet and apron carries a brimming pail through a sunlit pasture. Homer's loose, summery handling belongs to the Houghton Farm watercolours and oils that distilled American rural life in the post-Civil War decade.
credit: National Gallery of Art, Washington DC

title: The Orange Trees
description: Painted in the family garden at Yerres, the picture shows the artist's brother Martial reading beneath potted orange trees while a woman strolls in dappled sun. Caillebotte combines Impressionist light with the steep, photographically cropped perspective that distinguishes his contribution to the movement.
credit: 3QHnQ99LLC7m3A at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Reaper
description: A solitary harvester swings his scythe through tall grain under a high summer sun, the field's ochre stalks brushed with rapid, almost graphic strokes. The watercolor belongs to Homer's Houghton Farm period, when he reworked rural American labor in the lean, light-keyed manner he had absorbed during his recent stay in Cullercoats.

title: The Road of the War Prisoners
description: Vasily Vereshchagin (Russian, 1842-1904). The Road of the War Prisoners, 1878-1879. Oil on canvas, 71 1/4 x 117 11/16 x 2 1/4 in.

title: The Seine at Vétheuil
description: The Seine at Vétheuil, the riverside village where Monet settled in 1878 after leaving Argenteuil. Soft poplars and reflections built up in short, drily loaded strokes typify the more sober palette of his late-1870s landscapes.
credit: Collection Olivier Senn, Taken on 15 May 2019

provenance.collection: MuMa Museum of modern art André Malraux
provenance.location: MuMa Museum of modern art André Malraux

title: The Zaporozhye Cossacks Replying to the Sultan
description: A boisterous huddle of Ukrainian Cossacks dictates a profanity-laced letter to the Ottoman sultan, their laughter rolling across the tabletop. Repin worked on the picture for over a decade, building each character from ethnographic studies into a celebration of stubborn Slavic insolence and freedom.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Vétheuil in Winter
description: Drift ice clogs the Seine beneath the half-frozen village, painted during the brutal winter of 1879-80 when the river broke up in spectacular fashion. The chalky palette of bone-white and pewter records one of the coldest seasons of the century.

title: Warm Afternoon
description: A young woman in a sunbonnet sits on the warm grass of a hillside pasture, a study of stillness and rural reverie. Painted during Homer's Houghton Farm summers, the watercolor shows the artist's command of transparent washes and reserved white paper, qualities that helped redefine the medium's ambitions in America.
credit: National Gallery of Art, Washington, D. C., online collection

title: Winners
description: Drawn from Vereshchagin's Balkan series, the painting confronts the aftermath of battle as scavengers strip the bodies of the fallen. The Russian realist refused to glamorize war, building documentary detail into a deliberately disquieting field of corpses and crows.

title: At the Café
description: Patrons crowded along the marble counter of a Parisian brasserie, observed with the cool detachment Manet brought to modern social life. Sketchy contours, a high horizon and the cropped figures at the edges signal his late, increasingly Impressionist-inflected handling.
credit: GgEZL0iu5Gl9FA at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: At the Père Lathuille Restaurant
description: On the terrace of a fashionable suburban restaurant, the proprietor's son leans intimately toward a lady lunching alone. Painted en plein air during Manet's late embrace of Impressionist technique, the canvas glows with dappled sunlight and feathery, summery greens.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Camille Monet on her deathbed
description: Monet painted his first wife within hours of her death from uterine cancer at age thirty-two, the brushwork dissolving her features into a veil of violet, grey and white. He later confessed unease at finding himself analysing her colour rather than mourning her.

title: Girl and Laurel
description: A young woman in a straw hat stands among flowering mountain laurel, her face shaded against the New England summer light. The composition belongs to Homer's mid-career sequence of solitary rural figures absorbed in landscape.

title: Grand Duchess Sofia at the Novodevichy Convent
description: Repin depicts the deposed regent Sofia Alekseyevna confined at Novodevichy Convent in 1698, her glare turned outward as a hanged Streltsy is glimpsed through the barred window. The picture compresses defeat, fury, and political claustrophobia into a single suffocating cell.

title: In the Conservatory
description: A fashionable Parisian couple — Monsieur and Madame Guillemet — pause among hothouse foliage, their hands almost meeting on a garden bench. Manet exhibited the canvas at the 1879 Salon, where its crisp drawing and modern subject bridged his Realist roots and Impressionist company.
credit: Curves adjusted from File:In the Conservatory.jpg . Originally: Google Arts & Culture: Home - pic 7 th level of zoom out of 8. Scaled down.

title: James Abbott McNeill Whistler
description: A small etching from Whistler's Venice set, produced after the artist arrived in the city following his bankruptcy. Sparing, calligraphic line on laid paper sketches a moored vessel against open water, the unworked paper standing in for sky and reflection.

title: Jeunes filles au bord de la mer
description: Three pensive nymphs are arranged in a frieze along a pale shore, an Arcadian reverie rather than a specific place. Puvis de Chavannes paints in the chalky, fresco-like matte tonality that made him a touchstone for Symbolists and for the young Picasso and Seurat.
credit: CAGcDHZdQpElXA at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Louise Jane Jopling
description: The painter and writer Louise Jopling, herself a celebrated society figure, presented in a slim black gown against a muted ground. By this date Millais had moved well beyond his Pre-Raphaelite beginnings into a fluent, Velázquez-inflected portrait manner aimed squarely at fashionable London.
credit: one or more third parties have made copyright claims against Wikimedia Commons in relation to the work from which this is sourced or a purely mechanical reproduction thereof . This may be due to recognition of the " sweat of the brow " doctrine, allowing works to be eligible for protection through skill and labour, and not purely by originality as is the case in the United States (where this website is hosted). These claims may or may not be valid in all jurisdictions. As such, use of this image in the jurisdiction of the claimant or other countries may be regarded as copyright infringement . Please see Commons:When to use the PD-Art tag for more information.

title: Marie Buloz Pailleron
description: A portrait of the writer Marie Buloz Pailleron painted out of doors at the family estate of Ronjoux, an unusually plein-air approach for a society commission. Sargent, then a young pupil of Carolus-Duran, sets her white dress against deep summer foliage in fluid, Manet-like brushwork.

title: Plum Trees in Blossom
description: An orchard at Vétheuil seen through a haze of white spring blossom. Monet treats the subject — long established in Japanese printmaking, which he collected — as an all-over screen of pale flecks against pale ground, the trees almost dissolving into the field.

title: Poppy Field near Vétheuil
description: Crimson poppies scatter through summer grass on the slopes above Vétheuil, where Monet lived in straitened circumstances after leaving Argenteuil. The motif of wildflowers as red dabs against green became a signature device, repeated through the late 1870s.
credit: 1: Unknown source Unknown source 2: Stiftung Sammlung E.G. Bührle 3: ArtDaily.com

title: Still Life with Pheasants and Plovers
description: Game birds laid out on a bare table in muted earth tones, a rare still life from a year of personal hardship for Monet following his wife Camille's death. The handling is broader and more sober than his contemporary garden pictures.

title: Susanna and the Elders
description: The Danish painter takes up the biblical bather in a polished academic idiom learned in Bonnat's Paris atelier. Susanna is set against a shadowed garden wall, the elders pressed close to a single edge of the canvas to sharpen the moment of intrusion.

title: The Doorway
description: Print; Prints
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: The Gold Scab: Eruption in Frilthy Lucre
description: The Gold Scab: Eruption in Frilthy Lucre (The Creditor), 1879, oil on canvas. De Young Museum

title: The Road to Vétheuil
description: A rutted country road leads the eye between hedgerows toward the village rooftops on the Seine. Painted soon after Monet settled at Vétheuil following financial collapse in Paris, the canvas favours dry, broken brushwork over tonal finish.
credit: 7wHPKlnfBc-apA at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Road to Vétheuil
description: A snow-effect view of the road leading into Vétheuil, the Seine-side village where Monet lived from 1878. The picture belongs to his series of winter landscapes painted en plein air, the broken brushwork registering the muted blues and lilacs of low northern light on settled snow.

provenance.collection: Museum of Fine Arts, St. Petersburg, Florida, USA
provenance.location: Museum of Fine Arts, St. Petersburg, Florida, USA

title: The Seine at Lavacourt
description: A sunlit stretch of the Seine opposite Vétheuil, painted in the years Monet spent there following his wife Camille's death. The exhibition-scale canvas, accepted at the 1880 Salon, shows him reconciling Impressionist broken touch with the broader, calmer compositions of mid-career.

title: The Snake Charmer
description: A nude boy displays a python before a row of seated Ottoman soldiers, the tiled wall behind them rendered with archaeological exactitude. Long held up as the emblem of Saidian Orientalism after appearing on the cover of Edward Said's book, it shows Gérôme's appetite for stagecraft and luxurious surface.
credit: 1. Unknown source Unknown source 2. ArtDaily.org 3. Bridgeman Art Library : Object 339940 4./5. Clark Art Institute, Williamstown

title: Van gogh f1040 jh100
description: An early drawing from the Borinage period, when the artist was working as a lay preacher among Belgian coal miners and teaching himself to draw from manuals and Millet prints. The sheet's dense, laborious hatching reflects his self-taught struggle with the figure.

title: Vase of Flowers
description: An early still life painted while Seurat was still absorbing Barbizon and Impressionist precedents. The dense impasto and earthy palette show him before the systematic divisionism of dots that would define his mature work later in the decade.

title: Vétheuil
description: The riverside village rises from the Seine to its squat Romanesque church, mirrored in the slow water. Monet painted dozens of variants of this view during his impoverished Vétheuil years, working out of a small studio boat.

title: Woman with a Pearl Necklace in a Loge
description: A young woman in pale evening dress sits in a theatre box, her image doubled in the curving mirror of the loge behind her. Cassatt was the only American invited to exhibit with the French Impressionists, and her theatre series adopts Degas's spatial play with reflection and electric light.
credit: twFaMNqHtSxehg at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: 100 Aspects of the Moon - 67
description: Minamoto no Tsunemoto draws his bow on a sika deer that has wandered into the imperial palace grounds, an autumn incident recorded in the year 932. From the One Hundred Aspects of the Moon, the design contrasts the courtier's taut profile with a low harvest moon hanging over palace eaves.

title: Balcony
description: Two top-hatted men gaze down from the wrought-iron balcony of the artist's apartment at 31 Boulevard Haussmann, the same Parisian vantage point Caillebotte used in several canvases of the period. The plunging perspective and modern bourgeois subject typify his engagement with Haussmann's reshaped city.

title: Banks of the Seine
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the National Gallery of Art . Please see the Gallery's Open Access Policy .

title: Bouquet of Mallows
description: A loose still life of pink mallows arranged in a glass vase, painted at Vetheuil during a difficult personal year for Monet. The tabletop dissolves into rapid, comma-like strokes that anticipate the all-over surface of his late garden pictures.

title: By the Water or Near the Lake
description: A young woman in a straw hat leans by a sun-dappled lake, the foliage dissolved into iridescent strokes of green and pink. The picture exemplifies Renoir's sociable, plein-air Impressionism of the early 1880s.

title: Clear Sailing
description: A small watercolor study of a sailboat on open water, painted with Homer's characteristic economy of wash. Sky and sea are summarized in a few transparent layers, leaving the white paper to carry the brightness of the canvas sail.
credit: Philadelphia Museum of Art, online collection

title: Debacle at Vétheuil
description: Painted after the spectacular Seine ice break-up of January 1880, the canvas shows broken floes drifting past the bare poplars at Vétheuil. The subject — provoked by a thaw following one of the century's harshest winters — gave Monet a chance to study fragmented reflections and pearl-grey winter light.

title: Five O'Clock Tea
description: Two women take afternoon tea in a Philadelphia parlour, the silver service gleaming in the foreground while the visitor lifts her cup to obscure her face. Cassatt presents bourgeois female ritual with an Impressionist's looseness of touch, emphasising decorative still-life passages over narrative.

title: Jerusalem Artichoke Flowers
description: Yellow blooms of helianthus rise on tall stems in a thicket of green, the canvas worked in feathery, broken touches. Painted at Vétheuil during a fertile garden period, it shows Monet's habit of treating cultivated flowers as motifs for studies in light and saturated colour.
credit: National Gallery of Art, Washington, D. C., online collection

title: Luncheon of the Boating Party
description: Renoir's friends gather on the terrace of the Maison Fournaise at Chatou, lingering over wine and fruit after a row on the Seine. The composition weaves portraiture, still life, and shimmering river light into one celebrated image of modern leisure.
credit: mgHsTKDNJVzPAg at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Madame Ramón Subercaseaux
description: The Chilean diplomat's wife is shown seated at a piano in an interior of pale silvery tones, her loose white dress catching the light. The composition belongs to Sargent's early Parisian period and was admired at the 1881 Salon.

title: Monet w579 springtime in vetheuil
description: A blossoming orchard at Vetheuil, the Seine village where Monet lived after Argenteuil. Loose feathered brushwork builds the white spring foliage against soft greens, the palette typical of his early move toward broken colour and atmospheric light.

title: Monet w611
description: A study of willows along the riverbank, a recurring Vetheuil motif. Trailing branches are stroked in vertical green dashes against reflective water, the composition built almost entirely from flickering verticals and horizontals of broken colour.

title: Monet w646 jetty at fecamp in rough weather
description: The Norman fishing port of Fecamp under storm conditions, painted on one of Monet's coastal campaigns. Choppy water and a bleached sky are scumbled with quick palette-knife passages, recording the weather as much as the harbour's architecture.

title: Poppy Field
description: A field of red poppies set against summer greens, the kind of motif Monet revisited near Vetheuil and Giverny. Dabs of vermilion are scattered with calligraphic ease across a haze of grass, demonstrating his interest in saturated complementary colour.

title: Portrait of Mademoiselle Irène Cahen d'Anvers
description: The eight-year-old daughter of a Parisian banking family sits in profile, her copper hair flowing loose against a soft blue-green ground. Renoir's feathery brush captures both the child's reverie and the upholstered comfort of haute-bourgeois life.

title: Religious Procession in Kursk Province
description: A vast religious procession winds across a sunburnt steppe, sweeping clergy, peasants, gendarmes, and crippled pilgrims into a single dust-clouded cross-section of imperial Russian society. Repin's panoramic canvas is a touchstone of Peredvizhniki social realism.

title: Small Lake with Boat
description: An early oil sketch on cardboard from Munch's teenage years, depicting a still pond with a moored rowboat. The plein-air naturalism, painted before his Symbolist breakthrough, shows him absorbing Norwegian landscape conventions in compact, atmospheric form.

title: Study of a Nude
description: An academic life study from Gauguin's early years, predating the Tahitian primitivism for which he is now remembered. The figure is modelled with conventional tonal shading, evidence of the studio training he would soon discard for synthetism.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: The Bath
description: An Orientalist hammam scene in which a black servant pours water over a seated bather. Gérôme returned often to the bathhouse subject after his travels to Cairo and Constantinople, exploiting marble, brass and steam for cool tonal contrasts and an air of ethnographic reportage.

provenance.collection: Fine Arts Museums of San Francisco

title: The Break-up of the Ice
description: The breakup of ice on the Seine after a hard winter near Vétheuil, with floes drifting past bare riverbanks. Monet returned to this motif repeatedly in 1880, using broken brushwork to register the shifting interplay of water, ice and reflected sky.

title: The Man on the Balcony
description: A top-hatted bourgeois leans on the wrought-iron railing of a Haussmann balcony, surveying the boulevard below. Caillebotte, an engineer by training, repeatedly used the steep diagonal of Paris's new avenues to investigate the modern viewer's detached urban gaze.

title: The Reply of the Zaporozhian Cossacks to Sultan of Turkey
description: A preparatory oil sketch for Repin's celebrated history painting, showing Cossacks erupting in raucous laughter as they dictate an obscene reply to the Ottoman sultan. The looser handling here preserves the immediacy and unbridled humour that the finished canvas would carry forward.

title: The Seine at Lavacourt
description: A panoramic view of the Seine at Lavacourt, opposite his Vétheuil home, submitted to the 1880 Salon as a calculated bid for official recognition. The handling is unusually controlled, the river a broad mirror of pearly cloud.
credit: 1. Unknown source Unknown source 2. Dallas Museum of Arts

title: The Wailing Wall
description: Worshippers gather at the Western Wall in Jerusalem, the great Herodian ashlar towering over them in raking sunlight. Gérôme based the picture on photographs and sketches from his Near Eastern travels, deploying the polished academic finish that defined his Orientalist output.
credit: qQHviZBabX55fQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Two Boys Watching Schooners
description: Two barefoot boys perch on a grassy headland, gazing seaward at distant schooners drifting across a flat New England bay. The watercolor pares the scene to bands of sky, water, and sunlit turf, characteristic of Homer's Gloucester summers and his interest in childhood as a vantage on the wider working coast.
credit: Art Institute of Chicago : online database: entry 16803

title: Venetian Onion Seller
description: A street vendor stands in the cool gloom of a Venetian calle, a string of onions slung from her hand. The picture belongs to Sargent's early Venetian campaigns of 1880-82, when he prowled working-class quarters off the tourist routes. Tonal greys and a single shaft of light recall Velazquez, whom he had recently studied in Madrid.

title: Vétheuil
description: A view of the Seine-side village of Vétheuil, where Monet settled with his struggling household in 1878. He renders the church and clustered roofs through a screen of shimmering river reflections, treating the whole town as a single envelope of haze and light.

title: Vétheuil in Summer
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: View of Vétheuil
description: The riverside village climbs from the Seine to its Gothic church tower, viewed from a boat moored among reeds. Monet rented a house at Vétheuil in 1878 and worked tirelessly along this stretch of water following Camille's death.

title: A forgotten soldier
description: A monumental anti-war canvas by the Russian battle painter Vereshchagin, showing a fallen soldier abandoned on the battlefield as carrion birds gather. The work is part of the artist's unflinching cycle exposing the human cost of imperial conflict.
credit: 1. kunstkopie 2. Bridgeman Art Library : Object 266195 3. artpoisk.info 4. vereschagin.com.ua

provenance.collection: V. V. Vereshchagin Mykolaiv Art Museum
provenance.location: V. V. Vereshchagin Mykolaiv Art Museum

title: After the attack. Plevna
description: Vereshchagin painted the carnage of the Russo-Turkish siege of Plevna from first-hand experience as a war correspondent attached to the Russian army. The unsentimental documentary realism he made his trademark exposes the human cost behind official communiqués of imperial conquest.

title: Alice Hoschedé in the Garden
description: Alice Hoschedé, who would become Monet's second wife, stands among foliage at Vétheuil. The portrait belongs to a small group of garden figures from this transitional period, in which Monet treats the sitter as one luminous accent within a tangle of green.

title: At Fécamp
description: A view near Fecamp on the Normandy coast, where Monet painted from the chalk cliffs in early 1881. Salt air and grey-green sea are rendered through agitated, short brushstrokes, the palette muted toward the silver tones of the Channel.

title: Before the attack. At Plevna
description: Before the attack. At Plevna

title: Boat Lying at Low Tide
description: A stranded fishing boat tilts on the Normandy beach at Fécamp, its hull dark against shimmering wet sand and a high pearl sky. The on-the-spot rendering shows Monet's growing interest in the sea coast, a subject he would mine across the next two decades.

title: Bouquet of Sunflowers
description: A dense bouquet of sunflowers arranged in a glazed vase, painted in Vétheuil years before Van Gogh took up the same motif in Arles. Monet builds the heavy heads from short, comma-like strokes of chrome yellow and ochre, contrasting their warmth against a cool blue-grey ground.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Cave Canem
description: A reclining nude rests in a Pompeian-style interior beside a watchful dog, the title quoting the famous mosaic threshold inscription 'beware of the dog'. Gérôme blends archaeological staging with cabinet-picture eroticism in a tightly polished surface.

title: Champ de coquelicots
description: A sun-flooded meadow of red poppies near Vétheuil, where Monet lived after Argenteuil. The horizon is pushed high so the field reads as a near-flat carpet of broken colour, a structure he would return to throughout his rural landscapes.
credit: hAFVe_EcSVyxCg at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Dr. Pozzi at Home
description: The celebrated Parisian gynecologist Samuel Pozzi stands full length in a flame-red dressing gown, fingers tugging at his collar and sash. Sargent's bravura essay in monochrome scarlet was painted in the sitter's own apartment and announced the young American's arrival in Parisian society.

title: Fécamp
description: A coastal view of Fécamp on the Normandy chalk cliffs, painted during a productive Channel-side campaign in the early 1880s. Monet works the cliff face and sea in parallel ribbons of pigment, registering the cool grey-green light of the Pays de Caux.

provenance.collection: MuMa Museum of modern art André Malraux
provenance.location: MuMa Museum of modern art André Malraux

title: John Newman
description: Cardinal Newman, leader of the Oxford Movement and convert to Rome, sits in scarlet biretta and mozzetta near the end of his life. Millais paints the theologian with the brisk, grand-manner brushwork of his late society portraiture. The picture was commissioned soon after Newman received the cardinal's hat from Leo XIII.
credit: one or more third parties have made copyright claims against Wikimedia Commons in relation to the work from which this is sourced or a purely mechanical reproduction thereof . This may be due to recognition of the " sweat of the brow " doctrine, allowing works to be eligible for protection through skill and labour, and not purely by originality as is the case in the United States (where this website is hosted). These claims may or may not be valid in all jurisdictions. As such, use of this image in the jurisdiction of the claimant or other countries may be regarded as copyright infringement . Please see Commons:When to use the PD-Art tag for more information.

title: Lydia at the Tapestry Loom
description: Cassatt's elder sister Lydia, often her sitter in these years, leans into her work at a vertical tapestry loom in soft afternoon light. The picture belongs to the artist's series of women absorbed in domestic occupations, painted with the loose Impressionist touch she had absorbed in Degas's circle.

title: Monet w651 morning by the sea
description: Dawn light over the Channel, painted during one of Monet's Normandy seasons. Pearly pinks and pale blues are layered in horizontal sweeps, anticipating the obsessive serial study of changing light that would define his later Rouen and Houses-of-Parliament campaigns.

title: Monet w678 lane in ile st martin
description: A path on the Ile Saint-Martin near Vetheuil, a small island in the Seine where Monet often set up his easel. Dappled summer light filters through trees in flickering touches of yellow and green, the composition framed by the receding lane.

title: Monet w700 near vetheuil
description: A view of the countryside surrounding Vetheuil, the Seine village Monet had occupied since 1878. Soft greens and lavender shadows are stroked in feathery, parallel touches, the composition closer to a tonal study than a topographic record.

title: Mr. Carlyle delivering the address on his installation as Lord Rector of Edinburgh University
description: A wood-engraved illustration documenting Thomas Carlyle's rectorial address at Edinburgh, reproduced for a mass readership in the Illustrated London News. Such press engravings were the period's chief vehicle for circulating images of public events before photographic reproduction took over.

title: Nocturne in Pink and Grey
description: A Nocturne in pink and grey, in which a stretch of river or shoreline dissolves into a few atmospheric washes. Whistler used these musical titles to insist that subject mattered less than the harmony of tones — the central tenet of his Aestheticist programme.
credit: 1. http://collections.frick.org/Obj1025$18936 2. The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: On the Terrasse
description: Two girls in flowered hats sit on a Chatou terrace overlooking the Seine, a basket of wool spilling between them. The picture is among Renoir's most ambitious figure compositions, set against a softly broken backdrop of foliage and river.

title: Temps calme
description: The Channel at Pourville under settled weather, painted during Monet's first sustained Norman coast campaign. Long horizontal bands of beach, sea and sky are differentiated almost entirely by close-valued blues and pearly greys.
credit: Artbook: Rudolf Staechelin’sche Familienstiftung Basel (Hrsg.): Nafea, die Sammlung Rudolf Staechelin, Basel .

title: The Hut in Trouville
description: A tarred fisherman's hut stands above the bared sands of the Normandy coast, painted on a windy summer return to Trouville. Quick, scrubbed strokes record the silvered tideline and the lift of grasses on the dune, hallmarks of Monet's plein-air practice.

title: The Piazza San Marco
description: Renoir's view of Venice's central square dissolves the basilica's Byzantine facade into shimmering touches of pink, gold, and blue. Painted during his Italian journey, the canvas treats architecture as an Impressionist screen of light, with figures and pigeons scattered across the sunlit pavement.
credit: -QFkcq_EZt-41Q at Google Cultural Institute , zoom level maximum

title: The poor fisherman
