description: A gaunt fisher stands in his moored skiff while a woman gathers flowers and an infant sleeps on the bank, the scene reduced to chalky, almost frescoed planes of pale color. The austere composition and melancholic mood made the picture a touchstone for Symbolist painters and the young Picasso.
credit: qwEqQiy4PXmwKw at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Sea in Fécamp
description: Das Meer bei Fécamp

title: The Seine at Vétheuil
description: A bend of the Seine at Vétheuil, the village where Monet lived through the bleakest years of his middle career. The river, opposite bank and sky are resolved into three horizontal bands of broken colour, a structural simplicity that points toward his coming serial work.

title: Wheatfield
description: A summer wheatfield treated as an almost abstract field of warm strokes under a high sky. The subject anticipates the haystacks Monet would begin painting in series a decade later, where the same agrarian motif became a vehicle for studying changing light.

title: Abelard and his Pupil Heloise
description: The medieval philosopher tutoring his young pupil in a richly furnished Parisian interior, the doomed pair already absorbed in one another. Blair Leighton — not the Frederic Leighton credited here — favoured this kind of fastidiously costumed romantic-historical anecdote.

title: Arnaut Blowing Smoke at the Nose of His Dog
description: A Balkan mercenary in Ottoman service exhales pipe smoke into the muzzle of a patient hound on a sunlit Cairo street. Gérôme's enamel-smooth surface and ethnographic exactitude are typical of his late Orientalist studies of idle, picturesque soldiery.

title: Cabin of the Customs Watch
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Charlotte Dubourg par Fantin-Latour
description: Fantin-Latour's sister-in-law sits in dark dress against a neutral ground, hands loosely clasped. The portrait carries the muted tonality and quiet psychological focus the artist learned from his admiration of the Old Masters and his friendship with Whistler; it now hangs in the Musée d'Orsay.

title: Church at Varengeville
description: The Norman cliff-top church catches early sun above a mist-veiled coastline near Dieppe. Monet worked on the Varengeville motif repeatedly during his 1882 stay, treating the small chapel as a study in shifting maritime light.

title: Cliff Walk at Pourville
description: Two women in white stand on a wind-swept Channel cliff above sailing boats, their parasols echoing the curve of clouds and grass. Painted during a working summer at Pourville in 1882, the canvas balances figural anchor against the broad Norman sky.
credit: Google Arts & Culture: Home - pic Maximum resolution.

title: Cliffs at Pourville
description: One of Monet's 1882 views from the Norman village of Pourville, where he worked along the cliff path between Dieppe and Varengeville. Tall grasses are whipped into rapid green-and-violet strokes, the Channel pushed to a high horizon so that the cliff and sky carry most of the picture's air.

title: Cliffs near Dieppe
description: Chalk cliffs rear above a narrow band of restless sea, the grasses on top whipped by a Channel wind. Monet spent the spring of 1882 working at Pourville and Varengeville, applying short, dragged strokes to capture the fissured stone and saline atmosphere of the Norman coast.

title: Cliffs near Pourville
description: A Norman coastal scene capturing the chalk cliffs of Pourville under shifting summer light. Monet sets a high horizon and broken brushwork against the sea, dissolving rock and water into a single atmospheric weave typical of his Normandy campaign.
credit: Rijksmuseum Twenthe https://www.rijksmuseumtwenthe.nl/content/355 Previously: Cloude Monet, Falaises près de Pourville (1882) , adagio808 from Enschede, The Netherlands, 2009-06-25 15:23

title: Eugen von Blaas Bildnis Arthur Graf Berchtold 1882
description: A formal portrait of Arthur Graf Berchtold, a Hungarian magnate and member of the Imperial Diet, posed with the upright bearing of a statesman. De Blaas, better known for genre scenes, here demonstrates his society-portrait register with crisp tailoring and a sober palette.

title: Fisherman's Cottage on the Cliffs at Varengeville
description: Fisherman's Cottage on the Cliffs at Varengeville

title: Fishermen on the Seine at Poissy
description: Anglers in small boats work a quiet stretch of the Seine framed by leafy banks, painted from Monet's floating studio. The Poissy years were unhappy ones, but they yielded several of his most luminous river scenes in delicate greens and silvers.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Flowers in a Crystal Vase
description: A late still life painted in Manet's final months, when illness confined him to small flower pieces brought by visitors. Carnations and clematis are set in a faceted crystal vase, the brushwork economical yet luminous against a darkened ground.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Foggy Morning at Pourville
description: Mist swallows the Norman cliffs above Pourville, where Monet spent the summer of 1882 pursuing the dissolving effects of marine atmosphere. Pale lavenders and silvered greens are knit together in short, parallel strokes, leaving the horizon as little more than a suggestion.

title: Girl Carrying a Basket
description: A young woman trudges along a windswept English coast with her basket, painted during Homer's stay at the fishing village of Cullercoats. The Northumbrian sojourn shifted his palette toward greyed blues and ochres and gave his figures the weathered seriousness that marks his late marines.
credit: National Gallery of Art, Washington DC

title: Jean-Léon Gerôme
description: A Turkish dignitary mourns a dead tiger laid out on a carpet in his palace court, attended by silent retainers. Gérôme's miniaturist finish — every tile, tassel and whisker rendered without visible brushwork — was central to his reputation among Salon and American collectors.

title: Low Tide at Pourville
description: Exposed sand flats stretch beneath the chalk cliffs of the Normandy fishing village, painted on one of Monet's productive 1882 campaigns along the coast. Quick, broken brushwork captures the shifting light and damp air, and X-rays reveal an earlier composition lying beneath the beach.

title: Monet w712 low tide at pourville
description: Exposed beach and chalk cliffs at Pourville, the small Normandy resort where Monet worked through the spring of 1882. Glistening wet sand is stroked in pale lavenders and ochres, capturing the broad horizontal sweep of the Channel coast at low water.

title: Monet w812 vase of chrysantemums
description: A still life of chrysanthemums massed in a vase, treated with the same broken-touch brushwork Monet brought to landscape. The flowers register as bursts of pigment rather than carefully drawn forms, the background kept tonal so colour can dominate.

title: Monet w816
description: A Channel-coast study from the run of seascapes Monet produced in 1882, painted on site through bracing weather. Sea, cliff, and sky are unified by a quick, granular touch and a restricted, salt-bleached palette.

title: Portrait of Dora Wheeler
description: Dora Wheeler, a designer trained alongside Chase in Munich, sits in a blue gown amid the embroidered hangings of her mother Candace's Associated Artists studio. The portrait won a medal at the 1883 Paris Salon and helped establish Chase's reputation for atmospheric, aesthetic-movement interiors.

title: Portrait of Père Paul
description: A rare portrait by Monet, depicting the white-aproned cook of the Hôtel Blanche at Pourville. Painted during the same Normandy stay that produced the cliff-walk pictures, it captures the chef's ruddy face and twin chins in dense, almost caricatural impasto.

title: Road at La Cavée
description: A sunken Norman lane cuts down toward the sea at Pourville, where Monet spent productive months in 1882. Banks of grass and gorse frame a small triangle of bright Channel water, a composition organised by the steep diagonal of the path.
credit: kAHKLO3n2oSnOQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Church at Varengeville
description: The little Saint-Valéry church perches on its eroding clifftop at Varengeville-sur-Mer, painted from the gully below with the Channel framed beyond. Monet returned to the motif from several angles during his 1882 Pourville stay, treating the building as a fixed point against shifting weather.

title: The Church of Varengeville
description: The medieval clifftop church of Saint-Valery at Varengeville, silhouetted against the Channel sky. Monet returned to this motif repeatedly in 1882, treating it as a study in tonal envelope, here flushed with the warm rose and orange of evening.

title: The Fisherman's House
description: A modest fisherman's cottage perched above the Norman coast, painted during Monet's productive campaigns at Pourville and Varengeville. Broken brushwork dissolves cliff, sea, and sky into a single weathered atmosphere, the cottage anchoring the composition against the windblown grass.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: The Gorge at Varengeville
description: Painted on the Normandy coast near Dieppe, this canvas looks down through a wooded ravine toward a sliver of the Channel. Monet's broken touches of green, ochre and lilac translate the cliff vegetation into vibrating colour.

title: The Ruckus
description: A near life-size scene of a flamenco dancer mid-stride before a row of seated musicians, lit by raking footlights that cast huge shadows on the back wall. Sargent worked the picture up from sketches made in Spain, marrying Velázquez's tonal economy to a thoroughly modern sense of theatrical staging.

title: The Tulip Folly
description: A cavalryman tramples a tulip bed while soldiers behind him slash at the flower beds, an allegory of the Dutch tulipomania crash of 1636-37. Gérôme's choice of so eccentric an episode reflects his fondness for moral and economic anecdotes drawn from history.
credit: Walters Art Museum : Home page Info about artwork

title: White Lilacs in a Glass Vase
description: A bouquet of white lilacs leans from a clear glass vase against a darkened ground, the flowers worked in loaded, gestural strokes. The picture belongs to the small floral still lifes Manet painted at the very end of his life, when illness had reduced his ambitions to flowers brought by visitors.

title: Agitated Sea at Etretat
description: Heavy surf breaks against the Normandy chalk cliffs at Etretat, the famous arches receding behind a churn of jade and white. Monet returned repeatedly to this stretch of coast in the early 1880s, working on multiple canvases at once to chase shifting light; this one hangs in the Musée des Beaux-Arts de Lyon.
credit: Consulter cette image photographiée par Gilles Alonso spécialiste de la photo en gigapixel en très haute définition (+1 milliard de pixels)

provenance.collection: Museum of Fine Arts of Lyon
provenance.location: Museum of Fine Arts of Lyon

title: At the beer garden
description: A crowded Munich beer garden seethes with waitresses, top hats and bourgeois families under the chestnut trees. Menzel, the chronicler of modern German urban life, packs the canvas with the sharp-eyed, almost photographic detail he gathered in pocket sketchbooks.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Boats on the Beach at Étretat
description: Monet paints fishing boats hauled up beneath the chalk cliffs of Etretat, a Norman coast he visited repeatedly in the 1880s. The chalky beach and overcast sea are built from short, broken strokes that record changing weather rather than narrative incident.

title: Chase mrs meigs piano organ 1883
description: Mrs. Meigs is shown at her piano-organ in a richly furnished aesthetic interior, her white dress glowing against dark panelling. Chase exhibited several such society portraits in the early 1880s, deploying the bravura tonal handling he had absorbed from Frans Hals and Velázquez.

title: Children on the Seashore
description: Painted on the rocky beaches of Guernsey, where Renoir summered in 1883, the canvas shows small bathers playing among warm ochre boulders. The Channel light prompts a brighter, drier palette and the firmer drawing of his transitional period.

title: Christian IX of Denmark with his family
dateCreated: between 1883 and 1886 ( Prince Valdemar of Denmark (Christian's son) married Princess Marie of Orléans on 20 October 1885. If she is not here, the painting should have been made before that date.)
description: Tuxen assembles Christian IX — the so-called father-in-law of Europe — with his children and royal grandchildren in the garden hall of Fredensborg Palace. Commissioned for the king's golden wedding anniversary, the group portrait records the Danish dynastic ties that linked the courts of Britain, Russia, and Greece.

title: Crab Fishing
description: Two figures lean from a beached dory to lift a crab pot, painted during Homer's productive Gloucester summer. The watercolor balances loose washes of harbor light with sharper notations of rope, basket and net.
credit: 1. Bridgeman Art Library : Object 247063 2. The Athenaeum: Home - info - pic 3. Worcester Art Museum

title: Dance at Bougival
description: A young couple turns on the floor of a riverside dance hall outside Paris, the woman's pink bonnet glowing against the dappled crowd. Renoir worked from sittings with Suzanne Valadon and Paul Lhote to fix Impressionist plein-air light into a tightly composed figural scene.

title: Fujiwara no Yasumasa Playing the Flute by Moonlight
description: A late triptych by Yoshitoshi shows the Heian courtier Fujiwara no Yasumasa playing his flute on a moonlit moor while the bandit Hakamadare, disarmed by the music, looks on. Long stretches of muted ground and the cool autumnal palette demonstrate Yoshitoshi's reformed late style for the Meiji woodblock market.
credit: Image: http://collections.lacma.org/sites/default/files/remote_images/piction/ma-31808204-O3.jpg Gallery: http://collections.lacma.org/node/191379 archive copy at the Wayback Machine

provenance.collection: Los Angeles County Museum of Art

title: Khnopff
description: A figure buries her face in her hand as a half-glimpsed pianist plays in the corner, the music itself unseen. Khnopff, a founding Belgian Symbolist, isolates inwardness and silence with his characteristic muted palette and severe, almost photographic restraint.

title: Madame X
description: Virginie Gautreau stands in profile in a black satin gown, her lavender-powdered skin set against a brown ground in a near-monochrome scheme. Sargent's salon submission caused such scandal in Paris over its eroticised pose and originally fallen strap that the painter eventually decamped to London.
credit: 4QGaPNGLuGOBCw at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Mont Sainte-Odile with Pagan Wall
description: A panoramic view in the Vosges of the wooded heights of Mont Sainte-Odile, ringed by the prehistoric drystone enclosure known as the Pagan Wall. Better remembered for his book illustrations, Doré here works at large scale in oil, treating his native Alsace with a dark Romantic grandeur.

provenance.collection: Strasbourg Museum of Modern and Contemporary Art
provenance.location: Strasbourg Museum of Modern and Contemporary Art

title: Moreno Garden, Bordighera
description: Painted on a winter trip to the Italian Riviera, the picture revels in the unfamiliar palette Monet found in Francesco Moreno's exotic garden, with its palms, citrus and Mediterranean blue. The artist wrote home that the light at Bordighera demanded tones of diamond and gemstone.

title: Portrait d'Edmond Aman-Jean par Georges Seurat
description: Seurat's friend the painter Aman-Jean is seen in profile against a darkened ground, a cigarette in hand, modeled entirely in delicate hatchings of conté crayon on textured paper. The drawing was the first work Seurat exhibited at the Salon and helped establish the velvety tonal manner of his black-and-white sheets.

title: Seated Nude: Study for 'Bathers at Asnières
description: A preparatory oil study for one of the bathers in Une Baignade, Asnieres, the large canvas that announced Seurat's ambitions in 1884. Built from broad criss-crossed strokes in the conté-drawing manner he called balayé, it precedes his fully developed pointillist technique.

title: The Diggers
description: Labourers at work levelling earth, a subject from the industrial fringes of Paris that Seurat tackled in his early career. The broad balayé brushwork and sober palette precede the ordered dots of his pointillist maturity.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: The Dream
description: A young traveller sleeps in a flowering meadow as three allegorical female figures, Love, Glory and Wealth, drift toward him through the dawn air. Puvis paints The Dream in the muted, fresco-like tonalities that made him a touchstone for the Symbolists.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: The Inspection
description: Mamluk-styled guards examine a weapon in a tiled Cairene interior, every brass boss and silk braid rendered in obsessive detail. Deutsch, an Austrian who settled in Paris, built his reputation on these meticulous Orientalist set-pieces assembled from photographs and studio props.

title: The Manneporte
description: The vast natural arch of the Manneporte rises from the surf at Étretat, one of three rock formations Monet painted obsessively during his Normandy campaigns of the early 1880s. The view from below dramatises the cliff's mass against breaking sea.

title: The Saddle Bazaar
description: A crowded Cairo saddler's stall hung with embroidered tack, leather cushions and ornate stirrups. Gérôme treats the merchandise as still life within a genre scene, balancing meticulous draftsmanship of leatherwork with the sun-flooded stone arcades he studied on repeated trips to Egypt.

title: The Two Majesties
description: A male lion crouches on a desert outcrop facing the setting sun, the two 'majesties' of the title. Gérôme strips the picture to silhouette and burning sky, pursuing in paint the same monumental animal studies that fed his bronze sculpture practice.

title: The Vale of Tears
description: A column of suffering humanity processes through a darkened valley toward the figure of Christ bearing the cross. Painted in Doré's final years and exhibited posthumously, the canvas extends his Bible illustrations into vast, sermon-like oil, with the steep tonal contrasts of his engravings.

provenance.collection: Musée des Beaux-Arts de la ville de Paris

title: Tsukioka Yoshitoshi
description: From Yoshitoshi's series Mirror of Warriors of Our Country, depicting the young Minamoto no Ushiwakamaru confronting the bandit Kumasaka Chōhan. The composition typifies the artist's dramatic late-Edo musha-e, with intricate armour patterning and a charged sense of action.
objectKey: collection-of-beauty/NDL-DC_1302763-Tsukioka_Yoshitoshi-芳年武者无類_源牛若丸・熊坂長範-明治16-crd.jpg
credit: National Diet Library Digital Collections: Persistent ID 1302763

title: A Roman Slave Market
description: Set on the steps of a Roman forum rather than a 19th-century souk, the canvas shows a captive presented to bidders before a backdrop of senators. It is one of six slave-market subjects Gérôme painted, distributing them between ancient Rome and contemporary Istanbul.

title: A Sunday on La Grande Jatte
description: Seurat's manifesto of Neo-Impressionism, built over two years from countless oil sketches and conté studies. Bourgeois Parisians promenade along the Seine in frieze-like stillness, their forms assembled from tiny dots of contrasting pigment — the optical method he called divisionism.

title: Bridge at Dolceacqua
description: Bridge at Dolceacqua

title: Dragonfly. Painter's daughter portrait
description: Repin's daughter Vera perches on a rough wooden fence against a luminous summer sky, her dangling legs and bright bow giving the picture its nickname. The high horizon and outdoor light show Repin absorbing plein-air practice into the intimate Russian tradition of family portraiture.

title: G. Caillebotte - Homme portant une blouse
Garden in Bordighera
description: Painted during Monet's winter trip to the Italian Riviera, this view of a Bordighera garden captures the silvery light he found so disorienting after Normandy. Twisting olives and palms are rendered in feathery, near-pastel strokes, the morning haze softening every contour into vibrating colour.

title: Hollowed Cliff near Étretat
description: The pierced cliff at Étretat, a motif Monet returned to repeatedly, framed here through the natural arch and seething water below. The paint is laid in directional strokes that follow the swell, the chalk faces caught in mauve and ochre half-light.

title: Japanese Anemones
description: Fantin-Latour built much of his reputation on still lifes painted in his Paris apartment for the English market. Here he sets autumn-flowering anemones in a glass vessel against a flat brown ground, modelling the petals with the cool, even illumination he preferred to the Impressionists' broken light.
credit: Aberdeen City Council (Archives, Gallery and Museums Collection)

provenance.collection: Aberdeen Archives, Gallery and Museums collections

title: Seated Nude: Study for 'Bathers at Asnières
description: A preparatory study for Une Baignade, Asnieres, this seated nude shows Seurat working out volume through hatched conte crayon on textured paper. The drawing predates his fully pointillist technique but already pursues the luminous tonal gradations he called irradiation.

title: Slave Market in Ancient Rome
description: An ancient-history scene staging a public auction in a Roman forum, with toga-clad bidders inspecting an enslaved woman on a raised block. Gérôme applies the same archaeological precision and enamelled finish he used for his Orientalist works, here turning a chilly academic eye on classical antiquity.
credit: 1. Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"g/gerome/slavmark" 2. The Hermitage, St. Petersburg

title: Small Country Farm at Bordighera
description: On a winter trip to the Italian Riviera, Monet found at Bordighera a tangle of olives, palms and ochre walls under bright Mediterranean light. He complained of the difficulty of catching the dazzle in pigment, and worked rapidly across some forty canvases during the three-month campaign.

title: St. John's vision of death
description: Death rides forth as the pale horseman of Revelation, illustrating Saint John's vision in the Apocalypse. The print belongs to Doré's widely circulated Bible illustrations, where dense wood-engraved hatching and theatrical lighting gave Victorian readers their dominant visual image of scripture.
credit: https://archive.org/details/daysofchivalryor00quat/page/136/mode/2up Days of Chivalry https://archive.org/details/AI156/page/n101/mode/2up Sagrada Biblia T1 https://archive.org/details/AI159/page/n331/mode/2up Sagrada Biblia T4

title: Strada Romana in Bordighera
description: A sun-drenched view of the old Roman road on the Italian Riviera, painted during Monet's three-month stay on the Mediterranean in early 1884. Hot pinks, lemons and mineral blues record what he described in letters as the difficulty of capturing the south's blinding light.
credit: Own work MuseumBarberini Taken on 19 April 2022

title: The Corniche near Monaco
description: Olive groves and pines tumble toward the Mediterranean from the cliff road outside Monaco. Painted during Monet's early 1884 trip to the Riviera with Renoir, the picture pushes into hotter, more saturated colour than anything he had attempted on the Seine.
credit: QwEto4QimXEOzg at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Great Bathers
description: A frieze of monumental nudes disports along a wooded bank, their crisp contours and pearly flesh marking Renoir's so-called Ingresque turn away from pure Impressionism. The picture occupied him for several years and now hangs at the Philadelphia Museum of Art.
credit: 2wE1mqTuUoBmtA at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Life Line
description: A coastguardsman pulls a fainting woman across the surf on a breeches buoy, the rope line cutting taut against churning spray. Homer studied actual life-saving drills on the New Jersey coast, and the obscured rescuer's face concentrates attention on the woman's limp red scarf.
credit: xQE9eAXWkRGPUQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Valley of the Nervia
description: The dry Ligurian valley opens out beneath ridge-line pines and a distant town, painted on Monet's January journey along the Riviera with Renoir. The high-key palette of pinks, ochres and lilacs reflects his fascination with Mediterranean light.

title: The Young Orphan
description: A pensive girl in mourning black sinks into a red upholstered chair, her solitary figure modeled against a sober interior. Chase's debt to Whistler and the Munich school is visible in the subdued palette and confident, economical handling of the dress.

title: They did not expect him
description: A Siberian exile reappears in his family's drawing room, the household frozen mid-reaction in a moment of recognition. Now in the Tretyakov Gallery, the painting is a touchstone of Russian Realism, organising psychology along sharp diagonal sightlines that link the returnee to mother, wife, and uncomprehending children.

title: View of Bordighera
description: Painted on Monet's 1884 Italian Riviera campaign at Bordighera, where the unfamiliar light and exotic vegetation pushed his palette toward hot pinks, oranges, and turquoise. The Mediterranean produced what he called colours he had to invent.

title: Villas at Bordighera
description: White Italianate villas nestle among twisting umbrella pines and palms above a glittering Mediterranean. Monet wrote to Alice Hoschedé from Bordighera that the colours demanded a palette of diamonds and gemstones to be rendered honestly.

title: Villas at Trouville
description: Pastel seaside villas line the Normandy coast at Trouville, a fashionable resort favoured by Parisians on holiday. Caillebotte's looser handling and brighter palette here reflect his shift from urban scenes toward the open-air subjects of his later career.

title: Adachi Moor
description: A scene from Yoshitoshi's series One Hundred Aspects of the Moon, illustrating the legend of the demon-hag of Adachigahara. The Meiji-era ukiyo-e master combines traditional brushwork with psychological intensity, sharpening a kabuki-derived horror tale into a single moonlit confrontation.
credit: Photograph of an original print dated 1885 owned by me

provenance.collection: Los Angeles County Museum of Art

title: Aleksander III receiving rural district elders in the yard of Petrovsky Palace in Moscow
description: A wide-format Russian historical scene of Tsar Alexander III addressing village elders gathered in the Petrovsky Palace courtyard during his coronation. Repin orchestrates the crowd with his characteristic eye for individuated peasant types and ceremonial pomp.

title: Carnation
description: Two girls in white pinafores light Japanese paper lanterns among carnations and tall lilies in a Cotswold garden at dusk. Sargent painted only in the brief twilight window each day across two summers in Broadway. The Tate canvas became the picture that rehabilitated his reputation in England after the Madame X scandal.
credit: Google Arts & Culture — lAGrEoFGzGZEEQ Tate Images ( http://www.tate-images.com/results.asp?image=N01615&wwwflag=3&imagepos=1 )

title: Claude Monet Painting by the Edge of a Wood
description: Sargent depicts his friend Monet at work outdoors at Giverny, easel propped beside a thicket as a young woman reads nearby. The picture is a personal homage from one painter to another, executed with the very plein-air looseness it documents.
credit: one or more third parties have made copyright claims against Wikimedia Commons in relation to the work from which this is sourced or a purely mechanical reproduction thereof . This may be due to recognition of the " sweat of the brow " doctrine, allowing works to be eligible for protection through skill and labour, and not purely by originality as is the case in the United States (where this website is hosted). These claims may or may not be valid in all jurisdictions. As such, use of this image in the jurisdiction of the claimant or other countries may be regarded as copyright infringement . Please see Commons:When to use the PD-Art tag for more information.

title: Coming into Giverny in the Snow
description: A snow-blanketed view of the village Monet had recently made his home, painted with muted pinks and blues that register the cold light of a winter afternoon. Such effets de neige let him test how a single subject changes under shifting weather and atmosphere.

title: Entrance to Giverny in Winter
description: A bare lane leads into the village of Giverny under winter light, with leafless trees and pale fields rendered in muted blues and ochres. Painted shortly after Monet settled in Giverny, the canvas catalogued as Wildenstein 967 belongs to a small group of winter views recording his new surroundings.

title: Étretat: The Beach and the Falaise d'Amont
description: Beached fishing caïques dominate the foreground as the chalk Falaise d'Amont curves out into a green sea. Monet returned to Étretat repeatedly in the 1880s, drawn by the sculpted cliffs Courbet had also painted a generation earlier.

title: House at Falaise in the Fog
description: A Norman cliffside cottage dissolves into a wash of grey-violet fog, the architecture barely legible against the saturated air. Monet painted the Falaise coast repeatedly in the mid-1880s, fascinated by how mist erases line and reduces form to colour.

title: Inaba Mountain Moon
description: Lit by a thin crescent moon, the young Toyotomi Hideyoshi leads a roped band of warriors up the sheer face of Inaba Mountain to surprise its castle. From the One Hundred Aspects of the Moon series, the design exploits steep diagonals and silvered rock to dramatize a famous episode of Sengoku-era stealth.

title: Ivan the Terrible and His Son Ivan on November 16th
description: The tsar cradles the dying son he has just struck down with his iron-tipped staff, blood smearing the Persian carpet. Held by the Tretyakov Gallery, the picture shocked contemporaries and was briefly banned by Alexander III for its raw psychological violence.

title: James Abbott McNeill Whistler
description: Chase portrays his fellow expatriate sympathiser in the dandyish black-and-grey register Whistler himself favoured, monocle and cane in hand. The two artists painted each other in London that summer; their friendship soured shortly after over the resulting likenesses.

title: Peaches
description: A small still life of peaches in a glass jar, catalogued as Wildenstein 952. Monet rarely worked in this format by the mid-1880s, but the close-keyed orange and pink tones and feathery brushwork align it with his contemporary Giverny garden pictures.

title: Rising moon on the mount Nanping
description: From the series One Hundred Aspects of the Moon, the print shows the warlord Cao Cao gazing at the moon rising over Mount Nanping on the eve of the Battle of Red Cliffs. Yoshitoshi distils the Chinese narrative into a spare vertical composition, a hallmark of his late ukiyo-e woodblock work.

title: Self-portrait
description: The Berlin painter regards the viewer directly in a sober, naturalistic likeness keyed to muted browns and ochres. Lepsius trained in Berlin and Paris and worked within the cultivated portraitist circles of late nineteenth-century Wilhelmine society.
credit: Selected work 33 from Self Portrait: Renaissance to Contemporary (Anthony Bond, Joanna Woodall, ISBN 978-1855143579 ).

title: Shark fishing
description: Two black fishermen in a small boat haul against a hooked shark amid Caribbean swells. The watercolor anticipates Homer's later Gulf Stream subjects, using a thrashing diagonal to crowd the picture plane with imminent danger.

title: Sunflowers in the Garden at Petit Gennevilliers
description: A dense bed of sunflowers fills the canvas, painted in the artist's own garden at Petit Gennevilliers on the banks of the Seine. The loose handling and saturated yellows reflect a late shift toward Impressionist plein-air practice, after he had largely turned from urban subjects to horticulture.

title: The Cliff of Aval
description: The famous Falaise d'Aval at Étretat, with its natural arch piercing the chalk cliff. Monet, who painted this Norman site repeatedly, here works with broken touches of cream, lilac and pale green to render limestone under shifting Channel light.
credit: 7AFALGbV2i_P5w at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Cliffs at Etretat
description: A view of the chalk cliffs and natural arches at Étretat on the Normandy coast, a motif Monet returned to repeatedly in the mid-1880s. He works the chalk face in pinks and creams against the green-blue Channel, alert to how the light shifts across the porous stone.

title: The Cliffs at Étretat
description: Monet returned repeatedly to the Normandy coast at Étretat to paint its arched chalk cliffs and the Aiguille needle rising from the surf. Here the wet shingle catches a cold marine light, broken brushwork translating wind, spray and shifting weather into pure sensation.

title: The Fog Warning / Halibut Fishing
description: A halibut fisherman in a dory pauses at the crest of a swell to gauge the schooner against a wall of advancing fog. The diagonal of the boat and the steep horizon convert a routine moment into a measured study of solitude at sea.
credit: GAEcgEvHorgctQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Great Pool at Bursa
description: An Orientalist interior depicting the great bath at Bursa in Ottoman Anatolia. Gérôme combined ethnographic observation from his eastern travels with academic precision, treating tilework, steam and bathers' bodies with the same hard-edged finish that made his Eastern scenes so widely reproduced.
credit: Gérôme, La grande piscine de brousse , leo.jeje , 2011-04-12 11:30

title: The Herring Net
credit: ygF9ozok7GQIvA at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Willow
description: A willow standing on the bank of the Epte near Giverny, caught in the pale, cool light of a spring morning. The pastel palette and feathery brushwork are typical of Monet's mid-1880s riverside studies, in which he began to anchor compositions around a single tree as a recurring motif.
credit: Das Willow (aka Frühjahr auf der Epte) von Claude Monet (1840-1926, France) | Kunstreproduktionen Claude Monet | wahooart.com

title: View of Fort Samson
description: A coastal view from Grandcamp on the Normandy shore, part of the seaside campaigns Seurat undertook each summer to test his divisionist method against changing light. Tiny calibrated dots dissolve fortification, sea and sky into a luminous, near-abstract grid.

title: At the Entrance to the Temple Mount
description: Bauernfeind, the leading German Orientalist of his generation, records a crowded Jerusalem street where pilgrims and traders converge at the gates of Haram al-Sharif. His near-photographic precision rests on sketches gathered during repeated journeys to Palestine.

title: Bonaparte ante la Esfinge
description: Subtitled "Œdipe", the picture shows Napoleon on horseback halted before the half-buried Sphinx during the 1798 Egyptian campaign. Gérôme, the leading Orientalist of the Paris Salon, treats sand, granite and uniform with archaeological exactitude, casting the encounter as one civilisational riddle posed to another.

title: Breton Girl
description: A young woman in the white coif and dark dress of Pont-Aven posed during Gauguin's first Brittany sojourn. The simplified silhouette and reserved palette anticipate the cloisonnist flattening he would push further in following summers among the Breton peasantry.

title: Carnival Evening
description: Two costumed lovers stand at the edge of a leafless winter wood beneath a cold full moon, a tiny lit lantern hovering at the treeline. One of Rousseau's earliest important canvases, it already shows the naive precision of silhouette and dreamlike stillness that would define the Douanier's vision.
credit: 3wFKsz9ULBaojw at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Dance at the Moulin de la Galette
description: Painted during Van Gogh's Paris years, when he lodged with brother Theo in Montmartre and absorbed Impressionist colour. The windmill of the Moulin de la Galette, a dance-hall landmark also painted by Renoir and Toulouse-Lautrec, appears here in muted earth tones still tied to his earlier Dutch palette.
credit: Dickinson Gallery, London and New York

title: Dance at the Moulin de la Galette
description: Painted during the Paris years, this view of the Montmartre windmill blends the muted palette of his earlier Dutch work with the lighter tonalities he was absorbing from the Impressionists. The motif, a popular dance hall draw for Parisian painters, is treated soberly rather than festively.

title: Dawn Moon of the Shinto Rites
description: A Shinto priest performs a dawn rite as the late moon hangs pale over a torii gate. The print uses Yoshitoshi's restrained late palette and softly graded sky to evoke the dim hour between ritual and daybreak, characteristic of the One Hundred Aspects series.

title: fields with tulips
description: Painted during Monet's brief stay in the Dutch bulb fields near Sassenheim, this canvas spreads horizontal bands of red and yellow tulips before distant windmills. The high-keyed palette previews the saturated colour Monet would later push at Giverny.

title: Flame Nettle in a Flowerpot
description: A potted coleus painted in Paris during Van Gogh's transition from the dark Dutch palette toward brighter, more broken color. The everyday flowerpot becomes an exercise in observing complementary reds and greens at close range.

title: Gravemarker Moon
description: The aged poet Ono no Komachi crouches by a wooden gravemarker beneath an autumn moon, contemplating the suitors she once spurned. Yoshitoshi pares the print to muted earth tones and a single stark vertical, turning the courtly love legend into a meditation on vanity and impermanence.

title: le tub
description: A pastel of a woman bathing in a shallow tub, viewed from a steep, almost overhead angle drawn from Japanese prints. Part of Degas's bather series shown at the eighth and final Impressionist exhibition in 1886, where critics remarked on the candid, keyhole intimacy of the pose.

title: Portrait of Poly
description: A weather-beaten Belle-Île fisherman, Hippolyte 'Poly' Guillaume, sits with arms folded, his red sash and seaman's cap painted in the same broken touches as the rocks Monet was studying on the Brittany island. The portrait, an unusual late venture into character study, entered the Musée Marmottan via the artist's family.

title: Pyramides at Port-Coton
description: The jagged sea stacks off Belle-Île-en-Mer, painted during Monet's bracing autumn campaign on the Breton coast. Granite is built up in dense, crusted strokes against a churning Atlantic, a darker and rougher palette than his Norman seascapes.

title: Rainy Weather
description: Rain over Étretat: the Norman cliffs and arched rock recede behind a grey curtain of weather, the Channel rendered in broken slate strokes. Monet often returned to Étretat through the 1880s, attracted by its dramatic chalk geology under shifting marine light.
credit: hAGxTh_ktg_DlQ at Google Cultural Institute , zoom level maximum

provenance.collection: National Museum of Art, Architecture and Design
provenance.location: National Museum of Art, Architecture and Design

title: Rocks at Belle-lle
description: A rocky inlet at Port-Domois on Belle-Île, painted during Monet's autumn 1886 campaign on the storm-battered Breton coast. The granite is worked in dense, dragged strokes of plum and ochre against an unusually saturated turquoise sea.
credit: HAHXgPLFma1p1Q at Google Cultural Institute , zoom level maximum

title: Self-portrait with pipe
description: An early self-portrait with the artist's clay pipe, painted shortly after his arrival in Paris while his palette was still close to that of his Dutch years. Sober earth tones model the bearded face against a neutral ground, before the chromatic experiments of the following year.
credit: awGqjJSvEkyNwg at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Skull of a Skeleton with Burning Cigarette
description: A small, mordant memento mori produced during Van Gogh's studies at the Antwerp academy, where students were drilled in anatomy from articulated skeletons. The flippant cigarette has been read as a sardonic jab at academic propriety as much as a meditation on mortality.
credit: Museum Page (the image was stitched from tiles - for assembly method used see The Potato Eaters )

title: Study of a Figure Outdoors: Woman with a Parasol
description: Suzanne Hoschedé strides up a grassy slope, parasol angled against the wind that flattens her white dress. The picture revisits the 1875 Argenteuil composition of Camille, now reworked as a near-anonymous figure study with the face deliberately blurred.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: The Blute-Fin Mill
description: A study of the Moulin de Blute-Fin, one of the surviving windmills on the butte of Montmartre that the artist returned to repeatedly after arriving in Paris. The palette remains dark and earthy in the manner of his Dutch period, even as the subject anticipates the lighter Parisian views to come.

title: The End of the Session" Alternative title
description: A studio interior in which a model steps down from the dais at the close of a session, while the artist's marble in progress stands beside her. Gerome, both painter and sculptor, treats the academic ritual with cool, polished precision and an eye for telling props.

title: The Hill of Montmartre with Stone Quarry
description: Painted shortly after the artist's arrival in Paris, this view of the working windmills and gypsum quarries on Montmartre still uses the muted, earth-toned palette he carried over from his Dutch period. The brushwork is loose and matter-of-fact, predating his encounter with Impressionist colour.

title: The Sahleb Vendor
description: A street vendor of sahleb, the hot orchid-root drink popular in Ottoman Cairo, pours a cup before a tiled wall. Deutsch worked from sketches and props gathered on visits to Egypt, finishing each picture in his Paris studio with jewel-like precision.

title: Tulip Fields at Sassenheim
description: Bands of red, yellow and white tulips stripe the polder near Sassenheim, a windmill anchoring the flat horizon. Monet's brief 1886 Holland trip yielded five tulip-field canvases, in which the riot of cultivated colour gave him a ready-made experiment in pure chroma.

title: Tulip fields in Holland
description: Bands of red and yellow tulips run to the horizon under a brisk sky, with windmill sails turning at the field's edge. Monet visited the Dutch bulb fields in spring 1886 expressly to confront these violently saturated stripes of colour.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Yoshitoshi tsuki
description: The warrior-monk Benkei stands at the prow of a storm-tossed vessel in Daimotsu Bay, calming the vengeful spirits of fallen Taira warriors with chanted prayers. From One Hundred Aspects of the Moon, Yoshitoshi pitches the scene against rolling waves and a clouded moon to dramatize the Heike legend.
credit: Polychrome woodblock print; mica on paper

title: Along the Seine
description: A riverside scene along the Seine on the outskirts of Paris, painted during the artist's Asnières period with Bernard and Signac. The handling shows the impact of Neo-Impressionist contact, with broken touches of pure colour modeling the embankment, water, and screen of trees.

title: Christmas Eve
description: A snow-covered Scottish vista at twilight, the windows of a distant house glowing against winter blue. Millais's late landscapes increasingly dispense with figures and narrative, trading Pre-Raphaelite detail for atmospheric tonal painting in the manner of his hero Turner.
credit: Christie's , LotFinder: entry 5631469 (sale 5969, lot 23, London, King Street, 13 December 2012)

title: Dance at the Moulin de la Galette
description: A view of the Moulin de la Galette, the Montmartre windmill turned popular dance garden depicted by Renoir and Toulouse-Lautrec. Van Gogh treats the wooden mill and surrounding fence soberly, its silhouette isolated against an overcast Paris sky.

title: Fix Bayonets! Hooray! Hooray!
description: A surging Russian infantry charge with fixed bayonets, drawn from Vereshchagin's late series on the 1812 Napoleonic invasion. The painter, a veteran himself, refused heroic abstraction, packing the canvas with the smoke, mud and panic of close-quarters assault.

title: Fruit Picking
description: An early Martinique canvas from Gauguin's 1887 stay near Saint-Pierre, showing local women gathering fruit beneath dense tropical foliage. Painted before his Tahitian voyages, the work already shows his shift away from Impressionist brushwork toward flatter, decorative arrangements of figure and landscape.
credit: 1 The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 . 2. Van Gogh Museum

title: Garden in Montmarte with lovers
description: A small enclosed garden on the slopes of Montmartre, with strolling couples among trees and wooden fencing. Painted during the Paris years, the canvas registers the artist's growing engagement with broken touches of complementary colour learned from his Neo-Impressionist contemporaries.

title: Glazunov by Repin
description: A bust-length portrait of the young composer Alexander Glazunov caught mid-thought, his gaze sliding away from the viewer. Repin works with the loose, painterly attack of his portrait practice in the late 1880s, when he documented much of the Russian musical and literary intelligentsia.

title: Google Art Project
description: A young woman trails her hand in the water from a small skiff, her dress and the boat's interior dissolving into the river's green reflections. The high viewpoint and cropped framing show Monet's lasting absorption of Japanese ukiyo-e composition.
credit: UQFKgSkcsCnEuQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Grapes
description: A still life of grapes, lemons, pears, and apples banked across the table, painted in Paris under the influence of Adolphe Monticelli's heavy paint and the Impressionists' fruit studies. Now at the Art Institute of Chicago, it shows Van Gogh testing a brighter, complementary-colour palette.

title: Grass and Butterflies
description: A close, near-aerial view of grass, weeds, and butterflies in a corner of garden, painted as a fragment of nature seen at very short range. Stems and blades are flicked on in fine, directional strokes that nearly abstract the patch into a tapestry of green and yellow.
credit: Christie's , LotFinder: entry 6169506 (sale 15977, lot 40A, New York, 11 November 2018, page no more available)

title: Huts under the Trees
description: Thatched cottages glimpsed through tree trunks, painted during Gauguin's stay in Martinique in 1887. The tropical light and broad colour areas mark his decisive turn away from Impressionist division of tone toward the synthetist style of Pont-Aven.

title: In the Norwegian Boat at Giverny
description: Three of Alice Hoschedé's daughters drift on the Epte in a Norwegian skiff, their pale dresses caught between dark river plants and sun-laced water. The high viewpoint and crop, learnt from Japanese prints, push the figures toward decorative pattern.

title: In the Woods at Giverny: Blanche Hoschedé at Her Easel with Suzanne Hoschedé Reading
description: Two of Alice Hoschedé's daughters work and read in the dappled woods near Giverny, where Monet had moved in 1883. Blanche, herself a painter, would later marry Monet's son Jean and become his nurse and companion in his final years.
credit: 5wF3eJ0lvxhbeg at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

provenance.collection: Los Angeles County Museum of Art
provenance.location: Los Angeles County Museum of Art

title: Jean-Pierre Hoschedé and Michel Monet on the Bank of the Epte
description: Monet's son Michel and stepson Jean-Pierre lie among grasses on the banks of the Epte, the small tributary of the Seine that ran past Giverny. The figures' white shirts vanish into a meadow rendered in feather-light strokes — a key example of Monet's late figural work.

title: Menter by Repin
description: A portrait of the Bavarian virtuoso pianist Sophie Menter, then teaching at the Saint Petersburg Conservatory, captured with the loose painterly bravura Repin reserved for his musical and literary friends. The Russian Realist treated such cultural sitters as documents of the late imperial intelligentsia.

title: Montmartre: mills and vegetable gardens
description: A view across the allotment gardens and windmills of upper Montmartre, then still a semi-rural quarter on the northern edge of Paris. Wooden fences, vegetable plots, and the silhouettes of the mills are arranged in receding bands beneath a wide, pale sky.

title: Nude in a Landscape
description: A small late-1880s nude from Renoir's so-called "manière aigre", when he tightened his contours under the influence of Ingres and Italian Renaissance drawing. The pearly figure is set against a lightly brushed landscape, oil on canvas, now on long-term loan from the Pearlman Foundation to the Princeton University Art Museum.

title: On the outskirts of Paris
description: Allotments and a windmill straggle across the heights of Montmartre at the edge of Paris. Painted during Van Gogh's two-year Paris stay, the picture absorbs Impressionist colour and the broken touch of Pissarro and Signac.

title: Plaster Cast of a Woman's Torso
description: A plaster cast of a female torso set against a warm ground, one of a series of academic still-lifes Van Gogh painted in Paris while studying at Cormon's atelier. The handling shows him working through colour theory on a deliberately neutral subject.

title: Portrait of Etienne-Lucien Martin
description: The Parisian restaurateur is rendered with brisk, hatched brushwork in warm browns and greens, the modeling already loosened by Van Gogh's exposure to Impressionist technique. The portrait dates from his Paris year, when he was experimenting with the divisionist touch of Seurat and Signac.

title: Portrait of Marguerite Khnopff
description: Khnopff portrays his sister Marguerite, a recurring presence in his work, with the cool, hieratic detachment that defines his Symbolist portraiture. The pale palette and flattened space distil her into an emblem of withdrawn, idealised femininity rather than likeness alone.

title: Seine-shore at the Pont de Clichy
description: An industrial bend of the Seine at the edge of Paris, treated with the broken, stippled brushwork the artist absorbed from Signac and Seurat during his Paris stay. Smokestacks and bridge ironwork are softened into a screen of pale pinks and blues.

title: Self portrait as a painter
description: Painted in Paris, the artist confronts the viewer at his easel with palette and brushes in hand, dressed in a workman's blue smock. The dense, divisionist touches of complementary colour absorbed from Seurat and Signac mark a decisive break from the dark Dutch palette of his earlier self-portraits.

title: Self-Portrait
description: A Paris-period self-portrait in which the artist tests Neo-Impressionist methods on his own face, breaking flesh, beard, and felt hat into short, separate touches of pure colour. The effect is mottled and atmospheric, registering the lessons absorbed from Signac and Seurat.

title: Self-Portrait with Grey Felt Hat
description: Painted during Van Gogh's Paris years, this self-portrait shows the artist absorbing Impressionist and Pointillist colour theory. Short, hatched strokes of complementary blue and orange radiate from his head, turning the felt hat into a halo of pure pigment. Now in the Van Gogh Museum, Amsterdam.

title: Square Saint-Pierre
description: A summer corner of an Asnières park from Van Gogh's Paris years, when he absorbed Impressionism and Neo-Impressionism in the company of Bernard and Signac. Short divided strokes of green, lemon, and rose lighten his palette decisively before he carried it south to Arles.

title: The Antiques Seller by Rudolf Weisse
description: A genre interior in oil on panel showing an antiques dealer surrounded by carpets, ceramics and weaponry from the Ottoman world. Weisse, a Czech-born painter active in Paris, specialised in such Orientalist bazaar scenes detailed with cabinet-picture precision.

title: The Boat
description: A varnished rowing skiff rests among reeds on the Epte at Giverny, its hull doubled in dark green water. The riverbank boats became a private motif of the late 1880s, and the painting now belongs to the Musée Marmottan Monet, formed from the bequest of the artist's son Michel.

title: The Carpet Merchant / Carpet Merchant in Cairo
description: A merchant unfurls a crimson carpet for buyers in a sunlit Cairo courtyard, the surrounding walls hung with further wares. Gérôme deploys his tight academic finish to revel in the textile's pile and the architectural geometry overhead.
credit: Minneapolis Institute of Arts via artsconnected.org

title: The Models
description: Three views of the same studio model — facing, in profile and from behind — set against the painter's own Grande Jatte. Seurat used the picture as a programmatic demonstration of pointillism applied to the academic motif of the nude.

title: The moon through a crumbling window
description: From One Hundred Aspects of the Moon, Yoshitoshi depicts the Zen patriarch Bodhidharma seated in meditation behind a ruined lattice. The late-Edo printmaker tempers the supernatural subject with naturalistic shading, marking his bridge between ukiyo-e and modern Japanese illustration.

title: The restaurant Rispal in Asnières
description: A view of a suburban café-restaurant on the banks of the Seine, painted during Van Gogh's Paris years when he frequented the riverside towns with Bernard and Signac. The lightened palette and short, divisionist-inflected strokes register his rapid absorption of Impressionist and Neo-Impressionist colour theory.
credit: 1. repro from art book 2. Google Art Project works from the Nelson-Atkins Museum of Art

title: The Wailing Wall by Bauernfeind
description: An Orientalist tableau of worshippers gathered in prayer at the Western Wall in Jerusalem. Bauernfeind, trained as an architect, renders the weathered ashlar masonry and embroidered prayer shawls with exacting documentary precision, capturing the devotional hush of the site.

title: 1993-151-1-pma2018
description: A Monet of 1888, catalogued as Wildenstein 1191 and now in the Philadelphia Museum of Art. By this date the artist was working in extended series, breaking form into mosaics of high-keyed colour to record a single motif under shifting light.

title: Antibes
description: A view across the Mediterranean toward the snow-capped Alps, made during Monet's four-month campaign on the Côte d'Azur in early 1888. The pink and turquoise palette he developed here pushed his colour further than ever, anticipating the serial canvases of the 1890s.

title: Antibes Seen from La Salis
description: Antibes Seen from La Salis

title: E1924-3-16-pma2018
description: A meander of the Epte near Giverny, the small river that fed Monet's water-garden. The canvas is built from short, broken touches that knit foliage, bank and reflection into a single shimmering surface, characteristic of his late 1880s plein-air practice.

title: Field with flowers near Arles
description: A flowering field outside Arles, with stands of red poppies and white wildflowers spread across a foreground of summer grass. Distant farm buildings break the horizon, the whole laid down in rapid, directional strokes that record the southern light Van Gogh sought in Provence.

title: Field with Poppies
description: A summer field around Arles scattered with poppies, painted in short crosshatched strokes that articulate grasses and blossoms as discrete dabs of saturated red and green. The tightly cropped foreground reflects the artist's interest in Japanese print composition.

title: Fishing boats on the beach at Les Saintes-Maries-de-la-Mer
description: Beached fishing smacks on the Mediterranean sand at Les Saintes-Maries-de-la-Mer, their masts and rigging picked out in clean primary colour. Made on a five-day excursion from Arles in June 1888, the picture marks Van Gogh's first encounter with the sea in the South of France.
credit: Google Arts & Culture: Home - pic Maximum resolution. (appears to have been taken down)

title: Grainstacks
description: A rimed haystack stands in a frosted field outside Giverny, its conical bulk caught between a violet shadow and a chilled mauve sky. The work belongs to the Grainstacks series with which Monet inaugurated his rigorous serial method, painting the same motif under successive light conditions.

title: Green and Silver: Beaulieu
description: A small Loire-valley landscape from Whistler's late period, painted on a wooden panel during a French sojourn. The title declares the picture's true subject — a tonal duet of green foliage and silvered river — in keeping with Whistler's insistence that paintings be read as musical arrangements of colour.
credit: IQEu_qM6fwiCLQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Haystacks at Giverny
description: An early grainstack study from the fields around Giverny, the motif Monet would canonise in his 1890–91 series. Even here the haystack functions as a coloured mass dissolving into ambient sunset light rather than as agricultural fact.

title: Mother of Pearl and Silver: The Andalusian
description: A standing female figure rendered in the pale, harmonised tones the title evokes, in keeping with Whistler's musical naming and his pursuit of arrangement over narrative. The subject becomes a vehicle for tonal close-keying rather than character study.

title: Open Air Breakfast
description: Chase's family takes breakfast in a leafy Brooklyn yard, the white tablecloth and hammock catching dappled sun. Loose brushwork and a bright outdoor palette align the scene with American Impressionism's domestic, garden-side mode.
credit: IwF0LR5CzDn15Q at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: P.1948.SC .276-scaled-aspect-ratio-16-9-3-scaled
description: A landscape from Monet's mature Impressionist period, catalogued as Wildenstein 1192. The painter's broken touch and high-keyed palette translate atmosphere and shifting light into the building blocks of the picture.

title: Plage de Juan-les-Pins' by Claude Monet
description: From Monet's Antibes campaign on the Côte d'Azur, the canvas dissolves pines, sea and Alps into a high-keyed Mediterranean palette of pinks, mauves and turquoise. The artist complained the southern light was too bright to paint, yet returned with dozens of these luminous beach studies.
credit: Dickinson Gallery, London and New York

title: Seascape near Les Saintes-Maries-de-la-Mer
description: A seascape made during the artist's brief stay at the Mediterranean fishing village of Saintes-Maries-de-la-Mer, with small fishing boats riding choppy water under a wind-driven sky. Short, stabbing strokes register the swell, while patches of pure colour mark sails and reflected light.

title: Starry Night Over the Rhône
description: Gaslamps along the Arles quay double in the dark water of the Rhône while the Great Bear hangs overhead. A small couple strolls in the foreground, anchoring Van Gogh's nocturne to the human scale of the river town.

title: Still Life: Vase with Twelve Sunflowers
description: A dozen sunflowers in a yellow earthenware vase set against a yellow ground, made in Arles to decorate the bedroom prepared for Gauguin's visit. The picture pushes saturated yellow into both subject and field, treated as a near-monochrome experiment in pigment.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: Sunset at Montmajour
description: Painted on the rocky outcrop near Arles, the work surveys the plain of La Crau through a foreground of scrub oak and limestone, the abbey ruins behind. Long doubted as authentic, it was reattributed in 2013 on the basis of letters, pigments, and the painter's own catalogue.

title: The Alyscamps
description: A view of the ancient Roman necropolis at Arles, painted during Gauguin's stay with Van Gogh in autumn 1888. The avenue of stone sarcophagi recedes between flame-orange trees, the foreground simplified into broad zones of saturated color that anticipate the cloisonnist style.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: The Alyscamps
description: The Roman necropolis at Arles, lined with poplars turning autumn yellow, is rendered with thick impasto and a steeply receding perspective. The work belongs to a small series painted alongside Gauguin during their fraught Arles collaboration.

title: The Bridge at Trinquetaille
description: The iron lifting bridge at Trinquetaille on the Rhône at Arles, painted with a tilted, plunging perspective down its broad stone steps. Cool blues and grays mark a deliberate departure from the high-keyed yellow palette of the artist's earlier Arles canvases.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: The Bridge of Langlois at Arles with laundresses
description: Washerwomen kneel along the canal bank as a horse-cart crosses the wooden drawbridge at Arles. Van Gogh returned to the Pont de Langlois several times in 1888, drawn to its clean geometry and Provençal light reminiscent of Japanese prints.

title: The Fort of Antibes
description: The old fortifications of Antibes seen across the bay, from Monet's 1888 Riviera campaign. The southern light pushed his palette toward saturated pinks, turquoises and ochres, the Mediterranean treated as a high-keyed counterpart to his northern coasts.

title: The harvest
description: A panoramic view of the harvest on the plain of La Crau outside Arles, with stacked sheaves, a blue cart, and distant farm buildings under a Provençal sky. Van Gogh structures the composition with receding parallel bands of yellow stubble, layering complementary blues and oranges to register the heat of high summer.

title: The Hermit
description: An aged ascetic pads along the shore of a still northern lake, drawing the eye into Nesterov's signature blend of Orthodox spirituality and quiet Russian landscape. Painted at the outset of his religious phase, the work was acquired by Pavel Tretyakov and now hangs in his Moscow gallery.

title: The Langlois Bridge at Arles
description: A small Provençal drawbridge over the Arles canal, with washerwomen at the bank and a horse-drawn cart crossing above. The subject obsessed Van Gogh in spring 1888, who returned to it in several oils, watercolours, and drawings for its echo of the Dutch canals of his childhood.
credit: Photo by Szilas at the Van Gogh, My Dream Exhibition in Budapest, 2013

title: The Painter on His Way to Work
description: A self-portrait of the painter trudging to a motif under the Provençal sun, easel and canvases on his back. The picture, formerly in the Kaiser-Friedrich-Museum in Magdeburg, was lost during the Second World War and survives only in reproduction.
credit: Vincent van Gogh: In der Provence , R.Piper & Co, Verlag, München 1977, ISBN 3492110703

title: The Postman
description: One of several portraits Van Gogh painted of his Arles friend Joseph Roulin, sorter at the local railway post office. The blue uniform and gilt-buttoned coat are flattened against a plain ground, the heavily impastoed beard and ruddy cheeks fixed with the bold contour of a print.

title: The Seine at La Grande Jatte
description: A quiet riverbank study made while Seurat was finishing his Grande Jatte campaigns. The Seine flows past the island in patiently calibrated dots of complementary colour, a small-format demonstration of the optical mixing his divisionist method depended on.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

provenance.collection: Royal Museums of Fine Arts of Belgium

title: The Sower
credit: .mw-parser-output .ubx-floatdefault[dir="ltr"],.mw-parser-output .ubx-floatopposite[dir="rtl"],.mw-parser-output .ubx-floatleft{float:left}.mw-parser-output .ubx-floatdefault[dir="rtl"],.mw-parser-output .ubx-floatopposite[dir="ltr"],.mw-parser-output .ubx-floatright{float:right}.mw-parser-output .ubx-floatnone{float:none} This user has uploaded 188 quality images to Wikimedia Commons . Self-portrait - Vincent van Gogh

title: The Starry Night
description: Starry Night over the Rhône, painted from the riverbank at Arles, with the gas lamps of the quay reflected as ragged streaks of yellow on the dark water. A foreground couple anchors the scene as the Great Bear wheels overhead, the constellations rendered as concentric haloed dots.
credit: uQE3XORhSK37Dw at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Surgeon Evgueni Vasilievich Pavlov in the Operating Theater
description: A documentary group composition showing the surgeon Evgeny Pavlov operating in a St Petersburg theatre, witnessed by gowned colleagues. Repin treats the modern clinic as a secular history piece, marshalling sharp tonal contrasts and a tightly bracketed perspective around the patient.

title: Tiger on the Watch
description: A Bengal tiger crouches on a rocky escarpment, its eyes locked on unseen prey below. The picture exemplifies Gérôme's late animalier subjects, executed with the same enamelled precision he brought to figure painting.
credit: SAFVOlZNHeEt6A at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Wheat field with View of Arles
description: Painted from a vantage above Arles in the productive summer of 1888, the canvas reads as a tapestry of yellow stubble, ripening crops and red roofs gathered around the town's spires. Van Gogh works in dense, directional brushstrokes that translate Provençal heat into structured pattern.

title: Wheat Stacks with Reaper
description: A reaper bends to his work amid sun-bleached fields under a churning sky, painted in the final months of Van Gogh's life at Auvers-sur-Oise. Thick parallel strokes of yellow and ochre carry the artist's recurring theme of the harvester as a figure of mortality and quiet acceptance.
credit: nAEx7tuIXuWdGQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: An Out-of-Doors Study
description: The painter Paul Helleu sketches outdoors beside his wife Alice, the canoe and grass rendered in broken Impressionist touch. Sargent painted the work at Fladbury during a summer of plein-air experiment under Monet's influence. The flushed greens and unfinished edges show him at his most explicitly Impressionist.
credit: 7wHaMBbXrHQstQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Cypresses
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Cypresses with two female figures
description: Two small figures stand beneath the flame-like silhouette of cypresses, trees the artist described as obsessing him for their dark sculptural force. The composition belongs to the Saint-Rémy cypress group, in which the species becomes a vehicle for swirling, vertical brushwork.

title: Enclosed Field with Rising Sun
description: A walled wheatfield is shown at sunrise, the sun cresting low above distant hills while curling brushstrokes animate the grain. Painted from the window of his room at the asylum in Saint-Rémy-de-Provence, it is one of a sustained series in which the same enclosed field is observed across seasons and times of day.

title: Eugen von Blaas - The Spider and the Fly - 1889
description: A young woman spins thread while a smiling suitor leans in, the title borrowed from Mary Howitt's cautionary verse turning the courtship scene into sly allegory. De Blaas modelled such tableaux on Venetian working girls he knew from his studio quarter near the Zattere.

title: Evening Hour
description: A peasant family gathers indoors at lamplight in a copy after Jean-François Millet. Working from a black-and-white print at Saint-Rémy, Van Gogh reimagines Millet's design in his own dense weave of complementary colour.

title: Evening Landscape at Moonrise
description: Evening landscape at moonrise - Van Gogh

title: Faded Laurels
description: An elderly soldier sits among trophies of past campaigns, his laurels withered and his armour relegated to memento. Edmund Blair Leighton, working in late-Victorian academic style, treats the chivalric subject with antiquarian precision and the warm anecdotal sentiment of the high Salon.
credit: Bukowskis – Arts & Business, Important Spring Sale 649, 14 June 2023, lot 809

title: Field with plowing farmers
description: A Provençal field worked by oxen and ploughman, painted while Van Gogh was a voluntary patient at the asylum of Saint-Paul-de-Mausole near Saint-Rémy. The furrows roll across the canvas in his characteristic ribbon-like impasto, an homage to the peasant labour Millet had pictured a generation earlier.
credit: 1. Copied from an art book 2. bing.com

title: Garden of Saint-Paul Hospital with Figure
description: A view into the walled garden of the Saint-Paul-de-Mausole asylum, where Van Gogh was a voluntary patient between 1889 and 1890. A solitary figure passes between trees rendered in writhing, rope-like strokes; the picture is one of many in which the cloister grounds become both subject and consolation.

title: Green wheat field
description: A Provençal field rendered in surging diagonal brushstrokes, the unripe wheat treated as a tapestry of green and blue rhythms. Painted at Saint-Rémy, the canvas demonstrates the artist's habit of converting agricultural labor's quiet subjects into studies of pictorial energy.

title: Landscape from Saint-Rémy
description: A view of the wheat fields and olive groves surrounding the asylum at Saint-Rémy-de-Provence, with the Alpilles ridges rising in the distance. Curling, parallel brushstrokes animate the soil, foliage, and sky alike, dissolving the topography into a continuous vibrating surface.
credit: AQGubXY-kY8jSg at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Lion tentant de saisir un papillon
description: A lion stretches from a rocky perch to bat at a butterfly, the towering predator turned momentarily playful. Gérôme treats the animal with the precise anatomical finish of his academic training, while the desert setting reflects the Orientalist subjects he favoured in his late career.

title: Olive Trees with the Alpilles in the Background
description: Gnarled olives churn in the foreground while the limestone Alpilles rise behind under a clouded sky, painted from the grounds of Saint-Paul asylum. Now at MoMA, it belongs to the group of about fifteen olive-grove canvases Van Gogh devoted to the trees during his Saint-Rémy year.
credit: Merged from fragments found at MoMA.org

title: Peach Trees in Blossom
description: A stand of peach trees in full bloom on the plain of La Crau near Arles, painted in the burst of early Provençal spring. Pink blossom is set against fresh green grass and a clear blue sky, part of a series the artist conceived as a tribute to his recently deceased cousin Anton Mauve.

title: Peasant Woman Binding Sheaves
description: Painted at the asylum in Saint-Rémy after a Millet print, part of Van Gogh's series translating the older peasant painter into his own thick, rhythmic brushwork. The stooping woman and golden sheaves are reworked in the saturated yellows and ultramarines of his final Provençal year, now in the Van Gogh Museum.

title: Portrait of general and statesman Mikhail Ivanovich Dragomirov
description: A bust-length state portrait of the Russian general and military theorist, hero of the crossing of the Danube in 1877. Repin works in his vigorous realist manner, building the weathered face and braided uniform from loaded, broken strokes.

title: Rain
credit: 9gHQk4P6B28sgA at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Self-portrait
description: Painted at the asylum of Saint-Rémy weeks after a severe breakdown, the artist faces the viewer in a green coat against a swirling pale-blue ground. The looping, vibrating brushstrokes that animate background and clothing alike are characteristic of the self-portraits with which Van Gogh tracked his own state of mind.

title: Self-portrait
description: A frontal self-portrait painted at the Saint-Rémy asylum, the face modeled in cool greens against a turquoise ground worked in tightly curling strokes. The artist holds himself rigid and clear-eyed, the swirling background registering the disquiet of the institutional setting.
credit: 9gFw_1Vou2CkwQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The bedroom
description: A view of the artist's own bedroom in the Yellow House at Arles, repeated in a slightly later replica painted at Saint-Rémy. Tilted floor, paired chairs, and framed prints are arranged in flat planes of colour intended, in his words, to suggest absolute rest.
credit: rgHdFPzCeCfnxQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Reaper
description: A solitary peasant cutting grain in a sun-blanched field, a subject the artist linked in letters to Millet and to thoughts of mortality. The wheat is built from sweeping yellow strokes that engulf the figure, the reaper's body almost dissolving into the matter he harvests.
credit: Christie's , LotFinder: entry 6089477 (sale 14239, lot 6, London, 27 June 2017)

title: The shepherdess
description: A young shepherdess leans on her staff among the flock, a free copy after Millet made during Van Gogh's confinement at Saint-Rémy. He treats Millet's composition almost as a musical score, transposing its tones into vibrant blues, ochres and greens.

title: The Starry Night
description: A swirling night sky churns over a quiet village dominated by a dark cypress and a slender church spire. Painted from the artist's room at the Saint-Paul-de-Mausole asylum in Saint-Rémy, it reworks the view from his window with thick impasto and rhythmic, spiralling brushwork.

title: The Starry Night
description: A cypress flames upward through a sky of churning spirals above a sleeping Provençal village. Painted from memory at the Saint-Paul-de-Mausole asylum in Saint-Rémy, the canvas welds observation, symbol and rhythmic impasto.

title: The Two Pearls
description: Two nudes recline together in a calm interior, the title's 'two pearls' likening their luminous flesh to gems. Le Quesne, a Parisian Salon academician, polishes the bodies to porcelain smoothness in keeping with the late nineteenth-century French taste for refined erotic allegory.

title: The Two Pearls
description: Le Quesne pairs two nude figures in an intimate, lamp-lit setting, the title casting their pale skin as twin pearls. Exhibited within the orbit of the 1889 Paris Salon, the picture exemplifies the polished sensuality favoured by mainstream French academic painting at the century's close.

title: The white cottage among the olive trees
description: A modest farmhouse glimpsed through writhing olive trunks, painted in the months at the Saint-Paul-de-Mausole asylum. The silver-green foliage is laid down in curling, comma-like strokes that turn the orchard into a continuous nervous surface.

title: Two farmers digging over
description: Two stooped laborers turning the earth, a subject the artist returned to throughout his career as homage to Millet. The Saint-Rémy treatment swaps the dark Dutch tonalities of his earlier peasant scenes for a brighter, more rhythmic handling of soil and sky.

title: Vision of Youth Bartholomew
description: The young Bartholomew, future St Sergius of Radonezh, encounters a black-clad monk beneath a slender oak in a hushed Russian landscape. Nesterov's debut religious canvas joins thin, lyrical brushwork with a pale northern palette, founding the devout pastoral mode he would pursue across his career.

title: Wheat Field with Cypresses
description: Wheat, olive trees, a flame-shaped cypress, and the limestone ridges of the Alpilles arranged in horizontal bands under a sky of swelling cumulus. One of three closely related canvases the artist made on this motif at Saint-Rémy in the summer of 1889.

title: Wheat Field with Cypresses
description: A wheat field at Saint-Rémy with twisting cypresses on the right and the Alpilles range behind, the sky churning with rolled, scroll-like clouds. The composition belongs to a small group of variants the artist regarded as among his strongest summer landscapes.

title: Wheat Field with Reaper and Sun
description: A solitary reaper bends amid blazing yellow stalks beneath a swollen sun. Painted from Van Gogh's window at the Saint-Paul asylum in Saint-Remy, the scene was for him an emblem of human labour and mortality bound to nature's cycles.

title: Wheatfield with a reaper
description: A walled wheat field at Saint-Rémy with a single reaper at work in the high sun, the grain rendered as a near-uniform sheet of yellow. The artist described the figure as an image of humanity being mown down like the wheat, an idea that recurs across the late Provençal landscapes.
credit: BgFGcS3ucZqeRA at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Zaandam2
description: Dutch windmills line a low horizon while flat water carries their reflections under a wide sky. The etching belongs to Whistler's later European tours, where his economical needlework and selective wiping of the plate convert a mundane port into a tonal study close in spirit to his Nocturnes.

title: A Street Scene
