description: A shin-hanga woodblock print depicting a quiet stretch of the Nikkō Kaidō, the historic highway leading to the Tokugawa mausoleum. Hasui captures the avenue's towering cryptomeria with the soft atmospheric tonality and precise gradation that define his landscape work.

title: Ōmori Coast
description: A view of the Ōmori coast on Tokyo Bay, drawn from Twenty Views of Tōkyō. Hasui registers the meeting of urban edge and water in soft, overcast tones, the shin-hanga answer to Hokusai and Hiroshige's earlier capital views.
credit: National Diet Library Digital Collections: Persistent ID 2586550

title: Sumō-wrestling toads by Hōson
description: Two toads grapple in a sumō ring while companions wait their turn, a gentle parody of the Edo wrestling print. Koson, working in the shin-hanga revival, brings naturalist observation to a comic subject with long roots in Japanese caricature.

title: Winter Night
description: From Munch's late period at Ekely, his estate outside Oslo, where the ageing painter circled the same garden and orchard motifs through every season. Bare trees stand against deep blue snow in raw, scumbled strokes, the loneliness familiar from his earlier symbolist work distilled into pure landscape.

title: Woman Before a Mirror
description: A bijin-ga in which a woman in a patterned kimono adjusts her hair before a round mirror, the reflection partly visible at the print's edge. Kotondo balances flat decorative pattern against a quietly observed private moment.
credit: Walters Art Museum : Home page Info about artwork

title: 35390682010 46099096b2 o w1449
description: A landscape catalogued as Wildenstein 1449 in Monet's oeuvre. The artist's standard practice in such canvases is to dissolve form into broken touches of complementary colour, recording transient light with a saturated, high-keyed palette.

title: A Coast Scene with Fishermen Hauling a Boat Ashore
description: Fishermen drag a boat up onto a windswept beach beneath a heavy, scudding sky. Part of the Iveagh Bequest at Kenwood, the picture sits in Turner's lifelong dialogue with seventeenth-century Dutch marine painters such as van de Velde and Cuyp. Salt spray, wet sand and weather become as much the subject as the labour.

title: Agnes Dürer
description: An intimate pen drawing inscribed "mein Agnes," showing the artist's wife in domestic dress with downcast eyes. The sheet, preserved at the Albertina in Vienna, is among the earliest surviving spousal portraits in Northern art.

title: Alain Chartier Alternative title: Margaret of Scotland
description: A romantic medieval scene in which Margaret of Scotland is said to have kissed the lips of the sleeping French poet Alain Chartier in tribute to his eloquence. The High Victorian subject suits Leighton's classicising drapery and carefully balanced courtly grouping.

title: Albinus
description: Male ecorche Rare Books Keywords: Anatomy

title: Along the River During the Qingming Festival 清明上河圖
description: A Qing court reinterpretation of the celebrated Song handscroll documenting Qingming festivities along the Bian River. Shen Yuan, an academician under the Qianlong emperor, executes the panorama in fine ink line touched with sparing ochre washes.
credit: Stitched and then resized from the original files located at: A Special Exhibition of Paintings on "Up the River During Qingming" in the Museum Collection (exhibit). Taipei: National Palace Museum.

title: An écorché figure
description: An écorché figure, rear view, with left arm extended, topmost layers of muscle stripped away, exposing lowest layers. Visible in the background is a Rhinoceros. Iconographic Collections Keywords: Bernhard Siegfried Albinus; Jan Wandelaar

title: Anatomy
description: Anatomy Iconographic Collections Keywords: Bernhard Siegfried Albinus; Charles Grignion; Bernhard Siegfried Albinus

title: Banks of the Seine at Lavacourt
description: The Seine winds past the village of Lavacourt across from Vétheuil, where Monet lived from 1878. The riverbank views he painted there mark the brightening palette and broken brushwork of his fully mature Impressionism, made under the strain of his wife Camille's final illness.
credit: Photo I took at the Frick Art Museum

title: Bust Portrait of a Young Man
description: A bust-length study of a young man, plainly dressed and turned slightly toward the viewer, kept in the Belvedere as part of the German Romantic holdings. Runge favoured this kind of clear, almost severe portrait pared back to face and gaze.

title: Carp leaping up a cascade
description: A koi battles upstream against a thundering waterfall, illustrating the Chinese legend that any carp scaling the falls of the Dragon Gate is transformed into a dragon. The motif, emblematic of perseverance and male coming-of-age, was a staple of East Asian ink painting and ukiyo-e print design.

title: Cattle Watering by a Stream
description: A pastoral scene of cattle pausing at a wooded stream, painted with the feathery, scumbled foliage typical of Gainsborough's mature landscapes. The work belongs to his Suffolk-derived rural idylls, indebted to Dutch seventeenth-century models yet handled with English freshness.
credit: xAEIetBVtfBolw at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: Cawing crow by Ohara Koson
description: A black crow opens its beak mid-call on a bare branch, the empty ground around it giving the cry pictorial space. Koson specialised in kachō-e (bird-and-flower) prints for the early-twentieth-century shin-hanga revival, exporting subdued, naturalistic studies like this one to Western collectors.

title: Danse Macabre. II. The Temptation
description: The Temptation, the second plate of Holbein's Dance of Death, depicts the serpent in Eden as the origin of mortality. Holbein opens the cycle with the Fall before Death claims figures across society in succeeding plates.
credit: Taken from Die imposante Galerie , originally uploaded by Soilwork

title: Die-kreuzabnahme-rubens
description: A Descent from the Cross after Rubens, transposing the master's Antwerp altarpiece composition into reproductive form. The diagonal sweep of Christ's pallid body lowered onto a white winding-sheet preserves the Baroque dramaturgy of the original.

title: Don Quijote illustrated by Gustav Dore V
description: A wood-engraved plate from Doré's celebrated illustrated edition of Cervantes' novel, in which the gaunt knight and his squire move through a finely hatched landscape. The attribution to Dürer is a cataloguing slip; the image belongs to Doré's nineteenth-century print cycle.

title: Elisabeth Vigée-Lebrun - Self-Portrait with Her Daughter
description: Vigée Le Brun cradles her daughter Julie in a tender embrace, both turning their bare-armed warmth to the viewer. Exhibited at the 1787 Salon, the canvas adapted antique Madonna types to revolutionary tastes for sentiment and natural maternal feeling.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"v/vigee/self_da"

title: Expulsion of Adam and Eve
description: The Expulsion of Adam and Eve, the third plate in Holbein's Dance of Death cycle, follows the Temptation as the source of human mortality. The angel drives the pair into a thorny world while Death takes up his fiddle to lead them onward.
credit: Taken from Die imposante Galerie , originally uploaded by Soilwork

title: Five Egrets Descending in Snow by Shôson
description: Five egrets glide through falling snow toward a quiet shoreline, their white forms dissolving against the pale ground. Koson, the leading kachoga (bird-and-flower) printmaker of the shin-hanga era, captures the species' winter behaviour with delicately graded woodblock impressions.

title: Fourth muscle man
description: Fourth muscle man. Rare Books Keywords: Anatomy; Vesalius

title: Garden of Eden
description: A Flemish Baroque collaboration in which Jan Wildens supplied the wooded paradise landscape and Paul de Vos populated it with the assembled animals of creation. Such division of labor was standard practice in Antwerp's specialist workshops, here producing a dense menagerie set within a luminous park-like clearing.

title: God Speed
description: A medievalising farewell scene of a knight setting forth, his lady extending a token from a balcony above. Leighton's late Academicism distils chivalric narrative into measured silhouettes and warm Mediterranean light borrowed from his studies of Italian quattrocento fresco.

title: Group with Parasols
description: Four figures recline on a grassy slope beneath parasols, their forms half-dissolved in dappled shade. Sargent treats the bourgeois leisure scene with rapid, broken brushwork, prizing the play of filtered light over portrait specificity.

title: Gynaeceum or ancient Greek Interior
description: A scene in the women's quarters of a Greek house, with nudes lounging amid braziers, drapery and patterned tile. The picture is one of Gérôme's Néo-Grec confections, dressing up an academic studio nude in archaeological props derived from Pompeian wall painting.

title: Hail Caesar! We Who Are About to Die Salute You
description: Jean-Leon Gerome's academic vision of the Roman arena shows gladiators saluting the emperor with the famous formula "Hail Caesar, those who are about to die salute you." The painting epitomizes the meticulous archaeological detail and theatrical staging of nineteenth-century French history painting.

title: Harlech Castle
description: Harlech Castle, from Tygwyn Ferry, Summer's Evening Twilig

title: Hiroshige
description: Figures hurry across a wooden bridge as a slate-grey downpour streaks the air in fine parallel lines, a device Hiroshige made his signature for sudden weather. The composition's daring crop and atmospheric mood would later astonish Whistler and Van Gogh.

title: Hiroshige A shrine among trees on a moor
description: A small wayside shrine stands half-hidden in a stand of trees on an open moor, its torii a quiet vertical accent in the landscape. Hiroshige favored such modest sacred sites in his rural views, treating them as pauses in the broader rhythm of travel.

title: Holbein d. J.; Danse Macabre
description: From the Dance of Death cycle, Holbein stages Death as an active intruder rather than an emblem, lunging into a tableau of contemporary life. The series helped fix the iconography of the danse macabre for the print era.
credit: Taken from Die imposante Galerie , originally uploaded by Soilwork

title: Holbein d. J.; Danse Macabre. XIV. The Abbot
description: The Abbot, plate XIV of the Dance of Death, shows Death yanking the prelate by his crozier, mocking ecclesiastical pomp with characteristic Reformation bite. The dense crosshatching and crowded folds typify Lützelburger's virtuoso cutting of Holbein's designs.
credit: Taken from Die imposante Galerie , originally uploaded by Soilwork

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 10
description: A plate from Holbein's Dance of Death cycle, in which Death intrudes upon a figure of contemporary estate, abbot, merchant, peasant or king. The miniature woodcuts, executed with Hans Lützelburger's exacting cutting, were an early printed vehicle for Reformation-era social critique.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 11
description: A woodcut from Holbein's celebrated Dance of Death cycle, designed in Basel and cut by Hans Lützelburger. Each compact scene stages Death intruding upon a figure of medieval estate, satirising clerical and worldly vanity with razor-sharp draughtsmanship.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 12
description: Part of the Dance of Death series first issued in Lyon in 1538, where Death visits every rung of the social ladder. The miniature woodblock concentrates dense narrative into a few square inches, a hallmark of Holbein's reformist printmaking.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 13
description: Holbein's pocket-sized memento mori reduces a moral sermon to a single arrested gesture: the skeleton's grasp, the victim's surprise. Cut by Lützelburger from Holbein's drawings, the prints circulated widely in Reformation Europe.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 15
description: Another scene from the Dance of Death series, in which Death claims a representative of the medieval social order. Holbein's tightly composed woodcuts pair grim humour with precise observation of costume and gesture.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 16
description: From the Dance of Death cycle published at Lyon, this plate confronts a worldly figure with the universal leveller. Holbein's drawing translates fluently into Lützelburger's miniature woodblock technique, reading clearly at thumbnail scale.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 17
description: A plate from Holbein's Imagines Mortis, conceived in Basel in the late 1520s. Death's skeletal cameo within an ordered domestic or civic scene gave the series its lasting force as moral and social commentary.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 19
description: The Advocate, plate XIX of the Dance of Death, finds the lawyer pocketing a coin while Death stands ready at his side. Holbein's reformist satire jabs at venal professions, the woodcut's miniature scale concentrating its sting.
credit: Taken from Die imposante Galerie , originally uploaded by Soilwork

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 20
description: A plate from the Dance of Death, where Holbein casts Death as a sly partner pulling a complacent sitter from their station. The series, cut by Lützelburger, distilled Reformation moral teaching into pictures small enough to slip into a pocket.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 21
description: From Holbein's Imagines Mortis, Death disrupts a scene of civic or domestic routine. The block's tight shading and confident outline carry Holbein's drawing into mass circulation across sixteenth-century Europe.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 22
description: Another plate in the Dance of Death sequence, joining its survey of estates from pope to peasant. Holbein's compositions remain models of pictorial economy, every line bearing narrative weight.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 23
description: From the Dance of Death, Holbein again pairs the living with the cavorting skeleton, their gestures locked in a brief, fatal duet. The Lyon edition of 1538 became a model for memento mori imagery for centuries.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 24
description: A scene from Holbein's Dance of Death cycle, in which Death steps into the everyday with grim levity. The series remains a high point of Northern Renaissance printmaking, sharpened by Reformation-era anticlerical wit.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 25
description: Plate from the Imagines Mortis cycle, where Holbein's Death overturns hierarchies one estate at a time. The miniature woodcut format encouraged private meditation on mortality alongside its public satire.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 26
description: Another tableau from the Dance of Death sequence, illustrating Death's universal claim. Holbein's draughtsmanship and Lützelburger's exacting cutting give the prints their crisp, almost engraving-like precision.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 27
description: From the Dance of Death series, a moment of social comedy turned funeral procession. Holbein's tiny woodblocks reward close looking, packing costume detail and pointed satire into a few centimetres.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 28
description: A Dance of Death plate carrying forward the cycle's procession through every rank of society. The format - small block, pithy scene, accompanying scriptural verse in the original edition - became the template for emblem books to come.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 29
description: Holbein places Death amid the routines of trade or labour, reminding the viewer that no occupation provides exemption. The Imagines Mortis remained in print across multiple Lyon editions through the sixteenth century.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 30
description: Another scene from the Dance of Death, marrying biting moral commentary to compact woodcut design. Holbein's cycle gave Reformation Europe its definitive picture of mortality stalking every estate.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 32
description: From the Dance of Death series, Holbein renders Death as both companion and antagonist, mirroring the gestures of the living. The plates retain a draughtsman's vivacity despite the resistance of the woodblock.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 33
description: A Dance of Death plate from Holbein's celebrated cycle, the figures locked in a brief contest of will and fate. Lützelburger's cutting captures every flicker of Holbein's pen-line at miniature scale.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 34
description: Part of Holbein's Imagines Mortis, the small woodcut sets a skeleton against a figure of social rank, a formula the artist varies with inexhaustible invention across the series.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 35
description: From the Dance of Death, Holbein uses the miniature woodcut as a stage for a quick, devastating morality play. The cycle pairs each image with a biblical citation underscoring its lesson.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 36
description: Another plate from the Dance of Death, depicting Death intruding on a moment of ordinary life or work. Holbein's Reformation-era satire is leavened by sympathy for the labouring poor among his subjects.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 37
description: A scene from the Dance of Death cycle, in which Holbein's skeleton mimics or mocks the trade of his victim. The miniature woodcut carries an outsized influence on later memento mori imagery across European print culture.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 38
description: From the Imagines Mortis series, this plate continues Holbein's procession through the social order. Designed in Basel and printed in Lyon, the cycle balances religious gravity with sharp-edged worldly humour.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 39
description: Another woodcut from Holbein's Dance of Death, the cycle's closing plates moving toward the Last Judgement. The series remains a touchstone of Northern Renaissance printmaking, both for its design and for Lützelburger's astonishing cutting.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 4
description: A woodcut from Hans Holbein the Younger's celebrated Dance of Death cycle, designed in the 1520s and first published at Lyon in 1538. Skeletons drag the living from every social rank, the moral message compressed into miniature scenes carved with extraordinary economy of line.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 40
description: Plate from Holbein's Dance of Death, the cycle of small woodcuts in which Death claims figures from every estate of medieval society. The compact composition is characteristic of Holbein's collaboration with the Lyon block-cutter Hans Lützelburger, whose precise carving gave the prints their incisive bite.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 41
description: From Holbein's Dance of Death, an unsparing memento mori in miniature woodcut. Death intrudes on daily life with theatrical bluntness, the densely worked black lines reflecting the early sixteenth-century northern fascination with mortality and judgement.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 42
description: A page from Holbein's Dance of Death, designed in Basel and printed at Lyon in 1538. The series subjected pope, emperor, merchant, and ploughman alike to Death's hand, channelling Reformation-era anxiety through tightly composed woodcut tableaux.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 43
description: From Holbein's Dance of Death cycle, in which a capering skeleton interrupts the routines of medieval life. The print's terse narrative and crisp black-line carving exemplify the small-format moralising woodcut perfected in early sixteenth-century Basel.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 44
description: Plate from Holbein's Dance of Death, a sequence of moral vignettes first issued at Lyon in 1538. Each scene confronts a different rank of society with mortality, the figures animated by Holbein's economical draftsmanship and the precision of Hans Lützelburger's cutting.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 45
description: An image from Holbein's Dance of Death, the German painter's most widely circulated print cycle. Death is staged as both intruder and equaliser, a theme rooted in late medieval European tradition but rendered with Renaissance compositional clarity.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 46
description: From Holbein's Dance of Death, a series of forty-one woodcuts in which Death appears as skeletal companion to every walk of life. The prints became a touchstone of Reformation-era moral imagery and shaped the iconography of mortality for centuries.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 47
description: Plate from Holbein's Dance of Death, designed around 1526 and printed in Lyon a decade later. Crammed into a tight rectangular field, the scene typifies the cycle's blend of social satire and pious warning.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 48
description: From Holbein's Dance of Death, where Death stalks every estate of society with grim impartiality. The miniature woodcut format favoured by Holbein concentrates psychological force into a few square inches of black line.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 49
description: A late plate in Holbein's Dance of Death cycle, a programme of small woodcuts whose dark humour and moral urgency defined the German Renaissance memento mori. The figures are sharply observed, the architectural settings reduced to telling props.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 5
description: Plate from Holbein's Dance of Death, the influential moralising sequence designed in Basel in the 1520s. Death descends without warning on princes and labourers alike, a theme intensified by the woodcut's confined format and emphatic shadows.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 6
description: From Holbein's Dance of Death cycle, in which the skeletal figure of mortality breaks into an everyday scene with sardonic relish. The series helped fix the visual vocabulary of the danse macabre for European print culture.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 7
description: An image from Holbein's Dance of Death, a Reformation-era meditation on mortality issued in Lyon in 1538. Death's intrusion is staged with theatrical compression, every gesture pared to its essential meaning.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 8
description: Plate from Holbein the Younger's Dance of Death, where skeletons enact a mocking equality across every social rank. The cut is sharply contoured, drawing on the Basel woodcut tradition that Holbein elevated to a new pictorial intelligence.

title: Holbein Danse Macabre 9
description: From Holbein's Dance of Death sequence, designed about 1526 and widely reprinted across Europe. Death's grip is sudden and absolute; Holbein's command of small-scale figure groups gives each plate the punch of a self-contained drama.

title: Hunting on the Lagoon
description: Venetian patricians shoot at cormorants from flat-bottomed boats on the still waters of the lagoon. Originally the lower half of a larger panel — its upper half is the Two Venetian Ladies in the Museo Correr — Carpaccio renders the scene in the crystalline Quattrocento detail typical of his narrative cycles.

title: Inada Kyuzo Shinsuke
description: A sheet from Yoshitoshi's series Eimei nijūhasshūku (Twenty-eight Famous Murders with Verse), produced with Yoshiiku in 1866–67. The print exemplifies the late ukiyo-e taste for muzan-e — bloody, theatrical scenes drawn from kabuki and historical legend.

title: Ingres - Mme Victor Baltard & sa fille Paule
description: A graphite portrait of Adeline Baltard, wife of the architect Victor Baltard, with their young daughter Paule on her lap. Ingres produced hundreds of such silvery pencil portraits for friends and patrons, prized for the precision of facial likeness against lightly indicated dress.

title: Interior
description: Gustave Caillebotte Intérieur

title: Kisokaido14 Itahana
description: The fourteenth station on the inland Kisokaidō highway, from the celebrated series Eisen and Hiroshige produced jointly in the 1830s. Eisen contributes a snowbound village under a heavy grey sky, peasants leaning into the wind in the bokashi-graded foreground.
credit: The Sixty-Nine Stations of the Kisokaido

title: Kuniyoshi Utagawa
description: A hawk poised in profile, its plumage detailed in the layered keyblock and colour blocks of an ukiyo-e print. Hawks were a favoured subject of late Edo print designers, the bird's predatory stillness suiting the medium's graphic economy.

title: Kuniyoshi Utagawa
description: A waterside Edo scene along the Sumida, the river that defined the city's ferry traffic, festivals and pleasure boating. Kuniyoshi, an Utagawa-school designer best known for warrior prints, here turns to a quieter genre subject in the bold linework typical of late ukiyo-e.

title: Kuniyoshi Utagawa
description: Devotees perform misogi, the Shinto cold-water purification rite, beneath a thundering cascade. Kuniyoshi exploits the vertical ōban format to dramatise the columnar fall of water, a theatrical staging that recalls his celebrated heroic and supernatural compositions.

title: Landscape with the Fall of Icarus
description: A ploughman, shepherd, and angler go about their work along a sunlit harbor while Icarus's legs vanish almost unnoticed into the sea at lower right. The composition reads as a quiet meditation on Ovid's myth and human indifference, later memorialized in Auden's poem.

provenance.collection: Royal Museums of Fine Arts of Belgium

title: Leonardo da Vinci - Virgin and Child with St Anne
description: Saint Anne supports the Virgin on her lap as Mary reaches for the Christ child, who plays with a lamb foreshadowing the Passion. Left unfinished at the artist's death and now in the Louvre, the panel is celebrated for its pyramidal grouping, soft sfumato modelling and atmospheric mountainous landscape.
credit: original file: C2RMF: Galerie de tableaux en très haute définition : image page

title: Lion Hunt
description: Mounted hunters and lions tangle in violent diagonal motion, men and beasts pulled into a single churning mass. This small oil sketch, held by the Alte Pinakothek in Munich, is a preparatory modello for one of Rubens's larger hunt scenes, with the brushwork left openly visible.

provenance.location: Alte Pinakothek, upper floor, small gallery 12

title: Lucretia
description: Veronese's Lucretia presses the dagger to her breast in the Roman matron's act of self-destruction after her violation by Tarquin. The painter dresses the heroine in Venetian silks, fusing classical exemplum with sixteenth-century courtly elegance.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"v/veronese/10/4lucretia"

title: MB-Mon-16 Monet Les-Petites-Dalles-bei-Ebbe
description: A coastal study of Les Petites-Dalles on the Normandy shore at low tide, where Monet records the chalk cliffs and exposed shingle in feathered, broken brushstrokes. The painting belongs to the artist's recurring engagement with the Channel coast, tracking shifting light and atmosphere over a familiar stretch of beach.

title: Mitsuke-juku
description: Mitsuke, the twenty-eighth station of the Tokaido road, depicting travelers crossing the Tenryu River by ferry. Hiroshige's woodblock design uses graded blue bokashi and a low horizon to evoke morning mist over the water.
credit: The Fifty-three Stations of the Tokaido

title: Monet 1401
description: A view of the fjord at Christiania (now Oslo), painted on Monet's 1895 Norwegian campaign while visiting his stepson. Snow-bound water and shoreline are rendered in cool blues and pinks, his northern palette pushed toward near-monochrome refinement.

title: Monet w 290 autumn effect in argenteuil
description: A view along the Seine at Argenteuil, where Monet was based through much of the 1870s, painted at the moment when foliage flares into copper and gold. The water carries the autumn colour back as a lattice of broken reflections, a hallmark of his river pictures from these years.

title: Monet w 763 the path
description: A garden or woodland path drawing the viewer between flanking masses of foliage, painted with the interwoven greens and lilacs typical of Monet's Giverny work. The motif reduces landscape to a corridor of pure colour and dappled shade.

title: Monet w0145
description: A snow-bound landscape at sunset, a subject Monet returned to repeatedly through his career to test the chromatic limits of white. Pale pinks and lilacs warm the drifts as the low sun stains the horizon.

title: Monet w1010
description: Catalogued in Wildenstein's raisonné among Monet's Giverny-era studies, the canvas works through his recurring concerns of water, foliage, and reflected sky. Pigment is laid in short broken strokes that read as both atmosphere and surface pattern.

title: Monet w1018
description: An undated landscape from Monet's mature Impressionist output, distinguished by its all-over weave of small, nervous strokes. The motif is subordinated to the choreography of light playing across surface.

title: Monet w1032
description: A coastal view of sailboats near the Aiguille (Rock Needle) at Étretat, the Norman landmark Monet painted obsessively through the 1880s. Sail and chalk arch are reduced to bright shapes against a sea of broken blue-greens.

title: Monet w1048
description: Étretat's Porte d'Amont arch under storm, painted from a clifftop vantage in heavy weather. Wind and surf are rendered in slashing diagonals of green and slate, the chalk headland barely holding its silhouette.

title: Monet w1061
description: A panoramic view of Vernon, the small Norman town just downriver from Giverny, with its church tower mirrored in the Seine. Monet flattens the composition into horizontal bands of water, masonry and sky, dissolving architecture into reflective light.

title: Monet w1121
description: Rain sweeping across the cliffs of Belle-Île, the sea and sky fused by a grey veil. Monet rendered the downpour with diagonal scumbles laid over a darker ground, an Atlantic counterpart to the foggier studies he would later make in London.

title: Monet w1138
description: Iris beds in flower at Giverny, where the artist deliberately planted in painterly bands of colour. Violet and yellow notes are stitched together with rapid strokes, prefiguring the immersive perspectives of the later Water Lilies.

title: Monet w1200
description: Morning light over a Giverny meadow, painted in the soft, iridescent register Monet reserved for daybreak. Pinks and pale blues are stippled into the grasses, the horizon line lifted high to flatten the field into a tapestry of colour.

title: Monet w1245
description: A spring effect in the Giverny garden, where Monet tracked the season's earliest blossom against fresh foliage. The surface is a dense weave of rose, cream and acid green, the structure of trees nearly absorbed into pure colour.

title: Monet w1336
description: Ice floes drifting on the Seine near Bennecourt, a subject Monet had treated since the great freeze of 1880. Cold violets and pale ochres render the half-frozen water, the bare bankside trees handled in spare vertical accents.

title: Monet w1524
description: From the Charing Cross Bridge series painted on visits to London between 1899 and 1904. Monet worked from a balcony at the Savoy, watching trains cross the Thames as fog and smoke turned bridge and water into shimmering bars of mauve and gold.

title: Monet w1558
description: Part of the Waterloo Bridge cycle observed from the Savoy Hotel, in which Monet pursued the Thames under every gradation of London weather. Here the overcast light flattens architecture and water into closely keyed greys and lilacs.

title: Monet w1656
description: A late work from the Giverny garden, painted as Monet's failing eyesight pushed his colour into hotter, more arbitrary registers. Form softens into broad, kneaded strokes that read as foliage and water rather than describing them.

title: Monet w1675
description: A Water Lilies canvas from Monet's long meditation on the lily pond he engineered at Giverny in 1893. Sky and pads share a single reflective surface, the eye pulled into a horizonless field of greens, blues and pink blossom.

title: Monet w1695
description: Another study from the Nymphéas, the cycle that occupied the artist for the last three decades of his life. The pond's surface dissolves boundary between water and sky, painted in long, drifting strokes of mauve, jade and cream.

title: Monet w1726
description: One of the water-lily paintings made in the studio at Giverny, where Monet returned obsessively to the pond he had dug himself. The viewpoint is tilted down so the surface fills the frame, broken only by floating pads and the soft mirror of overhanging willows.

title: Monet w1976
description: A late work in Monet's water-garden idiom, where pond, foliage and reflected sky merge into a single chromatic field. The surface is heavily worked, each pass of the brush adjusting temperature rather than drawing form.

title: Monet w224
description: A riverbank composition from Monet's Argenteuil years, where the Seine and its boats supplied an inexhaustible motif. The horizon sits low, leaving most of the canvas to sky and shimmering water.

title: Monet w24
description: A road by the Saint-Simeon farm near Honfleur, from Monet's earliest Normandy campaigns. The handling still carries the firm tonal modelling of Boudin and Jongkind, his coastal mentors, before the broken Impressionist stroke took hold.

title: Monet w288 the sheltered path
description: A wooded path enclosed by foliage, painted in the green-saturated register Monet favoured for shaded interiors of the landscape. Sunlight reaches the ground only in scattered patches, scumbled in pale yellow over deeper greens.

title: Monet w421
description: A landscape in Monet's mature Argenteuil-into-Vetheuil manner, with foliage rendered as a mosaic of small directional touches. Local colour is constantly inflected by reflected light from sky and ground.

title: Monet w526
description: A riverside motif in Monet's Vetheuil-period idiom, with broken touches of green, ochre and blue building both bank and water. The picture is held together by a single envelope of weather rather than firm drawing.

title: Monet w648
description: An undated landscape from the Normandy or Vetheuil years, showing the artist's signature open-air handling. The composition relies on broken colour and atmospheric perspective rather than drawn detail, a hallmark of his mature Impressionist practice.

title: Monet w849
description: A vase of poppies treated as a study in pure chromatic punch, the scarlet blossoms blazing against a muted ground. Monet handles the bouquet with the same plein-air immediacy he brought to landscape, letting brushwork stand for petal and leaf.

title: Monet w871
description: A study of olive trees made during Monet's stay at Bordighera on the Italian Riviera, where he marvelled at light he found nearly impossible to render. The silvery foliage is broken into nervous, overlapping strokes against intense Mediterranean blues.

title: Monet w875
description: Palms at Bordighera, painted on Monet's 1884 trip to the Riviera, where he wrote of needing a palette of diamonds and gemstones to match the light. The fronds are rendered as quick, calligraphic flicks against saturated southern hues.

title: Monet w904
description: The chalk cliffs at Les Petites-Dalles on the Normandy coast, viewed across an unsettled sea. Monet articulates the cliff face with cool mauves and creams while the water carries his characteristic broken, comma-shaped touch.

title: Monet w922
description: A close study of jonquils, the yellow blooms set against muted greens to make colour the principal subject. Monet treats the flowers with quick, loaded strokes, the kind of small still life he often painted between larger landscape campaigns.

title: Monet w928
description: A second take on jonquils from the same period, the bouquet handled with rapid, plein-air directness. The bright trumpets read as concentrated daubs of pigment rather than drawn forms, the ground kept loose and tonal.

title: Monet w982
description: Slender trees line the riverbank in a tapestry of green and lavender, their reflections broken into short rhythmic strokes. The motif belongs to the long sequence of Giverny river scenes that anchored Monet's late practice in his own garden landscape.

title: Monet w986
description: A Giverny meadow caught at first light, with dew and pale sun softening the field into pastel veils of pink and green. Monet's preoccupation with the morning effect produced canvases in which the subject is essentially the quality of the air itself.

title: Monet w996
description: A flowering field rendered as a dense weave of dabbed blossoms, the eye coaxed across the surface rather than into deep space. Such meadow paintings exemplify Monet's flattening of landscape into a tapestry of pure colour notes.

title: Natoire-Sibylle
description: A French Rococo study of a sibyl, the prophetess of antique tradition shown in a moment of inspired contemplation. The drawing employs the fluid contours and idealized features that characterized academic figure work in mid-eighteenth-century Paris.

title: Old Woman with a Basket of Coal
description: An aged peasant woman bent beneath a heavy basket of coal, her weathered face caught in firelit chiaroscuro. Painted in Rubens's late manner, the unsentimental study of labor and age departs from his courtly subjects, prizing physiognomic truth and tactile flesh.

title: Paolo Veronese 008
description: A reproduction documenting a painting by the Venetian master Paolo Veronese, photographed without its frame for archival study. Veronese's output spans grand religious cycles, mythologies, and ceiling allegories executed across sixteenth-century Venice.
credit: This image has an extracted file : Paolo Veronese 008 - selfportrait.jpg .

title: Paul Gustave Dore Andromeda
description: Gustave Dore's vision of Andromeda, the Ethiopian princess chained to a sea cliff awaiting Perseus's rescue. The composition exploits Dore's signature contrast of luminous flesh against turbulent rock and sky, a Romantic theatricality refined through years of book illustration. This file represents a digitally restored version of the image.

title: Portrait of a Monk
description: A bearded religious in dark habit emerges from a shadowed ground, his lined face caught in raking light. Rubens repeatedly studied such ascetic types as preparatory heads for his larger altarpieces of saints and church fathers.

title: Rakuchū rakugai zu
description: A panoramic bird's-eye survey of Kyoto in and around the capital, with thousands of tiny figures threading temples, markets and noble compounds amid gilded clouds. Eitoku codified the rakuchū rakugai zu format for the Momoyama elite; this pair was reportedly given by Oda Nobunaga to Uesugi Kenshin.

title: Red and Pink Poppies
description: Red and Pink Poppies
credit: Claude Monet - Red and Pink Poppies , Irina , 25 December 2013, 02:31

title: Ribbons and Laces for Very Pretty Faces
description: A pretty girl shopping for trimmings at an Old-English haberdasher's stall, the title quoting a popular street-cry. Blair Leighton's love of period costume drama and finely modelled drapery gives the trivial subject the weight of a small historical tableau.

title: Rogier van der Weyden - Triptych- The Crucifixion
description: Christ on the Cross occupies the central panel, flanked by donor figures and saints in the wings, set against gilded and architectural backgrounds. The triptych shows the precise drapery, restrained gesture and devotional intensity characteristic of fifteenth-century Netherlandish panel painting.

title: Saint Sebastian tended by two angels
description: After the Roman martyr's ordeal at the column, two angels withdraw the arrows and bind his wounds. Rubens treats Sebastian's nude body in the heroic manner of antique sculpture and Italian masters, softening the violence into a Baroque tableau of muscular grace.

title: Searching for Immortals
description: China; Album; Paintings
credit: This file was donated to Wikimedia Commons as part of a project by the Metropolitan Museum of Art . See the Image and Data Resources Open Access Policy

title: Snowscape
objectKey: collection-of-beauty/雪景图轴.传宋.马远.画.纸本设色.台北故宫博物院藏.jpg

title: Stitching the Standard
description: A young woman bends over a heraldic banner she is sewing for war, posed in profile against an architectural setting. Leighton uses the medieval-revival subject to display the sculptural drapery and serene contour that defined his presidency of the Royal Academy.

title: Summer
description: A personification of Summer as harvest and abundance, with peasants gathering grain beneath a glowing sky. Part of a seasonal cycle, the image links rural labor to Flemish landscape tradition and to Rubens's late absorption in country life at Het Steen.
credit: Web Gallery of Art : Image Info about artwork wga QS:P11807,"/r/rubens/5landsca/04landsc"

provenance.collection: Museum of Fine Arts of Nancy
provenance.location: Museum of Fine Arts of Nancy

title: Tab V
description: V, fetus attached to umbilical cord and placenta. Wellcome Images Keywords: Bernhard Siegfried Albinus

title: Tamakatzura Tamatori attacked by the octopus
description: The ama diver Tamatori clutches the Dragon King's pearl as sea creatures swarm in pursuit. Kuniyoshi treats the legendary recovery dive as a swirling, kinetic composition typical of his late ukiyo-e narrative prints.

title: Tamatori being pursued bya dragon
description: A second telling of the Tamatori legend, with the diver fleeing the underwater palace as a dragon and its attendants give chase. Kuniyoshi exploits the woodblock medium's capacity for sweeping diagonals and undulating tendrils of marine life.
credit: Visipix . Archived from the original on 2016-01-09 . Retrieved on 2005-09-08 .

title: The Accolade
description: A medieval-revival fantasy of a queen knighting a young warrior with the flat of a sword, set in a sun-streaked palace hall. The picture is by Edmund Blair Leighton, a late Victorian academic painter whose chivalric scenes drew on Pre-Raphaelite costume study and theatre design.

title: The Big wave from 100 views of the Fuji
description: A page from Hokusai's later, more contemplative woodblock series One Hundred Views of Mount Fuji, in which a curling wave frames the distant sacred peak. Printed in monochrome ink with discreet grey gradations, it reprises in book form the motif of his earlier Great Wave.

title: The Blue Grotto on Capri
description: Sunlight refracted through the submerged entrance of Capri's Grotta Azzurra fills the cavern with the cobalt glow that made it a Grand Tour pilgrimage site. The picture is in fact by Berlin landscapist Carl Friedrich Seiffert, who specialised in luminous Italian veduta.

title: The Butcher's Shop
description: A monumental treatment of a humble subject, with carcasses, scales and aproned tradesmen rendered at near life size. Carracci's interest in low-life genre, drawn from northern prints and direct observation, gives the scene an unusually frank, reformist realism for its date.
credit: fwE_t62Eg-rHIQ at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

title: The Dedication
description: A medieval lady offering or receiving a token of devotion in a candlelit chapel-like interior. Long known only through a 1908 photogravure, the picture is a classic exercise in Blair Leighton's chivalric romanticism, with meticulously rendered velvets and enamels.
credit: Christie's , LotFinder: entry 5755480 (sale 1177, lot 49, London, King Street, 12 December 2013)

title: The Disasters of War
description: Plate 4 of the Disasters of War, captioned 'The women give courage'. Goya shows female combatants resisting French troops during the Peninsular War, the etching and aquatint biting through the page in his characteristically stark contrasts of black and gnawed white.

title: The Disasters of War
description: Plate 46 of the Disasters of War, 'This is bad'. A friar is set upon amid scattered bodies, the scene drawn with the cramped, shallow space and bitten line that give the series its grim moral force. The plates were withheld until decades after Goya's death.

title: The Disasters of War
description: Plate 47 of the Disasters of War, 'This is how it happened'. A clergyman is dragged and despoiled in the chaos of war, Goya pairing terse caption and unsparing image in the documentary mode that distinguishes the series from any heroic battle tradition.

title: The End of the Song
description: ] Although The End of the Song illustrates no specific incident in history or literature, it recalls two well-known literary subjects, Tristram and Iseult and Paolo and Francesca . [. ] (Christie's)

title: The Hostage
description: A noblewoman delivered up as surety in a medieval baron's hall, her isolation underscored by the encircling knights and heavy stonework. The picture is one of Blair Leighton's late chivalric tableaux, painted with the costume-drama precision that kept his Royal Academy audience loyal.

title: The King and the Beggar-maid
description: Drawn from the Tennyson ballad of King Cophetua, the painting shows the monarch descending his throne to a barefoot beggar girl. Leighton handles the asymmetric encounter with marble-cool surfaces and tightly modelled drapery, in keeping with his Olympian late style.

title: The Lion and the Mouse
description: An Aesop subject likely painted in collaboration with Frans Snyders, who handled the animals while Rubens supplied the human figure. The freed lion crouches beside the sleeping man as the tiny mouse gnaws through the rope.

title: The Magdalen with the Smoking Flame
description: The penitent saint sits in shadow with hand on skull, contemplating an oil lamp whose smoking wick stands for the brevity of life. One of several Magdalen meditations La Tour painted, it reduces the iconography to flame, flesh and the geometry of darkness.
credit: nQFyqKK8PYIYTA at Google Cultural Institute maximum zoom level

provenance.collection: Los Angeles County Museum of Art
provenance.location: Los Angeles County Museum of Art

title: The Moscow Cathedrals and river Moskva
description: A view across the thawing Moskva toward the bulbous domes of the Kremlin cathedrals. Vereshchagin, better known as a war painter, here records the Russian capital with the same documentary precision he brought to his battlefield reportage.

title: The New-born
description: A tightly cropped nocturne in which a woman shields a candle as another presents a swaddled child, often read as a Nativity in contemporary dress. The smooth, sculptural surfaces and concealed flame are hallmarks of La Tour's late Lorraine candlelit manner.
credit: The Yorck Project (2002) 10.000 Meisterwerke der Malerei (DVD-ROM), distributed by DIRECTMEDIA Publishing GmbH. ISBN : 3936122202 .

title: The Nubian Palace Guard
description: A black-skinned guard in scarlet livery stands sentinel before a carved mashrabiyya screen, halberd at his shoulder. Deutsch returned repeatedly to the figure of the palace doorkeeper, polishing each surface to an almost photographic enamel finish.

title: The sleeping hermit
description: An aged hermit has nodded off in his rocky retreat, breviary slipping from his hand. Spitzweg favoured these solitary cassocked figures as Biedermeier emblems of harmless retreat from the noisy modern world.

title: The Spy
description: A solitary informant figure rendered with the documentary literalism for which the Russian battle painter was known. Vereshchagin's military scenes typically reject heroic convention in favor of the bleak human detail of campaign life.

title: The story of Nastagio degli Onesti
description: From the four-panel Boccaccio cycle commissioned by Lorenzo de' Medici for the Pucci-Bini wedding, this episode shows the cursed huntsman pursuing his lover through a pine wood at Ravenna. Botticelli unfolds the moral tale across staged tableaux of refined courtly pageantry.

title: The-moreno-garden-at-bordighera
description: Painted during Monet's 1884 stay on the Italian Riviera, the canvas captures the orange-and-olive grove of the Moreno estate at Bordighera. Tangled trunks and a brilliant Mediterranean palette mark the artist's response to a southern light that he likened to fairyland.

title: Three Friends of Winter 歲寒三友
description: An ink handscroll bringing together pine, bamboo and flowering plum, the classical scholarly emblems of resilience that endure through winter. Zhao Mengjian, a Song-dynasty literatus, brushes the trio in spare, calligraphic strokes that fuse poetry, painting and personal virtue.
credit: Upload of August 2011: Qin, Xiaoyi [editor] (1995). Songdai shuhua ceye mingpin tezhan - Famous Album Leaves of the Sung Dynasty . Taipei: Guoli gugong bowuyuan pianzhuan weiyuanhui. p. 222. Cited in: Ebrey, Patricia Buckley et al . Bamboo, Plum, and Other Plants . A Visual Sourcebook of Chinese Civilization (online reference work). Upload of April 2018: Not given. Upload of May 2024: Cropped from image downloaded at 墨林拔萃 冊 宋趙孟堅歲寒三友圖 . 故宮典藏資料檢索. Taipei: National Palace Museum.

title: Utagawa Kunisada
description: A snow scene at Ueno from a nishiki-e album by Kunisada, the leading designer of the late ukiyo-e school. The print uses the muted palette and silent atmosphere appropriate to the bosetsu, or evening snow, theme inherited from classical Chinese poetry.
credit: National Diet Library Digital Collections: Persistent ID 1301809

title: Vase of Flowers with a Coffee Cup
description: Vase of Flowers with a Coffee Cup
credit: Henri Fantin-Latour - Vase of Flowers with a Coffee Cup , Irina , 2014-04-05 21:59

title: Vesalius Fabrica p163
description: A woodcut from Andreas Vesalius's De humani corporis fabrica of 1543, the foundational atlas of Renaissance anatomy. Cut in Venice after drawings associated with Titian's workshop, the plates pose flayed figures in landscape settings to wed scientific demonstration to humanist art.

title: Vesalius Fabrica p184
description: A muscle-man plate from Vesalius's De humani corporis fabrica, depicting progressive layers of dissection with the cadaver theatrically standing in a Paduan-style landscape. The series broke with medieval anatomical convention by presenting direct observation as the basis of medical knowledge.

title: Water Lilies
description: A study of water lilies drifting on the surface of the Giverny pond, where reflected sky and submerged stems dissolve any fixed horizon. Monet returned to this motif obsessively in his final decades, building dense, broken touches of pigment into a shimmering field of colour.

title: Wildenstein 1996
description: Catalogued in Daniel Wildenstein's monumental Monet raisonné, this canvas belongs to the artist's late Normandy period, when he returned repeatedly to the cliffs near Pourville and Varengeville. Loose, broken brushwork dissolves form into atmospheric colour, the hallmark of his mature Impressionist practice.

title: Wildenstein 1996
description: From Monet's Giverny years, this work reflects his sustained meditation on water, reflection, and shifting light around the property he had transformed into a living motif. The palette favours violet-blues and greens applied in dense, woven strokes that flatten depth into surface.

title: Wildenstein 1996
description: An early Monet study from his formative years on the Channel coast, when he absorbed Boudin's plein-air practice and Jongkind's atmospheric handling of sky and sea. The brushwork is comparatively descriptive, anticipating the looser facture of the following decade.

title: Yoshitoshi The Ghost
description: A spectral female figure with loosened black hair and pallid skin hovers against an empty ground, a staple of the Japanese yurei tradition. Yoshitoshi was a defining late ukiyo-e designer of ghost imagery, drawing on kabuki stagecraft to give such apparitions their quiet, gravity-loosened menace.

title: 名绘集珍册 9 宋 李迪 春潮带雨
description: A Southern Song album leaf depicting a spring tide laden with rain, attributed to the court painter Li Di. Soft ink wash and atmospheric distance reflect the lyrical small-format landscape tradition cultivated at the Hangzhou academy.
objectKey: collection-of-beauty/名绘集珍册_9_宋_李迪_春潮带雨.jpg
